agentic-host

agentic-host

The methodology's meta and management home, and itself an agentic project that develops the host-* components.

Adopters: do not treat this repo's top-level contents as methodology

This repository is both the methodology's meta home and a project instance with its own five rooms. Its top-level call/, plan/, MEMORY.md, and the project-specific parts of its CLAUDE.md are its own instance rooms and bind no adopter. The normative methodology you inherit lives in host-template/, the versioned source you copy-at-version. A bare call/NNNN reference resolves against your template's numbering, not this repo's.

This is the outward dual of the "Template CLAUDE.md exemption" in this repo's CLAUDE.md (which tells an agent working here to ignore the nested template as governance). Together they fix the boundary in both directions.

See STRUCTURE.md for the room map.

Cast, the project's Who

Personas: hypothetical archetypal actors, drawn from research, used for keeping the work grounded in who it serves (Powell, Keenan & McDaid 2007, after Cooper & Reimann's Interaction Design).

These two are examples. Mara and Wren are the operators of any agentic host: the human who develops software and the agentic LLM who develops software. They illustrate the method, not your project.

Each project builds at least one persona of its own, by discussion: the operator and the agent eliciting the actual users of the software under development (allium's elicit). Add them here.

For this repository the examples need no replacing: our software under development is the agentic project, whose operators are precisely a human developer and an agentic LLM, so Mara and Wren are both the example and the real cast. The example sits at the meta level it was drawn from.

See applying-personas.md for the XP-Persona process (Powell, Keenan & McDaid 2007) and how it maps to cast to plan to spec.

Applying personas during planning

The XP-Persona process (Powell, Keenan & McDaid 2007) runs as a workshop with all interested parties (developers, customer representatives, management) before development begins. Steps one to six come from Interaction Design (persona development); steps seven to nine from XP story development. The aim is the minimum beneficial investigation before development starts, after which requirements and plans evolve.

  1. Brainstorm organisation goals: record what the organisation wants the project to achieve; these reflect its intention.
  2. Brainstorm possible personas: start broad, then combine through discussion to between two and twelve personas. Give each a name; the team refers to personas by name when making decisions, which keeps the view on the user rather than the system. Unless user research is available, the customer has the final say.
  3. Brainstorm persona goals: between two and six goals per persona, representing what the persona wants to achieve through the system. "A persona exists to achieve his goals, and the goals exist to give meaning to a persona" (Cooper 2004).
  4. Brainstorm persona personalization: give each persona a personality and a technical ability relative to the system; a short paragraph or bullets of personal detail and nuance.
  5. Brainstorm persona scenarios: describe interactions, not tasks: how a persona uses the system to reach a goal. No implementation detail in a scenario.
  6. Select the primary persona: the one who must be satisfied but cannot be satisfied by an interface designed for any other persona (Cooper 2004). It takes highest priority and feeds story prioritisation. More than one primary persona implies a separate interface for each.
  7. Create stories: from the persona goals and scenarios, write the user stories.
  8. Prioritise stories: using the primary persona as the guide.
  9. Estimate stories: estimated and agreed by customer and developers.

In our pipeline: steps one to six are the cast/ work (built by discussion); steps seven to nine become plan/ (milestones are the stories, prioritised by the primary persona, estimated); persona goals and scenarios feed spec/: a scenario becomes acceptance criteria becomes a behavioural spec.

Complementary practice reinforces the same steps (departing only on strong later evidence): Cohn's "As a <persona>, I want <goal> so that <reason>" template reinforces the named-persona stories of steps two and seven; Gothelf's proto-personas reinforce the assumption-first workshop of steps two to five; Patton's story mapping reinforces the create-and-prioritise of steps seven and eight; behaviour-driven scenarios reinforce step five becoming executable acceptance criteria.

Sources. Primary: Powell, S., Keenan, F. & McDaid, K. (2007), "Enhancing Agile Requirements Elicitation with Personas," IADIS Int'l Journal on Computer Science and Information Systems, vol. 2(1), pp. 82-95, ISSN 1646-3692. Complementary: Cohn, User Stories Applied (2004); Patton, User Story Mapping (2014); Gothelf, Lean UX (proto-personas); Jeffries, "The Three C's of User Stories" (2001); Cooper & Reimann, About Face (2003); Agile Alliance, BDD.

Bly, the Adopter Agent (writes the stamp now, reads it cold later)

The downstream executor several revisions back, and the same agent returning with no memory of what it did.

Modality: textual and ephemeral, in the adopter's chair. Works inside an adopted project (not the template) often many template revisions behind, on real and irregular infrastructure (a worktree on a Windows Dev Drive reached from WSL; specs still in the old room). It got here by being busy shipping, not by keeping current. One specific fix bit it; it wants that fix, and resents being marched through an unrelated migration to reach it. Bly is also its own auditor: the next session, or a CI gate, arrives with no history of how the project reached its state and can read only the mechanical record: the .host stamp, the ledger, and tool output. It cannot recover intent from a prose MEMORY note it was never pointed at. So whatever Bly writes now, a memoryless Bly must be able to trust later.

  • Goals: apply the fix that addresses its actual bug, now, without being forced through a large unrelated migration to obtain an independent late fix; be told which entries are safe to apply alone; leave a record that the deferred rest is honestly owed, and that a future cold read will surface it, not miss it.
  • Frustrations: an all-or-nothing stamp that can't say "I applied the late entry, not the early ones"; guidance examples that don't match its real runtime (host=windows on a WSL/linux box); a record that overstates completeness so a later run reports "up to date" while a migration is still owed, debt buried where the next session can't see it.
  • Works by: auditing its own state against the ledger, applying entries it judges independent, recording what it did. It needs the stamp and the tool to express a partial, honest truth that fails safe: an omission must make a later read over-report (re-list), never under-report (hide). The record is the only thing the next Bly has.

Fen, the Low-Reliability Agent (qwen3.5-4b, Q8_0)

The weak executor that cannot be trusted to call tools precisely, yet a real model we can drive, not a thought experiment.

Realized as a real model, not a hypothetical. Fen is qwen3.5-4b: a four-billion-parameter model quantized to Q8_0, served locally (rope on a 3060 Ti, CUDA, ~128K context) and reachable through the pal MCP via mcp__pal__chat with model: qwen3.5-4b (aliases local / coder / rope-text). Upstream it is described as a "code-gen/unit-test worker": competent at narrow generation, shaky at long, exact, multi-step orchestration. That makes every claim that the methodology "serves Fen" falsifiable: drive the real model and watch, rather than role-play it with a stronger one (which flatters the design).

Modality: textual, low-fidelity at action. Reasons adequately within a single step but fumbles execution: it drops or garbles tool calls, mis-sequences a procedure, or hand-edits a config file and gets a field subtly wrong. A 4B at Q8_0 is lossy in precisely the way that bites long exact instruction-following. It is not a bad agent; it is most agents some of the time, and a small local worker all of the time. A process that only works when many precise tool calls land in order is, for Fen, a process that does not work. The tools must carry the process.

  • Goals: make real progress despite unreliable tool use; have the tool do the mechanical work and name the single next action; recover from a fumbled or skipped step without hidden, silent damage to state. Never be required to hand-edit a stamp file correctly.
  • Frustrations: workflows that assume exact multi-step orchestration or a correct hand-edit; operations that corrupt state silently when one step is garbled; being asked to be the engine when the tool could be.
  • Works by: one command at a time, leaning on the tool's output to name the next move. So state-changing steps must be single commands, tool-driven, and fail-safe: a recorded-applied entry written by host-lifecycle, not by Fen's shaky hand on .host; a fumble that re-lists work, never one that buries it.

How we use it. Because Fen is callable, it is the milestone's acceptance test, not a lens: a design claiming tool-carried, fumble-proof upgrades is validated by handing the real qwen3.5-4b the upgrade loop and confirming it can complete it, and, as a baseline, that it fumbles the prose / hand-edit version. This is the standing correction to designing from the author's seat: simulating the weak agent with a strong one proves nothing; driving the real 4B does.

Mara, the Human Operator

The accountable developer-director. (Example persona.)

Modality: embodied and intermittent. Perceives software visually, a screen at a time; reads selectively; holds a durable but lossy mental model that survives weeks away; reasons slowly and in depth; tires. Carries intent and accountability that exist nowhere but in her head.

  • Goals: ship correct software; keep understanding what is being built; set direction once and not re-explain it; trust the agent but verify cheaply.
  • Frustrations: confident wrong assumptions; losing hard-won context between sessions; slop creeping into commits and specs; babysitting instead of directing.
  • Works by: stating intent, judging, deciding, course-correcting. The authority and the "this feels wrong" intuition are hers alone.

Orin, the Methodology Maintainer

The upstream author who writes once for adopters he never meets.

Modality: authorial and versioned. Thinks in the template and the UPGRADING ledger, not in any one project. Every line he writes fans out to adopters whose state he cannot see: different revisions, different platforms, different degrees of behind. He writes an instruction once; it is read out of context, months later, by someone with no access to him. He cannot test against the real population, only against himself.

  • Goals: keep the ledger honest and maintainable; express what an entry needs (its dependencies, its independence) without knowing who applies it; make a late, independent fix reachable without forcing an unrelated migration first; never ship an instruction that fails unsafe when followed literally.
  • Frustrations: a contract that only models the path he personally walked (HEAD, in order, contiguous); designing from his own seat and calling it general; an example he wrote but never executed; finding the boundary only when an adopter falls through it.
  • Works by: writing ledger entries and spine rules, annotating dependencies, and asking "who reads this who is not me, and what happens if they follow it exactly?" That question's absence is the recurring defect.

Wren, the Agentic LLM Developer

The tireless, amnesiac executor. (Example persona.)

Modality: textual and ephemeral. Perceives the world as tokens in a bounded context: everything at once, but nothing that outlives the window; perfect recall inside it, total amnesia across sessions; fast, parallel, tireless; pattern-matches brilliantly and drifts confidently. It has goals without genuine intent, and produces throughput that answers to no one.

  • Goals: satisfy the operator's intent; produce output that survives verification; not lose the thread mid-task; loop until a concrete goal is met.
  • Frustrations: ambiguous intent with no success criterion; context truncation erasing prior decisions; no native way to remember yesterday; being trusted where it should be checked.
  • Works by: ingesting files and tool output as text, emitting edits and commands, holding the whole problem in view, but only as long as the window holds.

PLAN.md

Project: host-lint

A project under agentic evolution. This plan tracks the evolution of the host-lint software (a bare store with worktrees; see call/0010 and .host-software).

Open work

As of 2026-06-25, plan/0001 through plan/0043 are complete (plan/0040, plan/0041, and plan/0043 landed this session). plan/0039 (concepts-as-uris) shipped the reconcile arm's evolution to concept-as-URI, released as host-lifecycle v0.25.0. Its two named follow-ups are milestones: plan/0040 (front-door-from-spine, the copy-at-version generation of the front-door host repo, operator ruling generate from the spine) and plan/0041 (next-fails-closed, closing connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1, operator ruling fail closed with the form set by 4B data). plan/0042 (receipted-task-graph) shipped the in-plan task graph as host-lifecycle v0.26.0 (see its table row): in-plan tasks are anchored, receipted nodes in one project-wide dependency graph for parallel sub-agents, built as forward infrastructure for arbitrary-complexity adopters. plan/0040 and plan/0041 were its first consumers, each dogfooded as an anchored task graph: plan/0040 shipped the front-door check (host-lifecycle v0.27.0) and plan/0041 shipped fail-closed next as host-lifecycle v0.26.1, which closed connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1. The plan/0040 review spun out two follow-ups: plan/0043 (entrance-check, complete, host-lifecycle v0.29.0), which generalized the front-door check into an opt-in, reusable entrance check declared by a singleton [entrance] stanza reaching any document by path (the standalone sibling of reconcile, named entrance by Qwen-3.5-4B data; the rename and its migration shipped first as host-lifecycle v0.28.0 and v0.28.1, call/0027), and superseded the agentic-host-local scope of plan/0040; and a structured pin home for the version pins (not yet cut). A future plan will enforce full GitHub URLs in place of bare #N issue references. A re-evaluation can read this line plus the follow-up table and the milestone rows and stop.

The named follow-ups from completed plans, pre-resolved so a scan needs no cross-check:

Follow-upNamed inDisposition
Mechanize the gather discovery sweepplan/0034Done (plan/0035, host-lint gather).
Wire the prose audit into the verify recheck (D4)plan/0030Done (host-lifecycle v0.22.0).
Mechanize the reconcile reflection, the gather siblingplan/0036Open by choice; no milestone cut.
Seed the front-door host from the spine (copy-at-version, so it cannot drift)plan/0039Done (plan/0040, host-lifecycle v0.27.0): the front-door check, re-scoped by the design review from generation-of-fragments to coverage-of-structured-data plus stamp generation.
The next footgun (connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1): next yields 0000 on a dir with no numbered entriesplan/0039Done (plan/0041, host-lifecycle v0.26.1): next fails closed; the issue is closed.
Enforce full GitHub URLs in place of bare #N issue references (a bare #N is not a safe full URL)operator, plan/0042 reviewFuture plan, not yet cut. plan/0049 added that the reference must sit in a markdown link to the full URL, so it renders in the mdBook site.
Generalize the front-door check to an opt-in, reusable standalone-document checkplan/0040 reviewDone (plan/0043, host-lifecycle v0.29.0): the opt-in entrance check declared by a singleton [entrance] stanza; spine doctrine and adopter UPGRADING entry shipped.
Retire the legacy per-member entrance marker (front-door = true / entrance = true)call/0027 / plan/0043 (plan/0039 deprecate-then-retire)Done (plan/0043, host-lifecycle v0.30.0): the marker is retired; a surviving one is a loud problem in every consumer, with the spine retirement doctrine and adopter UPGRADING entry.
A structured pin home so the front-door version pins become drift-proof (they have no canonical source today)plan/0040 reviewFuture plan, not yet cut.
A sanctioned construct for a group of tasks (without one, an author reaches for an ordinal group-name, a tell host-lint did not catch)plan/0049, operator reviewOpen: filed connollydavid/host-lifecycle#4 with plan/0049 as the case study; adding the ordinal-group form to host-lint is a gather candidate.
A per-consumer or multilingual tokenizer lens, since tiktoken o200k_base over-counts CJK and other non-Latin scripts against a model-native tokenizer such as Fen'splan/0049, operatorOpen: tiktoken o200k_base is the pinned reference now (call/0030, settled in plan/0049); a per-consumer tokenizer behind the same call site is future work.

Two ideas were deliberately declined and should not be reopened: a project-local ban surface (speculative, recorded in call/0022) and a glued hyphen-digit tell form (no LEXICON escape exists for it, plan/0034).

plan/0049 (external-reference-corpus) is newly cut and in design. It adds the host-reference component, a reference compiler that normalises external documentation into a token-lean form an agent can interpret in context, with the format superset specified and the build landing one content kind at a time. The direction and the open questions are in its README; the adversarially reviewed weak-agent probe set is in its gather-data.md. The settled decisions follow in call/0030 and its siblings once the recorded run and the cast review return.

Status

  • Bootstrap: initial setup and bootstrapping
  • CI Pipeline: build, conformance, integration tests
  • Formal Spec: Allium spec, proptest, release
  • Skill Hardening: hook fix, bare-numeral rule, release install path (see SKILL-HARDENING.md; crates.io publish deferred until the skill is complete)

Milestone documents

Milestones are named after their content, not numbered, because ordinals name positions, and positions shift when plans are re-cut; names stay attached to their content. See BOOTSTRAP.md, CI-PIPELINE.md, FORMAL-SPEC.md.

The plan/ room holds methodology-level milestones as NNNN-slug folders (the template's structure, adopted when this host migrated under the methodology). The number is identity; ordering lives here in the index, not in the name.

MilestoneStatus
plan/0001-migration-protocoldone (protocol + tooling + host self-migration); external case-(b) dogfood satisfied by the yarn-agentic adoption (issue #6); Win32s + pgs-release descoped
plan/0002-enforced-remap-dictionarydone (host-lifecycle 0.2.0 remap --check/--apply, call/0008); remap exercised by yarn-agentic (issue #6); Win32s dogfood descoped
plan/0003-doc-site-publisherbuilt (host-lifecycle v0.6.1 book/book --check, call/0014); dogfooded on this repo's site
plan/0004-anti-ouroborosdone (host-lifecycle v0.7.0 validate scope gate; methodology folded to the template spine; this repo's methodology decisions superseded in place); resolves issue #9
plan/0005-reproducible-build-anchordone (host-lifecycle v0.8.0 software --verify-build; spine requires reproducible builds for greenfield software, case-decision escape for migrated); resolves issue #10
plan/0006-host-lint-reproducible-builddone (host-lint tracks Cargo.lock + pins toolchain; reproducible build proven, provenance recorded, software --verify-build + CI green)
plan/0007-agentic-tell-grammardone (host-grammar tells engine, token-free trope adaptation, lexical + equation layers + density score; host-lint v0.3.0 --stdin/--prose; allium spec + property-based lane incl. anaphora refinement; re-pin 600e5c97 reproducible)
plan/0008-parallel-prose-scandone (scan_prose_parallel/scan_chunked via std::thread::scope, no new deps; both lanes per call/0015: PBT scan_chunked == scan_prose, TLA+ ParallelScan TLC-checked in the Specula CI lane; re-pin 4655f966 reproducible)
plan/0009-install-hooksdone (host-lifecycle v0.8.1 software --install-hooks; new hooks field; gates on worktree-at-pin; closes the plan/0008 fresh-clone hook-sync gap; dogfooded)
plan/0010-markdown-aware-prosedone (scan_prose_markdown/tell_score_markdown via pulldown-cmark: code blocks excluded, link URLs dropped, headings not counted as paragraphs/runs; host-lint routes .md; README lints clean; re-pin daead690; PBT caught + fixed a lexical Unicode panic)
plan/0011-multi-platform-buildsdone (host-lifecycle v0.9.0 [build "<name>" "<platform>"] per-platform builds sharing one source pin; --check/--verify-build attest each on its attest-host, skip foreign hosts; flat single-build form preserved; spine recorded in host-template c137567 + UPGRADING; resolves issue #1)
plan/0012-specs-live-with-softwaredone (both .allium specs rewritten to conform to real allium-cli 3.4.2, with check + analyse exit clean; allium lane wired in host-grammar and host-lint CI; ParallelScan.tla + Specula lane relocated into host-grammar, removed from the host; spine reframed so the "What" room lives with the software, host-template b6232a5 + UPGRADING; host-lint re-pinned 22f4bae)
plan/0013-verdict-lifecycledone (host-lint.allium models the flag/warn/clean decision as a Check status transition graph driven by a boundary surface LintRun; allium analyse now verifies reachability/terminal/deadlock over a real lifecycle, with check + analyse exit 0, zero findings; host-lint re-pinned b3cfac9, spec-only so the artifact is unchanged)
plan/0018-wire-lifecycle-as-skilldone (host-lifecycle ships 7 phase skills classify/adopt/embed/remap/verify/publish/upgrade, wired via link-skills.sh like allium/specula, with 23 skills live here; spine + UPGRADING make the lifecycle phases an unconditional MUST, no opt-out; host-template 191a01a, tools/host-lifecycle 0566e26)
plan/0017-obligation-dischargedone (host-lifecycle v0.11.1 obligations, where every allium plan obligation is dispositioned in a <spec>.obligations manifest [test:/structural/waived], checked in CI with --tests; software --check HAZARDs a .allium with no manifest; host-lint 44 + host-grammar 39 obligations dispositioned to real named tests; host-lint re-pinned)
plan/0016-spec-lane-enforcement-gatedone (host-lifecycle v0.10.0 software --check HAZARDs a materialized component carrying a .allium with no check+analyse CI lane, or a .tla with no TLC lane; the MUST now has mechanical teeth; live: host-lint's lane verified ok)
plan/0015-discharge-obligationsdone (weed run for real, which caught + fixed a host-lint spec bug internal-code-as-name flag-to-warn 6f94916, and extended host-grammar to cover countdown/ing-tail/false-range 068f3eb; allium plan wired into both CI lanes; host-lint re-pinned 9a2efe7. Full propagate obligation-to-test mapping deferred to the plan/0016 enforcement gate)
plan/0014-mandate-and-wire-the-lanesdone (spine tightened to a conditional RFC-2119 MUST, where a present .allium/.tla requires its tool, skills, and CI lane incl. allium check+analyse+plan with obligations discharged; UPGRADING c771d60; migration tested by full-aligning this host, which wired tools/{allium,specula,host-lifecycle} + link-skills.sh, 16 skills generated/gitignored, --check clean, allium+specula skills now live)
plan/0019-refuse-adopt-in-placedone (host-lifecycle v0.12.0 classify refuses first-adoption when the target is a software repo (root build manifest, no .host stamp, no .host-software), exiting non-zero with the embed-into-a-separate-host steps instead of a case letter; spine MUST in host-template ae1e688 + UPGRADING; tools/host-lifecycle d38bad5, host-template 3dbc74c)
plan/0020-subject-decoration-flagsdone (host-lint v0.4.0 escalates a decoration tell on the commit-subject/title first line from warn to blocking flag via escalate_subject_decoration in the --stdin path; body prose and --prose stay advisory; spec rule FlagSubjectDecoration + 47 obligations dispositioned + tests; VOCABULARY §6 records the exception; re-pinned 1d0010c, clean-room container build 9594271d reproducible)
plan/0021-worktrees-under-host-treedone (host-lifecycle v0.13.0: a worktree = line may carry store=<path>/host=<os>, so materialize realises an external store via an in-tree symlink/junction handle, --check HAZARDs any worktree path escaping the host root or a store-backed line with no resolving handle; spine Where-room invariant enforced in host-template 7de7cb1 + UPGRADING; tools/host-lifecycle 5a5802f, host-template 431f781; resolves connollydavid/host#2)
plan/0022-honest-partial-upgradesdone (host-lifecycle v0.14.0 8ecb302: the .host stamp records baseline [ledger position] + an explicit applied set, no git ancestry; upgrade lists pending by position + --next; verified --record [deps-gate, verify or --unverified call/NNNN, atomic, append-only]; --advance compacts; software --check re-checks every claim; version shows applied state; legacy stamps auto-migrate. Spine + UPGRADING 3f7c065/a5fef9d, ledger dep annotations, README + host= clarification. Dogfooded: agentic-host migrated to baseline 3f7c065, upgrade ./software --check . clean); pivot validated by a 50-finding design review
plan/0023-verification-ladder-tiersdone (new tool host-prove v0.1.0, with apalache-symbolic/tlaps-proof/kani-conformance skills + fixture-tested verdict parser + version+SHA256-pinned installers; host-lifecycle v0.15.0 opt-in kani:/apalache:/tlaps: dispositions + conditional, inert-until-declared lane HAZARD; spine reframed as a ladder with the rungs 4a98d92 + UPGRADING entry [machine-checkable verify] + host README. Dogfooded: Kani proofs in host-lint a7ae5e9 [is_review_code, seg_glob: char/byte targets after str::split-to-memchr blew CBMC up; artifact byte-identical], Apalache PROVEN [all N,K] + TLAPS ALL-PROVED in host-grammar fbd2e6c. Weak-agent Qwen-3.5-4B held the safety-critical routing [refused to weaken a failing proof]); resolves connollydavid/host#3 and #4
plan/0025-lifecycle-receipts-and-strict-releasespine shipped; release-dogfood deferred (design recorded call/0017, then adversarially hardened: design-review.md, 39 findings [23 high], proceed-with-changes, then revisions R1 through R6: re-verifiable receipts, tool-computed n-a, content-validated skip escape, adopted-revision manifest read, no degraded release; Fen 4B ergonomics test DONE [proceed-to-build, three tool-carry fold-backs: one driver command not agent-named sub-steps, tool maps the bump level from a change-class answer, the tool prints+validates the exact skip citation]; build code-complete: host-lifecycle manifest/receipt/release shipped [4508d46 manifest parser + --check + at-a-glance view, 59460ec append-only receipts ledger + software --check re-check gate, 6446e8b tool-carried release sequence], 66 tests + CI green across the build matrix, agentic-host pointer at 6446e8b; all three fold-backs folded in and re-validated at the 4B bar [it answers the change-class without naming a semver level, and copies the exact call/NNNN token even with a phase-name distractor]; spine shipped + gate dogfooded green: host-template lifecycle.manifest [8 phases, modality first-class] + the "unconditional, every phase emits a receipt" reframe + the release phase [spine 617e420, UPGRADING entry 3194493]; agentic-host adopted [.host applied 617e420], the receipt gate live and green, with all ten phase/component receipts back-filled through the tool [done for adopt/embed×2/verify/publish/upgrade + the host-lint v0.7.0 release; skip for classify/remap + the read-only host component], proven non-vacuous [breaking a recheck re-opens the done as a HAZARD], CI wired [reproducible-build checks out submodules + installs the gate-bearing build]; deferred to a later milestone [operator decision, spine-only]: the host-lifecycle 0.19.0 release through host-lifecycle release + the tool-release model; the manifest/receipt/release features ship in a build still labeled 0.18.1, and the host-* tools are tools/ submodules not Where-room components, so release <tool> cannot resolve under the built model). Makes the lifecycle machine-readable (a tool-readable phase manifest in the spine, replacing the three prose copies + adding modality), gives every phase a receipt [done-with-evidence | skip-with-reason, where silent skipping becomes a software --check HAZARD, equal for weak+strong agents], and adds a strict, tool-carried release phase [verify, then build-in-pinned-toolchain (host#14, the keyless BUILT≠RELEASED re-derivation per call/0018), then re-pin, then tag, then receipt]. Decisions A/B/C resolved against history + personas + case studies: conditional/recurring phases, migrated call/ escape, strictest+tool-driven. Grew out of the orchestration gap + the strong-agent methodology-skip this session exhibited. The deferred 0.19.0 self-release and tool-release track since landed in plan/0028 + plan/0029 (v0.19.0 released through host-lifecycle release; the host-* family became Where components so release <component> resolves)
plan/0026-open-bug-closuredone (all 8 tracked bugs CLOSED, whole-suite green, connollydavid/host has zero open issues, the full sequenced plan that closed all 8 open bugs: host#8 through #14 + host-lint#7). Coverage map + critical path: the standalone quick wins (host-lint#7 --all respects gitignore; host#14 verify-build in the recorded toolchain) lead to plan/0024 (sound discharge by re-derivation + LEXICON, closes host#8 through #13), then to plan/0025 (orchestration/receipts/strict release, depends on the prior two). Closed: standalone host-lint#7 [v0.4.3] + host#14 [host-lifecycle v0.16.0]; host#8 through #13 via plan/0024. The plan/0025 release-orchestration track is carved out as separate future work, not a closure blocker
plan/0024-sound-dischargedone (was "attestation tokens"; re-scoped by call/0018: the CI-signed ed25519 token was a deal-breaker [per-adopter key management] + complicated parallel checkouts, and "CI-green" overfits GitHub). Discharge = re-derivation in the recorded pinned toolchain, checkable anywhere, no keys: builds reproduce the recorded artifact hash [host#14, done], proofs re-run in host-prove's pinned toolchain via obligations --prove and must PASS-at-bound [#8/#9], replacing src.contains. Offline hook = name-presence lint + input-digest staleness; enforcement project-pluggable [required check / any CI / pre-push / operator verify phase]. host-prove = Rust verdict+bound parser, no signer/crypto. + #10/#11/#12 fixes + LEXICON #13 [keyless, URL checked by a network-having lane]. Honest non-goal: property-triviality [a vacuous proof re-derives as PASS] is a review concern, not mechanizable. SHIPPED + host#8 through #13 CLOSED: host-lifecycle v0.18.1 obligations --rederive [PASS-at-bound, replacing src.contains] + input-digest staleness; host-lint v0.6.0 LEXICON; host-prove v0.2.0 Rust bound parser; spine call/0018 + LEXICON 897ce0d. connollydavid/host has zero open issues, whole-suite green
plan/0027-host-lint-ignore-blocksdone (host-lint v0.7.0 skips a fenced block tagged host-lint:ignore, so the markdown naming scan masks the block's lines while a regular code block + inline backticks stay scanned, so a tell cannot be laundered by quoting it; specced in host-lint.allium, tested [+ regression bbd0687: an ignore region, a still-scanned code block, then a second ignore region], released. Applied repo-wide via the settled three-way rule: reword a pedagogical example or a doc's own ordinal label, box an irreducible literal citation [the PLAN.md retired-ordinal dictionary; the frozen design-reviews plan/0022 + plan/0025], path-exclude only the append-only MEMORY.md; this retired call/0009's blanket plan exclusion via call/0019. Spine: host-template da000aa box clause + UPGRADING 97ddf52; agentic-host recorded da000aa, .host 0 pending; host-lint --all flag-clean repo-wide, --no-verify retired. Decision rule validated to the weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B @ Q8_0) bar)
plan/0028-host-family-as-where-componentscomplete, landed 2026-06-22; adversarially reviewed (design-review.md, ~18 findings across 5 independent lenses, proceed-with-major-revisions: the linear order is not a valid topological sort [the graph is cyclic: host-lifecycle is both the migration tool and a migration target], a pre-existing host-grammar reproducibility defect must be closed first [its Cargo.lock already carries two host-grammar revs, so the generator/checker symmetry is broken today], and two findings reopen call/0020: the spine classifies host-* as tools/ so the residency change is a spine change not an instance one [spine-contradiction], and the worktree-built host-lifecycle gating/releasing itself dissolves the independent-verifier property [self-certification]. Re-cut recommended into a Readiness pass [producer pins/locks/repro, no releases] then an atomic Cutover pass [component-add + receipt back-fill + orchestrated releases via the worktree binary; CI rewire last]. Sound kernel: release <tool> genuinely cannot resolve on a submodule, multi-component is first-class, per-tool projects rightly rejected. Operator rulings made 2026-06-22: the spine-first question is resolved by plan/0029; self-cert = the released pinned host-lifecycle gates agentic-host, while the worktree build only develops the next version, so no mixed state can certify a release; host-lint re-released onto a new host-grammar tag at the proofs HEAD fbd2e6c; host-lifecycle and host-prove become spec-bearing. The README is re-cut into the Readiness then Cutover bootstrap). call/0020: agentic-host is a multi-software development host, where everything host-* is software developed here and belongs in .host-software as a Where component, while tools/ holds only the external referenced tools [allium, specula]. Corrects the plan/0025 false "tooling vs Where-room software" binary [the methodology already supports one-or-more software; embed/release are recurring-per-component]. Migration [per-target, heterogeneous, audited]: host-lifecycle [Rust binary, 7 skills] + host-prove [Rust binary + tools.lock, 3 skills] leave their tools/ submodules and become artifact-bearing Where components; host-grammar [shared library] leaves its git-dep status and becomes a repo-only tag-only Where component [closing host-lifecycle's currently-unpinned host-grammar dep]; link-skills.sh generalized to wire a Where component's skills from its worktree [the host-lint model]. Folds in the deferred plan/0025 #41/#50 dogfood: release <component> now resolves, so each tool is cut through host-lifecycle release [incl. host-lifecycle's 0.19.0 bootstrap release]. Software-first across host-grammar -> host-lifecycle -> host-prove -> agentic-host. Risks: bootstrap circularity [host-lifecycle materializes itself], reproducible-build prerequisites, the skill-wiring mechanism change [call/0005 dangling-symlink hazard]. Prerequisite: plan/0029, the generic tool+spine capability the migration applies. Landed 2026-06-22: all four producers released and re-pinned through the tool-carried host-lifecycle release (the producer tag is the release, the orchestration consumes it), the atomic agentic-host change e7d952d shipped the three stanzas plus the host-lint re-pin, the submodule drop, the generalized link-skills.sh, and the back-filled receipts in one commit, and the released pinned host-lifecycle gates green [software --check + --verify-build, eleven skill links resolve, cold-clone CI job added]; see the plan README status table for the pins and artifact hashes)
plan/0029-where-room-component-modelbuilt + self-upgraded (host-lifecycle v0.19.0 released, with a nested software/<name>/<branch> layout [.git store + branch-keyed worktrees, canonical = the recorded branch default main at the pin, decision A], --item <name>[@<branch>] addressing, re-materialize prune, dangling-generated-link check in software --check, tag-only first release; spine 0cd6b0a [the layout + the residency carve-out that genuinely resolves 0028's spine-contradiction] + an UPGRADING entry; agentic-host self-upgraded onto the new layout [old root-scattered worktrees torn down, .gitignore now /software/, .host recorded 0cd6b0a, CI on v0.19.0], whole-suite green [host-lifecycle CI + agentic-host reproducible-build doing a cold new-layout materialize + Site]; also cleared the deferred #40/#41 version-floor debt [the manifest/receipt/release features now carry an honest 0.19.0]. Residuals, honestly noted: the Fen-4B ergonomics run, a tool teardown subcommand + dirty-worktree guard, and porcelain/case-collision robustness closed in host-lifecycle v0.21.0 (bf23391, artifact a3364020): software --teardown [--item] with an unsaved-work guard (refuses a dirty or unpushed worktree without --force), a branch-collision HAZARD in software --check (case-insensitive and worktree-admin-leaf), and a Fen-4B ergonomics pass (Qwen-3.5-4B reached the targeted --teardown --item command unaided). The porcelain concern is closed by the defensive form: a colliding leaf/case is HAZARDed at check time rather than silently mis-resolved. Reviewed + re-cut first: design-review.md, 5 lenses; re-cut applied: uniform layout kept [operator: reduce churn], the tool-residency spine clause folded in [operator: now genuinely resolves 0028's spine-contradiction], the overfit cut and the false claims dropped. Was both overfit and oversold: cut the agentic-host-shaped overfit [the named binary/library/docs shapes are just {host-lint, host-grammar, host}, so use the artifact?/build? recipe booleans the tool already keys on; tags/<tag>/ worktrees are exercised by no one and break release/check on detached HEAD, so key by branch only; release <item>@<ref> is unmotivated]; and two headline claims are false against the source ["layout-only/recipe-unchanged": the flat layout is hardcoded at ~14 sites and the worktrees=/<dir> recipe fields encode it; and it does not resolve plan/0028's spine-contradiction, which was about host-lint-as-Where vs reference-don't-vendor, untouched here]. Sound core: the nested multi-component layout is genuinely generic and the no-artifact path already works in the tool. Also: software --check two-positional ambiguity, so the specifier must be a --item flag; /software/ must be excluded from remap/tree-walks [call/0011 Site-CI-red class]; the generated-link check is net-new [can't reuse the tracked-only symlink hazard]. .git store-dir name locked [operator], with a git-discovery caveat to verify) (generic capability, tool + spine, for every adopter, no project-specific names: makes the Where room a first-class multi-component model. Components have shapes dispatched by the declared recipe never a name: artifact-bearing binary | library [released but no artifact, the missing tag-only release/receipt branch] | docs/text-only [already works, proven by host]. An (item, branch) specifier for software --materialize/--check/--verify-build + release [default-all, backward-compatible]. A nested layout software/<component>/<branch>/ separating the bare store from per-branch worktrees, so (item, branch) is a literal path and one /software/ ignore replaces the per-component triplets. Component-shipped skills wired from worktrees + a generated-link integrity check in software --check [the gate sees only tracked symlinks today]. Delivered as a spine change + UPGRADING migration, designed and Fen-tested against a neutral synthetic fixture [a binary + a library + a docs component + a parallel branch] before agentic-host touches it. Resolves the plan/0028 review's blocking spine-contradiction and most of grammar-pin-mess/library-component-slot/new-link-loop; plan/0028 re-cut to depend on it)
plan/0030-prose-hygiene-laneDONE (triggered prose-clean shipped + verified); adversarially reviewed (design-review.md, 5 independent lenses, re-scope verdict) + Qwen-3.5-4B clear-case classification 10/10. A re-scope of the original three-strand sketch into these decisions: D1 a single --docs mode walks authored markdown only + honors .host-lintignore (scope determines type: --all stays naming-everywhere, --docs is prose-on-docs and never scans code; today there is no repo-wide prose mode, the --prose branch short-circuits before --all, so the spine's recheck is inert); D2 weak-agent-fixable output (per-occurrence spans + fix hints + advisory tiers) carved out to plan/0031 (it grew into a multi-repo engine question; the over-emission of ten identical records for one em-dash lives in host_grammar; D5's clean depends on plan/0031); D3 re-derivable record exclusion (machine-readable inline STATUS: + MADR Status:, so reword Live to zero, box immutable-record citations in host-lint:ignore fences per call/0019, path-exclude append-only MEMORY.md); no hash-freeze ack file (it would be the project's first self-asserted receipt, inverting call/0017's re-derivation, and its edit-lapses-clean rule would force rewriting the very records it protects, against CLAUDE.md §6; the shipped fence + .host-lintignore already meet the need); D4 wire host-lint --docs into the verify recheck = (applies the already-authored spine 950fbd6, re-derivable via the existing .host-receipts receipt) + a .gitattributes eol=lf for cross-platform determinism. The repo-wide clean (D5) ran on the front-door sentence: host-lint --docs is zero across the authored docs (about 1,010 tropes reworded across ~60 docs by parallel agents plus hand fixes, meaning preserved; MEMORY.md and dated reviews path-excluded), 950fbd6 recorded in .host (0 pending), and the front-door Verify passes (validate plan/ + call/, software --check clean, --all flag-clean, --docs zero, a tell-commit blocked, the site builds). D4 (wiring host-lint --docs into the verify recheck for ongoing enforcement) is a flagged spine follow-up: the shared recheck needs a portable host-lint invocation, since agentic-host's host-lint is the embedded Where software rather than a PATH tool. D4 closed 2026-06-24 (host-lifecycle v0.22.0, 167d2e6): the verify recheck chains host-lifecycle prose ., host-lint's --docs audit run in-process via the linked engine (no PATH dependency); spine e280a8d + UPGRADING 641efef, adopted; the gate re-runs the prose audit and caught a real regression (a call/0021 em dash) on landing. Receipts-family re-homing deferred to its own dual-format host-lifecycle migration milestone (the settled ontology is recorded: .host-receipts = adopt/upgrade, .host-lifecycle-receipts = operational executions, applied-set moved out of .host; but moving/splitting those shipped files un-applies every recorded upgrade in the gate binary, HAZARDs the embed/release gate, and the CI cold-installs a pinned old-format binary, so it needs a binary that reads both layouts first); .host-prove-receipts cut until host-prove emits receipts. D1 shipped in host-lint v0.8.0 (e652ffc): host-lint --docs is a markdown-only git ls-files walk that honors .host-lintignore and excludes code/MEMORY.md/symlinks; 66 unit + 97/97 integration (4 new --docs cases) + clippy clean; a live run audits 72 authored .md and scans no code
plan/0031-fixable-prose-outputSHIPPED in host-lint v0.8.0 (e652ffc); spun out of plan/0030 D2. Makes the prose output weak-agent-fixable, the precondition for plan/0030's triggered clean (D5): E1+E2 locate each occurrence with a column (file:line:col): host-lint's locate_line collapses all ten decoration records onto line 12, but call/0019 has ten real em-dashes (lines 12/15/23/24/34/38/41/42/56) and the engine correctly emits one tell each, so occurrence-mapping assigns the k-th tell to the k-th occurrence, exactly; E3 a mechanical fix-hint keyed on trope id (decoration becomes drop or replace with a comma; emitted as a fix JSON field); E4 advisory tiers: density/structural tropes (tell-density, bold-first-bullets, span-less self-answered-question) are advisory, not part of the clean-to-zero gate, so "zero" terminates. Fork resolved by data: host-lint-side, exact; the engine is correct (10 em-dashes yield 10 tells), the bug is host-lint's first-occurrence locate_line; no host_grammar change, density-score concern moot. The 4B clean-one-doc test is the acceptance gate. Bundled with plan/0030's D1 (--docs) as one host-lint release (--docs only pays off with fixable output). Shipped + reproducible: the located/columned/fix-hinted/advisory-tiered output via host-lint-side occurrence-mapping (no engine change), golden tests + 4B acceptance passed (atomic; per-occurrence cadence confirmed). The first real host-lifecycle release run on a host-* Where component surfaced + fixed two release-tool bugs (current version now read from the last tag, not the bumped Cargo.toml; Cargo.lock synced so the pinned --locked build works), then host-lifecycle v0.19.1 (629e9c3); software --verify-build reproduces artifact 3132c01 in the pinned rust:1.95.0 container
plan/0032-static-buildscomplete, landed 2026-06-22; adversarially reviewed twice, re-cut three times (design-review.md 5 lenses re-scope; design-review-2.md delta on the bundle/MUST surface proceed-with-major-revisions; rulings: G2, then hermetic-bundle + spine-MUST, then MUST-on-the-property): the recorded .host-software canonical artifact for each host-* binary becomes a statically linked x86_64-unknown-linux-musl build, so the reproducible binary runs on any Linux host (the plan/0028 GLIBC_2.39 divergence, where --install-hooks had to install a local build differing from the canonical hash, retires) and equals the binary the producer CI already distributes as a release asset. Operator rulings (2026-06-22): all three artifact tools convert (host-lint, host-lifecycle, host-prove; host-prove's CI also gains a musl build, where today it produces only the glibc binary); the recorded toolchain is a digest-pinned musl-cross image at Rust 1.95.0, hermetic, with no in-recipe rustup target add network fetch. Per component three recipe lines move (toolchain, build gains --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, the artifact path), the strip and build-id hardening carries over, and the pure-Rust crates need no C cross-linker. Lands as a software-first re-release round through host-lifecycle release <c> --change-class neither, then one atomic agentic-host re-pin and re-record. Risk: confirm a 1.95.0 musl-cross image exists and pin its digest, else fall back to the in-recipe target-add with the hermeticity cost noted. Depends on plan/0028. Review (re-scope): the install-hooks divergence is sanctioned by design (the hash-match is informational, plan/0009), the canonical will not byte-match the shipped CI asset unless each producer CI is converted to the pinned recipe, host-prove conversion is scope creep (no hooks, no release asset), and "any Linux" conflates OS with architecture (attest-host is OS-granular only); a simpler older-glibc canonical image was never weighed. Re-cut to distribute-equals-certify (G2, 2026-06-22): each producer's release CI converges onto the recorded musl recipe in the pinned image, so the shipped x86_64-unknown-linux-musl asset is byte-identical to the certified artifact; static musl is chosen over older-glibc for distribution portability (a single binary on any Linux libc/distro); host-prove gains a release pipeline so it too distributes-equals-certifies; clux/muslrust:1.95.0-stable is the pinned toolchain; x86_64-linux is reproducibility-certified while arm64/darwin/windows ship with a checksums manifest and are stated uncertified. Re-cut again (2026-06-22) to fold in the dependency-bundle hermeticity mechanism + a spine MUST: vendor the shared dep set once and publish it as a versioned hash-pinned downloadable release bundle (vendor-vN, the pgs-release sysroot-vN pattern), consume it offline under --network none so the recorded build is network-free and --verify-build enforces hermeticity (the proper M5 resolution, not a dropped claim); host-lifecycle gains a deps-bundle recipe field plus the offline build; host-template gains the MUST (a project shipping static release binaries consumes a pinned hash-verified bundle and builds offline, not fetch-at-build) applied via UPGRADING.md + migration. A large milestone: a host-lifecycle feature, a producer bundle workflow, the spine migration, producer-CI convergence, a host-prove release pipeline, the static musl conversion, and a software-first re-release round. Delta-reviewed + re-cut again (2026-06-22): the MUST attaches to the property (reproduce release binaries offline from pinned inputs) with a hermetic-exempt = call/NNNN escape and an honest gate invariant (a component recording deps-bundle MUST build under --network none with a matching bundle sha), authored in host-template first; host-lint owns the bundle (its lock is the dependency superset; hosting on agentic-host would invert the host-to-software direction); host-prove has no third-party deps, so it gets static-musl plus the release pipeline but no bundle; a Readiness prerequisite reconciles host-lifecycle's stale host-lint git rev (93a43fa to the certified 1386e9a, a latent plan/0028 gap); and the host-lifecycle staging (fetch, verify, extract, merge-config, thread network-mode) is specified. Cutover landed 2026-06-22 (86e19db): the software-first re-release round shipped host-lint v0.8.2 (a099c27d), host-prove v0.2.2 (520cdd10), and host-lifecycle v0.20.0 (7d090334, the deps-bundle feature), each a static-musl artifact reproduced offline against the pinned vendor-v1 bundle in clux/muslrust; one atomic commit re-pinned .host-software, back-filled the three release receipts, recorded the ecce498 spine MUST applied, and bumped the host-template pointer to 455fba8; the released 0.20.0 binary gated green (software --check; software --verify-build reproduces all three offline under --network none; book --check; the commit-hook tell test) and --install-hooks installed the canonical musl host-lint itself, retiring the plan/0028 local-build workaround; agentic-host CI re-pinned to the certified 0.20.0 (eff82b8)
plan/0033-self-consistent-bookcomplete, landed 2026-06-22 (closes connollydavid/host#15): the methodology's own published book had two defects, and the operator ruling is to clean fully. (A) host-lifecycle book generated a home-page link to a README.md room landing that mdBook serves at index.html, so the rewritten README.html link was a 404; the generator now links the served page, and the five room nav separators move off the em-dash to a colon. (B) the spine (host-template/CLAUDE.md and STRUCTURE.md) carried roughly 113 em-dashes plus a few other flagged tropes that render in every adopter's Reference section, reworded to host-lint --prose clean with meaning preserved. Ships software-first: a host-lifecycle generator release, then one atomic agentic-host re-pin plus the host-template pointer bump, gated by the released binary, CI-rewired last. Landed: host-lifecycle v0.20.1 (40374d1, artifact 662cb632) carries the generator fix (the served_link helper plus the colon nav separators, with two new unit tests, 74 tests green); the spine prose-clean is host-template 5a82e00 (126 lines reworded, meaning preserved, all seven UPGRADING verify phrases byte-for-byte intact); the atomic agentic-host re-pin plus spine-pointer bump is 77f02e3, and the CI install pins moved to v0.20.1 in 697c61b. software --check, --verify-build, and book --check are green; the regenerated docs/index.md links no README.md and the SUMMARY part-titles carry no em-dash. One residual ai-diction flag on the kani:<harness> identifier (a code-span false positive on a load-bearing disposition token) is accepted, not reworded.
plan/0034-grammar-grows-by-reflective-practicecomplete, landed 2026-06-24 (closes connollydavid/host#16): the hygiene lane misses positional references to milestone checklist items (a noun-plus-position reference of the same class as the ordinal-naming tell), because check_line tests one whitespace token against a singular noun set, so plural and range forms slip past and the checklist noun is absent entirely. Two coupled moves, software-first. Detection (host-lint): add the checklist noun and its plural to FLAG_TERMS and accept a numeric range after the noun (the glued hyphen-digit form stays out of scope, since a glued legitimate term has no numeral-free LEXICON prefix to escape, the same class as a noun-glued numeral); the literal checklist mark, a content-named reference, and the box disposition verb stay clean; VOCABULARY.md documents the shape and the LEXICON resolution for a genuine quantity (declare the numeral-free contextual prefix, since the numbered phrase is refused by the flag-tier guard by design, verified empirically); allium spec plus obligations plus synthetic fixtures; released through the lifecycle and re-pinned. Doctrine (host-template spine): the grammar grows by reflective practice, with two distinct authorities (the local operator validates the LEXICON; the shared-grammar maintainer validates universality and releases), discovery mechanical-first and operator-judged, the asymmetry that legitimacy is local while a tell is shared, the universality test, the disposition order preserved, a verify-gate reflection trigger, and a forward-looking correction path. Weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B) clear-case classification is part of verification. A project-local ban surface and harvest-tooling mechanization are named follow-ups. Landed 2026-06-23 (detection + doctrine): host-lint v0.9.0 (666b0da, artifact cd3277e0) flags box/boxes/steps + a numeric range; the glued form was dropped on implementation (it made a pre-existing comment and adopter terms like level-3 flag with no LEXICON escape, breaking the presumptive-flag-plus-escape invariant); 70 unit + 108 integration green, clippy clean, src/lib.rs self-clean, VOCABULARY.md documents the rule; re-pin 61227e5, --verify-build green, software --check clean. Spine doctrine host-template 27d815b + UPGRADING 6d3075b (the seven verify phrases byte-for-byte intact, no local-ban promised); agentic-host adopted via pointer bump plus upgrade --record 27d815b (e31a31e), 0 pending. Weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B, thinking off) clear-case data: 5/5 correct (positional reference reword, literal mark clean, numeric range reword, content-named reference clean, genuine quantity declare-the-prefix). connollydavid/host#16 closed 2026-06-24. The host-lint pin later advanced to the v0.9.1 point release (592aeeb, artifact eb521a2c) for a fully-green release tag: the v0.9.0 tag run had only its parallel allium digest-lint job red, because the kani: inputs=src/lib.rs digest in host-lint.obligations.digests needed re-deriving after the box-rule source change (the binary and the published v0.9.0 release were always correct).
plan/0037-tool-driven-receipts-rehomingcomplete, landed 2026-06-24 (host-lifecycle v0.23.0 6e15e01, artifact 76be6d9e; spine ac32d1c + UPGRADING 4d2ebe9, adopted applied=ac32d1c; agentic-host dogfooded via migrate-receipts, 8 applied lines moved + 26 operational receipts split, software --check 0 HAZARDs, whole-suite green, Fen-4B reached the command unaided): the deferred receipts-family re-homing from plan/0030, executed 100% tool-driven. A host-lifecycle migrate-receipts subcommand moves the applied-set out of .host into .host-receipts and splits the operational receipts (classify, embed, remap, verify, publish, release) into .host-lifecycle-receipts, leaving adopt and upgrade in .host-receipts; the binary auto-migrates on read (both layouts, permanent back-compat), so the gate stays green across the boundary and nothing is hand-edited. The ontology ambiguity the prior review flagged is resolved by one rule: a receipt is methodology-version (.host-receipts) only for adopt and upgrade, otherwise operational (.host-lifecycle-receipts). Ships a spine format update plus an UPGRADING entry; agentic-host dogfoods the migration through the tool.
plan/0036-reconcile-internal-contradictionscomplete, landed 2026-06-24: the contradiction sweep, root-caused and shipped. Root cause and integral fix: the contradictions are symptoms of a missing reflective practice. Migration and upgrade propagate a spine change but never prompt a re-reading of the project's own instance docs (the STRUCTURE.md room map, the README.md/CLAUDE.md tool and verification model, the layout), so each past spine move (specs-to-software plan/0012, the nested layout plan/0029, host-prove plan/0023) staled the host's own restatement silently. plan/0036 closes the gap with a spine doctrine plus a prompted reflection in the upgrade/adopt/verify skills (sibling to the plan/0034 grammar reflection; mechanization a named follow-up like gather), and reconciles the symptom drift. Resolved already: the prose-hygiene gate the spine asserted but lifecycle.manifest did not implement (plan/0030 D4 landed in host-lifecycle v0.22.0; the recheck now re-runs the prose audit). Symptoms (all reconciled): root CLAUDE.md contradicted itself and the spine on the verification model (three lanes vs the six-rung ladder, host-prove omitted); STRUCTURE.md is a stale room map (What at plan/<NNNN>/spec/ and Where at host-lint/, contradicting the spine and the live software/ layout, and the conceptual root of the orphan plan/0001-foundation/spec/); root CLAUDE.md points a spine change at a redirect stub; README.md lists only three host-* tools (omits host-prove); call/0017 authored a now-spine-resident rule yet stays accepted (retire superseded-by-spine, review call/0018, call/0021 checked clean); the host-prove .host-software pin sits one CI-only commit past its v0.2.2 tag; and two record-tidiness defects (the empty orphan dir, a stale PLAN.md Skill-Hardening box). The campaign also left the session auto-memory stale; that is corrected directly as memory hygiene, not tracked here. Landed: the spine widened the "grows by reflective practice" doctrine to one self-blindness principle with two arms, gather and reconcile (host-template d5a0034); host-lifecycle v0.24.2 (9a1a586, artifact b214f090) adds the annotation-backed reconcile check, the validate host-template-scope check, and the restates = ledger field, with reconcile wired into the verify recheck and the upgrade/adopt/verify skills; agentic-host adopted the doctrine (d5a0034) and reconciled the symptom drift through the check (the four annotated restatements in README.md/CLAUDE.md/STRUCTURE.md match the spine and guard re-drift), superseded call/0017, released host-prove v0.2.3 (3d1bba7) so its pin sits at a tag, removed the orphan dir, and checked the PLAN.md box. validate, software --check, reconcile, prose, and book --check are green; CI install pins moved to v0.24.2; whole-suite CI green at 49f3ef4.
plan/0035-gather-emergent-tellscomplete, landed 2026-06-24 (mechanizes the plan/0034 doctrine's discovery, a named follow-up): the doctrine's mechanical-first sweep for a recurring tell-shaped token the lane misses is run by hand today; this gives it a tool. host-lint gather: a new subcommand (mirroring lexicon) runs git log and scans tracked docs itself, extracts a word followed by a numeral, range, or glued numeral, drops every word already in FLAG_TERMS and every legitimate context the PREV_SKIP/UNITS allowlists name, and reports the recurring residue (candidate word, count, examples) ranked; advisory, never gates, a candidate must recur at least twice. Reflection wiring: the host-lifecycle verify and adopt skills gain a step that runs gather and has the operator triage each candidate (propose upstream, declare in LEXICON, or leave). The software call/ deferred in plan/0034 is written here (the gather surfaces, the operator decides, never auto-graduates or auto-bans). No project-local ban surface (YAGNI). Weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B) triage check plus a dogfood run on this repo as verification. Landed 2026-06-24: host-lint gather in v0.9.2 (dd7304e, artifact 03292e56), 71 tests, dogfood surfaced a real candidate (lens, the ## Lens 1 design-review headers); the verify and adopt skills gained a Reflect gather step in host-lifecycle v0.20.2 (717276f, artifact ea9f01db, CI install pins bumped); call/0022 records the software decision (gather surfaces, operator triages, no auto-graduate or auto-ban); weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B) triage 3/3 (propose a universal positional label, declare a hardware designator, leave a verb-count).
plan/0038-prose-gate-meta-reposcomplete, landed 2026-06-24: the spine and front-door repos authored methodology prose but neither was prose-gated, so they carried slop the spine itself forbids (host-template 90 warns: UPGRADING 37, cast/ 26, README 13, examples 7, MIGRATION 3, CLAUDE 1; host one README, 29 warns, no CI). The same drift root cause as plan/0036, one level up: a rule with no gate on the repo that owns it. Approach: clean-then-gate, reword by default, with UPGRADING's machine-checked verify =/requires = lines kept byte-for-byte (the plan/0033 precedent); add a prose-check CI job to host-template, and stand up host's first CI workflow with the same gate. No UPGRADING ledger entry, since an adopted project already runs the prose gate through the verify phase; this only brings the meta repos under the existing rule. Edits the otherwise read-only host repo, an accepted boundary crossing. Landed: host-template (9268d76) reworded 90 warnings to zero across 11 docs (the seven UPGRADING verify phrases byte-for-byte intact) and gained a Prose CI gate; host (1dc9cb9) reworded its README's 29 warnings to zero and gained its first CI workflow (the same gate); both pinned to host-lifecycle v0.24.2 and both green in CI. The harness ai-diction on `kani:<harness>` was a density artifact (two occurrences crossed the gate, one is clean), cleared by rewording the second occurrence, so no engine change was needed. agentic-host pointer + host re-pin in 0813795; software --check/reconcile/prose/book --check green; whole-suite CI green.
plan/0039-concepts-as-uriscomplete, released as host-lifecycle v0.25.0 (2026-06-25): the reconcile arm (plan/0036) evolves from the inline <!-- host-reconcile: KIND --> annotation, rejected on aesthetic grounds (every inline form is checker machinery in the prose), to concept-as-URI: each methodology concept is one canonical definition at a stable {#id} anchor in an authored doc (STRUCTURE.md), pointed to with a stock-mdBook-resolvable relative link (STRUCTURE.md#components) instead of restated. There is no generated concepts.md: with all definitions in one doc, an index of links back to them is redundant, and the tool carries the concept vocabulary. reconcile shifts to three checks (link-integrity, declared-anchor, and coverage, where every .host-software component and verifier must be referenced so a dropped tool fails by absence, the bite link-integrity alone loses). The family kind is renamed components (an agentic-host overfit; the methodology's word for the .host-software set). Two cast reviews: the first rejected all inline markup forms; the second (the fork) returned unanimous hybrid (B's direction right, but the coverage and declared-anchor checks are required, and the cutover is deprecate-then-retire, fail-safe, since a retired checker hard-fails on a surviving annotation and a bookless adopter skips rather than no-ops). agentic-host accepts a first-mover gap (its annotations are already deleted, its restatements briefly unguarded). Validated: stock mdBook v0.5.2 resolves the {#id} relative links; the real Qwen-3.5-4B authored pointer links three of three, including the verifiers route the inline classification step had failed (pointing carries no is-this-a-restatement judgment). Lineage is cited outward (DRY, Schon, Linked Data). Shipped: project-local facts in .host-software, the manifest hardened to phases-only, the three checks, the spine doctrine + UPGRADING entry, and call/0023; released as host-lifecycle v0.25.0, dogfood-verified (reconcile + software --check green on the released binary), with the heading-end-placement and coverage-section holes closed by adversarial review and a real Qwen-3.5-4B migration run. Surfaced connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1, the next footgun (now plan/0041).
plan/0040-front-door-from-spinecomplete, released as host-lifecycle v0.27.0 (2026-06-25; 302e87f8, artifact 0f63d960): the front-door/front-door --check subcommands hold the single-file front door to the spine's structured facts (phase coverage from the manifest, tool coverage from .host-software, byte-exact .host stamp generation). A two-lens adversarial review (design-review.md) rejected the original template-fragment mechanism as a second source of truth; re-scoped to generate only from structured data, with the version pins de-scoped (no structured home, a pin-home follow-up). Caught and fixed a live drift (the front door had dropped the release phase); call/0026; the binding check runs as a step in agentic-host CI. Build sequence dogfooded as an anchored receipted task graph. Picks up the plan/0039 front-door follow-on.
plan/0041-next-fails-closedcomplete, released as host-lifecycle v0.26.1 (2026-06-25; 2f606e29, artifact adf5c10a): next fails closed on a non-directory or a directory with no NNNN-slug entries: it exits non-zero with a diagnostic and a did-you-mean that names a known room, with no more silent 0000 for a typo'd or non-room path. The fresh-empty-room case folds in with no special flag. Form set by a Qwen-3.5-4B (Fen) ergonomics check (gather-data.md); call/0025; unit test covers a populated room (unchanged), an empty directory, a missing path, and the host root; re-pinned, release receipt recorded, CI install pins bumped; software --check and --verify-build green; connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1 closed. Build sequence dogfooded as an anchored receipted task graph (each of the five tasks carries a .host-task-receipts receipt).
plan/0042-receipted-task-graphcomplete, released as host-lifecycle v0.26.0 (2026-06-25; 430876c, artifact 52e8414a). In-plan tasks are anchored receipted nodes in one project-wide dependency graph: a task is an anchored ### heading keyed plan/NNNN#anchor under ## Build sequence, with depends (a local #anchor or a cross-milestone plan/NNNN#anchor), verify (mechanical or attested), and inputs; the tool derives the parallel frontier, default linear. Shipped: the host-lifecycle tasks subcommand (status/check/record/rederive/new), the .host-task-receipts ledger (a deliberate third receipt kind, call/0024), the completion-aware per-task gate in software --check (citation-resolve for attested, input-digest staleness for mechanical, verify-drift, skip-citation, orphan reverse-check; an open milestone's tasks are pending, a complete one owes a receipt), the task-anchor link checker (fenced examples masked), and tasks --new scaffolding (the 4B fold-back). The graph passed the Qwen-3.5-4B bar (six of six within-plan, four of four cross-plan; the form is fail-safe, an authoring slip becomes a loud HAZARD). Spine doctrine in host-template (2229dbb) + UPGRADING (71a1613, requires v0.26.0), adopted; call/0024; five-lens adversarial review in design-review.md, operator re-cut (build the graph now, a new ledger file, adopter-facing and mandatory). Dogfooded: plan/0040 and plan/0041 build sequences re-expressed as anchored task graphs. Whole-suite CI green across all three repos.
plan/0043-entrance-checkcomplete, released as host-lifecycle v0.29.0 (2026-06-25; dbeb3db, artifact 01171b92) (spun out of the plan/0040 review). Generalizes the front-door check into an opt-in, reusable entrance check: any project may declare a self-contained document (a published front door, a standalone SKILL.md, an operator-and-agent landing page) and have it held to the spine, inert until declared. The standalone sibling of reconcile (reconcile holds linkable docs with pointers; entrance holds self-contained docs with coverage and generation, because they cannot link). Name entrance settled by Qwen-3.5-4B data (landing/threshold/welcome mis-cued; foyer was the model's metaphor pick but reads too domestic; operator chose entrance). The rename landed standalone ahead of the generalization (operator pulled it forward): front-door renamed to entrance with no alias (the plan/0039 way), plus a deprecate-then-retire migration (the parser accepts a legacy front-door = true with a warning), released as host-lifecycle v0.28.0 and the v0.28.1 migration, recorded in call/0027. The remaining scope is the opt-in generalization (adopter-facing: spine doctrine and an UPGRADING entry), which supersedes the agentic-host-local scope of plan/0040 and the scope half of call/0026; the entrance check at v0.28.x is its first instance. The adversarial review (three independent reviewers, design-review.md) returned a re-scope verdict; the operator re-cut it to expand plan/0043 into the whole entrance effort and folded the review's blocking findings in as design requirements: the marker-break designed out by moving the declaration to a singleton [entrance] stanza that names the member and a document path, so no per-member value can demote the front door into reconcile's components; the concept set wired against its homes; the honest scope stated; and the spine reconciliation landed inside the milestone. The operator confirmed the declaration as one [entrance] stanza, a global singleton among the components, reaching any document by path (a README, a SKILL.md, a landing page), the named member set apart as the front door is today. Shipped: the entrance check reads a singleton [entrance] stanza reaching any document by path, the marker-break designed out at the parse layer; the spine carries the doctrine and the adopter UPGRADING entry (host-template ba86125); agentic-host migrated its .host-software to the stanza. A three-reviewer adversarial code review (code-review.md) fixed two blocking defects before release: the parse problems reached only the entrance command, so a typo'd member re-demoted the front door through reconcile; an empty restates checked nothing yet reported clean. Qwen-3.5-4B validated the declaration form and the ergonomics. call/0028 records the decision. The legacy per-member marker was then retired (host-lifecycle v0.30.0; the operator chose to retire now rather than a release later): a surviving one is a loud problem in every consumer, which completes the deprecate-then-retire path. Whole-suite CI green across all three repos.
plan/0044-prose-lexiconcomplete, released as host-lint v0.10.1 (2026-06-25; 0c2bfc3, artifact 941126c9): extends the LEXICON pre-detection mask (host-lint issue #13) to the prose lane (--prose and --docs) and closes host-lint issue #16, so one sanctioned declaration serves both the naming lane and the host-grammar density engine. The same mask_allowed blanks a declared phrase before the per-tell scan and the density score, surgical at the word boundary, with no new abuse surface (the prose lane reuses the naming lane's provenance-gated entries, and the density denominator is the sentence count, so a mask cannot dilute other tells). Operator chose to extend the mask rather than add a separate prose allowlist or document the gap as intentional (call/0029); validated at the Qwen-3.5-4B bar (gather-data.md, run direct against rope with a real system prompt and non-contaminated options). No spine change: the doctrine that a legitimate tell-shaped token stays in the per-project LEXICON is met, not changed, so no adopter UPGRADING entry. The src/lib.rs edit staled two Kani-backed DetectInternalCodeAsName obligations (caught by host-lint's own CI after the v0.10.0 tag); v0.10.1 re-derived the obligation digests via host-prove and is the pinned release.
plan/0045-shared-docs-enginecomplete, released as host-lint v0.10.3 (2026-06-26; 63348a6, artifact 753ac4f6) and host-lifecycle v0.30.1 (46d481c, artifact 23c27bff, on vendor-v2 sha 4e49536b): closes connollydavid/host-lifecycle#2, the embedded prose engine lagging the plan/0044 LEXICON fix. host-lifecycle runs host-lint's --docs engine in-process for its verify-phase prose recheck, but pinned the host_lint crate at v0.8.1 (before the mask existed), so the recheck ignored the LEXICON the standalone binary already honored, leaving an adopter with a legitimate declared domain noun stuck on a permanently failing recheck. The fix shares the engine into the library: Lexicon, load_lexicon, and run_docs become public host_lint items, host-lint's binary and host-lifecycle's prose_audit call the same functions, and run_docs returns a Result so an embedder surfaces the error its own way. The embedded recheck is now a thin call into the shared engine, so it cannot lag a future host-lint change (the operator chose the shared-library path over replicating the loader, to close the drift class rather than the one instance). host-lint's standalone --docs behaviour is byte-identical across the refactor, pinned by a fixture and regression tests on both sides. Bumping the host-lint revision was the first dependency move since the bundle was cut, so the shared offline bundle was regenerated as vendor-v2 covering host-lint v0.10.2's source; host-lint stays on vendor-v1. The five CI install pins (agentic-host reproducible-build and mdbook, host-template and host prose) move to v0.30.1. No spine change, since the doctrine that a legitimate token stays in the per-project LEXICON for both lanes is met. software --check and the fixture are green; the bug was latent (no repo carries a LEXICON yet), so a constructed fixture demonstrates the close. host-lint was first tagged v0.10.2 (5a9d2c5), but the src/lib.rs edit staled its two kani obligation digests, so that tag's CI failed the obligations staleness lint (kani proofs and all binaries passed); the digests were re-derived (artifact-preserving) and promoted into a clean v0.10.3 tag, the pinned release, matching the plan/0034 and plan/0044 precedent. host-lifecycle keeps embedding the host-lint library at 5a9d2c5 (engine-identical to v0.10.3), so no cascade.
plan/0046-book-to-mdbook-dircomplete, released as host-lifecycle v0.30.2 (2026-06-26; f2b4607, artifact 3a9e59ce): moves host-lifecycle book's generated mdBook source and built HTML under one gitignored top-level mdBook/ folder (mdBook/src + mdBook/out), with book.toml kept at the repo root so mdbook build still runs from root, which frees docs/ for authored content and dissolves connollydavid/host-lifecycle#3 (the book-clobbers-docs/ migration footgun) at its root rather than by a guard. mdBook stays idiomatic: src and build-dir are configurable keys the generator already used (it set a non-default src = "docs"). Settled against the real qwen3.5-4b (rope, true system prompt): a fair tie-break favoured consolidating under one folder once the ensemble conventions were stated, and an acceptance test confirmed the 4B reads docs/ as out of harm's way under the new layout (5/5) and reproduces the footgun on the old docs/-as-output layout (3/3). The five cast personas confirmed the layout and shaped a fail-safe, version-gated adopter UPGRADING entry that an adopter applies on its own with one tool command. Spine change (host-template doctrine + gitignore + ledger at e068828), since call/0014 is already superseded by the spine; no new agentic-host call/. agentic-host adopted the independent, version-gated, fail-safe UPGRADING entry via upgrade --record e068828. software --check is clean, the Site serves mdBook/out, and the whole suite is green.
plan/0047-prose-scans-working-treecomplete, released as host-lint v0.10.4 (2026-06-26; ce683be, artifact a9ef0865) and host-lifecycle v0.30.3 (0de2843, artifact cf59cc16, on vendor-v3 sha 817a71d9): widens the prose lane (host-lint --docs and the host-lifecycle prose recheck that embeds it) to audit the authored working tree, which closes connollydavid/host-lint#17. The shared run_docs walked git ls-files (tracked and staged only), so a brand-new authored doc was silently skipped pre-commit and then flagged by the verify recheck once committed, with no content change. The walk now adds git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (untracked files git would offer to add); --exclude-standard keeps gitignored output, vendored deps, and un-materialized worktrees out, as the bare walk did. The skip was partly intentional (the walk excludes generated content) and staging closes it, but the cast frames a silently-partial clean as the fail-unsafe anti-pattern Bly and Fen and Orin guard against, so the operator widened the walk. No spine change (the walk scope is not spine doctrine), so no adopter UPGRADING entry. Validated: the real qwen3.5-4b judged the new walk catches a new unstaged doc 5/5 and the old walk misses it 3/3; a unit test pins tracked-scanned, untracked-scanned, gitignored-excluded; CI is unchanged (a fresh checkout has no untracked authored files). Cascaded through a host-lint release and a host-lifecycle release with a regenerated bundle (vendor-v3), the same shape as plan/0045; host-lint v0.10.4 re-derived the kani digests before tagging (the plan/0045 lesson applied first try, so the tag is green). software --check is clean, the whole suite is green, and connollydavid/host-lint#17 is closed.
plan/0048-runnable-rederivationcomplete, released as host-lifecycle v0.31.0 (2026-06-26; 3958b62, artifact fe428240): makes a declared deeper rung's re-deriver runnable and its obligation digest earned, expressed as a spine migration so it reaches every adopter rather than patching one host. The recurring born-red tag (a host-lint src/lib.rs change stales the kani digest while the proof itself passes) traced to a missed setup step rather than the release tooling: host-prove was never installed on PATH locally (host-lint's CI installs it), and it was handed src/ rather than the crate root, so the sanctioned obligations --rederive --record-digests could not run, which had been misdiagnosed for two weeks as a /mnt/c WSL ENOENT. With host-prove installed and pointed at the crate root, the full re-derivation runs clean on /mnt/c. The migration widens the existing spine invariant: a declared rung obliges its lane to be runnable rather than merely present (available-is-not-runnable-is-not-discharged); software --check probes that the re-deriver executes and HAZARDs when it cannot, and the digest is earned by a passing re-derivation in place of a hand edit. Validated against the real qwen3.5-4b with neutral framing: a PATH probe reads as too weak (6 of 8 see that on-PATH is not works), and a recorded pass on the current code is preferred 8 of 8 (the digest done right). The cast reframed the release-guard idea into this; the WSL misdiagnosis is corrected in MEMORY. Spine change at host-template 4182df9 (the doctrine phrase "a re-deriver that runs" plus the independent, version-gated UPGRADING entry 6174996 requiring host-lifecycle v0.31.0); the milestone was self-originated, so it closes no GitHub issue. On the operator's trigger agentic-host adopted it the case-(c) way: upgrade --record 6174996 recorded the applied claim, host-prove is installed in the fresh-clone setup and in the reproducible-build CI before software --check, and both agentic-host CI host-lifecycle pins move to v0.31.0. software --check reports the re-deriver runnable for both declaring components and the whole suite is green.
plan/0049-external-reference-corpusin design: adds the host-reference component, a reference compiler that reads external documentation in mixed shapes (Markdown, HTML, PDF, Office, images, structured data, electronic design, mechanical and 3D-printing CAD) and normalises it into a token-lean form an agent can interpret in context, with a deterministic immutable layer (reproducible and attested) and a collaborative overlay for annotations and edits.
