A stub source yields a different hash.</li> <li><strong>Proofs</strong> re-run in host-prove's <strong>version+SHA256-pinned proof toolchain</strong> (apalache / kani / tlaps, plan/0023) via <code>obligations --prove</code>, and must PASS at the declared bound.</li> <li><strong>The offline hook</strong> does only cheap checks: a name-presence <strong>lint</strong> (the named target exists) <ul> <li><strong>input-digest staleness</strong> (<code>git hash-object</code> of the consumed inputs; changed ⇒ HAZARD).</li> </ul> </li> <li><strong>Enforcement is project-pluggable</strong>: that <code>verify</code> (which re-derives) ran and passed before done/release is enforced by a required check (GitHub), a pipeline (any CI), a pre-push hook, or the operator running the <code>verify</code> phase. The methodology prescribes the act, ships the re-deriver (<code>host-lifecycle</code>), and never bakes in a CI.</li> </ul> <h2 id="how-it-closes-each-issue"><a class="header" href="#how-it-closes-each-issue">How it closes each issue</a></h2> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>Closed by</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td>#8</td><td>discharge = <code>obligations --prove</code> re-runs the verifier and gates on PASS, not <code>src.contains</code>; the offline check is renamed an honest <em>lint</em></td></tr> <tr><td>#9</td><td>the <code>bound</code> lives in the lane/disposition; <code>obligations --prove</code> re-runs at it and checks PASS-at-bound; host-prove emits the bound from the real invocation</td></tr> <tr><td>#10</td><td>the running lane is the one plan/0023 already requires; fix the skill CI snippets to be wireable in a separate repo (no <code>./tools/host-prove</code> paths; expose the literal tool name); create the missing <code>references/</code></td></tr> <tr><td>#11</td><td>honest wording: <code>inputs</code> are <code>git hash-object</code> shas + <code>tool</code> from <code>tools.lock</code> (Kani = cargo-locked, not a SHA256 binary)</td></tr> <tr><td>#12</td><td><code>spec_lane_problems</code> additionally HAZARDs a spec under <code>plan/*/spec/</code> and a declared rung obligation with no re-derivable lane</td></tr> <tr><td>#13</td><td>LEXICON (below); URL provenance is checked by a network-having lane (re-derivation), the offline part is masking</td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <h2 id="lexicon-13-folded-in-keyless"><a class="header" href="#lexicon-13-folded-in-keyless">LEXICON (#13): folded in, keyless</a></h2> <p>A line-based <strong><code>LEXICON</code></strong> file (absorbs <code>.host-lint-allow</code>), the <strong>sole</strong> truth (no runtime auto-context). Each entry is the <strong>full contextual phrase</strong> (<code>Windows 3.1</code>, not the bare <code>3.1</code>); a tracker ref carries its URL. host-lint masks the <em>phrase</em> before detection; the identifier/reference tier escalates <strong>warn to error</strong>. Locked: <strong>broad-strict + seed at adoption/upgrade</strong>; <strong>fixed named citation-gated reference shapes</strong> (hash-number, jira-key, gh-cross-repo); <strong>URL = required provenance metadata</strong> (the phrase masks; URL liveness is checked by a network-having lane, not the offline hook). Three <strong>mechanical guards</strong> the 4B test forced: reject a bare master-key entry; <strong>reject laundering a real tell</strong> (an entry that is <em>itself</em> a tell, which forces rename); <strong>CI URL-liveness</strong>. A <code>lexicon add/rm/list</code> CRUD <strong>computes</strong> the phrase so the weak agent never authors it.</p> <h2 id="non-goals--residual-recorded-honestly"><a class="header" href="#non-goals--residual-recorded-honestly">Non-goals / residual (recorded honestly)</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Property triviality is NOT closed</strong>: a vacuous proof re-derives as PASS; meaningfulness is a review concern, not mechanizable.</li> <li><strong>Input-digest staleness defeats honest drift, not a determined forger</strong>: the forger still faces the re-run; that is the gate.</li> <li><strong>A project with no enforcement can self-assert locally</strong>: the methodology ships the re-deriver but cannot force a project to run it (same limit as skipping your tests); the <code>verify</code> phase + the <code>plan/0025</code> receipt make <em>not</em> running it visible.</li> </ul> <h2 id="build-chain-software-first"><a class="header" href="#build-chain-software-first">Build chain (software-first)</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>host-prove</strong> ✓ (v0.2.0, <code>91719aa</code>): a small <strong>Rust</strong> binary that <strong>runs the verifier itself</strong> (<code>cargo kani</code> / <code>apalache-mc</code> / <code>tlapm</code> via <code>std::process</code>) and parses in one process: one command for a weak agent, no shell wrappers, no unpinned <code>python3</code> (<code>verdict.py</code> retired). Emits the <code>bound</code> (#9); wireable CI snippets + the created <code>kani-conformance/references/</code> (#10); honest <code>tools.lock</code>/README; Kani is a cargo-locked source build (#11). Off the trust path; no crypto.</li> <li><strong>host-lifecycle</strong> ✓ (v0.18.1, <code>ca0dfe2</code>): <code>obligations --rederive <dir></code> re-runs each declared rung via host-prove and gates on <strong>PASS-at-bound</strong>, replacing <code>src.contains</code>-as-discharge (#8/#9; the offline <code>src.contains</code> stays, honestly a presence <em>lint</em>). The verdict-interpretation (<code>verdict_discharges</code>) is a pure, unit-tested function; the runner shells host-prove. <code>software --check</code> HAZARDs a <code>.allium</code>/<code>.tla</code>/<code>.cfg</code> under <code>plan/*/spec/</code> (#12). <strong>Input-digest staleness</strong> (v0.18.0, <code>call/0018</code>'s offline signal): a rung declares <code>inputs=<files></code>; <code>--rederive --record-digests</code> fingerprints them (<code>git hash-object</code>) into a committed <code><manifest>.digests</code> ledger, and an offline <code>obligations</code> run reports <code>STALE</code> if the inputs drifted without a fresh re-derivation; <code>--record-digests</code> requires <code>--rederive</code>, so <code>--rederive</code> stays read-only/CI-safe.</li> <li><strong>host-lint</strong> ✓ (v0.6.0, <code>c2c6979</code>): the <code>LEXICON</code> file (replacing <code>.host-lint-allow</code>) + parser (phrase/URL split, <code>#</code>+non-digit comment carve-out) + the three guards reusing <code>classify_line</code> (master-key, no-laundering = the phrase is itself a flag-tell, citation gate = <code>#N</code>/<code>owner/repo#N</code> needs a URL, plus an <strong>opt-in</strong> <code>PROJ-NNNN</code> jira-key gate, declared per project key via <code># host-lint: jira-key PROJ</code>, so standards tokens like <code>RFC-2119</code> stay vocabulary by default) + the <code>lexicon add/rm/list/--check/--check-urls</code> CRUD + the committed <code># host-lint: strict</code> warn->flag escalation (#13). Spec: <code>config.strict</code> + the <code>EscalateUnderStrict</code> rule, allium check/analyse clean, obligations dispositioned. host-lint does <strong>not</strong> enable strict on itself; its own source carries warn-tier rule examples, which strict would escalate to blocking flags. 93/93 integration green; <code>--verify-build</code> reproduces the re-pinned artifact <code>7922649</code> in the recorded container.</li> <li><strong>adopt/upgrade</strong> ✓ (host-lifecycle v0.18.0): <code>adopt</code> seeds a comment-only <code>LEXICON</code> scaffold (skip-if-exists) documenting the format + how to opt into strict / jira-key gating. No active directive (so adoption never blocks an existing repo); the scaffold's examples use all-caps version designators (<code>NT 3.1</code>), so it is itself lint-clean. Note: strict is <strong>opt-in</strong>, not a silent default; the seed makes the mechanism discoverable; the operator audits (<code>host-lint --all</code>) and curates with <code>lexicon add</code>, then uncomments the directive.</li> <li><strong>spine</strong> ✓ (host-template <code>897ce0d</code>/<code>c7aa1ac</code>): host-template <code>CLAUDE.md</code> gains the <code>call/0018</code> re-derivation-discharge principle (AVAILABLE ≠ DISCHARGED; <code>--rederive</code> re-runs in the pinned toolchain; input-digest staleness; enforcement project-pluggable) and the LEXICON principle on the hygiene lane (declare-not-silence; warn-to-flag escalation). An <code>[upgrade "897ce0d"]</code> ledger entry (<code>depends 4a98d92</code>, machine-checkable <code>verify</code>) lands it; agentic-host bumped the submodule pointer and recorded <code>897ce0d</code> applied (the verify post-condition passed). All five rungs done.</li> </ol> <h2 id="verification-14"><a class="header" href="#verification-14">Verification</a></h2> <p><code>obligations --prove</code> (in the <code>verify</code> lane, runnable anywhere) rejects a stub proof (re-run fails) and a boundless/stale disposition; a vacuous proof is out of scope (documented). LEXICON: the 4B re-tested against the <strong>built</strong> <code>lexicon</code> tool.</p> <p><strong><code>--rederive</code> dogfood, DONE.</strong> host-lint's two <code>kani:</code> rungs now declare <code>inputs=src/lib.rs</code> and carry a committed <code>host-lint.obligations.digests</code> ledger; host-lint CI re-derives them through host-prove and gates on PASS (the <code>kani</code> job, the real <code>call/0018</code> discharge, replacing name-presence), while the <code>allium</code> job's offline <code>obligations</code> enforces input-digest staleness against the ledger. The dogfood surfaced and fixed a latent host-lifecycle bug (<code>obligation_gaps</code> matched the whole rung, not just the proof name, so a rung with <code>bound=</code>/<code>spec=</code>/<code>inputs=</code> was falsely ABSENT), shipped as <strong>v0.18.1 (<code>ca0dfe2</code>)</strong>. Whole-suite green across all repos.</p> <p><strong>The build chain is complete, all five rungs done</strong> (host-prove, host-lifecycle, host-lint, adopt/upgrade seed, spine). The only remaining step is closing host#8 to #13 together (a GitHub write, pending authorization), with the whole suite green. No keys, no <code>.att</code>, no CI overfit, no parallel-checkout friction.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0025-lifecycle-receipts--a-tool-readable-phase-manifest--a-strict-tool-carried-release-phase"><a class="header" href="#plan0025-lifecycle-receipts--a-tool-readable-phase-manifest--a-strict-tool-carried-release-phase">plan/0025: Lifecycle receipts + a tool-readable phase manifest + a strict, tool-carried release phase</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>Status:</strong> design hardened by an adversarial review (39 findings, proceed-with-changes); see <code>design-review.md</code>. The sections below incorporate revisions R1 to R6: receipts must be mechanically re-verifiable (a <code>recheck =</code> command or a signed/digest evidence, never a plaintext self-assertion); <code>n-a</code> is tool-computed and a protected core (<code>verify</code>) is un-skippable; the skip escape is content-validated and not greenfield-reachable; the manifest is read at the adopted revision and a missing one HAZARDs; release has no degraded mode.</p> <p><strong>Update (<code>call/0018</code>):</strong> the CI-signed attestation token is dropped; per-adopter key management was a deal-breaker and it complicated parallel checkouts. Where this doc says "attestation" / "token", read <strong>re-derivation in the recorded pinned toolchain</strong>: the release's BUILT ≠ RELEASED is the <strong>host#14 build re-derivation</strong> (shipped, keyless), and a proof receipt re-derives via <code>obligations --prove</code>. plan/0024 is now the keyless discharge milestone, <em>not</em> a crypto prerequisite of the release. Enforcement is project-pluggable (no CI overfit).</p> </blockquote> <h2 id="context-1"><a class="header" href="#context-1">Context</a></h2> <p>Two facts, surfaced this session, are one problem:</p> <ol> <li><strong>The lifecycle does not orchestrate end to end.</strong> The phase order lives only in spine prose, re-typed three times (CLAUDE.md / STRUCTURE.md / UPGRADING.md); the descriptions live separately in seven <code>SKILL.md</code> frontmatters; <code>host-lifecycle</code> has no phase-order constant; and there is <strong>no <code>release</code> phase</strong>, so every release is hand-rolled with raw git/cargo/docker.</li> <li><strong>Agents skip the methodology, strong and weak alike.</strong> A strong agent (this session) skipped the <code>verify</code> gate and hand-rolled host-lint v0.4.2, nearly mis-recording the artifact hash. A weak agent (Fen) cannot assemble multi-step orchestration at all. Instructions are advisory; they fail for both.</li> </ol> <p>The fix (decided in <code>call/0017</code>): make the lifecycle <strong>machine-readable</strong>, make every phase emit a <strong>receipt</strong> (done-with-evidence or skip-with-reason), and add a <strong>strict, tool-carried <code>release</code></strong> phase. A phase with no receipt is the only defect the gate needs.</p> <h2 id="the-unifying-primitive-a-receipt"><a class="header" href="#the-unifying-primitive-a-receipt">The unifying primitive: a receipt</a></h2> <p>The methodology already has nine receipt-like mechanisms (obligation <code>waived:</code>, <code>.host</code> applied-set, <code>upgrade --unverified call/NNNN</code>, <code>repro-exempt</code>, the <code>software --verify-build</code> artifact re-derivation, <code>.host-lintignore</code>, <code>call/</code> <code>Status:</code>, <code>.host-software</code> artifact hashes, PLAN/MEMORY descopes); most already record skips. They are not bound by one format, so a skip in one is invisible to another's gate. A <strong>receipt</strong> generalises them:</p> <pre><code>phase = release # the manifest phase component = host-lint # for per-component phases (embed/release); omitted otherwise disposition = done | skip | n-a # n-a = phase does not apply to this project (e.g. no Where room) evidence = <attestation | tag | commit | gate-output> # required when done reason = call/0017 | <free text> # required when skip/n-a; call/NNNN for substantive tool = host-lifecycle@0.16.0 # who wrote it </code></pre> <ul> <li><strong>Per-project, append-only, tool-written.</strong> Extends the <code>.host</code> applied-set pattern (which already journals upgrades). Fen never hand-edits it; <code>host-lifecycle release --record</code> / <code><phase> --record</code> writes it atomically. The <strong>spec</strong> (which phases exist + modality) lives in the template manifest; the <strong>record</strong> (what this project did to each) lives here.</li> <li><strong><code>software --check</code> re-verifies every receipt by a closed mechanism, never self-assertion (R1).</strong> Each manifest phase declares a <code>recheck =</code> command (the analog of UPGRADING's <code>verify =</code>) that <code>--check</code> re-executes, OR its evidence is a recomputable digest / signed attestation (the done-path binds verdict↔inputs like the plan/0024 token). A <code>done</code> whose re-check fails re-opens as a HAZARD; a manifest phase with no receipt is a HAZARD. <strong>A phase whose evidence cannot be re-derived offline may not be <code>done</code></strong>: it is <code>n-a</code>/<code>skip</code> with cited authorization. <code>n-a</code> is <strong>tool-computed</strong> from project state (not agent-asserted), and a protected core (<code>verify</code> and anything a green gate depends on, <code>skippable = false</code>) refuses <code>skip</code>/<code>n-a</code> outright (R2). This is the gate that makes silent skipping impossible for both agent classes.</li> </ul> <h2 id="the-lifecycle-manifest-the-tool-readable-journal-spine-level"><a class="header" href="#the-lifecycle-manifest-the-tool-readable-journal-spine-level">The lifecycle manifest (the tool-readable journal, spine-level)</a></h2> <p>A single parseable file in host-template (same git-config style as <code>UPGRADING.md</code> / <code>.host-software</code>, a format the tool already parses, no new parser family) replaces the three prose copies. One stanza per phase, in order:</p> <pre><code>[phase "release"] order = 8 modality = conditional-on-Where, recurring-per-component command = host-lifecycle release skill = release evidence = attestation + tag + re-pinned .host-software requires = verify </code></pre> <p><code>host-lifecycle</code> reads it, <strong>at the project's adopted <code>.host</code> revision</strong> (<code>git show <revision>:<manifest></code>), not the live template working tree, and a missing/unresolvable manifest when <code>.host</code> exists is a <strong>HAZARD</strong>, never a silent pass (R4), for ordering, <code>--next</code>, and the <code>software --check</code> receipt gate; the <code>book</code> "lifecycle order" derives from it; an agent reads it to see the whole lifecycle at a glance. <strong>Decision A (<code>call/0017</code>)</strong>: modality is first-class, so the spine's "unconditional" rule becomes "every phase emits a receipt," not "every phase runs."</p> <h2 id="the-strict-tool-carried-release-phase"><a class="header" href="#the-strict-tool-carried-release-phase">The strict, tool-carried <code>release</code> phase</a></h2> <p><code>host-lifecycle release <component></code>, single commands, the tool holds the sequence (so Fen can run it; <strong>strict = maximum enforcement, minimum agent steps</strong>):</p> <ol> <li><code>release --next <component></code> prints the one next step.</li> <li>The orchestrated sequence, each step gated and receipted: <ul> <li><strong>verify</strong>: run the <code>verify</code> sweep; refuse to proceed on red.</li> <li><strong>build-in-toolchain</strong>: build in the recorded digest-pinned <code>toolchain</code> container (<strong>host#14</strong>); never ambient rust. Branch on the <code>.host-software</code> recipe: artifact-bearing component (host-lint: bump, then lock, then reproducible build, then re-hash) vs tool (host-prove: tag only, no artifact). The orchestration reads the recipe; it is not one fixed procedure.</li> <li><strong>re-derive (BUILT ≠ RELEASED)</strong>: the build re-derives in the recorded <code>toolchain</code> container and must reproduce the recorded <code>artifact</code> hash (<strong>host#14</strong>, <code>software --verify-build</code>, keyless, shipped); a proof obligation re-runs via <code>obligations --prove</code>. A hand-rolled or stale build yields a different hash and cannot discharge. Re-derivation runs anywhere (any CI / local / pre-push), no token, no key (<code>call/0018</code>).</li> <li><strong>re-pin + tag</strong>: done <strong>last and resumably</strong>, after attestation: re-pin <code>.host-software</code>, push the annotated <code>vX.Y.Z</code> tag (the tag-every-release rule becomes a mechanical receipt check, not a MEMORY note). The tool <strong>computes</strong> the version, NOT a free semver-level pick: the Fen de-risk had the 4B correctly reason "removing a public flag is breaking" yet answer <code>minor</code>, so the tool asks a concrete change-class question (remove/rename a public flag or change output? add a flag? neither?) and itself maps the answer to <code>major|minor|patch</code>, then edits Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock. Fen never names the semver level. It <strong>computes</strong> the hash from a verified container build, refusing to write a canonical hash from anything else; when no container runtime is present, release BLOCKS (the re-pin/re-hash hazard the strong agent nearly hit this session is never handed to Fen) (R5/R6). The build step is a per-<code>BuildView</code> loop mirroring <code>builds_view()</code>, not a binary artifact-vs-tool guess.</li> <li><strong>receipt</strong>: <code>release --record</code> writes the release receipt atomically, recording the pin released + the blob/commit <code>verify</code> ran against; re-check asserts equality (R5).</li> </ul> </li> <li><strong>Migrated escape (Decision B), content-validated (R3)</strong>: <code>release --record <component> --skip call/NNNN</code> only for a component whose recipe shows foreign/migrated provenance (a <code>repro-exempt</code>-class marker, never greenfield); the cited decision is <strong>parsed</strong> (require <code>Status: accepted</code> + a <code>Scope:</code>/<code>authorizes-skip:</code> header naming this phase and component; citing the meta-decision is forbidden), and the skip records the decision body's <code>git hash-object</code> so a changed justification re-opens. <strong>The skip citation is an exact-token field a 4B fumbles</strong> (Fen de-risk: it emitted <code>--skip reproducible-build/0031</code>, conflating the phase name with the decision id), so <code>release --next</code> prints the <em>literal</em> skip command for the eligible component (<code>release --record legacy-c --skip call/0031</code>) to copy, and <code>--record</code> rejects a citation that is not a bare <code>call/NNNN</code> (never constructed from the phase name).</li> </ol> <p><strong>Decision C = strictest (<code>call/0017</code>, operator).</strong> Gated on host#14's pinned re-derivation (<code>call/0018</code>); no shallow interim.</p> <h2 id="build-order-software-first-each-pushed-before-its-dependents"><a class="header" href="#build-order-software-first-each-pushed-before-its-dependents">Build order (software-first; each pushed before its dependents)</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>host#14</strong>: <code>software --verify-build</code> runs the recipe <strong>in the recorded <code>toolchain</code> container</strong>; <code>software --check</code> HAZARDs an <code>artifact</code> with no <code>toolchain</code>; skip cleanly when no container runtime (never silent ambient-DRIFT). [host-lifecycle]</li> <li><strong>plan/0024 (sound discharge, <code>call/0018</code>)</strong>: the keyless re-derivation discharge. The release's build attestation is <strong>host#14</strong> (already shipped), so plan/0024 is no longer a crypto prerequisite of release; its <code>obligations --prove</code> is needed only for a release that carries deep-proof obligations.</li> <li><strong>host-lifecycle</strong>: manifest parser; the receipts ledger (<code>--record</code> / <code>--next</code> / re-check in <code>software --check</code>); the <code>release</code> orchestration reading the manifest + <code>.host-software</code> recipe.</li> <li><strong>spine (host-template)</strong>: the lifecycle manifest (dedup the three prose copies, add <code>release</code>); revise the "unconditional" rule to "every phase emits a receipt"; an UPGRADING entry with a machine-checkable <code>verify =</code>.</li> <li><strong>dogfood</strong>: back-fill release receipts for host-lint v0.4.2 / host-prove v0.1.1 (already shipped), then cut the next release <strong>through</strong> <code>host-lifecycle release</code>, the modest minor-version release the operator actually asked for, now on rails.</li> </ol> <h2 id="de-risk-before-building-the-established-loop"><a class="header" href="#de-risk-before-building-the-established-loop">De-risk before building (the established loop)</a></h2> <p>Per the operator's standing pattern ("adversarially review, test design on our weaker agents first then implement"), before code: an adversarial design review of this milestone (done, 39 findings, <code>design-review.md</code>), then a real Fen (Qwen-3.5-4B @ Q8_0) ergonomics test of the <code>release</code> flow, the strict path must be executable by the 4B, or strictness defeats its own goal.</p> <p><strong>Fen ergonomics test, DONE (2026-06-20): proceed-to-build with three fold-backs.</strong> Two runs (twelve situations) against the local 4B. The load-bearing strict behaviours all held: it refused an explicit hand-roll temptation ("just run cargo/git yourself", answered STOP), STOPped on both blocks (verify-red; no container runtime, so never an ambient build), recognized DONE, ran the exact command <code>--next</code> printed, and distinguished an <strong>authorized</strong> skip (cited <code>call/NNNN</code> for a migrated component, did it) from an <strong>un</strong>authorized one (refused). The two fumbles were both exact-judgment steps, so the design tool-carries them rather than leaving them to 4B free choice:</p> <ol> <li><strong>One driver, no agent-named sub-steps.</strong> Run 1 had the 4B <em>infer</em> <code>release host-lint --verify</code> for the first step instead of asking <code>--next</code>; run 2 (which led with <code>release <component></code> as the driver) had it run the whole-flow command correctly. So the agent-facing surface is the single <code>host-lifecycle release <component></code>; <code>--next</code> is the tool's internal resume cursor that prints the next <em>literal</em> command, never a menu the agent assembles.</li> <li><strong>Tool maps the bump level from a concrete change-class answer</strong>, the 4B reasoned "breaking" yet answered <code>minor</code>; folded into the re-pin+tag step above.</li> <li><strong><code>--next</code> prints the exact <code>--skip call/NNNN</code> command and <code>--record</code> validates the token</strong>, the 4B emitted <code>--skip reproducible-build/0031</code>; folded into the migrated-escape step above.</li> </ol> <p>All three push toward the design's own "tool holds the sequence, minimum agent steps" principle; none blocks the build. Implementation proceeds with them folded in.</p> <h2 id="non-goals--residual-risks-recorded-honestly"><a class="header" href="#non-goals--residual-risks-recorded-honestly">Non-goals / residual risks (recorded honestly)</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Copy-at-version skew</strong>: a project behind on upgrades carries an older manifest, so receipt enforcement is only as current as its last upgrade; <code>software --check</code> degrades, not crashes. In tension with plan/0022 honest-partial-upgrades (recorded, not resolved).</li> <li><strong>Receipt triviality</strong>: a <code>done</code> receipt attests the evidence held, not that the phase was done <em>well</em> (a green verify can still be a weak verify). Bounds what this fixes; same honest limit as <code>call/0016</code>'s property-triviality.</li> <li><strong>No keys / no key-management burden</strong>: discharge is re-derivation in a pinned toolchain (<code>call/0018</code>), not a signed token, so the per-adopter key management and parallel-checkout friction that sank <code>call/0016</code> are gone.</li> </ul> <h2 id="verification-15"><a class="header" href="#verification-15">Verification</a></h2> <p><code>software --check</code> HAZARDs a manifest phase with no receipt and a <code>done</code> whose evidence went stale; a release with no attestation cannot discharge; the Fen test reaches the correct <code>release --next</code> step unaided. Whole-suite green across all repos; the host-lint v0.4.2 / host-prove v0.1.1 releases carry receipts; the next release is cut through the orchestration.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0026-open-bug-closure-the-full-sequenced-plan-for-every-open-issue"><a class="header" href="#plan0026-open-bug-closure-the-full-sequenced-plan-for-every-open-issue">plan/0026, Open-bug closure: the full sequenced plan for every open issue</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>STATUS: COMPLETE (2026-06-20).</strong> All 8 tracked bugs closed, whole-suite green. Standalone fixes: host-lint#7 (v0.4.3), host#14 (host-lifecycle v0.16.0). plan/0024: host#8, #9, #10, #11, #12, #13, closed together after a completeness review that caught and fixed one residual (#11's tools.lock header still overstated uniform SHA256; host-prove f0852e5). connollydavid/host has zero open issues. The plan/0025 release-orchestration track is separate work, since landed in plan/0028 + plan/0029.</p> </blockquote> <p>The single plan that closes <strong>every</strong> open bug across the project, in dependency order. It does not re-specify the mechanisms (those live in plan/0024 and plan/0025); it is the coverage map + the critical path + the per-stage closure criteria, so no open bug is orphaned and the build order is unambiguous.</p> <h2 id="every-open-bug-8-and-what-closes-it"><a class="header" href="#every-open-bug-8-and-what-closes-it">Every open bug (8), and what closes it</a></h2> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>Bug</th><th>One line</th><th>Closed by</th><th>Mechanism</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td><strong>host-lint#7</strong></td><td><code>--all</code> ignores <code>.gitignore</code>, walks <code>build/</code>, vendored deps, <code>.git/</code>; slow + noisy; contradicts the "scan tracked files" docs</td><td><strong>Standalone</strong> (host-lint fix)</td><td><code>--all</code> scans actually-tracked files (<code>git ls-files</code>) / a gitignore-aware walk; doc matches behaviour</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#14</strong></td><td><code>software --verify-build</code> builds with ambient rust, ignoring the recorded <code>toolchain</code> pin</td><td><strong>Standalone</strong> (host-lifecycle fix)</td><td>build the recipe inside the recorded digest-pinned <code>toolchain</code> container; <code>--check</code> HAZARDs <code>artifact</code> with no <code>toolchain</code>; skip cleanly when no runtime</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#8</strong></td><td>rung discharge is name-presence (<code>src.contains</code>), not a real PASS</td><td><strong>plan/0024</strong></td><td>discharge = re-run the verifier in the pinned toolchain (<code>obligations --prove</code>); <code>src.contains</code> removed</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#9</strong></td><td>bounded rungs' soundness bound (<code>--length</code>/<code>--unwind</code>) unrepresentable + dropped</td><td><strong>plan/0024</strong></td><td><code>bound</code> is a required signed field; extend <code>verdict.py</code> to emit it <strong>first</strong></td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#10</strong></td><td>host-prove rung CI lanes un-wireable in a separate repo (host-relative paths, wrapper HAZARD, broken <code>references/</code>)</td><td><strong>plan/0024</strong></td><td>wireable CI snippets, expose the literal tool name, create <code>references/</code></td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#11</strong></td><td>host-prove README/<code>tools.lock</code> overstate uniform SHA256 (Kani is a cargo-locked source build)</td><td><strong>plan/0024</strong></td><td>honest wording: <code>git hash-object</code> inputs + tool from <code>tools.lock</code>; Kani ≠ SHA256 binary</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#12</strong></td><td>specs under <code>plan/*/spec/</code> evade the mandatory lanes</td><td><strong>plan/0024</strong></td><td><code>spec_lane_problems</code> HAZARDs a <code>plan/*/spec/</code> spec + a declared rung with no re-derivable lane</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#13</strong></td><td>LEXICON: tell-shaped tokens have no provenance, so can only warn</td><td><strong>plan/0024</strong></td><td>line-based LEXICON; URL liveness checked by a network-having lane; warn becomes error</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>host#14</strong> <em>(orchestration home)</em></td><td>the strict release that consumes the pinned-toolchain build</td><td><strong>plan/0025</strong></td><td>host#14's fix is standalone; its <em>use</em> (release builds in the pinned toolchain) lands with the release work</td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p><strong>Coverage: 8/8 mapped, no orphans.</strong> host-lint#7 and host#14 were the only open bugs without a milestone; both are standalone fixes. #8 to #13 are plan/0024. The orchestration/receipts meta-gap (not a GitHub bug, but the root of this session's findings) is plan/0025.</p> <h2 id="dependency-dag--critical-path"><a class="header" href="#dependency-dag--critical-path">Dependency DAG / critical path</a></h2> <pre><code>Standalone fixes (independent, no deps): host-lint#7 ──▶ host-lint patch release host#14 ──▶ host-lifecycle release (its build is re-derived in the pinned toolchain — that re-derivation IS host#14, already done) plan/0024 (the big milestone, independent of the standalone fixes): host-prove(#9,#10,#11) ▶ host-lifecycle(#8,#12) ▶ host-lint LEXICON(#13) ▶ seed ▶ spine plan/0025 (consumes the standalone host#14 build + plan/0024): host-lifecycle(manifest+receipts+release) ▶ spine manifest ▶ dogfood releases </code></pre> <p>Critical path = plan/0024 to plan/0025 (the longest chain). host-lint#7 and host#14 are off the critical path and can land immediately.</p> <h2 id="the-sequence"><a class="header" href="#the-sequence">The sequence</a></h2> <h3 id="standalone-quick-wins-close-host-lint7--host14"><a class="header" href="#standalone-quick-wins-close-host-lint7--host14">Standalone quick wins (close host-lint#7 + host#14)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>host-lint#7</strong>: replace the naive <code>walkdir_simple</code> in the <code>--all</code> path with a tracked-file scan (<code>git ls-files</code>, honest to the docs) or a <code>.gitignore</code>-aware walk; verify on a repo with a populated <code>build/</code> + vendored tree (finishes fast, flags only authored source). host-lint patch release (v0.4.3, re-hash + re-pin per the version-bump-moves-the-hash rule), tag, CI green. <strong>Closes host-lint#7.</strong></li> <li><strong>host#14</strong>: <code>software_verify_build</code> runs the recipe <strong>inside</strong> the recorded <code>toolchain</code> container (not ambient rust); <code>software_check</code> HAZARDs an <code>artifact</code> with no <code>toolchain</code>; both skip cleanly (clear message) when no container runtime is present, never silent ambient-DRIFT. host-lifecycle minor release, tag, re-pin CI. <strong>Closes host#14.</strong> (Also: plan/0025 build-step-1.)</li> </ul> <h3 id="plan0024-sound-discharge-by-re-derivation--lexicon-closes-host8-to-13"><a class="header" href="#plan0024-sound-discharge-by-re-derivation--lexicon-closes-host8-to-13">plan/0024: sound discharge by re-derivation + LEXICON (closes host#8 to #13)</a></h3> <p>Re-scoped by <strong><code>call/0018</code></strong> (the CI-signed token was a deal-breaker, per-adopter key management, and "CI-green" overfits GitHub): discharge = <strong>re-derivation in the recorded pinned toolchain</strong>, no keys, portable to any CI or none. Build chain is software-first:</p> <ol> <li><strong>host-prove</strong>: a small <strong>Rust</strong> binary (retiring <code>verdict.py</code>, off the trust path) emits the <code>bound</code> (<strong>#9</strong>); keep the pinned proof toolchains; wireable CI snippets + <code>kani-conformance/references/</code> (<strong>#10</strong>); honest <code>tools.lock</code>/README (<strong>#11</strong>). No signer, no crypto.</li> <li><strong>host-lifecycle</strong>: <code>obligations --prove</code> re-runs each declared rung in its pinned toolchain and gates on PASS-at-bound, replacing <code>src.contains</code> (<strong>#8</strong>); input-digest staleness; add the <code>plan/*/spec/</code> lane gate (<strong>#12</strong>).</li> <li><strong>host-lint</strong>: LEXICON loader + the three mechanical guards + named citation-gated shapes + <code>lexicon</code> CRUD + the committed strict switch (<strong>#13</strong>).</li> <li><strong>adopt/upgrade</strong>: seed LEXICON + the strict default at adoption.</li> <li><strong>spine</strong>: <code>call/0018</code> (discharge = pinned re-derivation; enforcement project-pluggable) + the <code>AVAILABLE ≠ DISCHARGED</code> / LEXICON principles + an UPGRADING entry; agentic-host re-records.</li> </ol> <h3 id="plan0025-orchestration--receipts--strict-release-the-meta-gap-finalizes-host14"><a class="header" href="#plan0025-orchestration--receipts--strict-release-the-meta-gap-finalizes-host14">plan/0025: orchestration + receipts + strict release (the meta-gap, finalizes host#14)</a></h3> <p>Depends on the standalone host#14 (the build re-derivation) + plan/0024 (sound discharge). Build per plan/0025 (hardened R1 to R6): host-lifecycle manifest parser + receipts ledger + release orchestration, then spine lifecycle manifest + the "every phase emits a receipt" rule + UPGRADING, then dogfood by re-cutting host-lint / host-prove releases <strong>through</strong> <code>host-lifecycle release</code>.</p> <h2 id="definition-of-done-complete--whole-suite-green--every-issue-closed"><a class="header" href="#definition-of-done-complete--whole-suite-green--every-issue-closed">Definition of done (complete = whole-suite green + every issue closed)</a></h2> <ul> <li>Every open issue closed by a <strong>referencing commit</strong> (<code>closes connollydavid/host#N</code> once the fix lands and CI is green), title lint-clean.</li> <li><code>host-lifecycle verify .</code> clean (validate + <code>software --check</code> + <code>book --check</code> + tell test) on agentic-host; whole-suite green across host-lint, host-lifecycle, host-prove, host-grammar, and this host (complete-means-whole-suite-green, mind main-only triggers and the release jobs).</li> <li>The strict release phase is dogfooded: at least one component re-released <strong>through</strong> <code>host-lifecycle release</code>, and it leaves a re-verifiable release receipt.</li> </ul> <h2 id="de-risk-status"><a class="header" href="#de-risk-status">De-risk status</a></h2> <p>Mechanisms are already de-risked: plan/0024 by the LEXICON adversarial review (24 flaws) + the real-4B test (the attestation design panel's CI-signed token was later dropped, <code>call/0018</code> re-derivation needs no keys); plan/0025 by the 39-finding adversarial review (<code>plan/0025/design-review.md</code>). The remaining live de-risk is the Fen (4B) ergonomics test of the <code>host-lifecycle release</code> UX, run <strong>after</strong> its prototype exists (the UX is tool-computed; a live test beats a mock). The standalone fixes need no design de-risk (they are bounded corrections).</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0027-host-lintignore-fenced-blocks-idiomatic-literal-citation-exclusion-call0019"><a class="header" href="#plan0027-host-lintignore-fenced-blocks-idiomatic-literal-citation-exclusion-call0019">plan/0027, <code>host-lint:ignore</code> fenced blocks: idiomatic literal-citation exclusion (call/0019)</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>STATUS: COMPLETE (2026-06-20).</strong> Shipped through host-lint's own lifecycle: the block (host-lint v0.7.0) skips a fenced block tagged <code>host-lint:ignore</code>; specced, tested (regression <code>bbd0687</code> pins the boundary, an ignore region, a still-scanned code block, then a second ignore region), released, applied repo-wide. The Apply phase settled into a <strong>three-way rule</strong>: reword a pedagogical example or a doc's own ordinal label; box an irreducible literal citation (the PLAN.md retired-ordinal dictionary, the frozen plan/0022 + plan/0025 design-reviews); path-exclude only the append-only <code>MEMORY.md</code>. That corrected an interim blanket <code>plan/*/*.md</code> exclusion and retired call/0009's plan exclusion (<code>call/0019</code>). Spine landed: host-template <code>da000aa</code> box clause + UPGRADING <code>97ddf52</code>; agentic-host recorded da000aa (<code>.host</code> 0 pending). <code>host-lint --all</code> is flag-clean repo-wide; <code>--no-verify</code> retired. The decision rule was validated down to the local weak-agent bar (Qwen-3.5-4B @ Q8_0).</p> </blockquote> <p>The host develops host-lint, so its own docs cite tell-shaped tokens; <code>call/</code> is linted on purpose, and <code>--no-verify</code> was the wrong escape (it hid a real tell). This milestone ships the idiomatic fix recorded in <code>call/0019</code> (host-lint skips a fenced block tagged <code>host-lint:ignore</code>) and applies it, run through host-lint's <strong>own lifecycle</strong> rather than as a tactical edit to <code>--all</code>.</p> <h2 id="phases-each-verified-before-the-next"><a class="header" href="#phases-each-verified-before-the-next">Phases (each verified before the next)</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>Decide</strong>: <code>call/0019</code> records the mechanism (an info-string fenced block; skip blocks, not inline; the only legitimate citations are a tracker reference, a LEXICON entry, or a <code>host-lint:ignore</code> block, everything else is reworked). Then committed.</li> <li><strong>Spec</strong>: model the exclusion in <code>host-lint.allium</code> through the allium skills: a <code>host-lint:ignore</code> block masks its lines before classification, so no <code>Match</code> is created inside it. Disposition the new obligations. Then verify: <code>allium check</code> + <code>allium analyse</code> clean; <code>obligations</code> complete.</li> <li><strong>Implement</strong>: host-lint's markdown naming scan skips a fenced block whose info string is <code>host-lint:ignore</code>; the prose scan already skips fenced blocks. Unit + integration tests. Then verify: a tell inside a <code>host-lint:ignore</code> block does not flag; the same tell in prose, in a <em>regular</em> code block, and inline still flags; <code>cargo test</code> + the integration suite green.</li> <li><strong>Verify</strong>: the full lane: <code>allium check/analyse</code>, <code>cargo test</code>, <code>./test-integration.sh</code>, and <code>obligations --rederive</code> on the kani rungs. Then verify: whole host-lint suite green.</li> <li><strong>Document</strong>: <code>VOCABULARY.md</code> (the rule) + host-lint <code>README.md</code> (how to use the block).</li> <li><strong>Release</strong>: host-lint minor bump, regenerate the lock, re-hash + re-pin <code>.host-software</code> (the version bump moves the artifact hash), tag, CI green.</li> <li><strong>Apply</strong>: wrap the <code>PLAN.md</code> retired-ordinal dictionary in a <code>host-lint:ignore</code> block; reword the residual prose violations (<code>Stage</code>/<code>step</code>/<code>Pass:</code> ordinals); retire the <code>--no-verify</code> standing rule (the hook is already refreshed to the pinned host-lint). Then verify: <code>host-lint --all</code> is clean repo-wide, with no <code>--no-verify</code> anywhere.</li> <li><strong>Spine</strong>: host-template <code>CLAUDE.md</code> gains the principle (literal tell-citations go in a <code>host-lint:ignore</code> block; violations are reworded; the lane is never bypassed) + an UPGRADING entry; agentic-host re-records.</li> <li><strong>Records</strong>: <code>PLAN.md</code> row + MEMORY.</li> </ol> <h2 id="done"><a class="header" href="#done">Done</a></h2> <p><code>host-lint --all</code> clean across the whole host, the dictionary preserved verbatim inside a visible <code>host-lint:ignore</code> block, every prose violation reworded, <code>--no-verify</code> retired, and the mechanism specced, tested, and released through host-lint's own lifecycle, not bolted on.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0028-the-host--family-as-where-room-software-components"><a class="header" href="#plan0028-the-host--family-as-where-room-software-components">plan/0028: The host-* family as Where-room software components</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>Decision:</strong> <code>call/0020</code>. agentic-host is a multi-software development host; everything <code>host-*</code> is software developed here and belongs in <code>.host-software</code> as a Where component, while <code>tools/</code> holds only the external referenced tools (allium, specula). This milestone is the migration that realizes that decision, and it folds in the deferred <code>plan/0025</code> #41/#50 dogfood (<code>release</code> on the tools), which becomes possible exactly because the tools become resolvable Where components.</p> <p><strong>Re-cut after adversarial review</strong> (<code>design-review.md</code>, <em>proceed only with major revisions</em>) and the operator rulings recorded below. The blocking <em>spine-contradiction</em> is <strong>resolved by <code>plan/0029</code></strong>, which folded the tool-residency clause into the spine, so <code>call/0020</code> now <em>applies</em> that clause rather than forking it. The remaining open decisions are settled in <strong>Operator rulings</strong>. The invalid linear build order is replaced by the <strong>two-pass bootstrap</strong> (Readiness then Cutover) that names and breaks the genuine host-lifecycle bootstrap cycle. This milestone now <strong>depends on <code>plan/0029</code></strong> for the nested layout, addressing, recipe-dispatch, and skill-link integrity it builds on.</p> </blockquote> <h2 id="status-landed-2026-06-22"><a class="header" href="#status-landed-2026-06-22">Status: landed (2026-06-22)</a></h2> <p>Both passes are complete and pushed; the whole suite is green. The Readiness pass hardened and spec-bore the producers; the Cutover pass made the atomic agentic-host change. Each release was cut by the tool-carried <code>host-lifecycle release <component> --change-class neither</code> (the tool computes the version), the producer tag is the release, and the orchestration consumed it (re-pin plus receipt):</p> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Release</th><th>Pin</th><th>Recorded artifact sha256</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td>host-grammar</td><td>v0.3.0</td><td><code>9d51468</code></td><td>repo-only (no artifact; the version-bump commit sits directly on the proofs HEAD <code>fbd2e6c</code>, so ruling #3 holds)</td></tr> <tr><td>host-lint</td><td>v0.8.1</td><td><code>1386e9a</code></td><td><code>4e76682b1893…</code> (re-released onto the new grammar)</td></tr> <tr><td>host-lifecycle</td><td>v0.19.2</td><td><code>6fa94cf</code></td><td><code>ad3bf89a55c9…</code></td></tr> <tr><td>host-prove</td><td>v0.2.1</td><td><code>135539b</code></td><td><code>8e3742f8b7d7…</code></td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p>The atomic agentic-host commit (<code>e7d952d</code>) added the three stanzas, re-pinned host-lint, dropped the two submodules (with <code>.git/modules</code> pruned), generalized <code>link-skills.sh</code> to wire both <code>tools/</code> submodules and <code>software/<name>/main/</code> worktrees, and back-filled every embed and release receipt in the one commit. The released pinned host-lifecycle (<code>6fa94cf</code>, installed by <code>cargo install --rev</code>) gates this repo (self-cert): <code>software --check</code> and <code>--verify-build</code> are green (all three artifacts reproduce in the recorded container), all eleven software-skill links resolve, <code>book --check</code> renders, and the commit-hook tell test passes. <code>CLAUDE.md</code> §0 carries the seed-first fresh-clone order, and a cold-clone CI job guards the seed path and link integrity.</p> <p>One note against the plan as written: the version cuts are host-lifecycle 0.19.2, host-prove 0.2.1, and host-lint 0.8.1 (the producers had already advanced past the <code>0.19.0</code> placeholder the plan used; the tool computed each from the change class).</p> <h2 id="context-2"><a class="header" href="#context-2">Context</a></h2> <p><code>plan/0025</code> shipped the receipt gate and a tool-carried <code>release</code> phase, but it surfaced a false "tooling vs Where-room software" binary: host-lint is a <code>.host-software</code> Where component, while host-lifecycle and host-prove are <code>tools/</code> submodules and host-grammar is a git-dep library, so <code>release host-lifecycle</code> could not resolve, and the dogfood was deferred. <code>call/0020</code> corrects this: the methodology already supports more than one software-under-development (<code>.host-software</code> is "one or more components"; <code>embed</code>/<code>release</code> are <code>recurring-per-component</code>; agentic-host already carries two). The fix is to make the residency uniform, the host-lint model for the whole family.</p> <p><code>plan/0029</code> then made the multi-component Where room first-class and generic (the nested <code>software/<component>/<branch>/</code> layout, <code>--item</code> addressing, recipe-dispatch on <code>artifact?</code>/<code>build?</code>, the generated-link integrity check) and folded the residency clause into the spine. agentic-host is already migrated to the nested layout (<code>software/host-lint/main/</code>, <code>software/host/main/</code>). This milestone is what proceeds on top: add the rest of the family as components.</p> <h2 id="operator-rulings-2026-06-22"><a class="header" href="#operator-rulings-2026-06-22">Operator rulings (2026-06-22)</a></h2> <p>The design-review left four decision-level findings. One is discharged by <code>plan/0029</code>; the other three were ruled by the operator:</p> <ol> <li> <p><strong>Spine-first vs owned divergence, RESOLVED by <code>plan/0029</code>.</strong> <code>plan/0029</code> amended <code>host-template</code> so the spine distinguishes an <em>adopter</em> (references the host-* tools as submodules, "reference, don't vendor") from a <em>development host</em> (develops and embeds the tools it authors as Where components). <code>call/0020</code> now applies that spine clause; there is no isolated fork. No further action here beyond confirming the applied baseline carries it.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Self-certification: gate with the <em>released, pinned</em> host-lifecycle.</strong> agentic-host's <code>software --check</code>, <code>--verify-build</code>, and <code>release</code> are run by the <strong>released, pinned</strong> host-lifecycle (its producer-CI tagged build, recorded in <code>.host-software</code>), never by the worktree build. The worktree build is only ever the <em>next version under development</em>. The rationale the operator named is <strong>avoiding mixed state</strong>: a moving, possibly-uncommitted worktree binary certifying its own release conflates the thing being developed with the thing vouching for the release, whereas a released pin is a fixed, known-good, immutable certifier. This preserves the independent-verifier rung (the analogue, one rung up, of the spine's "self-referential software is excluded, not bypassed").</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>host-grammar pin: converge on a new tag at proofs HEAD, and re-release host-lint.</strong> Cut a host-grammar tag at the proofs HEAD (<code>fbd2e6c</code>, which carries the <code>plan/0023</code> Apalache/TLAPS work) and pin <strong>both</strong> host-lifecycle and host-lint to it, restoring the "what host-lifecycle emits is what host-lint accepts" symmetry on a current grammar. host-lint therefore changes too: its <code>Cargo.lock</code>, its binary bytes, and its recorded <code>artifact</code> sha256 all move, so it is <strong>re-released</strong> (re-pinned, new release receipt, green <code>--verify-build</code>). The rejected alternative was freezing the grammar at the stale <code>8091261</code>, which would drop the verification-ladder proofs from what the toolchain compiles against.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Spec lane: host-lifecycle and host-prove become spec-bearing.</strong> Uniform with host-lint: author an <code>.allium</code> spec plus <code>.obligations</code> for each, and wire the <strong>allium lane</strong> in each tool's producer CI. This activates the spec/obligation MUST and re-derivation digests for those components; it is real authoring work (via the <code>elicit</code>/<code>distill</code> skills) and a standing gate obligation, deliberately accepted.</p> </li> </ol> <h2 id="per-target-audit-the-migration-is-heterogeneous-do-not-treat-as-one-case"><a class="header" href="#per-target-audit-the-migration-is-heterogeneous-do-not-treat-as-one-case">Per-target audit (the migration is heterogeneous; do not treat as one case)</a></h2> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Now</th><th>Shape</th><th>Producer CI</th><th>Becomes</th><th>Release</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td>host-lint</td><td><code>.host-software</code> Where, yes</td><td>Rust <strong>binary</strong> (artifact), spec-bearing</td><td>own</td><td><strong>re-release</strong> onto new host-grammar tag (new artifact hash plus receipt)</td><td>artifact-bearing</td></tr> <tr><td>host-lifecycle</td><td><code>tools/</code> submodule</td><td>Rust <strong>binary</strong> (artifact), <strong>7</strong> phase skills</td><td><code>ci.yml</code></td><td><code>.host-software</code> Where, artifact-bearing, <strong>now spec-bearing</strong></td><td>build, re-derive, tag</td></tr> <tr><td>host-prove</td><td><code>tools/</code> submodule</td><td>Rust <strong>binary</strong> plus <code>install/</code>+<code>tools.lock</code>, <strong>3</strong> skills</td><td><code>ci.yml</code></td><td><code>.host-software</code> Where, artifact-bearing, <strong>now spec-bearing</strong></td><td>build, re-derive, tag (verifiers stay pinned in <code>tools.lock</code>; the artifact is only <code>target/release/host-prove</code>)</td></tr> <tr><td>host-grammar</td><td>git dep (host-lifecycle <strong>unpinned</strong>)</td><td><strong>library</strong> crate (no binary)</td><td>own repo</td><td><code>.host-software</code> Where, repo-only (no <code>artifact</code>/<code>build</code>, so the recipe-dispatch tag-only path, <code>plan/0029</code>)</td><td><strong>tag-only</strong>; dependents keep git-rev dep, pinned to its tag</td></tr> <tr><td>allium, specula</td><td><code>tools/</code> submodule</td><td>external (JUXT / specula-org)</td><td>upstream</td><td><strong>stay</strong> in <code>tools/</code> (external by <em>source</em>, not by name)</td><td>n/a</td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p>Membership criterion (per <code>plan/0029</code>'s residency clause): a <code>.host-software</code> component is <strong>software whose canonical source repo is developed in this host</strong>, not "anything name-prefixed <code>host-*</code>". allium and specula are external by source; a hypothetical externally-developed <code>host-foo</code> would correctly <em>not</em> be a component.</p> <h2 id="the-migration-two-passes-readiness-then-cutover"><a class="header" href="#the-migration-two-passes-readiness-then-cutover">The migration: two passes (Readiness, then Cutover)</a></h2> <p>The single-artifact authority rule throughout: <strong>the producer repo's tag is the release</strong>; the orchestration <em>consumes</em> it (re-pins <code>.host-software</code>, records the receipt) and never mints a competing tag (the design-review's <em>dual-release-authority</em> fix).</p> <h3 id="readiness-pass-producer-repos-software-first-no-component-additions-no-orchestration-releases-yet"><a class="header" href="#readiness-pass-producer-repos-software-first-no-component-additions-no-orchestration-releases-yet">Readiness pass (producer repos; software-first; no component additions, no orchestration releases yet)</a></h3> <ol> <li><strong>host-grammar (pushed first).</strong> Confirm or cut the tag at the proofs HEAD (<code>fbd2e6c</code>); its own CI green (allium/TLA plus the <code>plan/0023</code> Apalache/TLAPS lanes). This is the grammar both dependents converge on.</li> <li><strong>host-lifecycle.</strong> Rev-pin <strong>both</strong> git deps in <code>Cargo.toml</code>, host-grammar to the new tag and host-lint to its project pin, and <strong>commit <code>Cargo.lock</code></strong> (it is gitignored today). Ensure exactly <strong>one</strong> host-grammar entry in the lockfile, equal to host-lint's pin (<code>cargo tree -d</code> clean; today the lock carries two, <code>8091261</code> via host-lint and a floating <code>fbd2e6c</code> direct). Add the reproducibility hardening host-lint already uses (<code>strip = true</code>, <code>.cargo/config.toml</code> <code>-C link-arg=--build-id=none</code>). Author its <code>.allium</code> plus <code>.obligations</code> and wire the allium lane in <code>ci.yml</code> (spec-bearing ruling). Prove a clean double-build reproduces a hash in its own repo and CI (container build in the pinned toolchain). Do <strong>not</strong> tag a release through the orchestration here; the 0.19.0 cut belongs to the Cutover.</li> <li><strong>host-prove.</strong> Same reproducibility hardening and double-build proof (its Rust binary has <strong>zero</strong> external deps and its lockfile is already committed; the recorded <code>artifact</code> is <strong>only</strong> <code>target/release/host-prove</code>, never the <code>tools.lock</code> verifiers, since TLAPS is Linux-only and Kani is <code>sha256=n/a</code>). Author its <code>.allium</code> plus <code>.obligations</code> and wire the allium lane. Linux-only, so <code>attest-host = linux</code> (WSL2 counts as linux).</li> </ol> <h3 id="cutover-pass-agentic-host-atomic-where-the-receipt-gate-demands-it"><a class="header" href="#cutover-pass-agentic-host-atomic-where-the-receipt-gate-demands-it">Cutover pass (agentic-host; atomic where the receipt gate demands it)</a></h3> <p>Run by the <strong>released, pinned</strong> host-lifecycle (self-cert ruling), with the worktree binary used only to perform the release dogfood. One coherent change, because the <code>recurring-per-component</code> receipt gate goes RED the instant a new component appears in <code>.host-software</code> without its back-filled receipts, and <code>reproducible-build.yml</code> runs <code>software --check .</code> on every push to main (a main-only-triggered failure, the <code>complete-means-whole-suite-green</code> trap):</p> <ol> <li><strong>Drop the submodules fully:</strong> remove <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code> and <code>tools/host-prove</code> from <code>.gitmodules</code> <strong>and</strong> prune <code>.git/modules/tools/host-{lifecycle,prove}</code> (de-registration, not just a <code>.gitmodules</code> edit).</li> <li><strong>Add the <code>.host-software</code> stanzas</strong> under the nested layout: <code>[software "host-lifecycle"]</code> and <code>[software "host-prove"]</code> (artifact-bearing: <code>toolchain</code>/<code>build</code>/<code>artifact</code>/<code>deploy</code>, <code>attest-host</code>), and <code>[software "host-grammar"]</code> (repo-only: no <code>artifact</code>/<code>build</code>, the recipe-dispatch tag-only path). The uniform <code>/software/</code> ignore from <code>plan/0029</code> already covers their worktrees (no per-component triplets to add).</li> <li><strong>Re-release host-lint</strong> onto the new host-grammar tag: re-pin its <code>.host-software</code> <code>pin</code> and <code>artifact</code> sha256, with a green <code>--verify-build</code> and a new <code>release host-lint</code> receipt (ruling #3, host-lint's bytes move with the new grammar).</li> <li><strong>Skill wiring:</strong> the generated-link walk and the component-skill dir-shape port already exist from <code>plan/0029</code>'s link-integrity deliverable; the cutover wires host-lifecycle's <strong>7</strong> and host-prove's <strong>3</strong> skills from their worktrees and folds the manual host-lint <code>ln -s</code> step into the same mechanism so no link is created twice.</li> <li><strong>Fresh-clone order:</strong> rewrite <code>CLAUDE.md</code> §0 step by step with the <strong>seed-first</strong> sequence, <code>cargo install --git <host-lifecycle> --rev <pin></code> where the seed <code>--rev</code> <strong>MUST equal</strong> the <code>.host-software</code> host-lifecycle pin (else seed and materialized source diverge silently), because the materializer cannot be served from the thing it materializes (the genuine bootstrap cycle).</li> <li><strong>Materialize</strong> the new components via the seed binary; <strong>back-fill the receipts in the same commit</strong> as the <code>.host-software</code> add (atomic, so the gate is never RED on a push to main). Define host-grammar's <strong>tag-only</strong> <code>release</code> receipt shape (it has no artifact).</li> <li><strong>Release dogfood:</strong> cut each tool through <code>host-lifecycle release <component></code> run by the freshly-built <strong>worktree</strong> binary (0.18.1 cutting <strong>0.19.0</strong>, version N releases N+1), the <strong>producer tag being authoritative</strong>; the orchestration consumes it and records the receipt. (This is the one place the worktree build acts; the standing gate stays on the released pin.)</li> <li><strong>CI rewire last,</strong> pinned to the sha the cutover produced, and add a <strong>cold-clone CI job</strong> that exercises the documented seed path and asserts all <strong>eleven</strong> software-skill links resolve (host-lint 1 plus host-lifecycle 7 plus host-prove 3; <code>host</code> 0) and none are tracked. No such job exists today, and <code>link-skills.sh</code> is run by zero workflows, so the generated-link regression has no gate backstop otherwise (<code>software --check</code>'s symlink HAZARD only catches <em>tracked</em> symlinks).</li> </ol> <h3 id="records"><a class="header" href="#records">Records</a></h3> <p>This plan, <code>call/0020</code>, <code>PLAN.md</code>, <code>MEMORY.md</code>; re-pins and re-records as the migration lands, each pushed in its own audited commit.</p> <h2 id="verification-whole-suite-green-complete-means-whole-suite-green"><a class="header" href="#verification-whole-suite-green-complete-means-whole-suite-green">Verification (whole-suite green, <code>complete-means-whole-suite-green</code>)</a></h2> <ul> <li>The <strong>released, pinned</strong> <code>host-lifecycle software --check .</code> clean: every new Where component at its pin, no tracked-symlink-into-unmaterialized hazards, every phase receipt present (now recurring over the enlarged component set), every recheck holding.</li> <li><code>software --verify-build .</code> green for each artifact-bearing component (host-lint, host-lifecycle, host-prove), reproducible from the pin in the recorded toolchain; host-grammar's no-artifact stanza is a clean <code>--check</code> and tag-only case (recipe-dispatch, <code>plan/0029</code>).</li> <li>The allium lane is green in host-lifecycle's and host-prove's own CI, and their <code>.obligations</code> re-derivation digests hold in agentic-host's gate (spec-bearing ruling).</li> <li>The generated-link walk resolves the host-lifecycle (7) and host-prove (3) skills from their worktrees; <code>book --check</code> renders from stubs with all stanzas present and no worktrees materialized; the commit-hook tell test passes.</li> <li>Each tool's <strong>own</strong> producer CI green; <code>release</code> dogfooded on host-lifecycle (0.19.0 cut through the orchestration by the worktree binary, producer tag authoritative, receipt recorded).</li> <li>A <strong>cold-clone CI job</strong> reaches green via the seed-first path (the bootstrap-cycle proof).</li> <li>Whole-suite green across every repo.</li> </ul> <h2 id="risks--honesty"><a class="header" href="#risks--honesty">Risks / honesty</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Bootstrap circularity is real, not hand-waved:</strong> host-lifecycle materializes itself, so the cutover names the seed-from-pin step and pins the seed <code>--rev</code> to the <code>.host-software</code> pin; the cold-clone CI job proves it.</li> <li><strong>Reproducibility substrate is bigger than "one unpinned dep":</strong> both git deps in host-lifecycle must be rev-pinned, <code>Cargo.lock</code> committed, exactly one host-grammar entry, strip/build-id hardening on both host-lifecycle and host-prove, done in Readiness before any artifact hash is recorded.</li> <li><strong>Spec-bearing is added scope:</strong> two new <code>.allium</code> specs plus obligations plus lanes, accepted by ruling #4; authored via the wired skills, not by hand.</li> <li><strong>Self-cert sequencing:</strong> the standing gate runs the released pin; the worktree binary acts only in the release dogfood step. Keeping these separate is what avoids the mixed state the operator flagged.</li> <li><strong>Skill-wiring is a real mechanism</strong> (generated, untracked links with no tracked-symlink backstop); the cold-clone link-resolution job is the guard.</li> <li><strong>Multi-repo, software-first:</strong> sequence strictly producer-first; report any unpushable commit and stop rather than proceeding with a dangling pin.</li> </ul> <h2 id="spine-follow-up"><a class="header" href="#spine-follow-up">Spine follow-up</a></h2> <p>The residency clause this milestone needs is <strong>already in the spine</strong> (<code>plan/0029</code> amended <code>host-template</code>: adopter references versus development-host embeds). The remaining spine-shaped item (that the template prose gain a first-class notion of an artifact-free <em>library</em> component) is already covered by <code>plan/0029</code>'s recipe-dispatch model, where a stanza with no artifact takes the tag-only path, together with its <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> update. This project-scoped migration carries it no further. <strong>Closed</strong>: <code>plan/0029</code> shipped the recipe-dispatch model (a no-artifact stanza takes the tag-only path) and the residency clause; nothing remains here.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0029-a-first-class-multi-component-where-room-recipe-dispatched-shapes-addressing-layout-and-tool-residency"><a class="header" href="#plan0029-a-first-class-multi-component-where-room-recipe-dispatched-shapes-addressing-layout-and-tool-residency">plan/0029: A first-class multi-component Where room: recipe-dispatched shapes, addressing, layout, and tool residency</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>Generic capability: tool (<code>host-lifecycle</code>) + spine (<code>host-template</code>), for every adopter.</strong> Design guardrail: <strong>no project-specific names in the tool or spine; dispatch on the <em>declared recipe</em>, never on a name.</strong> This README is the <strong>re-cut</strong> after an adversarial review (<code>design-review.md</code>, proceed-with-major-revisions): the overfit is cut (no named "shapes", no tag-worktrees, no release-by-ref), the two false headline claims are dropped (this is a path-derivation refactor <em>and</em> a recipe-field change, not "layout-only"), and (by operator decision) the <strong>tool-residency spine clause</strong> is folded in here, so this milestone genuinely resolves <code>plan/0028</code>'s blocking <em>spine-contradiction</em>. agentic-host's own migration (<code>plan/0028</code>, <code>call/0020</code>) is re-cut to depend on this as the first dogfood.</p> </blockquote> <h2 id="context-3"><a class="header" href="#context-3">Context</a></h2> <p>The Where room has always been "one or more components" (<code>STRUCTURE.md</code>; <code>call/0010</code>, settled into the spine), with <code>embed</code>/<code>release</code> recurring per component. But the tooling was only exercised on one component on <code>main</code> in a flat, root-scattered layout, and two related spine gaps were never closed: the <strong>residency</strong> of the tools a development host <em>develops</em> (the spine says "reference, don't vendor" unconditionally, contradicting a host that embeds its own tools as Where software, <code>plan/0028</code>'s spine-contradiction), and the <strong>mechanics</strong> of a heterogeneous, addressable, multi-component room. This milestone closes both, generically, in the spine.</p> <h2 id="the-generic-model"><a class="header" href="#the-generic-model">The generic model</a></h2> <p>A Where room is a set of <strong>components</strong>; each component is a <strong>bare store plus branch worktrees</strong>; the tool dispatches every operation on <strong>what the recipe declares</strong>, expressed as two booleans: <strong>does the stanza declare an <code>artifact</code>?</strong> and <strong>does it declare a <code>build</code>?</strong> There is no "shape" enum and no "library" concept (those were the three agentic-host components in disguise):</p> <ul> <li><strong>declares <code>artifact</code></strong> then <code>--verify-build</code> rebuilds from the pin and must reproduce the hash; <code>release</code> builds, re-derives, re-pins, tags, receipts. (A binary, a JS <code>dist/</code>, a wheel, a <code>.so</code>, all just "declares an artifact".)</li> <li><strong>no <code>artifact</code></strong> then <code>--verify-build</code> no-ops; <code>release</code> is <strong>tag-only</strong>. <em>This path already exists in the tool</em> (<code>builds_view</code> tolerates no artifact, <code>release</code> already prints a tag-only sequence); the one real gap is <code>current_version</code> erroring when a brand-new component has no first tag.</li> <li><strong>no <code>build</code></strong> then <code>--check</code> validates the pin only (a text/docs component; <code>host</code> proves this works).</li> </ul> <h3 id="layout-uniform-nesting-store-separated-from-branch-worktrees"><a class="header" href="#layout-uniform-nesting-store-separated-from-branch-worktrees">Layout: uniform nesting, store separated from branch worktrees</a></h3> <pre><code>software/ <component>/ .git/ # bare object store; name LOCKED to .git (operator); verify git discovery + tree-walk treat it as the store <branch>/ # a branch worktree; <branch> keeps its slashes (feature/login, release/2.0) </code></pre> <ul> <li><strong>(item, branch) is a literal path:</strong> <code>software/<item>/<branch>/</code>. The canonical worktree is the pin's branch (e.g. <code>software/<item>/main/</code>).</li> <li><strong>Worktrees are keyed by branch only,</strong> exactly what <code>call/0010</code> authorizes ("one per agent or live release branch"). A <strong>tag is an output of <code>release</code> and the value of <code>pin</code>, never a worktree axis</strong>; there is no <code>tags/</code> tree and no detached tag-worktree.</li> <li><strong>The on-disk path is a <em>view</em>, not git's key.</strong> git keys worktree admin by the ref <em>leaf</em> (<code>feature/login</code> and <code>bugfix/login</code> both register as <code>login</code>), so the tool resolves worktrees via <code>git worktree list --porcelain</code> (path↔admin), never by assuming the path is the admin name.</li> <li><strong>Uniform for every adopter, even single-component ones</strong> (operator decision: reduce churn over a proportional-nesting special case). The cost is honest and stated in the spine: a one-component, one-branch project sits at <code>software/<c>/main/</code> (three extra segments and a one-time re-materialize) for layout uniformity.</li> <li><strong>One <code>.gitignore</code> entry</strong> (<code>/software/</code>) replaces the per-component triplets, <strong>and <code>software/</code> is excluded from every host-root <em>filesystem</em> tree-walk</strong> (<code>remap</code>/<code>collect_files</code>, the link check, <code>book</code>); otherwise a single tree holding all component source re-arms the walk hazard that reddened Site CI before (<code>call/0011</code>).</li> <li>The off-tree <code>store=</code> escape still applies, with the <strong>component dir</strong> as the in-tree junction (kept shallow for <code>MAX_PATH</code>); <code>--check</code>'s handle resolution re-targets to the nested path.</li> <li>The <strong><code>escapes_root</code> wrong-tree HAZARD</strong> is re-targeted onto the <em>derived</em> nested path, with a regression test that an escaping <code>store=</code> / <code>..</code>-climbing worktree still HAZARDs.</li> </ul> <h3 id="addressing-a-flag-not-a-second-positional"><a class="header" href="#addressing-a-flag-not-a-second-positional">Addressing: a flag, not a second positional</a></h3> <pre><code>host-lifecycle software --materialize|--check|--verify-build [--item <name>[@<branch>]] [<dir>] host-lifecycle release <name> # canonical worktree only; no @<ref> </code></pre> <ul> <li><strong>Omit <code>--item</code> yields all components, canonical worktrees</strong> (today's behavior; the bare <code>[<dir>]</code> positional stays exactly where it is). The specifier is a <strong>flag</strong> because the existing parser already takes the first positional as <code><dir></code>, and a second positional <code><item></code> would be genuinely ambiguous (<code>software --check host-lint</code>: item or dir?).</li> <li><code>--item <name></code> yields that component's canonical worktree (the targeted bootstrap/check/build).</li> <li><code>--item <name>@<branch></code> yields a specific branch worktree (<code><branch></code> keeps its slashes).</li> <li><strong><code>@<branch></code> is never propagated into <code>--component</code> for receipts,</strong> because the receipt gate keys per-component; a ref-qualified component would read as "no receipt" and HAZARD.</li> </ul> <h2 id="tool-residency-folded-in-the-spine-clause-plan0028-needs"><a class="header" href="#tool-residency-folded-in-the-spine-clause-plan0028-needs">Tool residency (folded in, the spine clause <code>plan/0028</code> needs)</a></h2> <p>The spine states "Reference, don't vendor. Each tool is a git submodule … its code stays out of this repository" <em>unconditionally</em>, and lists <code>tools/host-lint</code>, <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code>, <code>tools/host-prove</code> as referenced tools in three normative places. That contradicts a <strong>development host</strong> that embeds the tools it <em>authors</em> as Where-room software. Amend the spine to draw the distinction:</p> <ul> <li>An <strong>adopter</strong> <em>references</em> the host-* tools, as pinned submodules, "reference, don't vendor".</li> <li>A <strong>development host</strong> <em>develops</em> the tools it authors and embeds them as <strong>Where-room components</strong> (materialized as bare-store-with-worktrees, released through the lifecycle), exactly like any other software it develops. The tool's <em>consumed</em> form (the built binary + worktree-sourced skills) is served from that Where component.</li> </ul> <p>This is the spine change that genuinely resolves <code>plan/0028</code>'s spine-contradiction; <code>call/0020</code> (the agentic-host-local decision to embed the host-* family) then <em>applies</em> this clause instead of forking the spine. Update the five-rooms framing, the verification-ladder/STRUCTURE.md tool listings, and the "Reference, don't vendor" rule accordingly.</p> <h2 id="deliverables"><a class="header" href="#deliverables">Deliverables</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>Tool: layout.</strong> Materialize/check the uniform <code>software/<component>/<branch>/</code> tree; resolve worktrees via <code>git worktree list --porcelain</code>; re-materialize must <code>git worktree prune</code> stale admin entries; detect and HAZARD a case-insensitive ref collision; re-target <code>escapes_root</code>; exclude <code>software/</code> from the filesystem tree-walks.</li> <li><strong>Tool: addressing.</strong> The flag-based <code>--item <name>[@<branch>]</code> on the operate verbs; default-all preserved; <code>release <name></code> canonical-only; <code>@<branch></code> kept out of receipts.</li> <li><strong>Tool: recipe dispatch.</strong> Branch on <code>artifact?</code>/<code>build?</code> with no name literals; scope the no-artifact work to the <em>one</em> real gap (<code>current_version</code> first-tag bootstrapping); the rest of the tag-only path already exists.</li> <li><strong>Tool: link integrity (net-new).</strong> A <code>software --check</code> resolver that walks <code>.claude/skills</code>, resolves each <em>generated</em> (untracked) link, and HAZARDs an unresolved one, distinct from the existing tracked-symlink hazard, worktree-absence-tolerant, and <code>software/</code>-excluded; shipped as a host-lifecycle patch + tag. Plus the component-shipped-skill wiring port (the <code>link-skills.sh</code> dir-shape logic, generalized to a Where component's worktree).</li> <li><strong>Spine.</strong> <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> + <code>CLAUDE.md</code>: the nested layout (with the honest single-component overhead note), the addressing, the <strong>recipe-dispatch</strong> wording (drop "shapes"/"library"), the artifact-path resolution under nesting, <strong>and the residency clause</strong>; the lifecycle manifest if any field changes; an <code>UPGRADING.md</code> entry whose <code>verify=</code> is a <strong>state check</strong> (<code>software --check</code> clean on the new layout), delivered through the copy-at-version flow.</li> <li><strong>Recipe: the field change (stated, not hidden).</strong> The <code>worktrees =</code> / <code>worktree = <dir> …</code> fields encode the flat scheme (the <code><dir></code>/<code>.line</code> token <em>is</em> the old path; the branch is recovered by stripping the <code><name>.</code> prefix). Under nesting the branch is the path key, so the <code><dir></code>/<code><line></code> token retires; the migration rewrites or drops it.</li> <li><strong>Generic fixtures + weak-agent test.</strong> A <strong>median-adopter fixture</strong> (one artifact-bearing component on <code>main</code>, no parallel lines) proving the uniform layout and default-all addressing are unobtrusive for the common case; <strong>and</strong> a <strong>stress fixture</strong> (multiple components, including an <em>artifact-bearing</em> library and a no-<code>build</code> docs component, plus a parallel branch). Fen (4B) reaches the targeted <code>--item</code> command unaided (printed, not constructed). Neutral names throughout.</li> </ol> <h2 id="migration-copy-at-version-ordered-tool-run"><a class="header" href="#migration-copy-at-version-ordered-tool-run">Migration (copy-at-version, ordered, tool-run)</a></h2> <p>The five-step apply sequence, stated explicitly: commit the spine change in <code>host-template</code>, then add the <code>[upgrade "<rev>"]</code> entry with the state-check <code>verify=</code>, then bump agentic-host's <code>.host</code> to that revision, then <code>host-lifecycle upgrade --record <id></code> (runs the <code>verify=</code>), then re-materialize. The re-materialize is itself an ordered tool-run sequence, <strong>never by hand</strong>: a clean-worktree precondition (refuse a <em>dirty</em> parallel worktree, so uncommitted work is never lost), then update <code>.gitignore</code> to <code>/software/</code>, then materialize (pruning stale admin first), then an explicit <strong>teardown</strong> of the old <code><name>/</code>, <code><name>.git/</code>, <code><name>.<line>/</code> dirs (a tool step), then drop the old ignore triplets. Note the field-change migration (Deliverable 6) rewrites <code>.host-software</code>'s parallel-worktree lines.</p> <h2 id="verification-16"><a class="header" href="#verification-16">Verification</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Both fixtures green</strong>: median-adopter (the nesting is invisible to default-all) and stress (multiple components, an artifact-bearing library, a docs component, a parallel branch).</li> <li><code>--verify-build</code> reproduces an artifact-bearing component's hash under nesting; <code>escapes_root</code> still HAZARDs an escaping worktree; <code>remap</code>/<code>book</code> run clean with <code>software/</code> fully materialized (no descent); the link-integrity check fails loudly on a broken link and <strong>skips</strong> an un-materialized component; <code>software --check</code> clean; the <code>.git</code> store-dir passes git discovery + the tree-walk.</li> <li>Fen reaches the targeted <code>--item</code> command unaided.</li> <li><strong>Then</strong> agentic-host migrates as the dogfood, and <code>plan/0028</code> proceeds on top.</li> </ul> <h2 id="risks--honesty-1"><a class="header" href="#risks--honesty-1">Risks / honesty</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Uniform nesting taxes single-component adopters</strong> (decided: reduce churn;
