stated in the spine, not presented as a pure win).</li> <li><strong>The real surface is large</strong>: the ~14-site path-derivation refactor, the <code>escapes_root</code> re-target, the recipe field change, and the net-new generated-link walk, <em>not</em> "layout-only".</li> <li><strong>Filesystem edges</strong>: case-insensitive collapse (this WSL2 <code>/mnt/c</code>), <code>MAX_PATH</code> at depth, the worktree-admin-leaf-keying, and the <code>.git</code> store-dir discovery caveat, all must be tested, not assumed.</li> <li><strong>Folding the residency clause enlarges the spine change</strong>, but it is the clause <code>plan/0028</code> needs; coupling them avoids landing a half-resolved spine.</li> </ul> <h2 id="relationship-to-other-milestones"><a class="header" href="#relationship-to-other-milestones">Relationship to other milestones</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong><code>plan/0028</code></strong> depends on this for the layout, addressing, recipe-dispatch, skill wiring, <strong>and</strong> the residency clause that resolves its spine-contradiction.</li> <li>Resolves <code>plan/0028</code>'s <em>spine-contradiction</em> (residency clause here), <em>library-component-slot</em> (recipe dispatch), and <em>new-link-loop</em> (the link-integrity check).</li> </ul> <h2 id="residuals-closed-host-lifecycle-v0210"><a class="header" href="#residuals-closed-host-lifecycle-v0210">Residuals closed (host-lifecycle v0.21.0)</a></h2> <p>The deferred refinements landed in host-lifecycle v0.21.0 (<code>bf23391</code>, artifact <code>a3364020</code>): a <code>software --teardown [--item <name>[@<branch>]] [--force]</code> subcommand that removes a component's worktrees and bare store and <strong>refuses to destroy a worktree holding uncommitted or unpushed work</strong> unless <code>--force</code>; a branch-collision HAZARD in <code>software --check</code> for branches that collide as a path (case-folding, the <code>/mnt/c</code> edge) or in git's worktree admin (a shared ref leaf); and a Fen (Qwen-3.5-4B) ergonomics pass, where the weak agent reached the targeted <code>--teardown --item</code> command unaided. The "resolve worktrees via porcelain" risk is closed defensively: a colliding leaf or case is HAZARDed at check time rather than silently mis-resolved.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0030-the-prose-hygiene-lane-a-walking-docs-audit---docs-weak-agent-fixable-output-and-re-derivable-record-exclusion"><a class="header" href="#plan0030-the-prose-hygiene-lane-a-walking-docs-audit---docs-weak-agent-fixable-output-and-re-derivable-record-exclusion">plan/0030: The prose-hygiene lane: a walking docs audit (<code>--docs</code>), weak-agent-fixable output, and re-derivable record exclusion</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>Re-scoped after a five-lens adversarial review</strong> (<code>design-review.md</code>) and a Qwen-3.5-4B classification probe. The original sketch bundled three strands: a repo-wide prose walk, a hash-pinned <code>.host-lint-receipts</code> ack file, and a receipts-family re-homing. The review came back convergent and grounded in the repo's own shipped code and prior decisions, and the operator took two decisions from it:</p> <ol> <li><strong>Exclude immutable records by a re-derivable predicate + the existing fence, not a self-asserted hash ack.</strong> A <code>(path, hash, reason)</code> freeze would be the project's first self-asserted receipt (inverting <code>call/0017</code>'s "evidence is re-derived, never self-asserted"), and its "edit lapses the ack, then clean to zero" rule would <em>force rewriting the immutable records it protects</em> (against <code>CLAUDE.md</code> §6). The need is already met: <code>.host-lintignore</code> whole-file-excludes the append-only memory log, and the <code>host-lint:ignore</code> fence (<code>call/0019</code>, spine <code>da000aa</code>) line-precisely boxes irreducible citations, and the prose scanner already skips fenced blocks.</li> <li><strong>Defer the receipts-family re-homing to its own milestone.</strong> Moving the applied-set out of <code>.host</code> and splitting <code>.host-receipts</code> rewrites two shipped, gate-bearing files; the installed binary breaks (the gate reads the applied-set only from <code>.host</code> and reads a single <code>.host-receipts</code>), and CI cold-installs a pinned old-format binary. That is a dual-format <code>host-lifecycle</code> migration, orthogonal to the prose need. The settled ontology stands; only its build is sequenced after. See <strong>Deferred</strong> below.</li> </ol> <p>This milestone is what remains: make the prose audit a <strong>real, walking, weak-agent-executable gate lane</strong>, with immutable records excluded soundly by the <em>existing</em> mechanisms.</p> <p><strong>D5 done (triggered on the front-door sentence):</strong> <code>host-lint --docs</code> is zero across the authored docs (about 1,010 tropes reworded across ~60 docs, meaning preserved; <code>MEMORY.md</code> and dated reviews path-excluded), 950fbd6 is recorded in <code>.host</code>, and the front-door Verify passes. <strong>D4</strong> (wiring <code>--docs</code> into the verify recheck for ongoing enforcement) is a flagged spine follow-up: the shared recheck needs a portable host-lint invocation, since agentic-host's host-lint is the embedded Where software, not a PATH tool. <strong>Closed 2026-06-24</strong> (host-lifecycle v0.22.0): the recheck chains <code>host-lifecycle prose .</code>, host-lint's <code>--docs</code> audit run in-process via the linked engine, so the gate needs no host-lint on PATH. Spine <code>e280a8d</code> + UPGRADING <code>641efef</code>, adopted in agentic-host; <code>software --check</code> re-runs the prose audit, and it immediately caught a real regression (a decoration em dash in <code>call/0021</code>).</p> </blockquote> <h2 id="context-4"><a class="header" href="#context-4">Context</a></h2> <p><code>host-lint --prose</code> (the tropes.fyi LLM-slop detector, <code>plan/0007</code>/<code>0008</code>/<code>0010</code>) ships and works on a file or stdin, but it is <strong>unused by the lifecycle</strong> and <strong>does not walk</strong>: the <code>--prose</code> branch (<code>main.rs:453</code>) short-circuits before <code>--all</code> and never loads <code>.host-lintignore</code>, so there is no repo-wide prose audit today. The spine already carries the standing rule: <strong>"Prose hygiene is the same lane, applied continuously"</strong> (host-template <code>950fbd6</code>, <code>CLAUDE.md:220-229</code>): the <code>verify</code> phase applies the prose audit and <code>software --check</code> re-verifies by re-running the prose audit, <code>MEMORY.md</code> excepted via <code>.host-lintignore</code>. But that rule's enforcement (a <code>recheck =</code> in <code>lifecycle.manifest</code>) is <strong>inert until a repo-wide prose mode walks the tree</strong> (D1 adds it as <code>host-lint --docs</code>) and <code>950fbd6</code> is <strong>pending</strong> in <code>.host</code> (recording it now would HAZARD on the ~1,652 existing trope-lines). The front-door README (<code>software/host/main/README.md</code>) already encodes the prose clean as a triggered migration step.</p> <p>So the lane is authored but toothless. The deliverables below make it real and dogfoodable down to the weak-agent (Fen / Qwen-3.5-4B) bar.</p> <h2 id="deliverables-1"><a class="header" href="#deliverables-1">Deliverables</a></h2> <h3 id="d1---docs-a-repo-wide-prose-mode-over-authored-docs-host-lint-shipped-v080"><a class="header" href="#d1---docs-a-repo-wide-prose-mode-over-authored-docs-host-lint-shipped-v080">D1: <code>--docs</code>, a repo-wide prose mode over authored docs <em>(host-lint, shipped v0.8.0)</em></a></h3> <p>Add a single, logically-named mode, <strong><code>host-lint --docs</code></strong>, for the repo-wide prose audit, rather than overloading <code>--prose --all</code>. The principle is <strong>scope determines type</strong>: naming tells hide anywhere, so <code>--all</code> scans every scannable file (code + docs); prose tropes live only in authored narrative, so <code>--docs</code> walks <strong>markdown only</strong>. It reuses the <code>--all</code> directory walk (<code>git ls-files</code>, <code>path_ignored</code>, the <code>book/</code> / embedded-worktree / <code>host-template/</code> exclusions, <code>.host-lintignore</code>) but <strong>restricts to <code>.md</code></strong> and <strong>skips generated markdown</strong> (<code>SUMMARY.md</code> from <code>host-lifecycle book</code>, a file the generator overwrites cannot be hand-cleaned). Prose-scanning <code>.rs</code>/<code>.toml</code>/<code>.sh</code> would flag em-dashes and tricolons in code comments and string literals (false positives, and a nonsensical clean-to-zero bar over source). <code>--all</code> (naming, everywhere) and <code>--docs</code> (prose, docs) are the two single-flag repo modes; <code>--prose [file]</code> stays for one doc or stdin. This is the capability <code>950fbd6</code>'s <code>recheck</code> already assumes.</p> <h3 id="d2-weak-agent-fixable-output-carved-out-to-plan0031"><a class="header" href="#d2-weak-agent-fixable-output-carved-out-to-plan0031">D2: weak-agent-fixable output, carved out to <code>plan/0031</code></a></h3> <p>The clean (not the classify) is the hard part for a weak agent, and the current output defeats it: one em-dash emits <strong>ten byte-identical records</strong> with no column, and some tropes report a whole-document diagnosis with no location at all. Making the output one-record-per-occurrence + columned + fix-hinted + advisory-tiered grew into a multi-repo engine question (the over-emission lives in <code>host_grammar</code>), so it is now its own milestone, <strong><code>plan/0031</code> (fixable prose output)</strong>. <code>plan/0030</code>'s D5 (the triggered clean) <strong>depends on <code>plan/0031</code></strong>: the clean is not weak-agent-executable until the output is locatable.</p> <h3 id="d3-re-derivable-immutable-record-classification-no-self-asserted-acks-methodology--small-tool-support"><a class="header" href="#d3-re-derivable-immutable-record-classification-no-self-asserted-acks-methodology--small-tool-support">D3: re-derivable immutable-record classification (no self-asserted acks) <em>(methodology + small tool support)</em></a></h3> <p>What gets cleaned versus boxed must be decided by a <strong>re-derivable predicate</strong>, never a human "reason" string. The disposition rule (extends <code>call/0019</code>'s reword / box / path-exclude, keyed on facts the tool can re-check):</p> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>Class</th><th>Re-derivable predicate</th><th>Disposition</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td><strong>Live</strong></td><td>editable doc; milestone <code>STATUS:</code> ≠ done; live/proposed governance</td><td>reword to <strong>zero</strong> locatable tropes (GATE)</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>Immutable record</strong></td><td>accepted/superseded MADR <code>Status:</code>; milestone <code>STATUS:</code> done; dated review artifact</td><td><strong>box</strong> the irreducible/quoted tropes in a <code>host-lint:ignore</code> fence (<code>call/0019</code>); the rest of the file stays linted</td></tr> <tr><td><strong>Append-only</strong></td><td>the agent's memory log (<code>MEMORY.md</code>)</td><td>whole-file <code>.host-lintignore</code> (already present)</td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p>To make "Live vs Immutable record" re-derivable rather than inferred from prose, <strong>add an inline <code>STATUS:</code> line to every <code>plan/NNNN/README.md</code></strong> (only 4 of 31 carry one today) and read the MADR <code>Status:</code> field. The convention is the existing form (a <code>> **STATUS: <STATE> (date).**</code> blockquote near the top) and the predicate reads the <code><STATE></code> token: <code>COMPLETE</code> / <code>done</code> / <code>shipped</code> yields <strong>Record</strong>, anything else (<code>planned</code>, <code>built</code>, in-progress) yields <strong>Live</strong>. The <code>.gitattributes</code> (<code>*.md text eol=lf</code>) lands now for cross-platform determinism; the 27 <code>STATUS:</code> additions are applied with the triggered clean (D5), where the classification is consumed. Classification becomes a field lookup a weak agent can do, not an adjudication of "deferred" vs "done" from a status paragraph. No new file format, no content hash, no edit-lapses-clean trap; the exclusion is the existing, diff-visible, line-precise fence + ignore, and a future real trope edited into a record is still caught (the fence boxes only the quoted span, the rest stays scanned).</p> <h3 id="d4-wire-the-lane-into-the-verify-gate-re-derivably-adopted-spine-copy--manifest"><a class="header" href="#d4-wire-the-lane-into-the-verify-gate-re-derivably-adopted-spine-copy--manifest">D4: wire the lane into the <code>verify</code> gate, re-derivably <em>(adopted spine copy + manifest)</em></a></h3> <p>Apply the already-authored spine rule <code>950fbd6</code>: add <code>host-lint --docs</code> to the <code>verify</code> phase <code>recheck =</code> in this project's <code>lifecycle.manifest</code>, so <code>software --check</code> re-runs the prose audit and a regressed doc re-opens the <strong>existing</strong> <code>.host-receipts</code> <code>verify</code> receipt as a HAZARD (re-derivation, per <code>call/0017</code>, <em>not</em> the deferred re-homing). Add a <code>.gitattributes</code> (<code>*.md text eol=lf</code>) so the walk is identical across the Windows/WSL/Linux-CI split. The <code>upgrade --record 950fbd6</code> happens as part of D5 (recording it before the docs are clean would HAZARD). The <code>950fbd6</code> ledger text names <code>host-lint --prose</code>, written before this mode existed; the recheck uses <code>host-lint --docs</code> (the repo-wide mode D1 adds); the spine wording can be refreshed in a later prose-rule touch.</p> <h3 id="d5-the-triggered-clean-waits-for-the-operators-front-door-sentence"><a class="header" href="#d5-the-triggered-clean-waits-for-the-operators-front-door-sentence">D5: the triggered clean (waits for the operator's front-door sentence)</a></h3> <p>On the literal sentence <em>"Read and follow https://github.com/connollydavid/host to keep this repository an agentic project."</em>, follow the front-door README to bring agentic-host to exemplar state: run <code>host-lint --docs</code>; for each flagged doc apply the D3 disposition (clean Live to zero locatable tropes; box record citations in fences; <code>MEMORY.md</code> already excluded); <strong>one doc per commit</strong> (audited docs, each a verifiable goal, where <code>--prose <doc></code> returns clean and <code>host-lint <doc></code> stays naming-clean before the next); then <code>host-lifecycle upgrade --record 950fbd6</code>; whole-suite green. <strong>STOP rule:</strong> if removing a trope would change a normative claim or force a naming tell (exit 1), box it or leave it and record; never mangle a doc to force the gate green. <code>plan/0031</code>'s fixable output (spans + fixes + advisory tiers) and D3 (re-derivable classes) are what make this executable at the 4B bar; until they ship, the clean is a manual cryptographic-and-judgement task and must not start.</p> <h2 id="deferred-the-receipts-family-re-homing-its-own-milestone"><a class="header" href="#deferred-the-receipts-family-re-homing-its-own-milestone">Deferred: the receipts-family re-homing (its own milestone)</a></h2> <p>The ontology settled with the operator stands and is recorded here for the follow-up milestone, built as a <strong>dual-format <code>host-lifecycle</code> migration</strong>, not bundled with this prose work:</p> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>File</th><th>Records</th><th>Written by</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td><code>.host</code></td><td>the stamp: template, adopted revision, name, baseline</td><td>host-lifecycle</td></tr> <tr><td><code>.host-receipts</code></td><td><strong>adoption + upgrade</strong>, the methodology-version trail (the <code>.host</code> <code>applied</code> set <strong>moves here</strong>)</td><td>host-lifecycle</td></tr> <tr><td><code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code></td><td>host-lifecycle <strong>operational executions</strong>: embed, release, materialize, verify-build, install-hooks</td><td>host-lifecycle</td></tr> <tr><td><code>.host-lint-receipts</code></td><td>host-lint executions (if ever needed beyond the re-derivable model)</td><td>host-lint</td></tr> <tr><td><code>.host-prove-receipts</code></td><td><strong>cut until host-prove emits receipts</strong> (no file for an absent consumer)</td><td>none</td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p>Uniform rule: <code>.host-<tool>-receipts</code> = the items that tool executed; <code>.host-receipts</code> (tool-less) = the project's methodology-version trail. Boundary: adopt/upgrade are methodology-version events (<code>.host-receipts</code>); embed/release/etc. are operational (<code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>). <strong>Why deferred:</strong> the shipped gate binary reads the applied-set only from <code>.host</code> (<code>applied_ids</code>) and the receipt gate reads a single <code>.host-receipts</code>; moving/splitting them silently un-applies every recorded upgrade and HAZARDs the embed/release gate, and CI cold-installs a pinned old-format binary, so it needs a binary that reads both layouts, then CI repin, then data move, then UPGRADING entry, atomically. This milestone deliberately reuses the <strong>existing</strong> <code>.host-receipts</code> <code>verify</code> receipt (D4), depending on none of it.</p> <h2 id="adversarial-review--weak-agent-data-inputs-to-the-re-scope"><a class="header" href="#adversarial-review--weak-agent-data-inputs-to-the-re-scope">Adversarial review + weak-agent data (inputs to the re-scope)</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Five independent lenses, convergent.</strong> Ontology (the "tool-executed" framing and the concern-based split disagree on 6 of 8 phases, so defer/clarify), migration (six blocking breaks in the shipped binary from re-homing), mechanism (the hash freeze re-derives the wrong proposition and weaponizes against <code>call/0017</code>/<code>call/0019</code>), weak-agent (line-not-span output, 1,652 trope-lines, unlocatable structural tropes), simplicity (STRAND 3 is a re-architecture smuggled beside a small feature; lean on the shipped fence + ignore). Recorded in <code>design-review.md</code>.</li> <li><strong>Qwen-3.5-4B:</strong> 10/10 correct on clear-case GATE/FREEZE/APPEND classification, "no ambiguity", necessary but not sufficient: the <em>clean</em> is the hard part (now <code>plan/0031</code>), and classification at the margin needs the machine-readable <code>STATUS:</code>/<code>Status:</code> of D3, not status-paragraph inference.</li> </ul> <h2 id="verification-17"><a class="header" href="#verification-17">Verification</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>D1:</strong> <code>host-lint --docs</code> from the repo root walks every tracked <strong><code>.md</code></strong> doc, excludes <code>book/</code> + the software worktrees + <code>host-template/</code> + generated <code>SUMMARY.md</code> + <code>.host-lintignore</code> entries, and skips non-markdown (no prose-scanning code); a unit/integration test asserts the walk + ignore honoring.</li> <li><strong>D2 in <code>plan/0031</code>:</strong> the fixable-output golden tests (one record per occurrence + column, mechanical <code>fix:</code>, advisory tiers) are verified in that milestone; <code>plan/0030</code>'s D5 clean depends on them.</li> <li><strong>D3:</strong> every <code>plan/NNNN/README.md</code> carries an inline <code>STATUS:</code>; the disposition table is documented; a classification helper (or the documented predicate) maps each doc to Live/Record/Append-only from path + <code>STATUS:</code>/<code>Status:</code> alone.</li> <li><strong>D4:</strong> <code>lifecycle.manifest</code> <code>verify</code> <code>recheck</code> includes <code>host-lint --docs</code>; breaking a doc to slop re-opens the <code>verify</code> receipt as a HAZARD (non-vacuous); <code>.gitattributes</code> present; <code>software --check .</code> green.</li> <li><strong>D5 (on trigger):</strong> <code>host-lint --docs</code> clean across Live docs (records boxed, <code>MEMORY.md</code> excepted); <code>.host</code> records <code>950fbd6</code>, 0 pending; whole-suite green (reproducible-build cold materialize + Site). Per-doc commits.</li> <li>New host-lint release re-pinned in <code>.host-software</code> with <code>software --verify-build</code> green.</li> </ul> <h2 id="push-order-software-first"><a class="header" href="#push-order-software-first">Push order (software-first)</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>host-lint:</strong> D1 (<code>--docs</code> markdown walk); new release; tests + CI green; (D2's output overhaul ships separately in <code>plan/0031</code>) re-pin in <code>.host-software</code>, <code>--verify-build</code>.</li> <li><strong>agentic-host:</strong> D3 (<code>STATUS:</code> lines + disposition table), D4 (<code>lifecycle.manifest</code> recheck + <code>.gitattributes</code>); <code>PLAN.md</code> row; <code>design-review.md</code>; <code>MEMORY.md</code> entry (separate commits, audited).</li> <li><strong>D5</strong> waits for the operator's literal front-door sentence; recorded then (<code>upgrade --record 950fbd6</code>).</li> </ol> <p>Spine note: no <code>host-template</code> change, since <code>950fbd6</code> (prose lane) and <code>da000aa</code> (box clause) are already authored; 0030 delivers the tool capability they assume and applies them. If the re-derivable <code>STATUS:</code> classification proves worth generalizing for every adopter, propose it as a follow-up spine clause.</p> <h2 id="risks--honesty-2"><a class="header" href="#risks--honesty-2">Risks / honesty</a></h2> <ul> <li>The fixable-output work (spans / fixes / advisory tiers) is <code>plan/0031</code>; its engine-vs-host-lint fork and the span-less-trope handling are settled there. <code>plan/0030</code> ships the lane, classification, and gate.</li> <li>The triggered clean is a long mechanical campaign (~1,652 lines); the per-doc verifiable-goal cadence (D5) is what keeps a weak agent from drifting, not optional.</li> <li>Deferring the re-homing means the receipts family stays as plan/0025 shipped it (one <code>.host-receipts</code>, applied-set in <code>.host</code>) until the follow-up migration; that is intentional, not an oversight.</li> <li>All host-lint pushes and the GitHub-facing work are covered by the standing software-first authorization; any unpushable commit stops the line and is reported.</li> </ul> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0031-fixable-prose-output-one-record-per-occurrence-a-column-a-mechanical-fix-advisory-tiers"><a class="header" href="#plan0031-fixable-prose-output-one-record-per-occurrence-a-column-a-mechanical-fix-advisory-tiers">plan/0031: Fixable prose output: one record per occurrence, a column, a mechanical fix, advisory tiers</a></h1> <blockquote> <p>Spun out of <code>plan/0030</code>'s D2 (operator: "this needs a plan"). <code>plan/0030</code> made the prose lane real and walkable (<code>host-lint --docs</code>, shipped); this milestone makes its <strong>output weak-agent-fixable</strong>, the precondition for <code>plan/0030</code>'s triggered clean (D5). The fix-site fork (engine vs host-lint) that this plan was opened to settle is <strong>now resolved by investigation</strong>: the defect is a <code>host-lint</code> localization bug, not an engine over-emission, so the whole milestone is <strong>host-lint-side</strong> (see <em>Diagnosis</em>). No <code>host_grammar</code> change.</p> <p><strong>Status: SHIPPED</strong> in host-lint <strong>v0.8.0</strong> (<code>e652ffc</code>), with occurrence-mapping + fix-hints + advisory tiers; golden tests + the <strong>4B acceptance</strong> (atomic) passed; <code>software --verify-build</code> reproduces the artifact in the pinned container. The release run was also the first real <code>host-lifecycle release</code> on a host-* Where component, and it surfaced + fixed two release-tool bugs (yielding host-lifecycle <strong>v0.19.1</strong>).</p> </blockquote> <h2 id="context-5"><a class="header" href="#context-5">Context</a></h2> <p><code>plan/0030</code> shipped <code>host-lint --docs</code>: the repo-wide prose audit walks authored markdown. But the prose <strong>output</strong> defeats a weak agent doing the clean (<code>plan/0030</code> D5), in three ways:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Mislocalization (the headline defect).</strong> <code>call/0019</code> has <strong>ten</strong> em-dashes (lines 12, 15, 23, 24, 34, 38, 41, 42, 56), and the engine correctly emits <strong>one</strong> <code>decoration</code> tell per em-dash, but <code>host-lint</code>'s <code>locate_line(input, excerpt)</code> returns the <strong>first</strong> line containing the excerpt, so all ten records collapse onto <code>line 12</code>. A weak agent sees ten identical records on <code>line 12</code> and cannot tell which dash is where, that there are ten distinct ones, or when it is done. There is also no <strong>column</strong>.</li> <li><strong>Span-less tropes.</strong> <code>tell-density</code> (a whole-document score), <code>bold-first-bullets</code> ("21/23 bullets open with bold lead-ins"), <code>anaphora</code>/<code>punchy-fragments</code> (cross-sentence patterns) report a synthetic diagnosis whose text does not appear literally in the source. A clean-to-<strong>zero</strong> bar over these is an unterminating loop: there is nothing to edit.</li> <li><strong>Volume.</strong> ~1,100+ <code>--docs</code> warning-lines across the authored docs; a long mechanical campaign needs reliable, located output or a weak agent drifts.</li> </ul> <h2 id="diagnosis-resolves-the-fork"><a class="header" href="#diagnosis-resolves-the-fork">Diagnosis (resolves the fork)</a></h2> <p>The fork was "fix the over-emission in the engine vs work around it in host-lint." Investigation showed there <strong>is no over-emission</strong>: for <code>call/0019</code>, em-dashes in the file = 10, <code>decoration</code> tells emitted = 10 (one-to-one). The engine is the source of truth and it is correct. host-lint's <code>locate_line</code> is the bug: it maps every tell to the <em>first</em> occurrence of its excerpt. So:</p> <ul> <li>The fix is <strong>host-lint-side and exact</strong>, not heuristic: there are exactly as many tells of a given <code>(id, excerpt)</code> as there are occurrences of that excerpt, so mapping the <em>k</em>-th tell to the <em>k</em>-th occurrence assigns each a distinct, correct position. No phantom duplicates to drop, no smearing.</li> <li><strong>No <code>host_grammar</code> change</strong>, no rev-bump, no touching its allium/property lanes.</li> <li>The <strong>density-score concern is moot,</strong> since <code>tell_score</code> counts correctly because the engine emits the right number of tells.</li> </ul> <h2 id="deliverables-all-host-lint"><a class="header" href="#deliverables-all-host-lint">Deliverables (all host-lint)</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>E1 + E2: locate each occurrence with a column.</strong> Replace <code>locate_line</code>'s first-occurrence search with <strong>occurrence-mapping</strong>: group tells by <code>(id, excerpt)</code>; for each group, walk the successive positions of the excerpt in the source and assign tell <em>k</em> to occurrence <em>k</em>, yielding <code>file:line:col</code>. Ten em-dashes yield ten records at their real <code>line:col</code>; one em-dash yields one record. Output becomes <code>path:line:col: …</code>.</li> <li><strong>E3: a mechanical fix-hint</strong> keyed on the trope <code>id</code> (e.g. <code>decoration</code> maps to "drop it, or replace with a comma/period"; <code>arrow</code> maps to "use a word"), in the text line and as a <code>fix</code> field in <code>--json</code>. Only mechanically-rewritable tropes carry a <code>fix</code>.</li> <li><strong>E4: advisory tiers.</strong> A tell whose excerpt does <strong>not</strong> appear literally in the source (the synthetic whole-document diagnoses: <code>tell-density</code>, <code>bold-first-bullets</code>, <code>anaphora</code>, <code>punchy-fragments</code>, a span-less self-answered-question) is <strong>advisory,</strong> emitted once for awareness but <strong>not</strong> part of the clean-to-zero gate. Locatable tropes drive the exit code; advisory ones do not. So "zero" is achievable and terminating. (The locatable-vs-advisory split is <em>derived</em>: found in source yields locatable, not found yields advisory, so no hardcoded id list to maintain.)</li> </ul> <h2 id="verification-18"><a class="header" href="#verification-18">Verification</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Golden tests</strong> (host-lint): the <code>call/0019</code> regression, ten <code>decoration</code> records at the ten real em-dash lines (not ten at line 12), each with a column; one em-dash yields one record; a mechanical trope carries a <code>fix</code>; a synthetic-diagnosis trope is advisory (present in output, does <strong>not</strong> make <code>--docs</code> exit non-zero on its own).</li> <li><strong>4B acceptance: PASSED (atomic).</strong> Qwen-3.5-4B (<code>unsloth/Qwen3.5-4B-MTP-GGUF</code>) applied the located + fix-hinted output correctly <strong>one fix at a time</strong>: a mechanical decoration fix (replacing em-dashes with commas) and a no-fix reword (<code>delve</code> to <code>look at</code>), and it <strong>correctly ignored the advisory <code>note</code></strong>. It could <em>not</em> do the six mixed edits in one shot (it echoed the doc, though it still parsed which to ignore). Finding: the format is weak-agent-usable at the <strong>per-occurrence</strong> granularity the clean uses; the compound-in-one-shot failure <strong>confirms</strong> <code>plan/0030</code> D5's one-doc / one-fix cadence and STOP rule (drive a weak agent atomically, never hand it the whole doc). No engine offset needed. A 27B sanity run agreed. (Golden tests prove the mechanics deterministically; the 4B proves the goal.)</li> <li>Whole-suite green (66 unit + integration + clippy); new host-lint release re-pinned in <code>.host-software</code> with <code>software --verify-build</code> green. <strong>Bundled with <code>plan/0030</code>'s D1 (<code>--docs</code>) as one host-lint release</strong>: <code>--docs</code> only pays off with fixable output, so they ship together.</li> </ul> <h2 id="push-order"><a class="header" href="#push-order">Push order</a></h2> <p>host-lint-only: <strong>host-lint</strong> (E1 to E4 + golden tests, on top of the already-built D1), then <code>cargo test</code> + clippy + integration green, then 4B acceptance, then bump version, then re-pin <code>.host-software</code>, then <code>--verify-build</code>, then CI. No upstream repo.</p> <h2 id="relationship-to-plan0030"><a class="header" href="#relationship-to-plan0030">Relationship to plan/0030</a></h2> <p><code>plan/0030</code> owns the <strong>lane</strong> (<code>--docs</code>, built), the re-derivable <strong>classification</strong> (<code>STATUS:</code> + <code>Status:</code>), and the <strong>gate wiring</strong> (D4). This milestone owns the <strong>fixable output</strong>, and its host-lint work ships in the <strong>same release</strong> as <code>plan/0030</code>'s D1. <code>plan/0030</code>'s D5 (the triggered repo-wide clean) depends on this: the clean is not weak-agent-executable until the output is located, columned, fix-hinted, and tiered.</p> <h2 id="risks--honesty-3"><a class="header" href="#risks--honesty-3">Risks / honesty</a></h2> <ul> <li>Occurrence-mapping matches by excerpt string. It is exact when tell-count == occurrence-count (the confirmed reality). If a future detector ever emits a tell whose excerpt is a non-literal normalization (curly-to-straight quotes, collapsed whitespace), that tell would not be found and would fall to the advisory tier (a conservative under-gate, not a wrong location). Worth a property test.</li> <li>Advisory-tiering changes the prose exit model slightly: a doc with <em>only</em> synthetic-diagnosis tropes now exits 0 under <code>--docs</code> (gate passes) instead of warn. That is the intended "zero is achievable" behavior; the existing <code>--stdin</code>/<code>--prose</code> tests assert on locatable tropes, which still warn.</li> <li>The 4B acceptance gate is the real check; golden tests prove the mechanics, the 4B proves the goal.</li> </ul> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0032-hermetic-static-builds-distribute-equals-certify-via-a-pinned-dependency-bundle"><a class="header" href="#plan0032-hermetic-static-builds-distribute-equals-certify-via-a-pinned-dependency-bundle">plan/0032: Hermetic static builds, distribute equals certify, via a pinned dependency bundle</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>Goal:</strong> each host-* tool's distributed <code>x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code> binary becomes a statically linked build that is also the <code>.host-software</code> canonical artifact <code>--verify-build</code> reproduces (distribute equals certify), and the build is genuinely hermetic: its dependencies come from a pinned, hash-verified bundle published as a downloadable release and consumed offline, not fetched at build time. The spine gains a MUST on the underlying property, and the host-* family is the first adopter. Depends on <code>plan/0028</code> and the reproducible-build anchor (<code>plan/0005</code>, <code>plan/0006</code>).</p> <p><strong>Adversarially reviewed twice</strong> (<code>design-review.md</code>, then <code>design-review-2.md</code> on the bundle and spine surface) and re-cut three times. This version resolves the delta review: the MUST attaches to the property with a <code>hermetic-exempt</code> escape and an honest gate invariant, host-lint owns the bundle, host-prove is a no-dependency case, the host-lifecycle staging is specified, and the producer git-rev inconsistency is reconciled first.</p> </blockquote> <h2 id="context-6"><a class="header" href="#context-6">Context</a></h2> <p>The reproducible-build anchor reproduces each tool's <code>.host-software</code> recipe inside the recorded digest-pinned <code>toolchain</code> container and checks the sha256 (<code>plan/0005</code>, <code>plan/0006</code>). Today that recipe builds a glibc-dynamic <code>target/release/<bin></code> in <code>rust:1.95.0</code>. Two gaps: the certified binary does not run on a host with an older glibc (the <code>plan/0028</code> <code>GLIBC_2.39</code> divergence), and the build fetches the <code>host-grammar</code> git dependency and the crates.io deps over the network, so it is not hermetic. The producer release CI separately cross-compiles static musl assets by a different recipe, so the shipped binary is not the certified one.</p> <p>The canonical <code>connollydavid/pgs-release</code> solved the analogous problem: <code>build-deps.yml</code> builds the static dependency sysroot once and publishes it as the <code>sysroot-v2</code> release, and <code>ffmpeg-release.yml</code> downloads that pinned sysroot and builds against it rather than rebuilding from upstream. The build inputs become one pinned, hash-verifiable bundle from the project's own release.</p> <p>For the pure-Rust host-* tools the dependency layer is the cargo dependency set. The same pattern applies: vendor the dependency sources once, publish them as a versioned hash-pinned release bundle, and have each build download and verify that bundle and build offline. Combined with the static musl link and a pinned toolchain image, the build is hermetic, reproducible, and portable, and the certified artifact is the distributed one.</p> <h2 id="decisions-operator-rulings-2026-06-22"><a class="header" href="#decisions-operator-rulings-2026-06-22">Decisions (operator rulings, 2026-06-22)</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>Distribute equals certify.</strong> Each tool's <code>x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code> release asset is built by the recorded recipe in the pinned image and bundle, byte-identical to what <code>--verify-build</code> reproduces.</li> <li><strong>Static musl, not an older-glibc image.</strong> Static musl gives a single binary that runs on any Linux libc and distro; an older-glibc image fixes only the dev-host-run symptom. Recorded and rejected on that ground.</li> <li><strong>All three artifact tools convert; host-prove gains a release pipeline.</strong> host-lint and host-lifecycle ship release assets; host-prove gains a release job (the musl asset plus a tagged release). All three get the static-musl plus offline <code>--network none</code> build.</li> <li><strong>The recorded toolchain is <code>clux/muslrust:1.95.0-stable</code>, digest-pinned.</strong> It ships the <code>x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code> target and std at Rust 1.95.0.</li> <li><strong>host-lint owns the dependency bundle.</strong> host-lint's lockfile is the dependency superset (host-lifecycle's third-party set is a subset; host-prove has no third-party dependencies). The bundle is published on host-lint's releases as <code>vendor-vN</code>. host-lifecycle consumes host-lint's bundle (it already depends on host-lint); host-prove vendors nothing and builds offline against an empty source set. Hosting the bundle on host-lint, not agentic-host, keeps each tool depending only on host-lint and never inverts the host-to-software direction.</li> <li><strong>The spine MUST is on the property, with an escape and an honest gate invariant.</strong> <code>host-template</code> requires that a component distributing release binaries MUST be able to reproduce them offline from pinned inputs. The downloadable bundle is the recommended mechanism, documented in build guidance, not the MUST itself. A <code>hermetic-exempt = call/NNNN</code> escape (mirroring the anchor's <code>repro-exempt</code>) covers a component that cannot vendor offline (a fetching <code>build.rs</code>, or a non-Rust toolchain). The enforceable gate invariant is specific: a component that records a <code>deps-bundle</code> MUST build offline under <code>--network none</code>, and its staged bundle sha MUST match the recorded hash. The MUST is authored in <code>host-template</code> first and migrated onto agentic-host; the agentic-host <code>call/</code> records only the software decision (the <code>deps-bundle</code> production-anchor change, instance-scoped), never the methodology MUST.</li> <li><strong>The anchor certifies <code>x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code>.</strong> <code>attest-host</code> is OS-granular, not arch-granular, so the x86_64 scope rests on the amd64 CI runner and the recorded target triple. arm64, darwin, and windows assets ship with a checksums manifest and are stated uncertified, an explicit trust boundary.</li> </ol> <h2 id="the-dependency-bundle"><a class="header" href="#the-dependency-bundle">The dependency bundle</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Producer (on host-lint).</strong> <code>cargo vendor --locked --sync <host-lifecycle>/Cargo.toml <out></code> run from host-lint's manifest (host-prove contributes nothing) produces one vendor directory covering host-lint's superset plus host-lifecycle's git <code>host-lint</code> crate. Its full stdout is captured: the <code>[source.*]</code> block has three keys, <code>crates-io</code> and the two git sources (<code>host-grammar</code> and <code>host-lint</code>), whose <code>?rev=</code> keys must match the lockfiles byte-for-byte. The directory is tarred with normalized metadata (<code>tar --sort=name --mtime=@0 --owner=0 --group=0 --numeric-owner</code>, <code>gzip -n</code>) so the tarball sha is reproducible, published as <code>vendor-vN</code> with its sha recorded. The scope is <code>--release</code>; the vendored <code>proptest</code> dev-dependency tree is unused by the release build.</li> <li><strong>Consumer.</strong> The build downloads <code>vendor-vN</code>, verifies the sha256, extracts it, merges the <code>[source.*]</code> stanzas into the existing <code>.cargo/config.toml</code> (preserving the build-id rustflags), and builds <code>--release --locked --offline --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code> under <code>--network none</code>.</li> <li><strong>One source of truth for the pin.</strong> The <code>deps-bundle = <url> <sha256></code> pin lives in <code>.host-software</code>. Each producer CI needs the same url and sha to converge; rather than two hand-edited copies, the producer repo commits a <code>deps-bundle.lock</code> that <code>software --check</code> asserts equals <code>.host-software</code>, so drift fails the gate.</li> </ul> <h2 id="the-host-lifecycle-feature"><a class="header" href="#the-host-lifecycle-feature">The host-lifecycle feature</a></h2> <p><code>host-lifecycle</code> gains a <code>deps-bundle</code> recipe field and a bundle-staging step shared by <code>--verify-build</code> and <code>release</code>: fetch the bundle on the host (the one controlled, pinned download), verify its sha256, extract it into the build worktree's <code>vendor/</code>, and merge the vendored-sources <code>[source.*]</code> stanzas into that worktree's <code>.cargo/config.toml</code>. <code>run_build_in_container</code> gains a network-mode parameter: a bundle-bearing component builds under <code>--network none</code>; a component with no <code>deps-bundle</code> keeps the existing <code>HOST_LIFECYCLE_DOCKER_NETWORK</code> behaviour, so the change is additive for not-yet-converted components. The gate asserts the staged bundle sha matches the recorded hash before building (the provenance proof; <code>--network none</code> is the egress proof). After a <code>release</code> build, the staged config edit is reverted and <code>vendor/</code> removed so the canonical worktree is not left dirty; <code>vendor/</code> is gitignored in each producer. <code>install_hooks</code> only copies the artifact, so it stays bundle-unaware. The producer <code>cargo vendor</code> job and the gate-driver <code>cargo install --rev</code> seed are network-allowed by design; the MUST scopes only to the per-component container build.</p> <h2 id="the-spine-change-host-template"><a class="header" href="#the-spine-change-host-template">The spine change (host-template)</a></h2> <p><code>host-template</code> build guidance gains the property MUST and documents the bundle as the recommended mechanism, plus the <code>hermetic-exempt = call/NNNN</code> escape and the <code>deps-bundle</code> recipe field and its gate invariant. An <code>UPGRADING.md</code> ledger entry records the requirement with a machine-checkable verify command, and the migration applies it onto agentic-host (the <code>.host</code> baseline advances and is re-recorded). The host-* tools are the first adopters; the methodology is authored in the template, not forked in the instance.</p> <h2 id="per-component-recipe-change"><a class="header" href="#per-component-recipe-change">Per-component recipe change</a></h2> <p>For host-lint and host-lifecycle (and host-prove for the toolchain and offline build, with no bundle):</p> <ul> <li><code>toolchain</code> = <code>clux/muslrust:1.95.0-stable@sha256:<digest recorded in Readiness></code>.</li> <li><code>deps-bundle</code> = <code><host-lint vendor-vN url> <sha256></code> (host-lint and host-lifecycle; host-prove omits it and builds offline against an empty source set).</li> <li><code>build</code> = <code>CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo build --release --locked --offline --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code>, with the static-linking and build-id flags pinned in the recipe (see Readiness).</li> <li><code>artifact</code> = <code>target/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl/release/<bin> <new sha256></code>.</li> </ul> <h2 id="producer-ci-convergence-distribute-equals-certify"><a class="header" href="#producer-ci-convergence-distribute-equals-certify">Producer-CI convergence (distribute equals certify)</a></h2> <p>Each producer's release job builds the <code>x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code> asset by the recorded recipe: the pinned <code>clux/muslrust:1.95.0-stable</code> image as a container job, the downloaded <code>vendor-vN</code> bundle (per its committed <code>deps-bundle.lock</code>), <code>--locked --offline</code>, and the same strip, build-id, and crt-static flags. The uploaded asset is then byte-identical to the artifact <code>--verify-build</code> reproduces. The arm64, darwin, and windows matrix jobs keep their mechanism but see the trust boundary in decision 7.</p> <h2 id="readiness-blocking-gates-before-any-hash-is-recorded"><a class="header" href="#readiness-blocking-gates-before-any-hash-is-recorded">Readiness (blocking gates, before any hash is recorded)</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>Reconcile</strong> host-lifecycle's <code>host-lint</code> git dependency to the certified host-lint pin (it currently pins the pre-bump <code>93a43fa</code>, not the released <code>1386e9a</code>), so the host-lint inside host-lifecycle is the certified one and the bundle carries a single host-lint revision.</li> <li>Confirm and digest-pin <code>clux/muslrust:1.95.0-stable</code>; record the digest.</li> <li>Produce the <code>vendor-vN</code> bundle (<code>cargo vendor --locked --sync</code>), capture and commit the full <code>[source.*]</code> config, normalize the tarball, and prove the bundle sha double-produces on two machines before recording it. Publish it on host-lint with a <code>deps-bundle.lock</code>.</li> <li>Add the <code>deps-bundle</code> field, the staging step, the config merge, and the <code>--network none</code> path to <code>host-lifecycle</code> (<code>run_build_in_container</code>, <code>software_verify_build</code>, <code>release</code>, and the <code>software --check</code> sha-and-drift assertions); unit-test it.</li> <li>Prove a reproducible double-build of each tool in the pinned image, offline against the bundle, under <code>--network none</code>; <code>readelf -n</code> confirms no <code>.note.gnu.build-id</code>. If the image shadows <code>.cargo/config.toml</code> via a <code>CARGO_TARGET_*_RUSTFLAGS</code>, move the build-id and crt-static flags into the recorded <code>build</code> via <code>RUSTFLAGS</code>. Prove the config merge leaves the artifact hash unchanged.</li> <li>Add the release job to host-prove's CI.</li> <li>Author the spine change in <code>host-template</code> first and push it before the migration: the property MUST with its recommended-bundle guidance, and the <code>hermetic-exempt</code> escape. Define the <code>deps-bundle</code> gate invariant and add an <code>UPGRADING.md</code> ledger entry there as well.</li> </ol> <h2 id="cutover-atomic-re-release-round-software-first"><a class="header" href="#cutover-atomic-re-release-round-software-first">Cutover (atomic re-release round, software-first)</a></h2> <p>The recipe change moves every artifact hash, so each tool re-releases through <code>host-lifecycle release <c> --change-class <class></code> (host-lint and host-prove are fix-only patch bumps, 0.8.2 and 0.2.2; host-lifecycle adds the <code>deps-bundle</code> feature, so a minor bump to 0.20.0). Per component: edit the recipe lines in the working tree, run <code>release</code> (which stages the bundle and builds offline in the pinned image), push the producer worktree and tag, then collect the new pin and hash. The musl-and-bundle-edited <code>.host-software</code> is never committed to main with a stale hash or pin: one host commit carries the toolchain, deps-bundle, build, artifact path and hash, pins, and back-filled receipts together. The spine migration (the <code>.host</code> baseline advance) lands with it or in its own audited commit. The <code>plan/0028</code>-class recurrences carry over: place the musl binary at the new recipe path before <code>--install-hooks</code>, and reinstall the host-lifecycle gate driver from its released commit before the final green.</p> <h2 id="verification-whole-suite-green"><a class="header" href="#verification-whole-suite-green">Verification (whole-suite green)</a></h2> <ul> <li>The <code>vendor-vN</code> bundle tarball sha double-produces on two machines (a verifiable input).</li> <li>Each tool's <code>x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code> build reproduces one sha256 on a double build in the pinned image, offline against the bundle under <code>--network none</code>, build-id-free (confirmed by <code>readelf</code>).</li> <li><code>software --verify-build .</code> green for all three under the released pinned host-lifecycle: the staged bundle sha matches the recorded hash and the build runs with no network (the two-part hermeticity proof, provenance plus egress).</li> <li>The producer release asset byte-matches the recorded canonical sha for that tag (distribute equals certify), and <code>software --check</code> confirms the producer <code>deps-bundle.lock</code> equals <code>.host-software</code>.</li> <li><code>software --install-hooks .</code> installs the canonical musl host-lint, which runs on the dev host.</li> <li><code>host-lifecycle upgrade .</code> shows the new baseline (the property MUST applied); <code>software --check</code>, <code>book --check</code>, the cold-clone CI job, and the commit-hook tell test stay green; whole-suite green across every repo.</li> </ul> <h2 id="risks--honesty-4"><a class="header" href="#risks--honesty-4">Risks / honesty</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>This is a large milestone</strong>: a <code>host-lifecycle</code> feature (the <code>deps-bundle</code> field, the staging, the config merge, the network-mode threading, the sha-and-drift gate), a producer bundle workflow on host-lint, a spine MUST with its migration, the producer-CI convergence, the host-prove release pipeline, the static musl conversion, and a software-first re-release round. The cost is deliberate, the price of a verifiable hermetic distribute-equals-certify build.</li> <li><strong>The hermeticity proof is two-part, not one.</strong> <code>--network none</code> proves no egress; the staged-bundle sha match proves provenance. Both are gate assertions, since <code>--network none</code> alone could pass while consuming an image-baked cache.</li> <li><strong><code>host-lifecycle</code> consumes host-lint's bundle</strong>, a controlled pinned download that precedes the network-free build; the producer <code>cargo vendor</code> and the gate-driver seed are network-allowed by design and are not gated.</li> <li><strong>The bundle is versioned and maintained</strong>: a dependency change requires a new <code>vendor-vN</code>, a re-pin in <code>.host-software</code> and the producer <code>deps-bundle.lock</code>, and a re-release. The <code>software --check</code> drift assertion keeps the two pins in step.</li> <li><strong><code>attest-host</code> is OS-granular, not arch-granular</strong>; only the linux-x86_64-musl asset is reproducibility-certified, the others ship with a checksums manifest and are stated uncertified. An arch-level attest is a host-lifecycle feature request, out of scope and <strong>not pursued</strong>: the checksums manifest is the stated boundary.</li> </ul> <h2 id="records-1"><a class="header" href="#records-1">Records</a></h2> <p>The <code>host-template</code> build guidance, its <code>UPGRADING.md</code> ledger entry, and the re-recorded <code>.host</code> baseline (the methodology MUST, authored in the template first); <code>call/0021</code> recording only the software <code>deps-bundle</code> production-anchor change (instance-scoped); this plan, <code>PLAN.md</code>, <code>MEMORY.md</code>; re-pins, receipts, the host-lint bundle release, and the producer-CI convergence as the round lands, each pushed in its own audited commit.</p> <h2 id="landed-2026-06-22"><a class="header" href="#landed-2026-06-22">Landed (2026-06-22)</a></h2> <p>The software-first re-release round and the atomic agentic-host change shipped:</p> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>component</th><th>tag</th><th>pin</th><th>static-musl artifact sha256</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td>host-lint</td><td><code>v0.8.2</code></td><td><code>ba479258</code></td><td><code>a099c27d8ce3912bec11f4f7e2140ef37b5c8d03f320aad2a882244e98f8bac8</code></td></tr> <tr><td>host-prove</td><td><code>v0.2.2</code></td><td><code>3ca95fc0</code></td><td><code>520cdd109d96c8996402792065cd9c0198f13f97ebefd62ff15edb8c0030f366</code></td></tr> <tr><td>host-lifecycle</td><td><code>v0.20.0</code></td><td><code>a38b0c07</code></td><td><code>7d0903340be787183661c4cb933af82886f22af436a179e8a856117386d950d0</code></td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p>Each artifact reproduced offline in <code>clux/muslrust:1.95.0-stable@sha256:15a72a4a…</code> against the pinned <code>vendor-v1</code> bundle (sha <code>f1141763…</code>), host-prove against an empty source set. One atomic commit (<code>86e19db</code>) re-pinned <code>.host-software</code>, back-filled the three release receipts, recorded <code>ecce498</code> applied, and bumped the <code>host-template</code> pointer to <code>455fba8</code>. The released <code>0.20.0</code> binary gated green: <code>software --check</code>, <code>software --verify-build</code> (reproduces all three offline under <code>--network none</code>), <code>book --check</code>, and the commit-hook tell test; <code>--install-hooks</code> installed the canonical musl host-lint, retiring the <code>plan/0028</code> local-build workaround. agentic-host CI re-pinned to the certified <code>0.20.0</code> (<code>eff82b8</code>).</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0033-the-generated-book-is-correct-and-obeys-the-prose-hygiene-rule"><a class="header" href="#plan0033-the-generated-book-is-correct-and-obeys-the-prose-hygiene-rule">plan/0033: the generated book is correct and obeys the prose-hygiene rule</a></h1> <blockquote> <p><strong>Goal:</strong> close <code>connollydavid/host#15</code>. The methodology's own published book has two defects. First, <code>host-lifecycle book</code> emits a generated home-page link to a room landing whose source is <code>README.md</code>, and mdBook serves that page at <code>index.html</code> while rewriting the in-content link to <code>README.html</code>, so the link is a 404. Second, the rendered book carries em-dashes that the prose-hygiene rule (<code>plan/0030</code>) forbids in authored docs: the five generated room separators and roughly 113 em-dashes in the spine (<code>host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> and <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>) that every adopter renders in the Reference section. Operator ruling: clean fully, so the book obeys the rule the methodology preaches.</p> </blockquote> <h2 id="context-7"><a class="header" href="#context-7">Context</a></h2> <p><code>host#15</code> was observed building the mdBook for an adopted project. Two findings:</p> <ol> <li> <p><strong>Dead room-landing link.</strong> <code>host-lifecycle book</code> generates the home page (<code>docs/index.md</code>) with a bullet per room linking the room's first page. For a room whose landing file is <code>README.md</code> (the Cast room is the example), the emitted link is <code>cast/README.md</code>. mdBook applies its README-to-index rule and serves that page at <code>cast/index.html</code>, but it rewrites the in-content link to <code>cast/README.html</code>, which is never generated. The home page (and the aggregated <code>print.html</code>) therefore carry a 404. The sidebar nav link works, because mdBook maps <code>SUMMARY</code> chapter links through the README-to-index rule; only the generated in-content link breaks.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>The methodology breaks its own prose-hygiene rule.</strong> <code>plan/0030</code> shipped a rule: authored docs carry zero prose tropes, and a decoration em-dash is a flagged trope. The rendered book shows em-dashes anyway, from two tool-controlled sources: the generated nav separators that <code>host-lifecycle book</code> writes for the five room part-titles, and the spine prose (<code>host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> and <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>) that renders verbatim in every adopter's Reference section. <code>host-lint --prose</code> flags roughly 96 em-dashes in the spine <code>CLAUDE.md</code> and 17 in <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>, plus a few other tropes (negative parallelism, a false range, a decoration arrow, an anaphora run).</p> </li> </ol> <h2 id="decision-operator-ruling-2026-06-22"><a class="header" href="#decision-operator-ruling-2026-06-22">Decision (operator ruling, 2026-06-22)</a></h2> <p><strong>Clean fully.</strong> Both tool-generated sources move off the em-dash, and the spine prose is reworded to <code>host-lint --prose</code> clean with meaning preserved. The alternative (an explicit exemption for the spine and the generated nav) was weighed and rejected: the methodology should obey the rule it asks every adopter to obey, and the issue itself notes that cleaning is the fix that makes the methodology consistent.</p> <h2 id="part-a-the-host-lifecycle-book-generator"><a class="header" href="#part-a-the-host-lifecycle-book-generator">Part A: the <code>host-lifecycle book</code> generator</a></h2> <p>Two changes in the <code>book</code> generator, with unit tests:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Home-page room links resolve.</strong> When the generated home overview links a room landing whose destination basename is <code>README.md</code>, link the page mdBook actually serves (<code><dir>/index.md</code>, which mdBook rewrites to <code><dir>/index.html</code>) rather than <code><dir>/README.md</code>. The <code>SUMMARY</code> nav links already resolve through mdBook's README-to-index rule and stay as they are.</li> <li><strong>Nav separators carry no em-dash.</strong> The five room part-titles (Cast, Plan, Software, Call, Reference) use a colon separator ("Cast: who") in place of the em-dash, so the generated sidebar and home overview obey the prose-hygiene rule on every page.</li> </ul> <p>The change alters the generated book output, so it ships as a <code>host-lifecycle</code> release (a fix to the generator). The recorded musl artifact moves, so <code>.host-software</code> re-pins and the release receipt is back-filled.</p> <h2 id="part-b-the-spine-prose"><a class="header" href="#part-b-the-spine-prose">Part B: the spine prose</a></h2> <p><code>host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> and <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> are reworded so <code>host-lint --prose</code> reports zero on both. Em-dashes become commas, colons, parentheses, or sentence breaks; the few other flagged tropes are reworded in place. Meaning is preserved exactly: only flagged punctuation and phrasing change, and every identifier, version string, citation, and rule stays byte-for-byte. The spine commit is pushed in <code>host-template</code>, then the agentic-host submodule pointer is bumped to it so the rendered Reference section is clean. This is a prose-only spine revision with no new adopter action, so it carries no <code>UPGRADING.md</code> ledger entry.</p> <h2 id="lifecycle-run-software-first"><a class="header" href="#lifecycle-run-software-first">Lifecycle run (software-first)</a></h2> <ol> <li>Implement Part A in the <code>host-lifecycle</code> worktree, with unit tests; <code>cargo test</code> and clippy clean.</li> <li>Implement Part B in the <code>host-template</code> worktree; verify <code>host-lint --prose</code> is zero on both files; commit and push <code>host-template</code>.</li> <li><code>host-lifecycle release host-lifecycle --change-class neither</code> (a generator fix), run its printed commit, push, and tag steps, and collect the new pin and musl artifact hash.</li> <li>One atomic agentic-host commit: re-pin <code>.host-software</code> <code>host-lifecycle</code>, back-fill the release receipt, and bump the <code>host-template</code> submodule pointer.</li> <li>Reinstall the gate driver from the released commit.</li> <li>Gate with the released binary: <code>software --check</code>, <code>software --verify-build</code> (the artifact reproduces), <code>book</code> then <code>book --check</code>, and a direct check that the generated <code>docs/index.md</code> no longer links a <code>README.md</code>, that the generated nav carries no em-dash, and that the rendered spine pages are prose-clean.</li> <li>CI rewire: move the agentic-host workflow <code>host-lifecycle</code> install pins to the released commit.</li> <li>Records: this plan, the <code>PLAN.md</code> row, <code>MEMORY.md</code>, and a close comment on <code>host#15</code>.</li> </ol> <h2 id="verification-whole-suite-green-1"><a class="header" href="#verification-whole-suite-green-1">Verification (whole-suite green)</a></h2> <ul> <li>A <code>cargo test</code> case proves the home overview links the served page for a <code>README.md</code> landing, and a case proves the generated nav part-titles carry no em-dash.</li> <li><code>host-lint --prose</code> is zero on <code>host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> and <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>.</li> <li>The regenerated <code>docs/index.md</code> contains no <code>](</code>-link ending in <code>README.md</code>, and the generated <code>docs/SUMMARY.md</code> part-titles carry no em-dash.</li> <li><code>software --check</code>, <code>software --verify-build</code>, and <code>book --check</code> are green; the producer and agentic-host CI runs are green across every repo.</li> </ul> <h2 id="records-2"><a class="header" href="#records-2">Records</a></h2> <p>This plan, the <code>PLAN.md</code> row, <code>MEMORY.md</code>, and the <code>host#15</code> close comment. No <code>call/</code> decision is needed: Part A is a tool bugfix and Part B is a prose-clean of the spine, neither a methodology decision.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0034-the-hygiene-grammar-grows-by-reflective-practice-fixes-host16"><a class="header" href="#plan0034-the-hygiene-grammar-grows-by-reflective-practice-fixes-host16">plan/0034: the hygiene grammar grows by reflective practice (fixes host#16)</a></h1> <p>Resolves connollydavid/host#16 (positional checklist-item references evade the ordinal lane).</p> <h2 id="context-8"><a class="header" href="#context-8">Context</a></h2> <p>host#16 reports that host-lint does not catch positional references to milestone checklist items. The shapes, quoted as the lane would receive them:</p> <pre><code class="language-host-lint:ignore">box 7 boxes 4-8 steps 3-5 step 3-5 </code></pre> <p>The meeting that produced this milestone established, against the adopter where the shape emerged (reviewed in a scratch checkout only, never referenced in tracked files):</p> <ul> <li><strong>The guard is blind to the shape.</strong> None of the positional references in that adopter's history or its two milestone READMEs were caught on the noun token; the only non-clean verdicts fired on an em-dash. Plural and range forms also evade, because <code>check_line</code> splits on whitespace and tests one token against a singular noun set.</li> <li><strong>Not a migration artifact.</strong> The shape first appears in milestones authored fresh, whose items already carried content titles. It is an emergent authoring habit, and it is not in our spine (host-template never defined a checklist convention).</li> <li><strong>The available content name was bypassed.</strong> A reference cited a sibling item by position even where a content name was at hand, so adding content anchors at the source would not have prevented it. The fix is detection plus doctrine, not a convention.</li> <li><strong>The deeper gap is methodological.</strong> The spine already forbade positional naming; an agent ignored a rule it had, and the lane stayed silent. We had detection as a refusal with no sanctioned positive path, and no stated account of how the shared grammar grows when a tell emerges in practice. That account is the real subject here.</li> </ul> <h2 id="where-the-rule-lives"><a class="header" href="#where-the-rule-lives">Where the rule lives</a></h2> <p>The naming noun set (<code>FLAG_TERMS</code>) and <code>VOCABULARY.md</code> are host-lint's own. host-lint consumes host-grammar only for the numeral helper and the prose-trope engine. A positional reference is a <strong>naming</strong> tell, so it graduates into host-lint, not host-grammar.</p> <h2 id="decision"><a class="header" href="#decision">Decision</a></h2> <p>Two coupled moves: fix the bug in host-lint, and author the growth doctrine in the spine. The operator practice the doctrine describes is runnable today, without new tooling.</p> <h3 id="the-doctrine-authored-in-host-template"><a class="header" href="#the-doctrine-authored-in-host-template">The doctrine (authored in host-template)</a></h3> <p>The hygiene grammar is a living, shared source that grows by reflective practice:</p> <ol> <li><strong>Two authorities, named distinctly.</strong> A project's local operator validates its <code>LEXICON</code> and decides what is worth proposing. The shared-grammar maintainer validates universality and releases the change. An adopter proposes a candidate upstream rather than editing software it consumes.</li> <li><strong>Discovery is mechanical-first, operator-judged, agent-assisted.</strong> The reliable path is a sweep of history and recent work for recurring shapes the lane misses, triaged by the operator. An agent rarely perceives its own register as a tell, so its reflection is the assist, prompted at the verify gate and at adoption, never the mechanism.</li> <li><strong>The asymmetry that routes a shape.</strong> Legitimacy is local, so it is declared in the per-project <code>LEXICON</code>. A tell recurs across projects and models, so a confirmed tell graduates into the shared grammar the lane enforces: a naming tell into host-lint, a prose trope into host-grammar.</li> <li><strong>The universality test.</strong> The operator asks whether the shape is a property of how models segment work, or of this domain's vocabulary. A candidate that fails the test is reworded, not graduated.</li> <li><strong>Disposition order is never inverted.</strong> Reword into content by default, because a name is almost always available. Box or declare an irreducible citation. Declare the legitimate contextual phrase when a shape is a genuine quantity rather than a position. Reserve a graduation for the residue that recurs once rewording is genuinely impossible.</li> <li><strong>Reflection has a concrete trigger.</strong> It is prompted at the verify gate before a milestone closes, and at adoption.</li> <li><strong>Growth looks forward and has a correction path.</strong> Harvesting proposes what to catch from now on, and does not retro-flag the immutable past, which migration disposes of by renaming live files, boxing frozen records, and path-excluding append-only logs. A grammar bump that newly flags existing live docs follows the disposition order: reword the live ones, box the frozen ones. A graduation that proves to over-flag is narrowed by a later grammar release.</li> </ol> <p>A software <code>call/</code> for harvest tooling is deferred until that tooling is built (see Follow-ups).</p> <h3 id="detection-host-lint"><a class="header" href="#detection-host-lint">Detection (host-lint)</a></h3> <p>A1. Add <code>box</code>, <code>boxes</code>, and <code>steps</code> to <code>FLAG_TERMS</code> and accept a numeric range (<code>N-M</code>) after the noun, in <code>src/lib.rs</code>. Severity stays a flag, like the existing ordinal nouns. The glued hyphen-digit form stays out of scope: a legitimate glued term has no numeral-free <code>LEXICON</code> prefix, so it could not be escaped, and it is the same class as a noun-glued numeral; a hyphenated reference is the minority shape, a documented residual. Verify by: synthetic fixtures for the box/boxes/steps and range shapes all flag. A2. Preserve the boundaries. The literal checklist mark stays clean (it carries no noun-plus-numeral), a content-named reference stays clean, and the <code>box</code> disposition verb stays clean (no trailing numeral). Verify by: the mark, a content reference, and the verb all clean; <code>phase</code>/<code>stage</code>/<code>step</code> behaviour unchanged (existing tests green). A3. <code>VOCABULARY.md</code>: document the shape, its rationale, the literal-mark boundary, the noun-versus-verb note, and the LEXICON resolution for a genuine quantity. Declare the <strong>numeral-free contextual prefix</strong> (the phrase without the number), because the numbered phrase is refused by the flag-tier guard by design. Verify by: host-lint self-lint clean on <code>VOCABULARY.md</code>. A4. Update <code>host-lint.allium</code> and <code>.obligations</code> for the rule; add unit and integration fixtures (synthetic, never adopter-derived). Verify by: <code>cargo test</code>, <code>./test-integration.sh</code>, clippy clean; allium lane green. A5. Gather weak-agent data. Drive Qwen-3.5-4B through the clear cases (a positional reference flags, a literal mark is clean, a genuine quantity is declared by its prefix) and confirm it reaches the correct verdict unaided. A6. Release host-lint through the lifecycle, re-pin <code>.host-software</code> with the re-derived artifact hash, and run <code>software --verify-build</code> and <code>software --check</code>. Verify by: release gate green, tag pushed, both checks clean, CI green.</p> <h3 id="doctrine-host-template-spine"><a class="header" href="#doctrine-host-template-spine">Doctrine (host-template spine)</a></h3> <p>B1. Author the doctrine in <code>CLAUDE.md</code> as the continuation of the Hygiene lane item, prose-clean, with no promise of an unbuilt local-ban surface. Verify by: host-lint prose mode clean on the edit; the existing UPGRADING verify phrases unchanged. B2. Add the <code>UPGRADING.md</code> ledger entry, <code>requires</code> pinned to the Part A host-lint version. Verify by: the entry parses and its verify-grep passes after the edit. B3. Push inside the submodule, bump the pointer, re-record <code>.host</code>, and run the migration so this repo applies its own new doctrine. Verify by: baseline advanced, zero pending; any of this repo's live docs that now flag are reworded or boxed.</p> <h3 id="records-and-close"><a class="header" href="#records-and-close">Records and close</a></h3> <p>C1. Update the <code>PLAN.md</code> row and this doc to reflect what landed; commit and push immediately as audited docs. C2. <code>MEMORY.md</code> entry: the routing fact (a naming tell lives in host-lint), the bypassed-content-name finding, the two-authority model, the verify-gate reflection trigger. Separate commit, pushed. C3. Close host#16 with the before-and-after numbers.</p> <h2 id="scope-boundaries"><a class="header" href="#scope-boundaries">Scope boundaries</a></h2> <ul> <li>Out: a project-local ban surface. Every tell seen so far is universal; building it is speculative.</li> <li>Out: mechanizing the harvest and the verify-gate reflection prompt in the skills. The doctrine is runnable today by the operator's manual sweep and an upstream proposal. Mechanizing it is a named follow-up.</li> <li>Out: host-grammar. The shape is a naming tell, owned by host-lint.</li> <li>Out: the adopter's residue. Their cleanup; immutable commits; never referenced in tracked files here.</li> </ul> <h2 id="verification-19"><a class="header" href="#verification-19">Verification</a></h2> <p>host-lint flags every synthetic positional shape and stays clean on the literal mark, content names, and the disposition verb; the existing ordinal nouns are unchanged; the weak-agent clear cases classify correctly; <code>software --check</code> and <code>--verify-build</code> are clean; the spine prose mode is clean with the existing verify phrases intact; <code>validate plan/ call/</code> passes; the book builds; a tell-commit is blocked by the hook; all repos are green; host#16 is closed.</p> <h2 id="risks-and-honesty"><a class="header" href="#risks-and-honesty">Risks and honesty</a></h2> <ul> <li>host-lint is a context-free shape matcher. It flags the positional shape; it does not, and cannot, spare an algorithmic phrase of the same shape on its own. The sanctioned escape is a <code>LEXICON</code> declaration of the numeral-free contextual prefix, human-validated, verified to mask the quantity while leaving genuine positional references flagged.</li> <li>That escape is correct but not frictionless for a weak agent, which must notice a blocking flag and declare the prefix rather than the numbered phrase. This is an honest cost of widening the noun set, and the reason the prefix pattern is documented in <code>VOCABULARY.md</code>.</li> <li>The detection move independently fixes the bug; the doctrine is the deliberate spine evolution. They are sequenced software-first, so a stall in the doctrine never blocks the shipped fix.</li> </ul> <h2 id="follow-ups"><a class="header" href="#follow-ups">Follow-ups</a></h2> <ul> <li>Mechanize the harvest and the verify-gate reflection prompt in the skills (a software milestone that would carry the deferred software <code>call/</code>). <strong>Closed by <code>plan/0035</code></strong> (host-lint <code>gather</code> + the verify/adopt reflection step shipped; the deferred software decision is <code>call/0022</code>).</li> <li>A constrained local-ban surface only if a genuinely non-universal tell is found. <strong>Dormant</strong>: none has appeared, so there is no trigger and nothing to build (reaffirmed in <code>plan/0035</code>).</li> </ul> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0035-gather-emergent-tells"><a class="header" href="#plan0035-gather-emergent-tells">plan/0035: gather emergent tells</a></h1> <p>Mechanizes the candidate-tell discovery the plan/0034 doctrine defines (the named follow-up).</p> <h2 id="context-9"><a class="header" href="#context-9">Context</a></h2> <p>plan/0034 authored the reflective grammar-growth doctrine: the shared tell corpus is incomplete and grows by reflective practice, with discovery mechanical-first (sweep the history and recent work for a recurring shape the lane does not catch), operator-validated, prompted at the verify gate and at adoption. That sweep is currently run by hand, as it was over a downstream adopter and at the gate. This milestone gives the doctrine a tool rather than only a description.</p> <h2 id="where-it-lives"><a class="header" href="#where-it-lives">Where it lives</a></h2> <p>host-lint owns the gather: it already holds the grammar (<code>FLAG_TERMS</code>) and the legitimacy allowlists (<code>PREV_SKIP</code> for version and figure contexts, <code>UNITS</code> for quantities) that separate a real candidate from noise, and it already dispatches subcommands (<code>lexicon</code>). host-lifecycle owns the <code>verify</code>, <code>adopt</code>, and <code>classify</code> skills, where the reflection prompt is wired.</p> <h2 id="the-gather-subcommand-host-lint"><a class="header" href="#the-gather-subcommand-host-lint">The gather subcommand (host-lint)</a></h2> <p><code>host-lint gather [<paths>]</code> runs <code>git log</code> over the repository and scans the tracked docs and comments itself, one command and parse-free for a weak agent. It extracts a word followed by a numeral, a numeric range, or a glued numeral; drops every word already in <code>FLAG_TERMS</code> (the lane catches it) and every legitimate context the allowlists name; and reports the recurring residue: the candidate word, its count, and example lines, ranked by count. It is advisory and exits zero, so it never gates. A candidate must recur (default at least twice) to surface, so a one-off is not treated as noise.</p> <p>Verify by: a synthetic corpus where a novel recurring word-plus-numeral shape is surfaced, a one-off is not, and a legitimate <code>figure 3</code> or <code>v2.1</code> is not; <code>cargo test</code> and the integration suite green; released through the lifecycle and re-pinned.</p> <h2 id="reflection-wiring-host-lifecycle-skills"><a class="header" href="#reflection-wiring-host-lifecycle-skills">Reflection wiring (host-lifecycle skills)</a></h2> <p>The <code>verify</code> skill gains a step: run <code>host-lint gather</code>, then the operator triages each candidate (propose it upstream, declare it in the <code>LEXICON</code>, or leave it). The <code>adopt</code> skill gains the same at the migration moment, where a project's whole prior history is in view (the richest sweep). The step is advisory; it prompts reflection and does not block the gate.</p> <p>Verify by: the <code>verify</code> and <code>adopt</code> skills name the gather step; a dogfood run of <code>host-lint gather</code> on this repository surfaces a sensible candidate set (the lane already catches our own tells, so the residue is small).</p> <h2 id="the-deferred-decision"><a class="header" href="#the-deferred-decision">The deferred decision</a></h2> <p>The software <code>call/</code> deferred in plan/0034 is written here, scoped to host-lint and host-lifecycle: the gather surfaces candidates mechanically, the operator validates, and the tool never auto-graduates a tell nor auto-bans one. It cites the spine doctrine by revision.</p> <h2 id="scope-boundaries-1"><a class="header" href="#scope-boundaries-1">Scope boundaries</a></h2> <ul> <li>No project-local ban surface (still YAGNI; every tell seen so far is universal).</li> <li>The gather is advisory and never gates; graduation stays a manual upstream proposal, per the doctrine.</li> <li>The gather surfaces; it does not decide. The operator triages.</li> </ul> <h2 id="verification-20"><a class="header" href="#verification-20">Verification</a></h2> <p>host-lint gather unit and integration fixtures pass; a dogfood run on this repository is sensible; the weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B) correctly triages a gather candidate; <code>software --check</code> and <code>--verify-build</code> clean; whole-suite CI green.</p> <h2 id="records-3"><a class="header" href="#records-3">Records</a></h2> <p>PLAN.md row, this doc, a MEMORY entry, and the software <code>call/</code>.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="reconcile-internal-contradictions"><a class="header" href="#reconcile-internal-contradictions">Reconcile internal contradictions</a></h1> <p><strong>Status: complete (landed 2026-06-24).</strong> The spine carries the widened doctrine (<code>host-template</code> <code>d5a0034</code>); host-lifecycle <code>v0.24.2</code> (<code>9a1a586</code>) ships the annotation-backed <code>reconcile</code> check, the <code>validate</code> host-template-scope check, and the <code>restates =</code> field, with reconcile wired into the verify recheck and the <code>upgrade</code>/<code>adopt</code>/<code>verify</code> skills; agentic-host adopted the doctrine and reconciled all seven symptoms below through the check; <code>validate</code>/<code>software --check</code>/<code>reconcile</code>/<code>prose</code>/ <code>book --check</code> are green and the whole-suite CI is green. See <code>PLAN.md</code> for the landed detail.</p> <h2 id="context-10"><a class="header" href="#context-10">Context</a></h2> <p>A deep audit found contradictions where the host's own docs restate methodology the spine has moved: the <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> room map, the <code>README.md</code> and <code>CLAUDE.md</code> tool list and verification model, the recorded Where layout.
