Each staled when a past spine change landed cleanly (specs-to-software in plan/0012, the nested layout in plan/0029, host-prove and the ladder in plan/0023) and nothing re-read the restatement.</p> <p>The contradictions are symptoms of one root cause: migration and upgrade propagate a spine change but never prompt a re-examination of the project's own restatements. Needing a deep manual audit to find this drift is itself the evidence that a reflective practice the methodology should carry is absent. This milestone closes the gap and reconciles the drift.</p> <p>A de-risk review (five lenses plus Qwen-3.5-4B, recorded in <code>design-review.md</code>) reshaped the doctrine: the draft's scope rule was inoperable (4B could not apply an abstract describes-versus-uses test), its blanket cadence was alarm fatigue, and its primary trigger was wrong for a development host. The settled design follows.</p> <h2 id="the-doctrine-the-root-cause-fix-in-the-spine"><a class="header" href="#the-doctrine-the-root-cause-fix-in-the-spine">The doctrine (the root-cause fix, in the spine)</a></h2> <p>Widen the existing "grows by reflective practice" doctrine to one principle: an agent perceives neither the register it emits nor the restatements its own change stales, so both are re-examined on purpose, prompted at the trust boundaries, mechanical-first, and operator-validated. Two arms under it:</p> <ul> <li><strong>gather</strong> (plan/0034, plan/0035): forward, emergent tells in the corpus, a confirmed tell graduates upstream, cadence-driven.</li> <li><strong>reconcile</strong> (new): backward, the project's own restatements of methodology, a confirmed drift fixed locally and never propagated, fired by a specific spine move.</li> </ul> <p>Settled rules:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Prefer pointing over paraphrasing.</strong> An instance doc should point at the spine, not restate it; a restatement that remains is a reconciliation liability reconcile maintains.</li> <li><strong>Scope is machine-checkable and annotation-backed, not a judgment</strong> (the <code>LEXICON</code> and obligations house style): a restatement that must stay carries a declared assertion the tool checks against a source of truth. That is what makes it operable at the weak-agent bar.</li> <li><strong>Trigger is conditional and host-aware.</strong> A new <code>restates =</code> field on the <code>UPGRADING</code> stanza marks a drift-capable entry, and reconcile fires on it, naming which restatement to re-read. For a development host that authors spine changes with no upgrade record, the <strong>verify gate</strong> is the binding trigger: it runs the check when a drift-capable change landed since the last reconcile receipt, and records <code>n-a</code> otherwise. Adoption runs the full reconcile once.</li> <li><strong>Disposition is three-way</strong> (reuse tell-disposition): reword a live restatement, box a frozen citation, forward-correct an immutable record (<code>call/</code>, a <code>Status: done</code> doc, <code>MEMORY.md</code>).</li> <li><strong>A sibling <code>validate</code> check</strong> HAZARDs an <code>accepted</code> <code>call/</code> decision whose <code>Scope:</code> names <code>host-template</code>, closing decision-status drift (the <code>call/0017</code> class) mechanically.</li> </ul> <p>Authority: this is methodology, so it lives in the spine (<code>host-template</code>), never a project <code>call/</code>. The count-versus-stanzas tool check is dropped as noise (the live family disagrees, 5 against 4 against 3); the tool check is a set-diff, and the verification-model check is a positive assertion that every rung-driver is named.</p> <h2 id="build-order"><a class="header" href="#build-order">Build order</a></h2> <ol> <li>Seed the two spine truth data the checks need: an explicit host-* tool-family list and a verification-model datum (the manifest carries neither today).</li> <li>host-lifecycle gains the annotation-backed reconcile check (a Where-root off <code>software/</code>, a spec path asserted under <code>plan/</code>, the host-* family set-diff, a verification-model omission), the <code>restates =</code> read, and the <code>validate</code> decision-scope check; released through the lifecycle and re-pinned.</li> <li>Spine: widen the doctrine and add the <code>UPGRADING</code> entry; wire the reconcile step into the <code>upgrade</code>, <code>adopt</code>, and <code>verify</code> skills (gather stays where it is).</li> <li>agentic-host adopts, then reconciles the symptom drift below as the doctrine's first dogfood run, through the new check rather than by hand.</li> </ol> <h2 id="the-symptom-drift-the-first-dogfood"><a class="header" href="#the-symptom-drift-the-first-dogfood">The symptom drift (the first dogfood)</a></h2> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>Finding</th><th>Evidence</th><th>Disposition</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td>Root <code>CLAUDE.md</code> contradicts itself and the spine on the verification model (three lanes, <code>host-prove</code> omitted, against the six-rung ladder).</td><td><code>CLAUDE.md:24</code> vs <code>:9</code>/<code>:11</code> and the spine ladder.</td><td>reword live</td></tr> <tr><td><code>STRUCTURE.md</code> is a stale room map: What at <code>plan/<NNNN>/spec/</code>, Where at <code>host-lint/</code>, only host-lint of five components named.</td><td><code>STRUCTURE.md:9</code>/<code>:11</code> vs <code>host-template/STRUCTURE.md</code> and the live <code>software/</code> tree.</td><td>reword live; also clears the orphan <code>plan/0001-foundation/spec/</code></td></tr> <tr><td>Root <code>CLAUDE.md</code> points a spine change at a redirect stub.</td><td><code>CLAUDE.md:26</code> vs <code>host-template/MIGRATION.md</code>.</td><td>reword live</td></tr> <tr><td><code>README.md</code> names only three host-* tools and omits <code>host-prove</code>.</td><td><code>README.md:4</code> vs <code>.host-software</code>.</td><td>reword live</td></tr> <tr><td><code>call/0017</code> authored a now-spine-resident rule yet stays <code>accepted</code>.</td><td><code>call/0017:5</code> (Scope names <code>host-template</code>); rule in <code>host-template/CLAUDE.md</code>, applied <code>617e420</code>.</td><td><code>Status: superseded by the spine</code>; review <code>call/0018</code>; <code>call/0021</code> clean</td></tr> <tr><td>The <code>host-prove</code> <code>.host-software</code> pin sits one CI-only commit past its <code>v0.2.2</code> tag.</td><td><code>pin = 5e01f58</code> (<code>v0.2.2-1-g5e01f58</code>); tag <code>3ca95fc</code>; the extra commit is <code>ci.yml</code> only.</td><td>re-pin to the tag, or record the rationale</td></tr> <tr><td>Two record-tidiness defects.</td><td>the empty orphan <code>plan/0001-foundation/spec/</code>; PLAN.md's Skill-Hardening box unchecked while <code>SKILL-HARDENING.md</code> is done.</td><td>remove the dir; check the box with the crates.io deferral noted</td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p>Resolved already in the campaign: the prose-hygiene gate the spine asserted but <code>lifecycle.manifest</code> did not implement (plan/0030 D4, host-lifecycle v0.22.0). The session auto-memory staled the same way and is corrected directly as memory hygiene, not tracked here.</p> <h2 id="verification-21"><a class="header" href="#verification-21">Verification</a></h2> <ul> <li>The spine carries the widened doctrine; an <code>UPGRADING</code> entry records it; the <code>upgrade</code>/<code>adopt</code>/<code>verify</code> skills prompt reconcile; agentic-host adopts it.</li> <li>The reconcile check flags a planted drift (a Where-root off <code>software/</code>, a missing rung-driver) and stays silent on a clean doc; <code>validate</code> HAZARDs a <code>host-template</code>-scoped <code>accepted</code> decision. A Fen (Qwen-3.5-4B) run reaches the reconcile action on a concrete flag unaided.</li> <li>The symptom drift above reconciles to clean: root <code>CLAUDE.md</code>, <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>, <code>README.md</code> match the spine and the live layout; <code>call/0017</code> is superseded; the <code>host-prove</code> pin is at its tag or cited; the orphan dir is gone; the PLAN.md box is checked.</li> <li><code>software --check</code>, <code>validate plan/ call/</code>, the prose gate, and the doc-site build are green; whole-suite green across the affected repos at close.</li> </ul> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="re-home-the-receipts-family-entirely-tool-driven"><a class="header" href="#re-home-the-receipts-family-entirely-tool-driven">Re-home the receipts family, entirely tool-driven</a></h1> <h2 id="context-11"><a class="header" href="#context-11">Context</a></h2> <p><code>plan/0030</code> settled the receipts ontology with the operator but deferred the move to its own milestone, because the shipped binary reads the applied-set only from <code>.host</code> (<code>applied_ids</code>) and the lifecycle receipts only from a single <code>.host-receipts</code>. Moving or splitting those files by hand would silently un-apply every recorded upgrade and HAZARD the embed and release gate, and CI cold-installs a pinned binary that knows only the old layout. So the re-homing must be <strong>100% tool-driven</strong>: a host-lifecycle subcommand performs the move and the split mechanically, the binary auto-migrates on read, and no file is hand-edited. This milestone reuses none of the prose work; it is the deferred follow-up named in <code>plan/0030</code>.</p> <h2 id="the-settled-ontology-with-the-6-of-8-split-resolved"><a class="header" href="#the-settled-ontology-with-the-6-of-8-split-resolved">The settled ontology, with the 6-of-8 split resolved</a></h2> <p>The prior review flagged that two framings (everything host-lifecycle executed, versus a concern-based split) disagree on six of the eight phases. Resolve it by one rule: a receipt lives in <code>.host-receipts</code> only if it is a <strong>methodology-version event</strong>, the act of moving the project to a template revision. <code>adopt</code> sets the baseline and <code>upgrade</code> advances it (carrying the applied-set). Every other phase host-lifecycle runs is <strong>operational</strong>, a thing done to the project or its components, and lives in <code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>.</p> <div class="table-wrapper"><table><thead><tr><th>File</th><th>Records</th><th>Written by</th></tr></thead><tbody> <tr><td><code>.host</code></td><td>the stamp: template, adopted revision, name, baseline</td><td>host-lifecycle</td></tr> <tr><td><code>.host-receipts</code></td><td><code>adopt</code> and <code>upgrade</code>, plus the applied-set (which <strong>moves here</strong> from <code>.host</code>): the methodology-version trail</td><td>host-lifecycle</td></tr> <tr><td><code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code></td><td>the operational phases: <code>classify</code>, <code>embed</code>, <code>remap</code>, <code>verify</code>, <code>publish</code>, <code>release</code></td><td>host-lifecycle</td></tr> <tr><td><code>.host-prove-receipts</code></td><td>cut until host-prove emits receipts (no file for an absent consumer)</td><td>none</td></tr> </tbody></table> </div> <p>The uniform rule stands: <code>.host-<tool>-receipts</code> holds what that tool executed, while the tool-less <code>.host-receipts</code> holds the project's methodology-version trail.</p> <h2 id="100-tool-driven-the-core-requirement"><a class="header" href="#100-tool-driven-the-core-requirement">100% tool-driven, the core requirement</a></h2> <ol> <li> <p><strong>A migration subcommand.</strong> <code>host-lifecycle migrate-receipts <dir></code> performs the whole re-homing mechanically and idempotently: it moves the applied-set out of <code>.host</code> into <code>.host-receipts</code>, splits the operational receipts out of <code>.host-receipts</code> into <code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>, and leaves the <code>adopt</code> and <code>upgrade</code> receipts in <code>.host-receipts</code>. It writes atomically, reports what moved, and a second run is a no-op (the project is already on the new layout). The agent runs one command; nothing is hand-edited. Operating the move ad hoc (editing the files) is a defect by construction, the same stance the lifecycle takes on every phase.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Auto-migrate on read (permanent back-compat).</strong> Every read path tolerates <strong>both</strong> layouts: <code>applied_ids</code> reads the legacy <code>applied =</code> lines in <code>.host</code> or the new applied-set in <code>.host-receipts</code>; the receipt gate unions <code>.host-receipts</code> and <code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>. So the gate stays green across the boundary, and an adopter that has not yet run <code>migrate-receipts</code> is still read correctly. The old-layout reader is permanent, retired only by a later decision once no supported adopter is on the old side. It mirrors the legacy single-<code>revision</code> <code>.host</code> stamp, which the binary still auto-migrates on read today.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Writes route by the ontology.</strong> After this lands, <code>upgrade --record</code> appends the applied record to <code>.host-receipts</code>, a new operational receipt is appended to <code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>, and an <code>adopt</code> or <code>upgrade</code> receipt stays in <code>.host-receipts</code>. A project still on the old layout is migrated by the next <code>migrate-receipts</code>, never by a surprise rewrite mid-command.</p> </li> </ol> <h2 id="delivery-software-first"><a class="header" href="#delivery-software-first">Delivery (software-first)</a></h2> <ol> <li><strong>host-lifecycle</strong> (a feature release): the dual-format reader, the <code>migrate-receipts</code> subcommand, and the ontology-routed writes. Tests: a round-trip (an old-layout fixture migrates to the new layout, with the gate green before and after), an idempotent re-run, and an auto-migrate-on-read of a legacy fixture with no migration run. Released through <code>host-lifecycle release</code>, re-pinned, CI install revs bumped.</li> <li><strong>Spine (host-template)</strong>: update the stamp and receipts description (the applied-set now lives in <code>.host-receipts</code>; the per-tool <code>.host-<tool>-receipts</code> rule), and add an <code>UPGRADING</code> entry whose action is to bump host-lifecycle and run <code>host-lifecycle migrate-receipts .</code>, with a machine-checkable state-check <code>verify</code>.</li> <li><strong>agentic-host</strong>: re-pin host-lifecycle, run <code>migrate-receipts</code> (the tool-driven dogfood, not a hand edit), bump the host-template pointer, record the applied entry, verify, CI green.</li> </ol> <h2 id="verification-22"><a class="header" href="#verification-22">Verification</a></h2> <ul> <li><code>migrate-receipts</code> on an old-layout fixture yields the new three-file layout; the receipt gate is green before and after; a second run is a no-op.</li> <li>A legacy-layout fixture (applied-set in <code>.host</code>, a single <code>.host-receipts</code>) is read correctly with no migration run (auto-migrate on read), gate green.</li> <li><code>software --check .</code> on agentic-host is green after the dogfood migration (the gate unions both receipt files; the applied-set is read from <code>.host-receipts</code>).</li> <li>The weak agent (Qwen-3.5-4B) reaches the single <code>migrate-receipts</code> command unaided.</li> <li>Whole-suite green (host-lifecycle CI, agentic-host Site and reproducible-build, the latter cold-installing the dual-format binary and reading the migrated layout).</li> </ul> <h2 id="risks-and-honesty-1"><a class="header" href="#risks-and-honesty-1">Risks and honesty</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>The dual-format reader ships before the move.</strong> Every read path must tolerate both layouts in the same release that adds <code>migrate-receipts</code>, or the move un-applies the upgrades and HAZARDs the gate. agentic-host migrates only after CI installs that release.</li> <li><strong>CI cold-install.</strong> The reproducible-build and Site jobs install host-lifecycle at the new pin, which carries the dual-format reader, so they read the migrated layout fine.</li> <li><strong>Ongoing, not a shim.</strong> The old-layout reader is permanent backward compatibility, not a one-time migration helper, because copy-at-version lets each adopter cross the boundary at its own pace.</li> <li><strong>Value is organizational.</strong> This is an ontology refinement, not a defect fix; the operator settled it in <code>plan/0030</code> and chose to execute it. It earns its risk by making the receipts model uniform and the applied-set live with the version trail it belongs to.</li> </ul> <h2 id="landed-2026-06-24"><a class="header" href="#landed-2026-06-24">Landed (2026-06-24)</a></h2> <p>Shipped 100% tool-driven in host-lifecycle <strong>v0.23.0</strong> (<code>6e15e01</code>, artifact <code>76be6d9e</code>): the <code>migrate-receipts</code> subcommand, the dual-format reader, and ontology-routed writes; 80 tests, clippy clean. The spine description update is <code>ac32d1c</code> and the <code>UPGRADING</code> ledger entry is <code>4d2ebe9</code>, adopted in agentic-host (<code>applied=ac32d1c</code>). agentic-host dogfooded the migration through the tool: the applied-set (8 entries) moved into <code>.host-receipts</code>, the 26 operational receipts split into <code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>, and adopt and upgrade stayed in <code>.host-receipts</code>. <code>software --check</code> reports 0 HAZARDs on the migrated layout, <code>--verify-build</code> reproduces, and the whole suite is green (host-lifecycle CI main and tag, agentic-host Site and the reproducible-build cold-clone reading the migrated layout). Fen (Qwen-3.5-4B) reached <code>host-lifecycle migrate-receipts .</code> unaided. The verify gate from <code>plan/0030</code> D4 caught an <code>ing-tail</code> trope in this very README during the release, a live demonstration that the gate enforces prose hygiene.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="prose-gate-the-meta-repos"><a class="header" href="#prose-gate-the-meta-repos">Prose-gate the meta repos</a></h1> <p><strong>Status: complete (landed 2026-06-24).</strong> host-template (<code>9268d76</code>) and host (<code>1dc9cb9</code>) are prose-clean and each carries a <code>Prose</code> CI gate pinned to host-lifecycle v0.24.2 (host's is its first CI workflow); both gates are green in CI. The agentic-host pointer and host pin moved in <code>0813795</code>; whole-suite CI is green. The one apparent blocker, the <code>harness</code> ai-diction on <code>`kani:<harness>`</code>, was a density artifact (two occurrences crossed the gate; one is clean), cleared by rewording the second occurrence, so no engine change was needed.</p> <h2 id="context-12"><a class="header" href="#context-12">Context</a></h2> <p>plan/0036 stopped the <em>instance</em> docs (this repo's <code>README</code>/<code>CLAUDE</code>/<code>STRUCTURE</code>) drifting from the spine, and the verify gate already runs <code>host-lifecycle prose .</code> over agentic-host's own tracked markdown. But the two repos that <em>author</em> the methodology prose are not gated, so their docs carry slop the spine itself forbids:</p> <ul> <li><strong>host-template</strong> (the spine): 90 prose-trope warnings. plan/0033 cleaned <code>CLAUDE.md</code> and <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>, but the rest were never swept: <code>UPGRADING.md</code> 37, <code>cast/</code> 26, <code>README.md</code> 13, the example <code>plan/</code> 7, <code>MIGRATION.md</code> 3, <code>call/0000</code> 3, <code>CLAUDE.md</code> 1.</li> <li><strong>host</strong> (the single-file front-door): one <code>README.md</code>, 29 warnings, and no CI at all.</li> </ul> <p>The spine states "authored docs carry zero prose tropes, as an ongoing rule"; this milestone makes the spine and the front-door obey the rule they publish. It is the same drift root cause as plan/0036, one level up: a rule with no gate on the repo that owns it.</p> <h2 id="approach-1"><a class="header" href="#approach-1">Approach</a></h2> <p><strong>Clean, then gate</strong> (a gate over dirty docs is red on day one). Reword by default, the methodology's standing disposition. One care-point: <code>UPGRADING.md</code>'s <code>verify =</code> and <code>requires =</code> lines are machine-checked, so they stay <strong>byte-for-byte</strong> (the plan/0033 precedent); only surrounding prose is reworded. The <code>cast/</code> personas and example <code>plan/</code>/<code>call/</code> docs are ordinary reword-in-place. This milestone edits the <strong>host</strong> repo, which agentic-host otherwise treats read-only, an accepted boundary crossing for this work.</p> <p>No <code>UPGRADING</code> ledger entry: an adopted project already runs the prose gate through the verify phase, so adopters inherit nothing new. This brings the meta repos under the existing rule via their own CI; it is not a spine rule change.</p> <h2 id="build-order-1"><a class="header" href="#build-order-1">Build order</a></h2> <ol> <li>Clean <strong>host-template</strong> to zero prose warnings (<code>UPGRADING</code> preserving the machine-checked lines, <code>cast/</code>, <code>README</code>, <code>MIGRATION</code>, <code>CLAUDE</code>, the example <code>plan/</code>/<code>call/</code>), meaning preserved. Verify with <code>host-lifecycle prose host-template</code>.</li> <li>Add a <strong>prose-check CI job</strong> to host-template (install the pinned host-lifecycle, run <code>host-lifecycle prose .</code>, fail on any trope). Commit and push host-template; bump pointer.</li> <li>Clean <strong>host</strong>'s <code>README.md</code> to zero warnings and add host's <strong>first CI workflow</strong> with the same prose gate. Commit and push host; re-pin <code>.host-software</code>.</li> <li>Verify both gates green in CI; agentic-host's own gate stays green; whole-suite green.</li> </ol> <h2 id="verification-23"><a class="header" href="#verification-23">Verification</a></h2> <ul> <li><code>host-lifecycle prose host-template</code> and <code>host-lifecycle prose software/host/main</code> both report clean (zero flag and zero warning).</li> <li>The host-template prose CI job and the new host prose CI job both pass.</li> <li>agentic-host <code>software --check</code>, <code>validate</code>, <code>reconcile</code>, <code>prose</code>, and <code>book --check</code> stay green; whole-suite CI green across the affected repos at close.</li> </ul> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0039-concepts-as-uris-reconcile-by-pointing-not-annotating"><a class="header" href="#plan0039-concepts-as-uris-reconcile-by-pointing-not-annotating">plan/0039: Concepts as URIs (reconcile by pointing, not annotating)</a></h1> <p><strong>Status: designed and validated, not yet built (2026-06-24).</strong> The design is settled; the build is the next milestone of work. The <code>family</code> to <code>components</code> rename and the removal of agentic-host's inline annotations are uncommitted in the working tree, held for the build.</p> <h2 id="context-13"><a class="header" href="#context-13">Context</a></h2> <p>The reconcile arm (plan/0036) guards a project's own restatements of methodology against drift after a spine change, using an inline annotation: a doc line carries <code><!-- host-reconcile: KIND --></code> and the tool checks that line against a single source of truth (the component set, the verifiers, the layout). The operator rejected every inline-annotation form on aesthetic grounds: a comment trailing a sentence, a comment pair wrapping a span, a markdown link carrying the directive, and an out-of-line metadata block. The objection is structural rather than cosmetic: any inline annotation is checker machinery sitting in the prose, and prose that reads clean is the whole point.</p> <p>A replacement was explored and validated. Each methodology concept becomes a URI: one canonical definition at a stable address, and every mention points to it with a plain link rather than restating it. This is the single-source-of-truth principle (DRY, Hunt and Thomas) made concrete, and the reflection-on-action lineage the doctrine already borrows (Schon); the addressing is the first Linked Data principle (Berners-Lee), names for things made dereferenceable. The decision records carry these as outward citations, kind to predecessors and peers; the project holds itself to the same rule with rigor, which is the inward face of the one principle.</p> <h2 id="the-design-refined-b"><a class="header" href="#the-design-refined-b">The design (refined-B)</a></h2> <p>Each concept (the project's <code>components</code>, the <code>verifiers</code>, the <code>software-root</code>, the <code>spec-home</code>) has one canonical definition at a stable <code>{#id}</code> anchor in an authored doc, <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>. Everywhere else points to it with a relative-path link (<code>STRUCTURE.md#components</code>), which renders and resolves under stock mdBook with no custom generator pass. There is <strong>no generated <code>concepts.md</code></strong>: with all four definitions in one doc, a page of links back to them is pure redundancy (see "Refinements this session"). Drift is caught by the coverage check, which holds each project-local home to its full <code>.host-software</code> set; the tool carries the concept vocabulary, so no file enumerates it.</p> <p>reconcile stops scanning inline annotations and runs three checks instead:</p> <ul> <li><strong>link-integrity</strong>: every concept link resolves to a declared concept anchor.</li> <li><strong>declared-anchor</strong>: a link target must be a known concept id, not any resolving fragment, so a link cannot point at the wrong concept and pass.</li> <li><strong>coverage</strong>: every component and verifier the source of truth names must be referenced somewhere, so dropping a tool fails by absence. This restores the bite the inline check had and link-integrity alone loses.</li> </ul> <p>The concept formerly named <code>family</code> is renamed <code>components</code>: the methodology's own word for the set recorded in <code>.host-software</code>. "Family" was an agentic-host overfit, since the host-* tools happen to share a prefix while an adopter's components need not be related.</p> <h2 id="scaling-by-uri-authority"><a class="header" href="#scaling-by-uri-authority">Scaling by URI authority</a></h2> <p>The split the inline mechanism muddled is resolved by who owns each definition. Spine-universal concepts (<code>software-root</code>, <code>spec-home</code>) live at URIs in the spine's namespace, one shared vocabulary every adopter links to. Project concepts (<code>components</code>, <code>verifiers</code>) live in the project's own namespace, sourced from that project's <code>.host-software</code> and wired lanes, never a spine constant. An adopter without a book or <code>.host-software</code> skips the project-scoped checks rather than dangling or silently passing.</p> <h2 id="two-cast-reviews"><a class="header" href="#two-cast-reviews">Two cast reviews</a></h2> <p>The design was reviewed twice through the cast, recorded here for the build.</p> <p>The first reviewed the markdown forms. Verdict: reject every inline form. The published prose must read clean (Mara); one link syntax for three meanings invites confident drift, and a stray bracket breaks it silently so the gate passes falsely (Wren). The pointer link survived as the one place a link reads naturally.</p> <p>The second reviewed the fork (retire inline reconcile for concept-as-URI, or keep it renamed). Verdict: unanimous hybrid. The direction is right, but link-integrity alone is looser than the inline check, because a dropped tool is never linked so nothing catches the absence; hence the coverage and declared-anchor checks are required. The cutover must fail safe: a retired checker hard-fails on a surviving annotation rather than ignoring it silently (Bly). Retiring a mechanism shipped days earlier is its own churn, so the path is deprecate-then-retire (Orin).</p> <h2 id="the-transition-deprecate-then-retire"><a class="header" href="#the-transition-deprecate-then-retire">The transition: deprecate-then-retire</a></h2> <ol> <li>Rename <code>family</code> to <code>components</code> across the binary, its tests, and the spine manifest (done in the working tree, the suite green).</li> <li>Mark inline reconcile deprecated, naming the concept-as-URI successor, and keep it checking under the new name so the bite holds during the transition.</li> <li>Add the concept homes (<code>{#id}</code> anchors) to <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>, and build the coverage, declared-anchor, and link-integrity checks.</li> <li>Convert the host's restatements to pointers.</li> <li>Retire the inline mechanism one spine revision later, via an <code>UPGRADING</code> entry that over-reports the owed migration and hard-fails any surviving annotation.</li> </ol> <p>agentic-host accepts running in the gap as the first mover: its inline annotations are already removed, so its restatements are briefly unguarded until the coverage check ships. The operator accepted this as a calculated risk; an adopter is not asked to take it, since the deprecate-then-retire path and the fail-safe cutover protect the adopter.</p> <h2 id="validation-already-in-hand"><a class="header" href="#validation-already-in-hand">Validation already in hand</a></h2> <ul> <li>The concept-as-URI scheme renders and resolves under stock mdBook v0.5.2: the <code>{#id}</code> heading anchors are honored, a relative <code>STRUCTURE.md#id</code> link rewrites to <code>STRUCTURE.html#id</code>, and every link resolves.</li> <li>The real weak agent (Qwen-3.5-4B) authored concept pointer links unaided and correct, three of three, the <code>verifiers</code> route among them, which the inline scheme's classification step had failed. The reason is structural: pointing carries no judgment of whether a span is a restatement, the step that exhausted the model before.</li> <li>The <code>family</code> to <code>components</code> rename builds clean with the whole suite green.</li> </ul> <h2 id="verification-for-the-build"><a class="header" href="#verification-for-the-build">Verification (for the build)</a></h2> <ul> <li>Each project-local concept's home in <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> names its full <code>.host-software</code> set (coverage), with no generated <code>concepts.md</code>.</li> <li>reconcile flags a dropped component (coverage), a link to an undeclared concept (declared-anchor), and a broken link (link-integrity), and stays silent on a clean set.</li> <li>A surviving inline annotation hard-fails once the mechanism is deprecated, and never silently passes.</li> <li>A weak-agent run reaches the right pointer on a concrete case unaided.</li> <li>An adopter without a book or <code>.host-software</code> skips the project-scoped checks rather than dangling.</li> <li>The host's verify gate (validate, reconcile, prose, book) is green, and the whole-suite CI is green across the affected repos.</li> </ul> <h2 id="refinements-this-session-the-design-as-it-now-stands"><a class="header" href="#refinements-this-session-the-design-as-it-now-stands">Refinements this session (the design as it now stands)</a></h2> <p>Settled in discussion, checked against the cast and the real Qwen-3.5-4B, and kept in plain words for the weak agent (<strong>spine, copy-at-version, point, front-door</strong>):</p> <ul> <li><strong>Project-local facts, enforced.</strong> <code>components</code> and <code>verifiers</code> come from the project's own <code>.host-software</code>, never the spine manifest. The manifest is hardened to <strong>phases only</strong> (<code>manifest --check</code> rejects any other stanza), so an overfit cannot creep back into the shared spine. <code>components</code> are the <code>.host-software</code> members; <code>verifiers</code> are a <code>[verification] drivers</code> stanza (absent means skipped, fail-safe). The cast and a real 4B run backed the move; the 4B's reliable failure is exact-format emission rather than the judgment, so the edit is tool-carried and the prose only points.</li> <li><strong><code>host</code> is the front door, not a component.</strong> Of the members, the four tools (host-lint, host-lifecycle, host-prove, host-grammar) are the <code>components</code>; the single-file <code>host</code> is the <strong>front door</strong>, the methodology's entry point, set apart by <code>front-door = true</code>. This corrects a stale comment that called it "repo-self": agentic-host, the dev environment, is not a member at all and stays invisible to adopters.</li> <li><strong>The principle reaches the front door.</strong> One <strong>spine</strong>; everything else is a <strong>copy-at-version</strong> of it or <strong>points</strong> at it, never a restatement. The front-door <code>host</code> and every adopter are both copies-at-version of the spine, so "an adopter becomes host" is just <em>a copy of the one source</em>. Seeding the front door from the spine, so it cannot drift, is the named next application; the doctrine states the rule here, the build follows.</li> </ul> <h2 id="records-4"><a class="header" href="#records-4">Records</a></h2> <p>This document, a MEMORY entry, and a software <code>call/</code> for the concept-as-URI decision authored at build time. The <code>host-lifecycle next</code> footgun surfaced while allocating this number is filed as connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0040-front-door-from-spine"><a class="header" href="#plan0040-front-door-from-spine">plan/0040: front-door from spine</a></h1> <p>Generate the front-door <code>host</code> repo from the spine, so its restated facts cannot drift. Named as a follow-on in plan/0039 (the front-door principle: one spine, everything else a copy-at-version of it or a pointer, never a restatement), now picked up.</p> <h2 id="problem"><a class="header" href="#problem">Problem</a></h2> <p>The front-door repo (<code>github.com/connollydavid/host</code>) is a single <code>README.md</code>, "the process": the adopt, migrate, and upgrade procedure an agent or a patient human follows to bring a repo under the methodology. About sixty percent of it is the unique procedure (the case a/b/c and mode preview/shallow/staged/deep matrices, the step-by-step, the selection rules). The rest <strong>restates the spine</strong>: the five-rooms table, the <code>.host</code> stamp format, the "Wire the tools" ladder and its pins, the lifecycle-phase list, and the lanes-are-mandatory rule.</p> <p>Those restatements are hand-authored and gated by nothing against <code>host-template</code>, so every spine move (the nested layout in plan/0029, host-prove in plan/0023, concept-as-URI in plan/0039) stales them silently. This is the self-blindness the reconcile arm closes inside a host (plan/0036, plan/0039), but the <code>host</code> repo is its own repo and sits outside any host's verify gate, so reconcile never sees it.</p> <h2 id="decision-operator-this-session"><a class="header" href="#decision-operator-this-session">Decision (operator, this session)</a></h2> <p>Realize copy-at-version by <strong>generating</strong> the front-door README from the spine: the spine-derived sections are regenerated from a pinned template revision so they cannot drift, rather than re-authored as independent prose. Two alternatives were weighed and declined:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Gate only</strong>: keep the README hand-authored and add a drift check. It catches drift but leaves the restatements in place, against the stated principle (never a restatement).</li> <li><strong>Point by link</strong>: replace each restatement with a link to the spine's canonical definition (the concept-as-URI move of plan/0039). It breaks the front door's self-containedness, the "point an agent at one file, no checkout" requirement that is the whole reason the front door is a single file.</li> </ul> <h2 id="the-design-proposed-for-adversarial-review"><a class="header" href="#the-design-proposed-for-adversarial-review">The design (proposed, for adversarial review)</a></h2> <p>The adversarial review superseded the fragment mechanism below; see <code>design-review.md</code> and the "Settled" section. This records what was proposed.</p> <p>The README is assembled from two kinds of section:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Spine-derived</strong> sections (the rooms, the stamp format, the ladder and pins, the phase list, the lanes rule), whose canonical source is the template at a pinned revision.</li> <li><strong>Procedure</strong> sections (the case and mode matrices, the step-by-step, the selection rules), the front door's own content.</li> </ul> <p>A host-lifecycle generator named <code>front-door</code> (a sibling of <code>book</code>) stitches the pinned spine-derived sections and the procedure sections into the committed <code>README.md</code>. A <code>front-door --check</code> regenerates and fails on any drift, the <code>book --check</code> and reproducible-build re-derivation pattern (regenerate from the pin, diff the committed output). The front-door repo records the seed revision in a <code>.host</code>-style stamp, so the copy-at-version anchor is explicit and a later spine move regenerates against the new revision.</p> <p>The spine-derived sections are <strong>audience-adapted</strong> for the adopter-doing-migration reader, not byte-identical to <code>CLAUDE.md</code>. So the template carries a front-door-audience canonical fragment for each shared section, which the generator reads at the pinned revision. The coverage discipline from reconcile carries over: the components and verifiers come from <code>.host-software</code>, so a dropped or added tool flows through without a hand edit.</p> <h2 id="open-decisions-settle-by-review-before-building"><a class="header" href="#open-decisions-settle-by-review-before-building">Open decisions (settle by review before building)</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Procedure home.</strong> The procedure sections live canonically in the <code>host</code> repo today, with the template pointing at them (<code>MIGRATION.md</code>). Generation can keep that split (template owns the spine-derived fragments, the host repo owns the procedure fragments, the generator stitches), or move the whole README source into the template. Recommendation: keep the procedure canonical in the host repo; the template owns only the spine-derived fragments. This preserves the <code>MIGRATION.md</code> direction and keeps the procedure with its audience.</li> <li><strong>Section granularity.</strong> Which blocks are spine-derived (regenerated) versus procedure (authored). A first cut: regenerate the rooms table, the <code>.host</code> stamp block, the Wire-the-tools ladder and pins, the lifecycle-phase list, and the lanes-mandatory paragraph; author the rest.</li> <li><strong>Check strictness.</strong> Byte-exact regeneration (the README is fully generated and the check fails on any change, like <code>book --check</code>) versus block-bounded (only the spine-derived blocks are checked for drift, so the procedure prose can be hand-edited between regenerations).</li> <li><strong>Scope.</strong> Keep this agentic-host-local maintenance of the one published <code>host</code> repo, with no adopter obligation and no <code>UPGRADING</code> ledger entry, since most adopters have no front door (the plan/0038 precedent for meta-repo work). The host-lifecycle <code>front-door</code> capability is generic tooling, exercised only by agentic-host for the published front door.</li> </ul> <h2 id="settled-design-review"><a class="header" href="#settled-design-review">Settled (design review)</a></h2> <p>The two-lens adversarial review (<code>design-review.md</code>) found the fragment mechanism flawed: a hand-authored template fragment is a second source of truth, so <code>front-door --check</code> would prove the README matches the fragment and never that the fragment matches <code>CLAUDE.md</code>. It relocates drift rather than removing it. The four decisions are settled as:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Procedure home.</strong> Keep the split; the host repo owns the procedure (the <code>MIGRATION.md</code> pointer is live and assigns the procedure there).</li> <li><strong>Section granularity.</strong> Split by structured source, not by spine-derived. Coverage-check the phases against <code>lifecycle.manifest</code> and the wired tools against the <code>.host-software</code> <code>[verification]</code> drivers plus the lifecycle engine; generate the <code>.host</code> stamp block from the tool's format. The version pins, the lanes-mandatory rule, and the tool descriptions have no structured home and stay authored. A structured pin home is a named follow-up.</li> <li><strong>Check strictness.</strong> Byte-exact on the generated stamp block, coverage on the structured fact-sets, run on every gate sweep. The procedure prose stays authored, so the front door's teaching quality is preserved.</li> <li><strong>Scope.</strong> agentic-host-local, no <code>UPGRADING</code> entry. The binding check runs as a step in agentic-host's CI (the reproducible-build job), where the spine sources are materialized; it is a separate step, not the shared spine recheck, so no adopter without a front door runs it. The front-door repo keeps its plan/0038 prose gate and cannot run the coverage check itself.</li> </ul> <h2 id="build-sequence"><a class="header" href="#build-sequence">Build sequence</a></h2> <p>The build sequence as a task graph (plan/0042). Each entry is an anchored task, the chain is linear, and each task carries a receipt in <code>.host-task-receipts</code>.</p> <h3 id="settle-open-decisions"><a class="header" href="#settle-open-decisions">Settle the open decisions by review</a></h3> <p>Settle the open decisions by adversarial review, recorded in a design-review subdoc with a proceed verdict.</p> <ul> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="add-front-door-generator"><a class="header" href="#add-front-door-generator">Add the front-door generator</a></h3> <p>Add the <code>front-door</code> and <code>front-door --check</code> subcommands to host-lifecycle: phase coverage against <code>lifecycle.manifest</code>, tool coverage against the <code>.host-software</code> <code>[verification]</code> drivers plus the lifecycle engine, and byte-exact generation of the <code>.host</code> stamp block. No template fragments (the review rejected them as a second source). The unit tests pass.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #settle-open-decisions</li> <li>verify: cd software/host-lifecycle/main && cargo test</li> </ul> <h3 id="section-and-regenerate"><a class="header" href="#section-and-regenerate">Bring the front door in line</a></h3> <p>Add the omitted <code>release</code> phase, confirm every wired tool is named, regenerate the <code>.host</code> stamp block, and write the seed stamp, so <code>front-door --check</code> is clean, the README reads unchanged in meaning, and <code>host-lifecycle prose</code> is clean.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #add-front-door-generator</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="wire-the-check"><a class="header" href="#wire-the-check">Wire the front-door check</a></h3> <p>Wire <code>front-door --check</code> as a step in the agentic-host CI (the reproducible-build job), where the spine sources are materialized, so a spine move that stales the front door is caught (a deliberately staled section fails the check). It is a separate step, not the shared spine recheck, so no adopter without a front door runs it. The front-door repo keeps its plan/0038 prose gate; it cannot run the coverage check itself, since it does not carry the sources. The whole-suite CI is green.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #section-and-regenerate</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="release-and-re-pin"><a class="header" href="#release-and-re-pin">Release and re-pin</a></h3> <p>Release host-lifecycle, re-pin <code>.host-software</code>, and record the receipt and a <code>call/</code> decision. The released binary gates green, and <code>software --check</code> and <code>--verify-build</code> are clean.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #wire-the-check</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h2 id="risks"><a class="header" href="#risks">Risks</a></h2> <ul> <li>The spine-derived sections are audience-adapted, so the template fragments must be written for the migration reader, not transcluded verbatim from <code>CLAUDE.md</code>. Getting the boundary wrong duplicates prose instead of removing it.</li> <li>Generation must preserve the README's teaching quality. It is a document a human or a weak agent follows literally, so a mechanically-stitched README that reads worse is a regression, not a fix.</li> <li>Bootstrap: the generator lives in host-lifecycle, which the methodology materializes, so regenerating the front door depends on a built host-lifecycle at the pinned revision.</li> </ul> <h2 id="status-1"><a class="header" href="#status-1">Status</a></h2> <p>complete, released as host-lifecycle v0.27.0 (2026-06-25; <code>302e87f8</code>, artifact <code>0f63d960</code>). The <code>front-door</code> and <code>front-door --check</code> subcommands hold the single-file front door (the <code>.host-software</code> member marked <code>front-door = true</code>) to the spine's structured facts: coverage of the lifecycle phases (the manifest) and the wired tools (the <code>.host-software</code> verifier drivers plus the lifecycle engine), and byte-exact generation of the <code>.host</code> stamp block. A two-lens adversarial review (design-review.md) rejected the original template-fragment mechanism as a second source of truth, so the design was re-scoped to generate only from structured data; the version pins, the lanes rule, and the tool prose have no structured home and stay authored, with a structured pin home as a named follow-up. The check found and the milestone fixed a live drift (the front door had dropped the <code>release</code> phase). The binding check runs as a step in agentic-host's CI (the reproducible-build job); the front-door repo keeps its plan/0038 prose gate and cannot run the coverage check itself. <code>call/0026</code> records the decision; the build sequence is dogfooded as an anchored receipted task graph (each of the five tasks carries a <code>.host-task-receipts</code> receipt). Whole-suite CI green.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0041-next-fails-closed"><a class="header" href="#plan0041-next-fails-closed">plan/0041: next fails closed</a></h1> <p>Make <code>host-lifecycle next <dir></code> fail closed when it is pointed at a directory with no numbered entries, instead of silently yielding <code>0000</code>. Closes connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1, the footgun surfaced during plan/0039.</p> <h2 id="problem-1"><a class="header" href="#problem-1">Problem</a></h2> <p><code>host-lifecycle next <dir></code> prints the next free <code>NNNN-slug</code> number as the maximum of the directory's numbered entries plus one, and falls back to <code>0000</code> when there are no numbered entries. So <code>next .</code> (the host root), a typo'd path, or any non-room path silently yields a valid-looking <code>0000</code>, and an agent can go on to create a colliding <code>0000-slug</code> in the wrong place. The repro is exact:</p> <pre><code>host-lifecycle next plan -> 0040 # correct host-lifecycle next call -> 0024 # correct host-lifecycle next . -> 0000 # the host root has no numbered entries </code></pre> <p>At the weak-agent bar this is fail-unsafe: a garbled argument returns a plausible wrong answer rather than an error.</p> <h2 id="decision-operator-this-session-1"><a class="header" href="#decision-operator-this-session-1">Decision (operator, this session)</a></h2> <p><code>next</code> <strong>fails closed</strong>. A directory with no <code>NNNN-slug</code> entries (the host root, a typo, a non-room path, or a freshly-scaffolded empty room) exits non-zero rather than printing <code>0000</code>. The fresh-empty-room case is folded into fail-closed rather than special-cased, because rooms gain a first entry quickly (the worked-example <code>call/0000</code>, the first milestone) and fail-safe beats fail-unsafe.</p> <p>The exact failure form (a usage message versus a distinct error message, the exit code, and whether a did-you-mean hint helps) is settled by a <strong>Qwen-3.5-4B ergonomics check</strong>: the form the weak agent recovers from correctly (re-points at a real room, or recognizes the first-entry case and proceeds with an explicit number) is the one shipped. If the data shows the empty-room path needs a hand, an explicit affordance is added then, not pre-emptively.</p> <h2 id="the-change-host-lifecycle"><a class="header" href="#the-change-host-lifecycle">The change (host-lifecycle)</a></h2> <ul> <li><code>next</code> distinguishes a missing or non-directory path from an existing directory with no numbered entries, and exits non-zero in both, with a message that names the likely fix (point at a room such as <code>plan/</code> or <code>call/</code>). The precise wording and exit code follow the 4B check.</li> <li>A unit test covers: a populated room returns the maximum plus one (unchanged); an empty existing directory exits non-zero; a missing path exits non-zero; the host root exits non-zero.</li> </ul> <h2 id="build-sequence-1"><a class="header" href="#build-sequence-1">Build sequence</a></h2> <p>The build sequence as a task graph (plan/0042): each entry is an anchored task. The chain is linear, and each task carries a receipt in <code>.host-task-receipts</code>.</p> <h3 id="gather-data"><a class="header" href="#gather-data">Gather the failure-form data</a></h3> <p>Show the 4B the footgun and the candidate failure forms (usage to stderr with a non-zero exit, a distinct error message, with and without the did-you-mean hint), and record which form it recovers from. The recorded data note names the chosen form.</p> <ul> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="implement-fail-closed"><a class="header" href="#implement-fail-closed">Implement the fail-closed next</a></h3> <p>Implement the fail-closed <code>next</code> with the chosen form and the unit test, so <code>next .</code> exits non-zero while <code>next plan</code> still prints the correct next number.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #gather-data</li> <li>verify: cd software/host-lifecycle/main && cargo test</li> </ul> <h3 id="record-the-call-decision"><a class="header" href="#record-the-call-decision">Record the call decision</a></h3> <p>Record a small <code>call/</code> decision (fail-closed <code>next</code>, the 4B-validated form).</p> <ul> <li>depends: #implement-fail-closed</li> <li>verify: host-lifecycle validate call/</li> </ul> <h3 id="release-and-re-pin"><a class="header" href="#release-and-re-pin">Release and re-pin</a></h3> <p>Release host-lifecycle (<code>--change-class neither</code>, a patch bump), re-pin <code>.host-software</code>, record the receipt, and bump the CI install pins. The released binary gates green, <code>software --check</code> and <code>--verify-build</code> are clean, and the whole-suite CI is green.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #record-the-call-decision</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="close-the-issue"><a class="header" href="#close-the-issue">Close the issue</a></h3> <p>Close connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1 with the release reference.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #release-and-re-pin</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h2 id="risks-1"><a class="header" href="#risks-1">Risks</a></h2> <ul> <li>A freshly-scaffolded empty room now errors. If that path is more common than assumed, the 4B data surfaces it and an explicit affordance (a clear create-the-first-entry message, or a <code>--first</code> flag) is added. The blast radius is low: the change is localized to the <code>next</code> function.</li> </ul> <h2 id="status-2"><a class="header" href="#status-2">Status</a></h2> <p>complete, released as host-lifecycle v0.26.1 (2026-06-25; <code>2f606e29</code>, artifact <code>adf5c10a</code>). <code>next</code> fails closed: a non-directory or a directory with no <code>NNNN-slug</code> entries exits non-zero (code <code>2</code>) with a diagnostic and a did-you-mean drawn from the known rooms, instead of the silent <code>0000</code> fallback that returned a plausible wrong number for a typo'd or non-room path. The fresh-empty-room case folds into fail-closed with no special flag. The form was settled by a Qwen-3.5-4B (Fen) ergonomics check (gather-data.md): the weak agent recovered the intended room from the diagnostic and recognized the empty room as a first-entry case. <code>call/0025</code> records the decision; a unit test covers a populated room (unchanged), an empty directory, a missing path, and the host root. Re-pinned, release receipt recorded, CI install pins bumped; <code>software --check</code> and <code>--verify-build</code> green; connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1 closed. The build sequence is dogfooded as an anchored receipted task graph (each of the five tasks carries a <code>.host-task-receipts</code> receipt).</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0042-receipted-task-graph"><a class="header" href="#plan0042-receipted-task-graph">plan/0042: receipted task graph</a></h1> <p>The numbered tasks inside a milestone become first-class: each is an anchored node with a stable identity, each emits a receipt when discharged, and a prerequisite edge (within a milestone or across milestones) turns the project's build sequences into one schedulable graph a coordinator can fan out to parallel sub-agents. Adopter-facing and mandatory. The design was settled by operator ruling after a five-lens adversarial review (see design-review.md).</p> <h2 id="problem-2"><a class="header" href="#problem-2">Problem</a></h2> <p>A milestone's <code>Build sequence</code> is a numbered prose list, where each step pairs an action with a verification check. Three gaps:</p> <ul> <li><strong>No receipt.</strong> Each lifecycle phase emits a receipt (done with re-derivable evidence, skip with a cited reason, HAZARD if missing), so a phase claim is audited. The in-plan tasks have no such discipline: a step marked done in prose is an unverifiable claim, the same defect the phase receipts close, one level down.</li> <li><strong>A task has no stable identity.</strong> A position renumbers when a plan is re-cut, so neither a receipt nor a dependency edge can key on it. A receipt that points at "step three" breaks the moment a step is inserted above it. The fix is a content-named anchor, the stable URI a receipt and an edge hang on.</li> <li><strong>The sequence is implicitly serial.</strong> A prose list cannot say which steps are independent or which depend on a task in another milestone. An arbitrary-complexity project (the methodology's real audience, not this repo's own linear history) has genuinely independent and cross-milestone tasks, and the list cannot express the graph a coordinator would schedule.</li> </ul> <p>A separate matter is the bare <code>#N</code> GitHub issue reference, which is a fragment rather than a full URL. It is real and out of scope here: a future plan will enforce full URLs for <code>#N</code> references. plan/0042's task references already use a full relative-path anchor (<code>plan/NNNN/README.md#anchor</code>), so this milestone does not depend on that future one.</p> <h2 id="decision-operator-this-session-after-the-adversarial-review"><a class="header" href="#decision-operator-this-session-after-the-adversarial-review">Decision (operator, this session, after the adversarial review)</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Anchored task nodes, for stable identity.</strong> A task is a <code>### </code> step-heading under the <code>## Build sequence</code> section whose anchor sits at the heading end (<code>### Gather data {#gather-data}</code>, the placement stock mdBook honors). A <code>### </code> heading elsewhere is ordinary prose; the parser rejects an anchored task outside the build-sequence section and a build-sequence task heading without an anchor. References use the plan/0039 anchored form (<code>[text](plan/NNNN/README.md#anchor)</code>).</li> <li><strong>A receipt per task, in a new <code>.host-task-receipts</code> ledger.</strong> Tool-written, keyed by <code>plan/NNNN#anchor</code>, never a hand-edited inline status. This is a deliberate third receipt kind beside the plan/0037 two-file ontology, recorded in a <code>call/</code> decision and a short ontology note; the review flagged the ontology cost and the operator accepted it for the physical separation.</li> <li><strong>A project-wide dependency graph, built now.</strong> A task may declare <code>depends</code>, a local <code>#anchor</code> or a cross-milestone <code>plan/NNNN#anchor</code>. A task with no <code>depends</code> depends on the previous task, so the default is linear. The tool resolves the edges into one project-wide graph and derives the ready frontier (the tasks whose <code>depends</code> all carry a done receipt); a coordinator may dispatch the frontier to parallel sub-agents. Built as forward infrastructure for arbitrary-complexity adopters, not gated on this repo's own linear milestones.</li> <li><strong>Adopter-facing and mandatory.</strong> The spine carries the task model and an <code>UPGRADING</code> entry; the receipt-per-task gate is a hard rule, the strongest discipline. agentic-host implements the capability and migrates its own plans (plan/0040 and plan/0041) as the dogfood.</li> </ul> <h3 id="the-authoring-rule-and-why-it-holds-at-the-weak-agent-bar"><a class="header" href="#the-authoring-rule-and-why-it-holds-at-the-weak-agent-bar">The authoring rule, and why it holds at the weak-agent bar</a></h3> <p>The dependency graph was gated on a Qwen-3.5-4B investigation and passed: six of six on within-milestone authoring and reading (including the serialize-when-unsure instinct), and four of four on cross-milestone references (read a <code>plan/NNNN#anchor</code> dependency, and author a mixed local-plus-cross-plan <code>depends</code> from a menu). A free-form probe confirmed the danger boundary: asked to find what can run in parallel, the 4B invents false independence to increase parallelism, which races two sub-agents, while a missing edge only over-serializes. So the rule is: <strong>an author declares each task's prerequisites (what must finish before it), and the tool derives parallelism.</strong> The prerequisite question is local and conservative; the parallelism is the tool's to compute.</p> <h2 id="the-design-settled-the-reviews-fixes-folded-in"><a class="header" href="#the-design-settled-the-reviews-fixes-folded-in">The design (settled, the review's fixes folded in)</a></h2> <ul> <li><strong>One global graph.</strong> Because an anchor is a project-wide URI and the ledger is keyed globally, the graph spans milestones. Cycle detection and the dangling-dependency check run over the whole resolved edge set, not one milestone, so a cross-plan cycle or a <code>depends</code> on a removed cross-plan anchor is a HAZARD.</li> <li><strong>Per-task gating, not per-milestone-complete.</strong> The review found that a milestone's completeness is prose with no structured referent. So the gate fires per task: a declared anchored task with no receipt is a HAZARD, exactly as the receipt gate already HAZARDs a manifest phase with no receipt. There is no new milestone-status surface.</li> <li><strong>The verify field, mechanical or attested.</strong> A task's check is a first-class field, either mechanical (a command the gate re-runs) or attested (an operator confirmation or a <code>call/NNNN</code> citation the gate resolves). A mechanical <code>done</code> with no command is a HAZARD; an attested <code>done</code> is discharged by the citation resolving, which is weaker than re-derivation and labeled as such. The command runs through a guarded runner (no recursion into the gate, a decided cross-platform path), which reuses the existing recheck mechanism rather than a new grammar.</li> <li><strong>Staleness, both halves of call/0018.</strong> A task records the input set its verify covers; <code>--record-digests</code> fingerprints them into the ledger, and a later offline run raises a STALE HAZARD if those inputs drift without a fresh re-derivation, with full re-derivation in the verify lane. This is the obligations digest mechanism applied to task receipts, the half the first draft dropped.</li> <li><strong>A separate task-anchor link checker.</strong> Resolving a <code>[text](plan/NNNN/README.md#anchor)</code> reference is a new checker, path-shape gated, in a namespace disjoint from the reconcile concept ids (a task heading must never shadow a concept home). Only link-integrity transfers; the declared-anchor and coverage bites do not apply to an open per-milestone anchor set. The parser also enforces the heading-end placement directly, so a misplaced anchor is caught before it can 404.</li> <li><strong>Skip is cited, drift is caught.</strong> A substantive <code>skip</code> carries a resolvable <code>call/NNNN</code> citation (parity with call/0017); the gate is non-omission assurance for declared work, not a completeness proof, and the doctrine says so. A reverse check holds every ledger key to a live anchor, so a renamed or removed task cannot leave a stale done behind.</li> <li><strong>Two task systems, one of record.</strong> The durable ledger is the audit record the tool writes after re-derivation; the agent's own ephemeral session task list is scratch scheduling and is never the source of a <code>done</code>.</li> <li><strong>Resource isolation is the parallel-dispatch precondition.</strong> A <code>depends</code> is an ordering constraint, not a mutual-exclusion lock, so a coordinator fans a frontier out to parallel sub-agents only when they are resource-isolated (separate worktrees); two tasks with disjoint prerequisites that write the same file are not safe to parallelize, and the doctrine states the assumption.</li> </ul> <h2 id="build-sequence-2"><a class="header" href="#build-sequence-2">Build sequence</a></h2> <ol> <li>Record the <code>call/</code> decision for the third receipt ledger and the ontology note, and settle the <code>depends</code> syntax (a host-lint-clean grammar, the first-task base case, the insertion semantics of the linear default). Verify: <code>validate call/</code> ok; the syntax is lint-clean by <code>host-lint --all</code>.</li> <li>Implement in host-lifecycle: parse the anchored tasks and <code>depends</code> (with the heading disambiguation and the end-placement check), build and validate the global graph (cross-plan resolution, cycle detection, dangling-dependency HAZARD), the <code>.host-task-receipts</code> ledger with input-digest staleness, the per-task gate, the verify-field runner, the status read, and the task-anchor link checker. Verify: unit tests, and a synthetic fixture that exercises a cross-milestone diamond and a broken reference.</li> <li>Add the spine doctrine (the anchored receipted task model, the prerequisite-not-parallelism rule, the resource-isolation precondition, the mandatory gate) and an <code>UPGRADING</code> entry. Verify: host-template prose clean; the entry's verify post-condition holds.</li> <li>Validate the full task form at the weak-agent bar (author a task with an anchor, a verify field, a local and a cross-plan <code>depends</code>, and its receipt). Verify: a recorded Qwen-3.5-4B run authors them correctly.</li> <li>Migrate agentic-host: re-express plan/0040 and plan/0041 as anchored receipted tasks and back-fill their receipts through the tool. Verify: <code>validate plan/</code>, the per-task gate, and the link checker clean; the two milestones read unchanged in meaning.</li> <li>Release host-lifecycle, re-pin <code>.host-software</code>, record the receipt and the <code>call/</code> decision, and bump the CI install pins. Verify: the released binary gates green; <code>software --check</code>, <code>--verify-build</code>, and the whole-suite CI green.</li> </ol> <h2 id="risks-2"><a class="header" href="#risks-2">Risks</a></h2> <ul> <li>The mandatory per-task gate raises the bar for every adopter, so the line between a task worth a receipt and a trivial step must stay crisp (an anchored <code>### </code> under <code>## Build sequence</code> is a task, a trivial step's receipt is a cheap attested or skip entry) or the gate becomes ritual.</li> <li>The global graph spans milestones, so a cross-plan edge couples two milestones' lifecycles; the dangling-dependency check must run on every gate so a re-cut in one milestone cannot silently wedge another.</li> <li>Author-written verify commands run during the gate, so the guarded runner and the no-recursion rule are load-bearing for safety.</li> </ul> <h2 id="status-3"><a class="header" href="#status-3">Status</a></h2> <p>complete, released as host-lifecycle v0.26.0 (2026-06-25). The <code>tasks</code> subcommand, the <code>.host-task-receipts</code> ledger (call/0024), the completion-aware per-task gate in <code>software --check</code>, the task-anchor link checker, and the <code>tasks --new</code> scaffolding shipped; the spine doctrine is in host-template (<code>2229dbb</code>) and adopted. plan/0040 and plan/0041 are dogfooded (their build sequences re-expressed as anchored task graphs). Whole-suite CI green across all three repos.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="plan0043-the-entrance-check-a-reusable-standalone-document-check"><a class="header" href="#plan0043-the-entrance-check-a-reusable-standalone-document-check">plan/0043: the entrance check (a reusable standalone-document check)</a></h1> <p>The front-door check (plan/0040, host-lifecycle v0.27.0) holds the one published entrance of agentic-host to the spine. The same problem recurs wherever a project keeps a self-contained document that restates the methodology and cannot link to it: an adopter-authored standalone <code>SKILL.md</code>, or any project's operator-and-agent landing page. This milestone is the whole effort: it generalizes the check into an opt-in, reusable capability declared by a single <code>[entrance]</code> stanza, builds the document and concept machinery the generalization needs, and reconciles the spine flag the standalone rename left diverged. It supersedes the agentic-host-local scope set in plan/0040.</p> <h2 id="problem-3"><a class="header" href="#problem-3">Problem</a></h2> <p>A self-contained document is read out of context (a single-file entry point, a skill loaded on its own), so it cannot point at a canonical definition with a link the way an in-host doc does (the reconcile arm). It restates, and the restatement stales silently when the spine moves. plan/0040 solved this for one document, hardwired: the tool finds the <code>.host-software</code> member marked <code>entrance = true</code> and checks its <code>README.md</code> against the manifest phases, the <code>.host-software</code> tools, and the <code>.host</code> stamp format. Nothing lets another document, or another project, opt in, and the marker is wired to a member's <code>README.md</code> by filename, so a differently-named document cannot be pointed at.</p> <p>Two facts the pre-generalization audit and the design review surfaced shape the work:</p> <ul> <li><strong>The marker is a spine concept.</strong> host-template (<code>CLAUDE.md</code>, <code>UPGRADING.md</code>) documents the marker as the adopter-facing way reconcile sets a single-file front door apart from <code>components</code>. The standalone rename (<code>call/0027</code>) moved the tool to <code>entrance = true</code> and left the spine on <code>front-door = true</code>, a divergence the deprecation shim absorbs. The generalization owes the spine reconciliation.</li> <li><strong>The per-member marker was overloaded.</strong> When the marker was a per-member value, the same predicate set the entrance and defined <code>components</code> (members minus the entrance), so a richer value form could un-mark the front door and re-demote it into <code>components</code>. The declaration below moves the marker to its own stanza, which names the member outright, so that overload dissolves by construction.</li> </ul> <h2 id="decision-operator"><a class="header" href="#decision-operator">Decision (operator)</a></h2> <p>plan/0043 expands to encompass every aspect of the entrance work, rather than narrowing to the spine reconciliation the design review recommended. The review's blocking findings become requirements the design meets, not reasons to defer. The declaration is a single <code>[entrance]</code> stanza, a global singleton among the <code>.host-software</code> members, so one entrance reaches any document by path while the marker-break is designed out at the parse layer. The standalone sibling framing (reconcile holds linkable docs honest with pointers; entrance holds self-contained docs honest with coverage and generation, because they cannot link) stands. The name <code>entrance</code> stands, settled by the Qwen-3.5-4B run in <code>gather-data.md</code>.</p> <h2 id="the-design"><a class="header" href="#the-design">The design</a></h2> <h3 id="the-declaration-a-singleton-entrance-stanza"><a class="header" href="#the-declaration-a-singleton-entrance-stanza">The declaration: a singleton <code>[entrance]</code> stanza</a></h3> <p>A project declares its entrance in one <code>[entrance]</code> stanza in <code>.host-software</code>, a global singleton among the member stanzas (a second stanza is a loud error). It names the member the entrance belongs to, the document within that member's worktree, and the concepts the document keeps complete:</p> <pre><code>[entrance] member = host document = README.md restates = phases tools </code></pre> <p><code>document</code> defaults to <code>README.md</code> and reaches any file in the member's worktree, so a <code>SKILL.md</code> or a landing page is declared by path rather than excluded by a hardwired filename. <code>restates = true</code> keeps the document complete against every concept (the front-door case), so a full front door never types an enumeration; a concept list keeps it complete against that subset. The concept names are a closed vocabulary the tool validates at parse time, so an unknown concept is a loud error rather than a silently ignored word.</p> <p>The named <code>member</code> is set apart from <code>components</code>, the one member singled out among the rest, exactly as the front door is today, so reconcile holds the component set to the others. Because the stanza names the member outright, there is no per-member value to misparse, and no value form can demote the entrance into the component set: the marker-break the review found dissolves. The Qwen-3.5-4B runs (<code>gather-data.md</code>) leaned to a member-level value for the member-and-README case and to a self-documenting <code>[entrance "…"]</code> stanza for clarity; the full-reach requirement (a document path the member flag cannot carry) and the singleton constraint settle the form on the dedicated stanza.</p> <h3 id="the-migration"><a class="header" href="#the-migration">The migration</a></h3> <p>The legacy per-member marker, both <code>front-door = true</code> and the <code>entrance = true</code> that agentic-host adopted in <code>call/0027</code>, is accepted by the deprecation shim as the front-door entrance during the transition, mapped to the equivalent stanza. The shim warns and names the stanza form. Its retirement landed in this milestone as host-lifecycle v0.30.0 (the operator chose to retire now, rather than a release later): a surviving per-member marker is a loud problem in every consumer, never the entrance, and the spine and agentic-host carry no legacy marker.</p> <h3 id="the-concept-set-checked-against-its-homes"><a class="header" href="#the-concept-set-checked-against-its-homes">The concept set, checked against its homes</a></h3> <p>The <code>restates</code> concepts select what the check holds, each against the structured home it already reads: the lifecycle phases against the manifest, the wired tools against the <code>.host-software</code> drivers, the <code>.host</code> stamp against the tool's canonical format, and the other <code>.host-software</code> concepts reconcile already names (<code>components</code>, <code>verifiers</code>, <code>software-root</code>, <code>spec-home</code>). The check holds the document complete against the declared set only, so a front door declaring <code>true</code> is held to every concept and a partial entrance is held to its subset. The concept vocabulary is the reconcile vocabulary plus the phases and the stamp, named once, so an adopter learns one set of words.</p> <h3 id="the-honest-scope"><a class="header" href="#the-honest-scope">The honest scope</a></h3> <p>The entrance check holds any document that restates a concept with a structured home. A document that restates only home-less teaching doctrine (a disposition rule, the conditional-lane MUST) declares no checkable concept, and its <code>restates</code> set says so plainly. This is plan/0040's settled principle carried forward: a fact is drift-proof through the tool only when the tool reads its home, and the milestone says so rather than claiming a coverage it cannot deliver. The generality earns itself as real entrances that restate home-backed concepts appear; the machinery is built now so they can opt in.</p> <h3 id="the-routing-rule"><a class="header" href="#the-routing-rule">The routing rule</a></h3> <p>Generate what is naturally a format or data block (the <code>.host</code> stamp), coverage-check what is naturally prose (a phase name, a tool name), and never put a generation marker in teaching prose (plan/0039). The declaration stays out of the document, in the <code>[entrance]</code> stanza, so the document reads clean.</p> <h3 id="the-reconcile-relationship"><a class="header" href="#the-reconcile-relationship">The reconcile relationship</a></h3> <p><code>entrance</code> stays a separate subcommand, the sibling of <code>reconcile</code>, settled by plan/0040 and confirmed by the review. reconcile resolves links and checks anchors over tracked markdown; entrance reads a materialized document, covers the manifest phases, and generates the stamp byte-exact. They share only the parsed <code>[entrance]</code> stanza (the entrance and the set-apart member), kept a single tested source so a change serves both consumers at once.</p> <h3 id="the-spine-reconciliation"><a class="header" href="#the-spine-reconciliation">The spine reconciliation</a></h3> <p>host-template replaces the <code>front-door = true</code> prose with the <code>[entrance]</code> stanza in <code>CLAUDE.md</code> and <code>UPGRADING.md</code>, documents the stanza and its <code>document</code> and <code>restates</code> fields, and ships an adopter <code>UPGRADING</code> ledger entry that migrates a legacy per-member marker to the stanza, whose verify post-condition holds <code>entrance --check</code>, <code>reconcile</code>, and <code>software --check</code> green so a misapplied migration shows loudly at apply time. The exit-code convention comment (exit <code>1</code> for the issues a command finds, exit <code>2</code> when it cannot proceed on its input), refined by the code review in <code>code-review.md</code>, folds into the same release.</p> <h2 id="build-sequence-3"><a class="header" href="#build-sequence-3">Build sequence</a></h2> <p>The build sequence as a task graph (plan/0042), each entry an anchored task carrying a receipt. The tool gains the stanza first, then the spine documents it, then agentic-host moves to it, so no state is half-migrated.</p> <h3 id="settle-open-decisions"><a class="header" href="#settle-open-decisions">Settle the open decisions by review</a></h3> <p>Settle the declaration form, the concept-set model, the reconcile relationship, and the spine reconciliation by adversarial review, recorded in <code>design-review.md</code> with the operator re-cut that expands the milestone, designs out the marker-break with the <code>[entrance]</code> stanza, and reaches any document by path under a global-singleton constraint.</p> <ul> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="implement-entrance"><a class="header" href="#implement-entrance">Implement the entrance generalization</a></h3> <p>Parse the singleton <code>[entrance]</code> stanza (one per project, the member, the <code>document</code> path defaulting to <code>README.md</code>, the <code>restates</code> concept set with a <code>true</code> sentinel and a closed vocabulary), resolve the document within the member's worktree, route the declared concepts through the check against their homes, set the named member apart from <code>components</code>, accept the legacy per-member marker by the shim, and fold in the exit-code convention comment. Regression tests pin the new parse: the stanza yields the entrance member and keeps it out of <code>components</code>, the document path resolves within the worktree, and a declared subset checks only its concepts. A second <code>[entrance]</code> stanza fails loudly, and so does an unknown concept name.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #settle-open-decisions</li> <li>verify: cd software/host-lifecycle/main && cargo test</li> </ul> <h3 id="spine-doctrine"><a class="header" href="#spine-doctrine">Add the spine doctrine and the ledger entry</a></h3> <p>Replace the <code>front-door = true</code> prose in host-template (<code>CLAUDE.md</code> and <code>UPGRADING.md</code>) with the <code>[entrance]</code> stanza, document its <code>document</code> and <code>restates</code> fields, add the entrance doctrine (an entrance is a declared self-contained document held to the spine, the standalone sibling of reconcile), and add the adopter <code>UPGRADING</code> ledger entry that migrates a legacy per-member marker to the stanza, whose verify post-condition holds <code>entrance --check</code>, <code>reconcile</code>, and <code>software --check</code>. host-template prose stays clean.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #implement-entrance</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="migrate-agentic-host"><a class="header" href="#migrate-agentic-host">Move agentic-host to the stanza</a></h3> <p>Migrate agentic-host's <code>.host-software</code> from <code>entrance = true</code> to the <code>[entrance]</code> stanza, adopt the host-template revision, and confirm <code>entrance --check</code>, <code>reconcile</code>, and <code>software --check</code> stay green on the new declaration.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #spine-doctrine</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="validate-4b"><a class="header" href="#validate-4b">Validate at the weak-agent bar</a></h3> <p>Validate the stanza ergonomics and an entrance use case (a member that declares a concept subset and a non-README document) with a recorded Qwen-3.5-4B run that confirms the singleton, the <code>document</code> path, and the sentinel-and-vocabulary guards.</p> <ul> <li>depends: #migrate-agentic-host</li> <li>verify: attested operator</li> </ul> <h3 id="release-and-re-pin"><a class="header" href="#release-and-re-pin">Release, re-pin, and record</a></h3> <p>Release
