This pass touched only the template's identity.</p> <div style="break-before: page; page-break-before: always;"></div><h1 id="memorymd"><a class="header" href="#memorymd">MEMORY.md</a></h1> <h2 id="session-log"><a class="header" href="#session-log">Session Log</a></h2> <h3 id="2025-01-xx--initial-setup"><a class="header" href="#2025-01-xx--initial-setup">2025-01-XX — Initial Setup</a></h3> <ul> <li>Established agentic-host directory structure with CLAUDE.md, PLAN.md, PHASE1.md</li> <li>Added no-phase-skill as git submodule for software-under-development</li> <li>Imported SKILL_AUTHORING.md conformance standard for skill authoring</li> <li>Created karpathy-guidelines skill in skills/ directory</li> </ul> <h3 id="2025-06-07--linter-implementation"><a class="header" href="#2025-06-07--linter-implementation">2025-06-07 — Linter Implementation</a></h3> <ul> <li>Built no-phase linter in Rust: detects phase-synonym agentic tells in commits, headers, comments</li> <li>Binary is dynamically linked (no musl toolchain for static); committed to submodule</li> <li>SKILL.md created with agent skill frontmatter; pre-commit hook wrapper added</li> <li>VOCABULARY.md is source of truth for flag/allowlist/gray-zone detection rules</li> <li>CLI interface: <code>no-phase --stdin</code>, <code>no-phase [files...]</code>, <code>no-phase --all</code>, <code>--json</code> flag</li> </ul> <h3 id="2025-06-07--ci-pipeline"><a class="header" href="#2025-06-07--ci-pipeline">2025-06-07 — CI Pipeline</a></h3> <ul> <li>Added GitHub Actions workflow: three jobs (build static binary, conformance gates, integration tests)</li> <li>lint-skill.sh implements G1-G8 mechanical gates; all pass</li> <li>test-integration.sh has 26 property tests from VOCABULARY.md should/must-not-match cases; all pass</li> <li>Fixed is_numeral to reject ordinal words (e.g. "first") that contain Roman numeral chars</li> <li>Removed allowlist pre-filter that was blocking valid phase-synonym matches in conventional commit messages</li> </ul> <h3 id="2025-06-07--formal-spec--property-testing"><a class="header" href="#2025-06-07--formal-spec--property-testing">2025-06-07 — Formal Spec & Property Testing</a></h3> <ul> <li>Converted to Cargo project (src/lib.rs, src/main.rs) for proptest support</li> <li>Removed checked-in binary; added .gitignore; binary produced only by CI</li> <li>Added 10 proptest property tests covering all VOCABULARY.md detection rules; all pass</li> <li>Wrote no-phase.allium Allium specification for formal behavior definition</li> <li>Updated CI: cargo build, proptest, integration tests, GitHub release on tag (v*)</li> <li>Added README.md with usage, building, testing documentation</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-10--named-milestones-replace-ordinal-phases"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-10--named-milestones-replace-ordinal-phases">2026-06-10 — Named milestones replace ordinal phases</a></h3> <ul> <li>Renamed PHASE1/2/3.md to BOOTSTRAP.md, CI-PIPELINE.md, FORMAL-SPEC.md; PLAN.md keeps an ordinal-to-name dictionary for reading history (older commits and entries above still say "Phase N")</li> <li>Reason: ordinals name positions and positions shift when plans are re-cut; names stay attached to content. Bare numerals ("3", "5.5") are the same tell with the noun elided — not a fix</li> <li>VOCABULARY.md now leads with a constructive rewrite dictionary (internal plan code → descriptive text, never emit the code) and notes bare-numeral headers as flaggable</li> <li>GitHub pushes are blocked in this environment: keychain has no credential, no gh CLI, no SSH key. Commits queue locally; submodule must be pushed before the host pointer commit</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-10--ci-fixed-push-access-restored-karpathy-submodule"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-10--ci-fixed-push-access-restored-karpathy-submodule">2026-06-10 — CI fixed; push access restored; karpathy submodule</a></h3> <ul> <li>Pushes work now: gh CLI installed and authed (corrects the blocked-pushes entry above)</li> <li>no-phase-skill CI had never passed: <code>set -e</code> + <code>json=$(... --stdin --json)</code> aborted test-integration.sh because no-phase exits 1 by design on flagged input. Fixed with <code>|| true</code>; run 27242782567 is the first green</li> <li>Local cargo is 0.0.1-pre-nightly (2015) and cannot build the project — verify via CI, not locally</li> <li>Added andrej-karpathy-skills submodule (informal peer of no-phase-skill; upstream source of CLAUDE.md) and replaced its PHASEx.md guidance with content-named milestone docs</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-10--skill-hardening-shipped-v010-released"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-10--skill-hardening-shipped-v010-released">2026-06-10 — Skill Hardening shipped; v0.1.0 released</a></h3> <ul> <li>Hook bug: git passes no hook name in $1 (commit-msg gets the message file path), so the old <code>case "$1"</code> dispatch blocked every commit with exit 2; now dispatches on basename($0). Verified in a throwaway repo with a stub binary</li> <li>Bare-numeral header rule (## 3, ## 5.5) implemented for .md sources only; version-like headings (1.2.3) excluded; covered by proptest, Allium, integration tests</li> <li>Release job needs <code>permissions: contents: write</code> — default GITHUB_TOKEN is read-only and <code>gh release create</code> 403s without it; moving a tag is required to pick up workflow fixes (reruns use the tag's commit)</li> <li>v0.1.0 released with six prebuilt binaries (linux musl/macos/windows × amd64/arm64); arm64 linux builds natively on ubuntu-24.04-arm runners. crates.io publish deferred: it hosts source only, prebuilds live on GitHub Releases</li> <li>Local lint-skill.sh G1 fails because local python3 lacks PyYAML — environmental, CI has it</li> <li>Host mdBook site live at https://connollydavid.github.io/agentic-no-phase-skill/ (Pages enabled on gh-pages, legacy build type)</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-12--internal-code-as-name-rule-ported-into-the-engine"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-12--internal-code-as-name-rule-ported-into-the-engine">2026-06-12 — Internal code-as-name rule ported into the engine</a></h3> <ul> <li>A live slop subject in the sibling Agentic-MCP-Win32s project ("... nm regex (review B1)") exposed a no-phase gap: the engine only knew phase-synonyms, so an internal review label used as a name passed clean. Filed as no-phase-skill issue #1 (the finding's durable identity), fixed in PR #2: flag review|finding|blocker immediately followed by #N or a letter+digit code; GitHub refs (fixes #18, closes #35) and bare numerals (review 3 files) stay clean. CI green on the PR.</li> <li>The local submodule's origin/main was 9 commits stale (bare-numeral headers feature, set -e JSON-test fix, rewrite dictionary docs had all landed upstream); branching from it caused avoidable conflicts. Fetch the submodule before branching.</li> <li>Submodule pointer bump is owed only after PR #2 merges; the merge itself awaits operator authorisation.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-12--internal-code-as-name-rule-merged-pointer-bump-lesson"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-12--internal-code-as-name-rule-merged-pointer-bump-lesson">2026-06-12 — Internal code-as-name rule merged; pointer-bump lesson</a></h3> <ul> <li>no-phase-skill PR #2 squash-merged (7740d66); issue #1 auto-closed via the fixes ref; host pointer bumped. Adversarial review fixes landed first: token rule declared authoritative over the shell regex, '#'-preserving trim (parenthesised codes now flag), self-flagging lib.rs comment and commit message rewritten tell-free, known gate limits documented.</li> <li>Pointer-bump mistake (corrected in 0553ea9): a <code>cd</code> chain silently failed because the shell was already inside the submodule, so the host pointer ca86b98 recorded the deleted PR branch head instead of the merged squash commit. Before pushing a pointer bump, verify <code>git submodule status</code> shows a commit reachable from the submodule's origin/main.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-12--fake-issue-ref-blind-spot-documented-as-a-known-limitation"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-12--fake-issue-ref-blind-spot-documented-as-a-known-limitation">2026-06-12 — Fake-issue-ref blind spot documented as a known limitation</a></h3> <ul> <li>no-phase-skill issue #3 (a bare #N from a private tracker masquerading as a GitHub ref evades the code-as-name rule) resolved docs-only in PR #4 (squash fa958ea): the offline matcher cannot resolve numbers against the live issue set, so the engine deliberately does not attempt it. VOCABULARY.md now states the obligation (cite issue numbers that exist; #N is not auto-vetted) and README records the false negative as by-design. The opt-in network resolver from the issue was skipped to keep the core offline.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-12--retroactive-audit-mode-shipped---log"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-12--retroactive-audit-mode-shipped---log">2026-06-12 — Retroactive audit mode shipped (--log)</a></h3> <ul> <li>no-phase-skill issue #5 (enforcement was forward-looking only; adopting/upgrading repos had no way to audit existing content against current rules) resolved in PR #6 (squash d3c280c): new <code>--log</code> mode runs every commit message from <code>git log -z</code> through the engine, sha-labelled output; README/SKILL.md document the one-shot adoption/upgrade audit (<code>--all</code> + <code>--log</code>). Baseline file skipped — stateless tool.</li> <li>Per operator direction, "history is immutable" is a default, not a rule: docs guide an opt-in cleanup — archive the original history on a pushed branch, amend/rebase/filter-repo the tells, force-push with lease, and attach a <code>Superseded-by: <new-sha></code> trailer to each replaced commit via <code>git notes</code> (push refs/notes/commits) so the archive stays coherent without rewriting it.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-12--retroactive-audit-smoke-test-on-the-host-repo"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-12--retroactive-audit-smoke-test-on-the-host-repo">2026-06-12 — Retroactive audit smoke test on the host repo</a></h3> <ul> <li>Both histories (host and submodule) lint clean with --log. The --all baseline is ~56 expected flags: the linter's own docs/tests quoting tells, PLAN.md's mandated ordinal dictionary, MEMORY.md's quoted slop subject, and one false positive ("Pass: ≤ 500 lines" in SKILL_AUTHORING.md, a gate verdict not a phase). Do not "fix" these. The only real finding — a stale "PHASEx.md (see section 5)" cross-reference in CLAUDE.md and its karpathy-guidelines copy — was cleaned in 6057776.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-13--skill-installed-into-the-host-repo"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-13--skill-installed-into-the-host-repo">2026-06-13 — Skill installed into the host repo</a></h3> <ul> <li>no-phase skill installed per its own SKILL.md: <code>.claude/skills/no-phase-skill</code> symlinks to the submodule (committed, ce78c63); binary + pre-commit + commit-msg hooks live in <code>.git/hooks</code> (local-only by git design — must be reinstalled per clone). Hooks verified: tell flagged exit 1, clean message exit 0.</li> <li>Adoption audit rerun clean: history exit 0; --all flag count rose ~56→75 solely because the walker follows the new symlink and scans the submodule twice. Minor --all caveat: walkdir_simple follows symlinks (duplicate scans; cyclic symlinks would loop).</li> <li>Recurring constraint: the pre-commit hook flags any commit staging MEMORY.md or PLAN.md, because both intentionally quote tells (baseline fixtures) and MEMORY is append-only so the lines cannot be reworded. Commit those files with <code>git commit --no-verify</code> after confirming the only flags are the recorded baseline ones; the commit-msg hook still gates the message itself.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-13--symlink-walker-bug-fixed"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-13--symlink-walker-bug-fixed">2026-06-13 — Symlink walker bug fixed</a></h3> <ul> <li>no-phase-skill issue #8 (the --all walker followed symlinks: duplicate scans, infinite loop on cycles) fixed in PR #9 (squash 07c9d40): entries whose symlink_metadata reports a symlink are skipped; integration tests cover once-only reporting and cycle termination. Host pointer bumped (e8b00bf). The host's --all baseline drops back to ~56 once the installed hook binary is refreshed.</li> <li>v0.1.1 released on 07c9d40 with all six platform binaries. Repo about boxes set tersely via <code>gh repo edit --description</code> (linted first); the host repo's homepage points at the Pages book.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-13--decimal-numerals--advisory-warn-tier"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-13--decimal-numerals--advisory-warn-tier">2026-06-13 — Decimal numerals + advisory warn tier</a></h3> <ul> <li>no-phase-skill issue #10 (matcher missed the bare-numeral degenerate form <code>5.5</code>/<code>(5.5)</code>/<code>5.5:</code> that VOCABULARY.md mandates) fixed in PR #11 (squash 84847f4). Three rules: Tier 1 — is_numeral now accepts single-decimal <code>N.N</code> (the real cause of the missed <code>(Phase 5.0)</code>; check_line never had a position gate, contrary to the issue's guess); Tier 2 — leading <code>N(.N)?:</code> label prefix flags (colon-then-space gate excludes clock times); Tier 3 — NEW advisory warn tier (exit 3) for the noun-elided form (<code>work-item 5.3</code>, bare dotted codes in prose/parens), excluding version strings/quantities/figure refs. Operator wanted Tier 3 to nudge ("reconsider"), not block — so warn is advisory: the pre-commit hook prints rc-3 but does not block, SKILL.md tells the agent to reconsider.</li> <li>New exit-code contract: 0 clean, 1 confirmed tell (blocks), 2 usage/git error, 3 warnings-only (advisory). Match gained a Severity; text output marks warnings <code>warning:</code>, JSON gained a <code>severity</code> field. Host pointer bumped (a0673d2).</li> <li>CI gotcha: <code>prop_assert!(cond)</code> with a single arg and an inline <code>format!("{}.{}", ..)</code> fails to compile — the macro reinterprets the <code>{}</code> braces. Bind the formatted string to a let first and pass it as an explicit <code>prop_assert!(cond, "x: {}", s)</code> message. The <code>build</code> jobs compile only lib+bin and stayed green; only the <code>test</code> job (which compiles tests) caught it.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-13--local-rust-toolchain-works-now-corrects-2026-06-10-entries"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-13--local-rust-toolchain-works-now-corrects-2026-06-10-entries">2026-06-13 — Local Rust toolchain works now (corrects 2026-06-10 entries)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The local toolchain is no longer broken: <code>cargo</code>/<code>rustc</code> are rustup-managed stable 1.95.0 (GNU host), and <code>cargo build --release</code>, <code>cargo test</code> (30 pass), <code>./test-integration.sh</code> (60/60), and <code>./lint-skill.sh</code> (G1-G8 all pass) all run clean locally. This supersedes the 2026-06-10 notes that cargo was <code>0.0.1-pre-nightly</code> and could not build (verify via CI), and that lint-skill.sh G1 failed for lack of PyYAML — python3 now has PyYAML 5.4.1.</li> <li>Going forward, build and test locally before pushing; CI is confirmation, not the only build path. <code>cargo</code>/<code>rustc</code>/<code>clippy</code> under ~/.cargo/bin are symlinks to the <code>rustup</code> shim, which resolves the active stable toolchain.</li> <li>CI does NOT run clippy — the <code>test</code> job runs only <code>cargo test</code>, <code>conformance</code> runs <code>lint-skill.sh</code>, and <code>build</code> runs <code>cargo build --release</code>. So clippy regressions are invisible to CI; run <code>cargo clippy --all-targets</code> locally. The repo is currently clippy-clean (the one <code>single_char_add_str</code> lint in main.rs was fixed in PR #12 / submodule 18c0329, host pointer 07510c2).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-13--repos-renamed-agentic-host--host-template"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-13--repos-renamed-agentic-host--host-template">2026-06-13 — Repos renamed: agentic-host + host-template</a></h3> <ul> <li>Retired two part-named/eponymous repo names per the name-by-function rule being designed in host issue #1. <code>andrej-karpathy-skills</code> → <strong><code>host-template</code></strong> (the template you instantiate to start a new agentic host; sole-sources CLAUDE.md, will home the planned token-free Rust lifecycle scaffolder). Host <code>agentic-no-phase-skill</code> → <strong><code>agentic-host</code></strong> (no longer solely no-phase; the canonical living host that develops the ecosystem). Naming system: <code>host-template</code> (template — the <code>template-</code> prefix marks the meta one) ↔ <code>agentic-host</code> (canonical host) ↔ <code>no-phase-skill</code> / future <code>agentic-<purpose>-skill</code> (the working skills/instances).</li> <li>Mechanics: <code>gh repo rename</code> on both (GitHub keeps redirects); <code>.gitmodules</code> section+path+url updated and <code>git mv andrej-karpathy-skills host-template</code> (submodule uninitialized, so just the gitlink moved); <code>book.toml</code> title + git-repository-url; <code>git remote set-url origin</code>. Commit 47e53a6. Local working dir left as <code>agentic-no-phase-skill</code> to avoid breaking session cwd; old origin/Pages URLs redirect. Pages canonical URL is now https://connollydavid.github.io/agentic-host/ . The <code>skills/karpathy-guidelines</code> skill is still eponymous — deliberately left for a separate decision.</li> <li>Hook gap found while committing: the host's installed <code>.git/hooks/commit-msg</code> is the STALE pre-warn-tier version (<code>if [ $? -ne 0 ]</code>), so it blocks on the advisory warn (rc=3) exactly like a hard flag. The mandated <code>Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8</code> trailer trips the warn tier (<code>4.8</code> read as a bare dotted code), so every commit now hits it — landed 47e53a6 with <code>--no-verify</code>. Backlog: refresh installed hooks from <code>no-phase-skill/pre-commit</code> (makes warn advisory), and/or add a warn-tier exclusion for git trailers / model-version tokens (<code>Co-Authored-By:</code>, <code>Opus 4.8</code>).</li> <li>Host issue #1 (plan-doc naming design) converged: posted maintainer rulings Q1–Q7 plus a revised Q5 adopting MADR (<code>decisions/NNNN-slug</code> from the <code>0000</code> bootstrap, no <code>ADR-</code> prefix, number-at-merge, phase-vs-decision disambiguated by home). No code/migration yet — gated behind Q7 sequencing (encode VOCABULARY.md → mdBook <code>{{#include}}</code> spike → migrate after #40).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-13--co-authored-by-trailers-exempted-from-detection-pr-13"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-13--co-authored-by-trailers-exempted-from-detection-pr-13">2026-06-13 — Co-Authored-By trailers exempted from detection (PR #13)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Fixed the warn-tier false positive from the rename entry above: <code>classify_line</code> now skips a <code>Co-Authored-By:</code> line entirely (case-insensitive, leading-whitespace tolerant) before flag <em>or</em> warn, because it is a discretionary attribution trailer — the co-author name and tool version (<code>Claude Opus 4.8</code>) are the author's to set, not the linter's to police. Even a phase-like co-author name is exempt by design (covered by the <code>Phase 2 Bot</code> test case). PR #13 squash-merged (submodule 1033ba9); host pointer ecc19b3. VOCABULARY.md documents the exemption under the warn section.</li> <li>This removes the <em>source</em> of the rc-3 on the mandated trailer, but the host's <strong>installed</strong> <code>.git/hooks/no-phase</code> binary and the stale commit-msg hook still predate the fix — so host commits still need <code>--no-verify</code> until the installed skill (binary + hooks) is refreshed from the merged submodule. That refresh is a separate local deployment step (hooks/binary are per-clone, not tracked).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-13--installed-hooks--binary-refreshed-corrects-still-needs---no-verify-just-above"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-13--installed-hooks--binary-refreshed-corrects-still-needs---no-verify-just-above">2026-06-13 — Installed hooks + binary refreshed (corrects "still needs --no-verify" just above)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Did the refresh: copied the exemption binary (<code>no-phase-skill/target/release/no-phase</code>) and the rc-aware hook script (<code>no-phase-skill/pre-commit</code>) into <code>.git/hooks/{no-phase,pre-commit,commit-msg}</code> + <code>chmod +x</code>. Verified against the live hooks: commit-msg passes the <code>Co-Authored-By</code> trailer (exit 0), prints advisory warns without blocking (exit 0 on <code>exec tools (5.5)</code>), and still blocks hard flags (exit 1 on <code>phase 2 of rollout</code>). Ordinary commits no longer need <code>--no-verify</code>.</li> <li>MEMORY.md / PLAN.md commits STILL need <code>--no-verify</code>, for a different reason than the trailer: the pre-commit hook scans staged file <em>content</em>, and both quote real tells as fixtures (<code>## Phase 1</code>, <code>5.5</code>, …) which are genuine rc-1 flags. By design, unchanged.</li> <li>The refresh is local and non-durable — hooks/binary live under <code>.git/</code> (per-clone, untracked), so a fresh clone reinstalls via the skill's install step, and any future linter change means rebuilding and re-copying the binary into <code>.git/hooks/</code>.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--host-template-content-no-phase-pointer-rebrand-licensing--provenance"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--host-template-content-no-phase-pointer-rebrand-licensing--provenance">2026-06-14 — host-template content: no-phase pointer, rebrand, licensing + provenance</a></h3> <ul> <li>Built out the renamed template repo (was inherited near-verbatim from the upstream Karpathy-guidelines plugin). README now has an optional, decoupled <strong>"Recommended: enforcement with no-phase"</strong> section (soft links to <code>connollydavid/no-phase-skill</code> + its SKILL.md/README — no submodule wiring, no assumed coexistence; composition happens at the instance, per the user's "mutual existence" caution). Plugin/marketplace rebranded to this repo: plugin name <code>host-template</code>, marketplace id <code>agentic-host</code>, owner/author David Connolly; install slug <code>host-template@agentic-host</code>. All install URLs repointed off <code>forrestchang/...</code> to <code>connollydavid/host-template</code>.</li> <li>Provenance investigated via the GitHub fork graph: our repo descends from the canonical <strong>andrej-karpathy-skills</strong> (174k★, now <code>multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills</code>; the <code>forrestchang/...</code> URL redirects there), created 2026-01-27 by <strong>Jiayuan Zhang (@forrestchang)</strong> + 7 contributors, ideas from Andrej Karpathy's tweet. Chain: <code>multica-ai</code> → <code>slartibardfast</code> (2026-03-08) → <code>connollydavid</code> (forked 2026-03-21, renamed host-template 2026-06-13/14). Upstream ships <strong>no LICENSE file</strong> — only "MIT" in README prose.</li> <li>Licensing decision: the prose MIT is enough to formalize a real <strong>MIT <code>LICENSE</code></strong>, © "Jiayuan Zhang and the andrej-karpathy-skills contributors" — <strong>no</strong> separate David Connolly copyright (our additions are thin; don't over-claim). GitHub detects MIT. <strong>No NOTICE file</strong> — that's an Apache convention; MIT carries attribution in the LICENSE. template main at <code>37acec1</code>.</li> <li>Two licensing gotchas hit and fixed: (1) a provenance paragraph placed <em>between</em> the copyright line and the MIT permission body makes GitHub detect "Other/NOASSERTION" — keep the MIT body standard, put provenance in README; (2) a single "© David Connolly" line wrongly omitted the real upstream authors. Consumption is by-reference (<code>is_template:false</code>), so the LICENSE doesn't leak into instantiated projects.</li> <li>Still parked (host side): the host's submodule pointer for the template is stale (<code>9a6e4cd</code>) and never bumped — open question whether <code>agentic-host</code>, as an instance, should vendor the template as a live submodule at all. Also unresolved: <code>skills/karpathy-guidelines</code> skill name (kept — it cites Karpathy, content-accurate) and the deeper README reframe from guidelines-doc to host-template.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--agentic-host-architecture-designed-lanes-5w-folders-personas-scaffold-not-yet-built"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--agentic-host-architecture-designed-lanes-5w-folders-personas-scaffold-not-yet-built">2026-06-14 — Agentic-host architecture designed (lanes, 5W folders, personas); scaffold not yet built</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Conceptual model:</strong> the agentic host is the externalized <em>thought</em> about the work; the software under development is the <em>action</em>. The host's rooms map to the five W's (+ How), and the template scaffolds the host's questions while leaving the software's to the developer. Mapping: <strong>Who</strong> → <code>cast/</code> (personas), <strong>What</strong> → <code>spec/</code> (behavioural <code>.allium</code> + temporal <code>.tla</code>), <strong>When</strong> → <code>plan/</code> (milestones), <strong>Where</strong> → the hosted-software submodule slot (developer's), <strong>Why</strong> → <code>call/</code> (decisions), <strong>How</strong> → <code>CLAUDE.md</code> + <code>tools/</code> (the lanes). The template provides When/What/Why/How + the Where-slot; Who and the Where-content are the developer's.</li> <li><strong>Folder naming locked</strong> (short, descriptive, ~4 letters, not literal W-words): <code>plan</code> / <code>spec</code> / <code>call</code> / <code>cast</code>. <code>obligations/</code>→<code>spec/</code>, <code>decisions/</code>→<code>call/</code> already applied in the host (commit <code>4d26115</code>). Ordering is canonical Who→How <strong>in the index</strong> (<code>SUMMARY.md</code>/<code>PLAN.md</code>), never via filename prefixes — "ordering lives in the index, not the name."</li> <li><strong>Three-lane verification stack</strong> (recorded as decisions <code>call/0002</code>, <code>call/0003</code>): hygiene → <strong>no-phase</strong> (Unlicense, ours); requirements + property-based testing → <strong>allium</strong> (<code>juxt/allium</code>, MIT — skills elicit/propagate/weed/…); timing + concurrency via TLA+ → <strong>Specula</strong> (<code>specula-org/Specula</code>, Apache-2.0 — a 5-step pipeline: Code Analysis→Spec Generation→Harness→Validation→Bug Confirmation). All three are <em>referenced git submodules</em> (<code>tools/…</code>); we orchestrate via CLAUDE.md + thin wrappers, <strong>never patch</strong> their files (instruct-not-patch, reference-not-vendor) → stay Apache-clean, submodule bumps trivial.</li> <li><strong>Licensing strategy:</strong> our parts → <strong>Unlicense</strong> (no-phase already is); allium (MIT) + Specula (Apache-2.0) referenced not vendored, licenses stay with their owners. Tool <em>outputs</em> (generated specs/obligations/bug-reports about our system) are <strong>project-owned, outside the tools' license scope</strong> — like a compiler's output isn't a derivative of GCC; verified against both repos' license text + intent (decision <code>call/0001</code>). Carve-out: verbatim tool boilerplate (a Specula trace-module, an allium template) keeps its license → reference it (<code>EXTENDS</code>/include), never paste.</li> <li><strong>Personas</strong> (<code>cast/</code>): example personas <strong>Mara</strong> (human operator) + <strong>Wren</strong> (agentic LLM developer). They are the host's <em>own</em> Who (generic to any agentic host); for this repo they double as the real Who (meta — our software-under-dev IS an agentic host). Each project builds ≥1 persona of its <em>software's</em> users by discussion (allium <code>elicit</code>). <code>cast/applying-personas.md</code> reproduces <strong>Powell, Keenan & McDaid (2007) "Enhancing Agile Requirements Elicitation with Personas"</strong> as the <strong>PRIMARY</strong> anchor (the user states it is instrumental to their thinking) — their 9-step XP-Persona workshop process, as an ordered list, matching their articulation; Cohn/Patton/Gothelf/BDD only reinforce, departing only on strong later evidence. Committed <code>53a1285</code>.</li> <li><strong>NOT yet built:</strong> the actual <code>host-template</code> scaffold — <code>.gitmodules</code> for the 3 <code>tools/</code> submodules, <code>.claude/skills</code> symlinks into the submodule skill dirs, the <code>cast</code>/<code>plan</code>/<code>spec</code>/<code>call</code> seed, README + Provenance, and the coupled step: rewrite the inherited Karpathy guidelines in our own words → then flip the template <code>LICENSE</code> MIT→Unlicense (can't flip until rewritten, since MIT covers the still-inherited prose). Open: where the methodology/<code>call/</code> decisions are sole-sourced — host vs template (user earlier said "CLAUDE.md is sole-sourced in host-template"); the <code>call/</code> log currently lives in the host.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--no-phase-skill-deep-renamed-to-host-lint-full-history-rewrite"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--no-phase-skill-deep-renamed-to-host-lint-full-history-rewrite">2026-06-14 — no-phase-skill deep-renamed to host-lint (full history rewrite)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The tool outgrew its name (it catches code-as-name, bare-numeral headers, decimals — not just "phase"), so it was re-aligned into the host-tool family. <strong>Naming principle: our own tools are <code>host-*</code></strong> (<code>host-grammar</code> = the rules crate, <code>host-lint</code> = the checker/detector [was no-phase], <code>host-lifecycle</code> = the generator/scaffolder); <strong>adopted third-party tools keep their identity</strong> (<code>allium</code>, <code>specula</code>). The split is ownership, not coupling.</li> <li>Deep rename via <code>git filter-repo</code>, "as if from the start": all 22 commits rewritten — <code>0</code> occurrences of <code>no-phase</code>/<code>no_phase</code> in history, <code>no-phase.allium</code>→<code>host-lint.allium</code>, package/binary/SKILL/VOCABULARY/workflow all <code>host-lint</code>. Repo renamed <code>no-phase-skill</code>→<code>host-lint</code>. Builds clean; detection + Co-Authored-By exemption verified. New main head <code>a9a7cd3</code>.</li> <li><strong>Recipe + gotcha for next time:</strong> push an <code>archive/<oldname></code> branch FIRST (preserves original history), then delete it locally so filter-repo doesn't rewrite it; run <code>filter-repo --replace-text <file> --replace-message <file> --path-rename old:new</code> (rules ordered longest-first so <code>no-phase-skill</code>→<code>host-lint</code> precedes <code>no-phase</code>→<code>host-lint</code>). <strong>filter-repo skips BINARY blobs</strong> — a binary named <code>no-phase</code> was committed in early history (later removed), so it survived the text pass and needed a second <code>filter-repo --invert-paths --path no-phase</code> to strip it (also good hygiene — binaries shouldn't be in history). Then force-push main + tags, <code>gh repo rename</code>.</li> <li>Aftercare done: <strong><code>archive/no-phase</code> branch is PROTECTED</strong> (deletions off, force-push off, enforce-admins on) so the original history can't be lost. The 3 GitHub <strong>releases (v0.1.x) were DELETED</strong> (their assets kept stale <code>no-phase-*</code> names) — <strong>tags kept</strong> (now on the rewritten commits; re-cut releases as <code>host-lint-*</code> when needed). The red CI on the force-push runs was a <strong>timing artifact</strong> (conformance G3 compared SKILL name <code>host-lint</code> to the repo dir, still <code>no-phase-skill</code> for the ~seconds before <code>gh repo rename</code> landed) — a fresh run once the repo is <code>host-lint</code> passes; build/test/G1-G8 all passed.</li> <li><strong>Dependent re-pointing done</strong> (old URL redirects + <code>archive/no-phase</code> keep old SHAs resolvable, so nothing was broken meanwhile): template <code>tools/no-phase-skill</code>→<code>tools/host-lint</code> + <code>.claude/skills</code> + README/STRUCTURE/call refs swept, <code>EXAMPLES.md</code> retired (<code>5ab0ba4</code>); host submodule + <code>.claude/skills/host-lint</code> symlink + CLAUDE/PLAN/BOOTSTRAP/CI-PIPELINE/FORMAL-SPEC/call refs swept in two commits (<code>13a0d4e</code> structure, <code>479c5fc</code> plan docs). <strong>MEMORY.md left untouched (append-only) — its earlier entries still say <code>no-phase</code> by design.</strong></li> <li><strong>Still pending:</strong> (1) the host's <em>installed</em> git hooks still call the <code>no-phase</code> binary (per-clone, local) — refresh to <code>host-lint</code>; (2) <code>host-grammar</code> is built locally but UNPUSHED and its README still says no-phase — fix before pushing; (3) <code>host-lifecycle</code> not yet created; (4) the <code>host-lint → host-grammar</code> dependency refactor (move shared primitives like <code>is_numeral</code> into host-grammar) — doing it pushes host-lint and confirms its CI green. (5) host issue #1 is stale (its phase-naming topic is superseded by the cast/spec/plan/call + three-lane design).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--host--tools-built-template-scaffold-up-template-rewrite-planned-compact-prep"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--host--tools-built-template-scaffold-up-template-rewrite-planned-compact-prep">2026-06-14 — host-* tools built; template scaffold up; template rewrite planned (compact prep)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The <code>host-*</code> family is now BUILT and green (the entries above designed it): <strong>host-grammar</strong> (<code>342f507</code>, v0.1.1, Unlicense) holds the shared rules incl. <code>is_numeral</code>; <strong>host-lint</strong> (<code>c0b19f4</code>, Unlicense, CI green) is the checker and now <code>pub use</code>-re-exports <code>host_grammar::is_numeral</code> (depends on host-grammar via git), so the checker and the generator share ONE numeral definition; <strong>host-lifecycle</strong> (<code>6dac2fc</code>, Unlicense) is the token-free generator — <code>validate <dir></code> / <code>next <dir></code> over host-grammar (smoke-tested: validate <code>call/</code> → ok, next → 0004). Principle: our tools are <code>host-*</code>; adopted tools keep their names (allium, specula).</li> <li>Template scaffold built (<code>host-template</code> @ <code>0e5dd7f</code>): <code>tools/{host-lint,host-lifecycle,allium,specula}</code> submodules, <code>.claude/skills</code> symlinks, <code>cast</code>/<code>plan</code>/<code>call</code> seed, <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>; <code>EXAMPLES.md</code> retired; all old refs swept to <code>host-lint</code>.</li> <li><strong>The remaining template work is PLANNED in <code>TEMPLATE-REWRITE.md</code></strong> (host milestone doc, <code>0a72b13</code>): retire the inherited <code>skills/karpathy-guidelines</code> + <code>.claude-plugin</code>, rewrite <code>CLAUDE.md</code> as the canonical methodology in our own words, reframe the README, flip <code>LICENSE</code> MIT→Unlicense + Provenance. <strong>Decisions resolved: D1</strong> — full cut, but <strong>tightly framed for weaker LLMs / lesser harnesses</strong> (crisp, explicit, directive; "followable by a weak agent" is the success criterion). <strong>D2</strong> — defer the host↔template sole-source sync (the host building the template whose methodology it follows is a self-hosting bootstrap — a C compiler compiling a C compiler — reconcile later).</li> <li>Tidy-ups still open: bump <code>tools/host-lint</code> submodule pointers (template + host) <code>a9a7cd3</code>→<code>c0b19f4</code>; refresh the host's installed git hooks (still the <code>no-phase</code> binary, per-clone/local) to <code>host-lint</code>; update/close stale issue #1.</li> <li>Repo heads (all pushed, clean): host <code>agentic-host</code> <code>0a72b13</code> · template <code>0e5dd7f</code> · host-lint <code>c0b19f4</code> · host-grammar <code>342f507</code> · host-lifecycle <code>6dac2fc</code>. New repos <code>host-grammar</code>/<code>host-lifecycle</code> are public, Unlicense, default branch <code>main</code>.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--template-rewrite-executed-all-template-rewrite-tidy-ups-done"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--template-rewrite-executed-all-template-rewrite-tidy-ups-done">2026-06-14 — template rewrite executed; all TEMPLATE-REWRITE tidy-ups done</a></h3> <ul> <li>Executed <code>TEMPLATE-REWRITE.md</code>: the template is now the canonical agentic host, not the inherited Karpathy skin. Template <code>0e5dd7f</code>→<code>59315bb</code>. <code>CLAUDE.md</code> rewritten as the operating manual in our own words (five rooms; four principles; names/numbers; call/spec/cast; three lanes; host-* tools; audited plans + append-only memory; submodule discipline) — framed tight/directive for weaker agents. README reframed as the template; <code>LICENSE</code> flipped MIT→Unlicense; <code>skills/karpathy-guidelines</code> + <code>.claude-plugin</code> retired; STRUCTURE "not yet wired" note dropped. Provenance (Karpathy via Jiayuan Zhang; personas via Powell/Keenan/McDaid 2007) carried as acknowledgement, not a license obligation. Rewrite commit <code>668b521</code>.</li> <li>Tidy-ups (all done): (1) bumped <code>tools/host-lint</code> pointers — template <code>59315bb</code>, host <code>cddf673</code> — <code>a9a7cd3</code>→<code>c0b19f4</code>; <strong>verified direction</strong>: <code>c0b19f4</code> is host-lint remote <code>main</code> head and the child of <code>a9a7cd3</code>, so forward (the <code>git describe</code> <code>v0.1.2-2-ga9a7cd3</code> had made it look ambiguous — always check ancestry, not describe). (2) Refreshed the host's installed hooks: renamed <code>.git/hooks/no-phase</code>→<code>host-lint</code>, reinstalled the canonical <code>pre-commit</code> (refs <code>$SCRIPT_DIR/host-lint</code>) as both <code>pre-commit</code>+<code>commit-msg</code>; verified blocks a tell / passes clean / passes Co-Authored-By trailer (local, per-clone, uncommitted). (3) Closed issue #1 with a resolution comment — superseded by cast/spec/plan/call + three lanes; its proposed "don't zero-pad" departure was settled the OTHER way (we DO pad via host-grammar, since the number is generated+checked, not eyeballed).</li> <li><strong>D2 still deferred</strong>: host <code>CLAUDE.md</code> and template <code>CLAUDE.md</code> now state the methodology twice (self-hosting bootstrap) — reconcile later. Tracked at the bottom of <code>TEMPLATE-REWRITE.md</code>.</li> <li>Repo heads (all pushed, clean): host <code>agentic-host</code> <code>c35d8a8</code> · template <code>59315bb</code> · host-lint <code>c0b19f4</code> · host-grammar <code>342f507</code> · host-lifecycle <code>6dac2fc</code>.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--host-claudemd-exempts-the-templates-claudemd-orphaned-host-cruft-retired"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--host-claudemd-exempts-the-templates-claudemd-orphaned-host-cruft-retired">2026-06-14 — host CLAUDE.md exempts the template's CLAUDE.md; orphaned host cruft retired</a></h3> <ul> <li>Added an explicit exemption to the host root <code>CLAUDE.md</code> §0: <strong>do not treat <code>host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> as instructions for this repo.</strong> It is template payload (the operating manual for projects instantiated <em>from</em> the template, addressed to an agent in one of those projects). The host root CLAUDE.md is the sole authority here. Rationale: an agent editing inside the template submodule can auto-load the nested CLAUDE.md and mistake template payload for host governance. This is the practical guardrail for the deferred D2 duplication (both files state the methodology until host↔template sole-source is reconciled). Commit <code>1c05cba</code>.</li> <li>Retired the host's own orphaned <code>skills/karpathy-guidelines/</code> (inherited from the host's fork origin; unreferenced — no symlink/plugin, absent from SUMMARY/nav). The template retired its copy in the rewrite; this clears the host's. Commit <code>78e86fd</code>. Host head now <code>78e86fd</code>.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--migration-protocol-built-host-dogfood-migrated-case-c"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--migration-protocol-built-host-dogfood-migrated-case-c">2026-06-14 — migration protocol built; host dogfood-migrated (case c)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Built the methodology-migration protocol end to end (decisions <code>call/0004</code> template-is-versioned-source, <code>call/0005</code> cased+moded protocol). <strong>host-lifecycle</strong> gained migrate verbs — <code>adopt <dir> <rev> [--dry-run]</code> (idempotent room scaffold + <code>.host</code> stamp), <code>version</code>, <code>classify</code> (a|b|c) — pure helpers unit-tested, date via Hinnant civil-from-days (deterministic), CI added; pushed <code>acd551e</code>, tag v0.1.1. <strong>Local cargo builds</strong> (1.95.0) — the old "CI-only" note is stale; verify locally.</li> <li><strong><code>host-template/MIGRATION.md</code></strong> is the protocol payload: two orthogonal axes — <strong>case</strong> (a none / b foreign / c ours-prior) decides governance; <strong>mode</strong> (Preview / Shallow / Staged / Deep + selection rule, history-immutable-by-default) decides blast radius. Template <code>d57c29a</code>; host pointer bumped; tools/host-lifecycle bumped to acd551e.</li> <li><strong>The <code>.host</code> stamp</strong> (template + revision + adopted-date, repo root) is the copy-at-version record; case-(c) upgrades diff from <code>revision</code>. classify keys on it (stamp→c, CLAUDE.md-no-stamp→b, neither→a).</li> <li><strong>Host self-migrated (case c, Shallow):</strong> stamped @ template <code>d57c29a</code>; <code>CLAUDE.md</code> §0 now articulates the agentic-host model + copy-at-version sourcing (chose an <strong>additive</strong> note over rewriting the four principles — wholesale churn of equivalent prose would violate our own §3; the spine was semantically current, only its sourcing was unrecorded); established the <code>plan/</code> room with <code>plan/0001-migration-protocol/</code>. The host's legacy root milestone docs (BOOTSTRAP etc.) predate <code>plan/</code> and are left as-is (fold-in is optional later).</li> <li><strong>Pre-existing, not from this work:</strong> local <code>mdbook</code> rejects <code>book.toml</code>'s <code>multilingual = false</code> (newer mdbook removed the field) — the site build dies at config parse. Check whether the host's Pages CI uses a compatible mdbook; a one-line removal fixes it if not. Out of scope for the migration; flagged.</li> <li>Follow-on dogfoods are their own milestones (not done): <strong>Agentic-MCP-Win32s</strong> (case c Shallow — re-point renamed submodules, rename its <code>PHASE1–7.md</code>, reconcile AGENTS.md) and <strong>pgs-release</strong> (case b Staged-Shallow, NOT Deep — its FFmpeg patch-series provenance forbids history rewrite; ~14 PHASE files + ~150 cross-refs).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--win32s-pr-1-reviewed-host-lint-gains-a-sanctioned-vocabulary-allow-list"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--win32s-pr-1-reviewed-host-lint-gains-a-sanctioned-vocabulary-allow-list">2026-06-14 — Win32s PR #1 reviewed; host-lint gains a sanctioned-vocabulary allow-list</a></h3> <ul> <li>Reviewed the first external dogfood migration (Agentic-MCP-Win32s PR #1, agent-produced). The mechanical work was right (re-point renamed submodules, <code>.host</code> stamp, hardcoded-ref fixes, cast/call scaffold, plan-folder renames, <code>mcp-win32s</code> untouched) but it <strong>over-reached</strong>: to chase "zero tells" it rewrote <strong>append-only <code>MEMORY.md</code></strong> and <strong>closed, immutable milestone bodies</strong>, dissolving precise identifiers (<code>finding #7</code>, work-item codes) into vague prose and introducing factual rot; it also added a live <code>@host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> import (contradicts copy-at-version <code>call/0004</code>) and minuted an invented "operator-authorized override" in its <code>call/0001</code>. <strong>Do not merge as-is.</strong> Root cause = a missing capability, not just judgement: host-lint had no way to <em>acknowledge</em> a sanctioned token, so the only path to clean was to mutate protected content.</li> <li>Fix built (host-lint only, per the user): <strong><code>.host-lint-allow</code></strong> repo-root file lists legitimate vocabulary (one phrase per line, <code>#</code> comments). Each phrase is masked out before classification — case-insensitive, <strong>word-boundaried</strong> so <code>phase 1</code> clears <code>phase 1</code> but not <code>phase 12</code>, and a different tell on the same line still flags. Applies in every mode (<code>--stdin</code>/files/<code>--all</code>/<code>--log</code>); missing file = unchanged behaviour (opt-in). Engine: <code>scan_text_with_allow</code> + <code>mask_allowed</code> in <code>lib.rs</code>; the binary loads the file from the repo root and threads the list through. host-lint <code>968d988</code> (main); host pointer <code>2dbf766</code>. Decision <code>call/0006</code>.</li> <li><strong>Scope decision (user):</strong> sanctioned-tokens-only, <strong>not</strong> a per-site acknowledged baseline. Rationale in <code>call/0006</code>: the migration move for ordinal milestone docs is to <em>rename</em> them to content names, so the acute need is exempting genuine vocabulary; a baseline (the "acknowledge a real legacy tell we won't touch" tool) is deferred to a concrete forcing case — <strong>pgs-release</strong> history is the likely one. Future migration agents: there is no baseline feature yet; do not expect one.</li> <li>The Win32s migration will be <strong>redone</strong> by the other agent once this feature lands; the briefs at <code>~/agentic-mcp-win32s-migration-brief.md</code> and <code>~/pgs-release-migration-brief.md</code> stand, now backed by the allow-list for legitimate version strings / filenames.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--adoption-is-a-clean-break-call0007-append-only-gets-a-governed-escape-valve-pgs-release-provenance-corrected"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--adoption-is-a-clean-break-call0007-append-only-gets-a-governed-escape-valve-pgs-release-provenance-corrected">2026-06-14 — adoption is a clean break (call/0007); append-only gets a governed escape valve; pgs-release provenance corrected</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong><code>call/0007</code> refines <code>call/0005</code>:</strong> correlate mode with intent — <strong>adoption → clean break</strong> to a pristine state; <strong>upgrade (stamped rev→rev) → Shallow delta</strong> (copier-update shaped). A clean break is three risk tiers: (1) <strong>live files</strong> rewritten freely to content names; (2) <strong>owned append-only record</strong> (<code>MEMORY.md</code>, closed bodies) rewritten only <strong>archive-first + map-only + recorded</strong>; (3) <strong>git history acknowledged</strong>, not rewritten (re-shaing rarely beats the value). Token logic: clean break costs more once, less per future session — favours active repos.</li> <li><strong><code>CLAUDE.md</code> §6 amended:</strong> append-only now has exactly one sanctioned exception — a one-time, archive-first, map-only, recorded transformation (the document analog of a Deep rewrite). Never free-form, never self-authorized. This is the rule Win32s PR #1 violated with an invented override.</li> <li><strong>pgs-release provenance was WRONG and is corrected (examined 2026-06-14):</strong> it is <strong>not a fork</strong> (567 KB, no parent) — planning docs + four vendor submodules; the FFmpeg fork (<code>connollydavid/FFmpeg</code>) and patch series are <strong>ours</strong>; host history is ~199 commits, <strong>100% David Connolly</strong>, tells in a minority of <code>docs:</code> subjects. The "external FFmpeg provenance forbids a rewrite" premise was a misattribution. pgs-release host migration stays <strong>Staged-Shallow by COST</strong> (re-pointing 11 release tags <code>n*-pgsN.N</code> + two branches ≫ value of denumbering a few <code>docs:</code> subjects), <strong>not</strong> by prohibition. The corrected provenance test: the bar is "shas external consumers depend on," not "touches a patch series" — a patch series we author is rewrite-tolerant (rebase is its native lifecycle).</li> <li><strong>Emerging next concept (under discussion, not built):</strong> an <strong>enforced mapping dictionary</strong> for adoption — a single declared old-concept→canonical-new-name registry, applied <strong>deterministically/token-free</strong> (host-lifecycle) so the tier-2 map-only rule is mechanically guaranteed rather than prose discipline. Purpose: <strong>prevent drift between concepts</strong> — both spatial (parallel agents/files renaming the same concept inconsistently, as PR #1 did — it invented "network-and-transport milestone") and temporal (future sessions resolving old names the same way). It is the deterministic complement to host-lint (detector) and <code>.host-lint-allow</code> (sanctioned vocabulary).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--record-handling-resolved-exclude-via-host-lintignore-call0009-remap--ignore-tooling-shipped"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--record-handling-resolved-exclude-via-host-lintignore-call0009-remap--ignore-tooling-shipped">2026-06-14 — record handling resolved: exclude via .host-lintignore (call/0009); remap + ignore tooling shipped</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong><code>.host-lintignore</code></strong> added to host-lint (<code>4dc9814</code>): gitignore-lite path exclusion honoured by <code>--all</code> only (exact paths, single-segment <code>*</code> glob, trailing-slash dir prefix). Engine stays general/policy-free; the migration writes the file. <strong><code>host-lifecycle remap</code></strong> (<code>8522bb0</code>, v0.2.0) also honours it so excluded paths aren't flagged or rewritten.</li> <li><strong>Decision <code>call/0009</code>:</strong> a migrated project <strong>excludes its append-only record</strong> (<code>MEMORY.md</code>, closed <code>plan/*/README.md</code> bodies) from the audit rather than rewriting it. <strong>Verified on Win32s:</strong> nothing re-scans the tree — the hooks lint only the commit subject + staged diff, and CI runs no linter (mdbook only); so old names in the record cost <strong>zero</strong> ongoing. Rewriting the dense record produces awkward prose (<code>"the bridge core bridge first-green"</code>) and ~25 review-codes (<code>finding #7</code>, <code>R1</code>/<code>F1</code>/<code>G1</code>) resist naming — one-time cost, no recurring payback. So exclusion is token-optimal; the live layer is still clean-broken. The renamed <code>plan/<NNNN-slug>/</code> folders are the implicit old→new map.</li> <li><strong>Win32s migration approach (final):</strong> structural/live clean break via the <code>remap</code> dictionary + folders-as-map; record left verbatim and excluded; submodules re-pointed (<code>host-lint</code>, <code>host-template</code>; <code>mcp-win32s</code> untouched). The instructions at <code>~/agentic-mcp-win32s-retry-instructions.md</code> carry the slug map and exact tool commands. <strong>The migration is reattempted from a fresh session; PR #1 is closed.</strong></li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-14--software-embedding-is-a-bare-store--worktrees-call0010-host-self-migrated"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-14--software-embedding-is-a-bare-store--worktrees-call0010-host-self-migrated">2026-06-14 — software embedding is a bare store + worktrees (call/0010); host self-migrated</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Decision <code>call/0010</code>:</strong> the <em>Where</em> room (software under test) embeds as a <strong>bare store + named worktrees</strong>, not a single gitlink submodule — <code><name>.git/</code> (object store) + canonical worktree <code><name>/</code> (audited, CI-run) + parallel <code><name>.<line>/</code> (one per agent or live release branch). The pin moves from a gitlink to a <strong><code>.host-software</code> recipe</strong> (one <code>[software "<name>"]</code> stanza per component, mirroring <code>.gitmodules</code>); <code>host-lifecycle</code> will own the "is <code><name>/</code> at its pin?" check. Motive: parallel agents <strong>and</strong> several production releases materialized at once — which one tree cannot do. Cost (accepted): worktrees share objects but not environment (each its own build dir / nested submodule checkouts). Idiom + prior art confirmed by websearch (bare-<code>.git</code>-parent + sibling worktrees; worktrees don't auto-init submodules).</li> <li><strong>Host is adopter zero — self-migrated <code>host-lint</code></strong> (submodule → bare store) the same session, dogfooding the decision. Conversion is <strong>reference-preserving</strong>: the canonical worktree keeps the path <code>host-lint/</code>, so build commands and the hook binary path (<code>host-lint/target/release/host-lint</code>) still resolve; only the embedding changed. Pin preserved exactly at <code>2ef5399</code> (no software commit moved). Steps: bare-clone first (verify pin) → <code>git rm --cached</code> + drop <code>.gitmodules</code> stanza + <code>rm -rf .git/modules/host-lint</code> → <code>worktree add</code> → <strong>rebuild the binary</strong> (<code>target/</code> is gitignored, so the conversion destroys it — needed <code>cargo</code> locally). <code>.gitignore</code> excludes <code>/host-lint/ /host-lint.git/ /host-lint.*/</code>. Template + stamp upgraded to <code>1bf4f16</code> (the revision documenting the layout). Full rsync backup taken first at <code>~/agentic-host-backup-2026-06-14-pre-bareworktree</code>.</li> <li><strong>Migration rule (submodule → bare), in <code>call/0010</code> + template <code>MIGRATION.md</code>:</strong> preserve the gitlink SHA as the pin; de-register the gitlink; write <code>.host-software</code>; gitignore the trees; "pointer-bump" discipline becomes "pin-update." <code>host-lint --all</code> from the host root stays noisy (it walks the filesystem incl. the worktree, template, and decision docs that quote tells) — pre-existing, not a regression; the host gates on <code>--subject</code>/<code>--staged</code> hooks, not <code>--all</code>.</li> <li><strong>Open/next:</strong> Win32s agent will do the same submodule→bare conversion on <code>mcp-win32s</code> as a follow-up PR (after the methodology-adoption PR #2 landed); a greenfield project will demo fresh case-(a) adoption with the layout. <code>host-lifecycle</code> setup/check verb for <code>.host-software</code> is <strong>not built yet</strong> (convention + decision only).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-15--the-host-lifecycle-software-verb-is-built-and-wired-in-corrects-not-built-yet-above"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-15--the-host-lifecycle-software-verb-is-built-and-wired-in-corrects-not-built-yet-above">2026-06-15 — the <code>host-lifecycle software</code> verb is built and wired in (corrects "not built yet" above)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong><code>host-lifecycle</code> v0.3.0</strong> (main <code>d1a7646</code>, 6 binaries released) adds <strong><code>software --materialize|--check <dir></code></strong>. <code>--materialize</code> parses <code>.host-software</code> (one <code>[software "<name>"]</code> stanza per component, git-config-style) and clones each <code><name>.git</code> bare store + canonical worktree at <code>pin</code> (+ refspec fix, nested submodule init, parallel worktrees on a branch named by the <code><line></code> suffix), idempotently. <code>--check</code> verifies each canonical worktree is at its recorded pin — the audit that replaces <code>git submodule status</code> (exit 1 on drift/missing). 11 tests incl. a git round-trip; clippy clean. Validated E2E against the live host: <code>software --check .</code> → <code>ok host-lint/ @ 2ef5399</code>.</li> <li><strong>Canonical worktree materializes detached at the pin</strong> (the SHA, not a branch) — the precise audit anchor; branch off it to work, update the recipe <code>pin</code> when you merge.</li> <li><strong>Wired into the methodology (template <code>8c28e33</code>):</strong> template <code>CLAUDE.md</code> spine, <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>, <code>MIGRATION.md</code> now describe the Where room as a bare store with worktrees (not a submodule) and name the verb; template <strong><code>call/0004</code></strong> records the decision (the template's own copy — the host's is <code>call/0010</code>; the host↔template duplication is the acknowledged deferred one); template <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code> bumped to <code>d1a7646</code>. Host adopted template <code>8c28e33</code> (pointer + stamp). So a greenfield repo adopting the template gets the verb and docs that name it.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-15--the-bare-worktree-conversion-broke-the-site-ci-worktree-absence-coherence"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-15--the-bare-worktree-conversion-broke-the-site-ci-worktree-absence-coherence">2026-06-15 — the bare-worktree conversion broke the Site CI (worktree-absence coherence)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The self-migration turned the host's mdBook <strong>Site CI red at <code>1bd3b62</code></strong> (last green <code>d0f0bea</code>). Cause: <code>.claude/skills/host-lint -> ../../host-lint</code> <strong>dangles in CI</strong>. A <em>submodule</em> left an empty dir on checkout (the symlink resolved to it); a <em>gitignored worktree</em> is simply <strong>absent</strong> in CI (it is materialized from <code>.host-software</code>, which the docs workflow never runs). With <code>book.toml src = "."</code>, mdBook scanned the dangling link and failed. Fix (<code>5406196</code>): prune broken symlinks before <code>mdbook build</code> (<code>find . -type l ! -exec test -e {} \; -delete</code>) and drop <code>multilingual = false</code> (newer local mdBook rejects it, so local≠CI). Site green again.</li> <li><strong>Lesson — the general class ("worktree-absence coherence"):</strong> the bare+worktree model removes the <strong>auto-presence</strong> a submodule gave the software. Any host artifact that references <code><software>/</code> (skill symlinks, <code>src</code>-scans, hook binary paths, CI/build steps) is valid locally (materialized) but <strong>breaks in a fresh-clone/CI context</strong> unless that context materializes first or is resilient. "Complete" must mean the whole suite green — a feature built while a sibling CI is red is not complete. → motivates an <strong>intra-software coherence rule + a fresh-clone verify step</strong> (proposed for the template).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-15--coherence-hardening-built-version-to-version-upgrade-ledger--reader-host-lifecycle-v040"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-15--coherence-hardening-built-version-to-version-upgrade-ledger--reader-host-lifecycle-v040">2026-06-15 — coherence hardening built; version-to-version upgrade ledger + reader (host-lifecycle v0.4.0)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Coherence (call/0005 template, call/0011 host) shipped:</strong> <code>host-lifecycle software --check</code> now also flags a <strong><code>HAZARD</code></strong> for any host-tracked symlink resolving into a worktree path (bounded to symlinks — path-strings can't be told from prose statically). <strong>v0.3.1.</strong> Prevention on the host: <code>.claude/skills/host-lint</code> untracked + gitignored, recreated after materialize. Template <code>CLAUDE.md</code> carries the rule (don't track worktree-dependent artifacts; un-materialized CI must stay green; "done" = whole suite green).</li> <li><strong>Version-to-version upgrade (the gap the user named):</strong> a case-(c) upgrade must apply not just spine <strong>doc</strong> deltas but the <strong>structural migrations</strong> a revision span introduced (re-embed the software, bump a tool) — which a <code>git diff</code> does not surface. Solution: template <strong><code>UPGRADING.md</code> ledger</strong>, one <code>[upgrade "<revision>"]</code> stanza per action keyed by the revision it landed at, + <strong><code>host-lifecycle upgrade <dir></code></strong> (v0.4.0) which prints every entry <strong>strictly newer</strong> than the repo's <code>.host</code> stamp, decided by <strong>git ancestry</strong> against the template (so same-day revisions order correctly — a date cannot). An entry the local template can't resolve = repo is behind it = pending. Fetch the template to target first.</li> <li><strong>Proven end-to-end:</strong> host stamped <code>8c28e33</code> → <code>upgrade .</code> listed exactly the coherence action (<code>325f2cf</code>), excluded bare-worktree (<code>8c28e33</code>, already had). After re-stamp to <code>bcd1631</code> → "up to date". <code>software --check</code> clean.</li> <li><strong>State:</strong> host-lifecycle <strong>v0.4.0</strong> (<code>1e21c99</code>, coherence detection + upgrade reader). Template <strong><code>bcd1631</code></strong>: <code>call/0005</code> + coherence/upgrade rules + <code>UPGRADING.md</code> + <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code> v0.4.0. Host adopted <code>bcd1631</code>.</li> <li><strong>Win32s readiness:</strong> Win32s migrate is an <strong>upgrade <code>7dd7556</code> → <code>bcd1631</code></strong>, not a one-off conversion — <code>host-lifecycle upgrade</code> will list both the bare-store conversion <em>and</em> coherence. Recon: Win32s has <strong>no tracked symlinks</strong> (its <code>phase</code>/<code>phase-gate</code> skills are real dirs, not links into the software) and a plain <code>mdbook.yml</code>, so the host's exact symlink break won't recur there; its conversion is lower-risk. Still needs the upgrade brief framed off the ledger. <strong>Done — PR #3</strong> (<code>7dd7556 → bcd1631</code>): ledger-driven, pin <code>e52e667</code> preserved, gitlink de-registered, coherence no-op (no symlinks), all gating prose-only. Reviewed ready-to-merge; <strong>watch the post-merge Site CI</strong> (main-only trigger, so the "green" is a local un-materialized sim).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-15--coherence-generalized-to-tool-submodules-abc-template-ate-its-own-dogfood"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-15--coherence-generalized-to-tool-submodules-abc-template-ate-its-own-dogfood">2026-06-15 — coherence generalized to tool submodules (A+B+C); template ate its own dogfood</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>The finding (Win32s agent, + I'd seen and deferred it):</strong> <code>call/0005</code> claimed to generalize but its rule + detector were <strong>software-worktree-only</strong>, while the <strong>template itself</strong> tracked <strong>17 <code>.claude/skills/*</code> symlinks into its tool submodules — 16 dangling</strong> under partial init (a host that builds only the tool it needs). <code>software --check</code> reported "no hazards" while they dangled (it keyed on <code>.host-software</code> worktree paths). Any tree-walking tool (mdBook <code>src="."</code>) trips over them — green in CI (submodule empty → no symlinks), broken on a contributor's machine. Self-inconsistency.</li> <li><strong>A — prevention.</strong> Untracked the template's 17 symlinks + gitignored <code>.claude/skills/</code>; <strong><code>link-skills.sh</code></strong> regenerates a link per <em>materialized</em> tool (skipping uninitialized submodules → no dangle). The template now eats its dogfood.</li> <li><strong>B — detection generalized (<code>host-lifecycle</code> v0.4.1).</strong> <code>software --check</code>'s HAZARD scan no longer keys on worktree paths; it flags <strong>any tracked symlink whose resolved target is not itself tracked in the repo</strong> — catching software-worktree symlinks <em>and</em> tool-submodule sub-path symlinks, zero-FP (a symlink to a submodule <em>root</em>, a tracked gitlink, resolves to git's empty dir and is not flagged). Submodule-boundary-respecting (<code>git ls-files</code>), so it guards a repo's <strong>own</strong> tree, not across a submodule.</li> <li><strong>C — docs scoping.</strong> STRUCTURE.md now recommends a scoped mdBook <code>src</code> (a <code>docs/</code> dir + <code>{{#include}}</code>) over <code>src="."</code>, which walks submodules/worktrees and trips on un-materialized content.</li> <li><strong>Wiring.</strong> <code>call/0005</code> amended to "any separately-materialized path"; <code>UPGRADING.md</code> entry <code>@71d12a8</code> (requires v0.4.1); template <code>0401bff</code>; host adopted (<code>upgrade .</code> listed the action, then up-to-date). The roles are complementary: <strong>A</strong> removes the template instance, <strong>B</strong> guards a host's own tree, <strong>C</strong> stops hosts walking <em>into</em> a submodule — the detector can't cross the submodule boundary, so A (not B) is what fixes the template.</li> <li><strong>gh-title gate corrected: block on flag, not on warn (<code>0811dbe</code>).</strong> The CLAUDE.md GitHub rule said <code>host-lint --stdin</code> "must exit 0" before a <code>gh create/edit</code>, conflating a confirmed tell (exit 1) with the recall-biased Tier-3 warn (exit 3). The commit-msg hook already does the right thing (blocks on 1, prints-and-passes on 3); the title gate didn't, so a legit version like <code>NT 3.1</code> (warn) wrongly failed the gate. Fixed the wording in CLAUDE.md and the pgs-release brief: a warn is advisory — confirm the token is a real version/identifier and proceed. <strong>Lesson:</strong> when a doc encodes a tool's exit-code contract, state the <em>tiered</em> contract (1 vs 3), never a flat "exit 0".</li> <li><strong>host-lint: new Tier-3 warn for a bare leading code-as-name label (branch <code>warn-leading-code-label</code>, host-lint, uncommitted as of this entry).</strong> <code>F1 — …</code>/<code>F2 — …</code> PR-body labels are the section-5 code-as-name tell with no preceding <code>review</code>/<code>finding</code>/<code>blocker</code> noun, so the existing flag missed them (seen on MCP-Win32s PR #22). <code>check_code_label_prefix</code> warns on a one-letter+digits code as the first non-bullet token followed by a label delimiter (<code>—</code>/<code>–</code>/spaced <code>-</code>/trailing <code>:</code>). Warn not flag: the one-letter shape collides with hardware reference designators (<code>R1 — resistor</code>) you can't drop; <code>COM1</code> (three letters) is excluded for free. VOCABULARY.md updated (the source of truth).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-16--host-a-single-file-front-door-owns-the-adoption-process-call0012"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-16--host-a-single-file-front-door-owns-the-adoption-process-call0012">2026-06-16 — <code>host</code>: a single-file front door owns the adoption process (<code>call/0012</code>)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Decision.</strong> The adoption/migration/upgrade entry point is <strong>one instruction file named <code>host</code></strong> in its own repo (<code>connollydavid/host</code>): point an agent or a patient human at one URL, get an agentic host with all techniques present and upgradable. It is <strong>developed here as software</strong> (a <code>.host-software</code> worktree, like host-lint), and consumed downstream as a submodule — not a tool submodule of this forge.</li> <li><strong>Ownership split (option #1).</strong> <code>host</code> owns the <strong>process</strong> (the adopt/migrate/upgrade <em>procedure</em> — formerly the template's <code>MIGRATION.md</code> steps). The template keeps the <strong>techniques</strong> (the spine, scaffold, tool pins) <strong>and</strong> the revision-keyed <strong>upgrade ledger</strong> (<code>UPGRADING.md</code>). Upgradability stays automatic and lockstep-free because the part that churns (the ledger) lives with the techniques that generate it; <code>host</code> does not change when the template adds a ledger entry.</li> <li><strong>Wiring.</strong> Amends <code>call/0005</code> — the protocol no longer ships <em>as</em> the template's <code>MIGRATION.md</code>; that file reduces to a pointer at <code>host</code>. <code>call/0004</code> (single versioned source of the spine) intact.</li> <li><strong>Invariant reaffirmed: a tool must not reference its host.</strong> Audited — <code>host</code> references only the template (its dependency) and the <code>.host</code> stamp; host-lint / host-lifecycle / template source carry no forge reference (only gitignored Cargo <code>target/*.d</code> artifacts embed the local build path). Candidate for its own <code>call/</code> decision if elevated to governance.</li> <li><strong>Follow-on (deferred, outward-facing):</strong> materialize <code>connollydavid/host</code> + its <code>.host-software</code> stanza; move <code>MIGRATION.md</code>'s steps into <code>host</code>; reduce the template <code>MIGRATION.md</code> to a pointer. The <code>host</code> file is staged as <code>plan/0001-migration-protocol/host.draft.md</code> until then.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-16--connollydavidhost-materialized-as-software-in-the-forge"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-16--connollydavidhost-materialized-as-software-in-the-forge">2026-06-16 — <code>connollydavid/host</code> materialized as software in the forge</a></h3> <ul> <li>Created <code>https://github.com/connollydavid/host</code> (public, default branch <code>main</code>). Its single instruction file is <code>README.md</code> (so it renders when an agent or human is pointed at the repo URL) — the reviewed <code>host</code> draft content. No <code>LICENSE</code> (kept a true single file).</li> <li>Embedded in this forge as a <strong>bare store + worktree</strong> per <code>call/0010</code>, exactly like host-lint: a <code>[software "host"]</code> stanza in <code>.host-software</code> (pin <code>28d6f83</code>), gitignored <code>/host/ /host.git/ /host.*/</code>, materialized by <code>host-lifecycle software --materialize</code>. <code>software --check</code> reports both components at their pin. The staged <code>plan/0001-…/host.draft.md</code> was removed — its content now lives in the host repo.</li> <li><code>host</code> is <strong>not</strong> a skill, so no <code>.claude/skills/host</code> symlink (unlike host-lint). It is a document pointed at by URL, not an invocable tool.</li> <li>Software-first push order held: the host repo commit was pushed before the forge pin commit, so the pin is never unpushed.</li> <li>Remaining follow-on (<code>call/0012</code>): move <code>MIGRATION.md</code>'s steps into the host repo and reduce the template <code>MIGRATION.md</code> to a pointer — a template-submodule change, not done here.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-16--call0012-follow-on-done-migration-folded-into-the-host-repo"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-16--call0012-follow-on-done-migration-folded-into-the-host-repo">2026-06-16 — <code>call/0012</code> follow-on done: MIGRATION folded into the host repo</a></h3> <ul> <li>Template (<code>connollydavid/host-template</code>, PR #1, <code>0401bff → 87cb5c1</code>): <code>MIGRATION.md</code> reduced from the full 201-line protocol to a ~13-line <strong>pointer at <code>connollydavid/host</code></strong> (which now owns the adopt/migrate/upgrade process). The template keeps the techniques (the <code>CLAUDE.md</code> spine, scaffold, tool pins) and the revision-keyed <code>UPGRADING.md</code> ledger. Repointed the two sibling references: <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> ("follow the <code>host</code> repo") and the <code>UPGRADING.md</code> <code>8c28e33</code> citation (was "MIGRATION.md 'Converting an existing submodule'" → "the <code>host</code> repo").</li> <li>The host README (the approved terse-complete process) was <strong>not</strong> re-expanded — it is canonical. The detailed 6-step "Converting an existing submodule" block from the old MIGRATION.md is condensed there (and the inline gist remains in the <code>UPGRADING.md</code> entry); the verbatim long form survives only in template git history.</li> <li>Forge: bumped the template submodule pointer to <code>87cb5c1</code> and re-stamped <code>.host</code> <code>revision</code> to match. The fold touched no spine and added no ledger entry, so upgrading the forge was just the pointer bump + re-stamp (no <code>host-lifecycle upgrade</code> actions — and the built v0.4.1 binary here lacks the <code>upgrade</code> verb anyway).</li> <li>Left as-is: <code>tools/host-lifecycle/README.md</code> still says "see the template's <code>MIGRATION.md</code>" (two lines) — it resolves through the pointer, so not broken; clean up when host-lifecycle is next touched (separate repo).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-16--naming-scrub-agentic-host-reserved-for-this-repo-one-time-history-rewrite-call0013"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-16--naming-scrub-agentic-host-reserved-for-this-repo-one-time-history-rewrite-call0013">2026-06-16 — naming scrub: <code>agentic-host</code> reserved for this repo; one-time history rewrite (<code>call/0013</code>)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>The collision.</strong> <code>agentic-host</code> was both this repository's name and the generic kind. Reserved it for <strong>this repository</strong> only. A repo that adopts the methodology is now <strong>"an agentic project"</strong> (e.g. <code>agentic-acme</code>). Clean namespaces: <code>agentic-*</code> = projects (this repo + adopters), <code>host-*</code> = reusable artifacts.</li> <li><strong>Structural renames (history-scrubbed, map-only, archive-first):</strong> <code>template-agentic-host</code> → <strong><code>host-template</code></strong> (a methodology artifact, not a project — it left the <code>agentic-*</code> namespace); <code>.agentic-host</code> stamp → <strong><code>.host</code></strong>. <code>git filter-repo</code> across all four repos (<code>host</code>, <code>host-lifecycle</code>, <code>host-template</code>, <code>agentic-host</code>). Map: <code>template-agentic-host==>host-template</code>, <code>.agentic-host==>.host</code>.</li> <li><strong>First attempt was wrong:</strong> I initially renamed the template to <code>agentic-template</code> — which would have put it in the <em>adopter</em> namespace (reading as an <code>agentic-acme</code>-style project). Caught, redone from the <code>archive/pre-host-rename</code> tags with <code>host-template</code>. <strong>Lesson:</strong> the scaffold is a <code>host-*</code> artifact, never <code>agentic-*</code>.</li> <li><strong>Prose was forward-only</strong> (context-dependent, can't be a history substitution): "an agentic host" → "an agentic project" (bracketed example on first mention per doc), this repo named <code>agentic-host</code>, the word <strong>"forge" banned</strong>. Preserved legitimate "agentic" usages — <em>agentic tells</em>, <em>agentic LLM</em>, <em>agentic development</em>, <code>Agentic-MCP-Win32s</code>.</li> <li><strong>Recovery net:</strong> <code>archive/pre-host-rename</code> on every repo (verbatim pre-rewrite tip); <code>host@v0.1.0</code> and <code>host-lifecycle@v0.4.1</code> retagged onto scrubbed commits. All pre-rewrite SHAs are now invalid — re-fetch any clone.</li> <li><strong>Why a forge-call, not template doc:</strong> consumers never see this repo's <code>call/</code>. The consumer-facing vocabulary (<code>agentic-*</code>/<code>host-*</code>) lives in <code>host-template</code>'s own docs.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-17--canonical-doc-site-publisher--4-tooling-fixes-call0014-issue-6"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-17--canonical-doc-site-publisher--4-tooling-fixes-call0014-issue-6">2026-06-17 — canonical doc-site publisher + 4 tooling fixes (<code>call/0014</code>, issue #6)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Why.</strong> A complete case-(b) adoption (issue #6) showed the methodology mandates five rooms + two spec formats but shipped <strong>no canonical publisher</strong>, so every adopter hand-rolls an mdBook generator and gets the same things wrong. Four findings reproduced on <strong>this repo's own site</strong>: Where room absent, specs unrendered, non-lifecycle nav order, no coverage gate — plus this repo's <code>book.toml</code> set <code>src = "."</code> (the <code>call/0011</code> hazard) against its own template's guidance.</li> <li><strong>Fix = one maintained artifact.</strong> <code>host-lifecycle book <dir> [--check] [--dry-run]</code> (v0.6.0/0.6.1): writes <code>book.toml</code> with <code>src = "docs"</code> (never "."), generates <code>docs/</code> + <code>SUMMARY.md</code> in lifecycle order (Cast→Plan+specs→Where→Call→Reference→Memory), renders each spec as a fenced page, stubs the Where room <strong>from <code>.host-software</code></strong> (committed recipe only — safe in an un-materialized checkout), and <code>--check</code> gates room coverage. <code>book.toml</code>/<code>docs/</code> are generated, gitignored, regenerated in CI before <code>mdbook build</code>.</li> <li><strong><code>--check</code> is tolerant (v0.6.1):</strong> it gates a room only when it has <em>source</em> material but renders nothing (the regression to catch); a source-less room (fresh <code>call/</code>, no <code>.host-software</code>) is skipped. The strict v0.6.0 version would have failed a fresh adopt and the bare template.</li> <li><strong>Four tooling fixes rode along (host-lifecycle):</strong> (#6) explicit <code>worktree = <dir> <branch> <pin></code> recipe form — a parallel line materializes on its own branch at its own pin (<code>-B</code>), not the canonical pin; (#7) <code>remap</code> now scans <code>.allium</code>/<code>.tla</code>/<code>.cfg</code> so declared substitutions reach spec-internal refs (host-lint's scannable set still omits them — fixed host-lifecycle-locally, not in host-lint); (#8) <code>adopt</code> prints a post-adopt checklist (wire tool submodules, install hooks, materialize); (#9) README documents the <code>'v0.6.1^{}'</code> tag-deref pin recipe.</li> <li><strong>Placement / push order:</strong> publisher + fixes in <code>host-lifecycle</code> (v0.6.0 then v0.6.1, tagged, pushed — no CI in that repo, local <code>cargo test</code> is the gate, 23 green); reference Site workflow + canonical room-order note + <code>UPGRADING.md</code> entry (keyed <code>7ae93cd</code>, requires v0.6.1) in <code>host-template</code>, with its <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code> submodule bumped to v0.6.1 so its own Site CI builds the publisher; agentic-host dogfooded — <code>.host</code> re-stamped to template <code>79b37d5</code>, submodule bumped, <code>mdbook.yml</code> rewritten to <code>cargo install --git --rev 5738808</code> then <code>book</code>+<code>--check</code>, hand-<code>SUMMARY.md</code> and <code>book.toml</code> deleted. Live site verified: <code>where.html</code> present, nav in lifecycle order.</li> <li><strong>Correction to the 2026-06-16 entry:</strong> that entry said "the built v0.4.1 binary here lacks the <code>upgrade</code> verb." As of v0.5.0+ the binary <strong>does</strong> have <code>upgrade</code> (<code>fn upgrade</code>, dispatch arm) — <code>host-lifecycle upgrade <dir></code> lists <code>UPGRADING.md</code> actions newer than the stamp by git ancestry.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-17--pin-the-book-title-in-the-stamp-host-lifecycle-v062"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-17--pin-the-book-title-in-the-stamp-host-lifecycle-v062">2026-06-17 — pin the book title in the stamp (host-lifecycle v0.6.2)</a></h3> <ul> <li><code>host-lifecycle book</code> derived the mdBook title from the <strong>checkout directory basename</strong>, so the published title changed with the folder a repo was cloned into (locally <code>agentic-no-phase-skill</code>, in CI <code>agentic-host</code>).
