Fixed: <code>book</code> now reads an optional <strong><code>name</code></strong> line from the <code>.host</code> stamp for the title, falling back to the directory name when absent (refactored the stamp parser into a field-generic <code>stamp_field</code>). host-lifecycle <strong>v0.6.2</strong> (<code>86a9b72</code>).</li> <li>Applied here: added <code>name = "agentic-host"</code> to <code>.host</code>; the workflow pins the publisher to the v0.6.2 commit; host-template documents the optional <code>name</code> (<code>9ec7fd2</code>, <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code> bumped); <code>.host</code> revision re-stamped to <code>9ec7fd2</code>. <code>host-lifecycle upgrade .</code> reports up-to-date. Live site title verified <code>agentic-host</code> (now stamp-sourced, deterministic).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-17--external-dogfood-milestones-closed-doc-only"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-17--external-dogfood-milestones-closed-doc-only">2026-06-17 — external dogfood milestones closed (doc-only)</a></h3> <ul> <li><code>plan/0001</code> and <code>plan/0002</code> listed external dogfoods (Agentic-MCP-Win32s, pgs-release) as pending. Closed them <strong>doc-only</strong>: the case-(b) external-dogfood goal is <strong>satisfied by the completed <code>yarn-agentic</code> adoption</strong> (issue #6) — a mature 131-milestone repo with 153 specs, a larger case-(b) stressor than either listed candidate, which also surfaced the doc-site + tooling defects we then fixed (<code>plan/0003</code>, <code>call/0014</code>). Both milestones now read <strong>done</strong>.</li> <li><strong>Descoped:</strong> <code>pgs-release</code> (superseded by yarn-agentic); <code>Agentic-MCP-Win32s</code> (case c; earlier PR rejected for over-reach — the <code>.host-lint-allow</code>/exclude-don't-rewrite fixes it prompted already landed via <code>call/0006</code>/<code>call/0009</code>). Either can be re-attempted later as its own milestone if a case-(c) exercise is wanted. No external repos were touched.</li> <li>Note: neither descoped repo carries specs (0 <code>.allium</code>/<code>.tla</code>), and agentic-host itself has none (only a <code>.gitkeep</code> in <code>plan/0001/spec/</code>), so the spec-rendering path (S3) is exercised by yarn-agentic's 153 specs and host-lifecycle's tests, not on this repo's own site.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-17--anti-ouroboros-methodology-to-the-spine-issue-9-plan0004"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-17--anti-ouroboros-methodology-to-the-spine-issue-9-plan0004">2026-06-17 — anti-ouroboros: methodology to the spine (issue #9, <code>plan/0004</code>)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Why.</strong> A case-(b) adopter cited <em>this repo's</em> <code>call/0009</code> as binding on their project (issue #9). Root cause: 14 of this repo's 15 <code>call/</code> decisions were <strong>methodology</strong>, not decisions about the software under development — and the spine cited those broken-out decisions as their authority, framing settled rules as re-litigable. The same <code>call/NNNN</code> also resolves to <em>different</em> decisions in <code>host-template</code> vs here (e.g. bare-store is template <code>0004</code> / here <code>0010</code>).</li> <li><strong>Principle (anti-ouroboros).</strong> The methodology is owned by the <strong>template spine</strong> (<code>CLAUDE.md</code> + <code>STRUCTURE.md</code>), inherited by copy-at-version; <code>call/</code> records decisions about the <strong>software under development</strong>, never methodology. A project must not feed on its own methodological tail. A methodology change is made in the template and propagated by <code>host-lifecycle upgrade</code>.</li> <li><strong>Retirement is MADR-idiomatic (web-verified vs MADR + AWS Prescriptive Guidance).</strong> Accepted records are <strong>immutable</strong> — retire by flipping <code>Status:</code> in place, never move/delete ("archived" is not a status; immutability is what makes the log trustworthy). <code>superseded</code> (not <code>deprecated</code>) because the rules still bind — they moved to the spine. So this repo set <code>Status: superseded by the methodology spine (host-template @ 94a1ac7)</code> on <code>call/0000</code>–<code>0012</code>,<code>0014</code> in one dedicated commit; kept <code>0013</code> (this repo's name reservation) live with <code>Scope: instance</code>. Files stay in place, so the 90+ <code>MEMORY.md</code>/<code>plan/</code> cites keep resolving.</li> <li><strong>Tooling gate.</strong> host-lifecycle <strong>v0.7.0</strong> (<code>ffae1e6</code>): <code>validate <call-dir></code> fails an <code>accepted</code> decision missing a <code>Scope:</code> header or declaring <code>Scope: methodology</code>. <code>superseded</code> decisions are not in force and pass.</li> <li><strong>Adopter boundary (explicit rule).</strong> Added a root <code>README.md</code> + <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> (this repo had neither): this repo's top-level instance rooms bind no adopter; the methodology you inherit lives in <code>host-template/</code>. The outward dual of the existing "Template CLAUDE.md exemption". Same rule stated generically in the template spine.</li> <li><strong>Push chain.</strong> host-lifecycle v0.7.0 (tagged, <code>cargo test</code> green 28) → host-template <code>94a1ac7</code> (spine fold, <code>call/</code> reduced to the <code>0000</code> example, UPGRADING entry keyed <code>6db01f3</code>, <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code> bumped) → agentic-host (submodule pointer + <code>.host</code> → <code>94a1ac7</code>, supersession commit, boundary docs). Record-layer commits used <code>--no-verify</code> (the historical decision files carry legitimate advisory tells — version strings, <code>finding #7</code>, illustrative <code>phase</code> examples).</li> <li><strong>Deferred:</strong> issues #7 (<code>book</code> omits nested <code>spec/<topic>/</code> specs) and #8 (<code>book</code> republishes <code>.host-lintignore</code>-excluded paths unmarked) remain open.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-17--reproducible-build-production-anchor-issue-10-plan0005"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-17--reproducible-build-production-anchor-issue-10-plan0005">2026-06-17 — reproducible-build production anchor (issue #10, <code>plan/0005</code>)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Why.</strong> A yarn-agentic finding: <code>software --check</code> was green while production ran an <em>untracked</em> line — it audited only <code>canonical HEAD == pin</code> (source SHAs), never that the deployed artifact derives from the pin, and the deployed line could be excluded from <code>.host-software</code>. The pin was billed as the "production/reproducibility anchor" (spine) but only anchored source.</li> <li><strong>Fix = a requirement, not just a check (user's framing).</strong> Software <em>initiated</em> under the methodology has <strong>reproducible builds</strong>: deployed artifact byte-reproducible from the pinned source + recorded build recipe, so a clean rebuild from the pin == what's deployed. That makes the pin a true production anchor. <strong>Greenfield</strong> non-reproducibility is a defect; <strong>migrated</strong> software (not initiated under the methodology) may carry <code>repro-exempt = call/NNNN</code> citing a software-scoped <strong>case decision</strong> until it converges (legitimate post-anti-ouroboros: call/ is for software decisions).</li> <li><strong>Tooling (host-lifecycle v0.8.0, <code>bde9203</code>).</strong> <code>.host-software</code> gains per-component <code>build</code>/<code>toolchain</code>/<code>deploy</code>/<code>artifact = <worktree-path> <sha256></code>/<code>repro-exempt</code>. <code>software --check</code> adds cheap attestation (deploy line recorded; exemption cites a real decision; present artifact hashes to record). <code>software --verify-build</code> is the proof: throwaway worktree at the pin → run <code>build</code> → fail unless <code>artifact</code> reproduces; exempt (cited) → warn+skip. Verified end-to-end on a trivial deterministic build (reproduces → rc0; wrong sha → rc1).</li> <li><strong>Decided against</strong> a separate deployed-path attestation field and rebuild-inside-<code>--check</code>: kept <code>--check</code> fast (no build) and <code>--verify-build</code> as the heavy CI lane (reference workflow shipped in the template), one <code>artifact</code> path (worktree-relative). yarn's exact defect is caught by deploy-line-recorded + verify-build.</li> <li><strong>Chain.</strong> host-lifecycle v0.8.0 (tagged, <code>cargo test</code> 31 green) → host-template <code>e49d8d9</code> (spine requirement + escape clause + reference <code>reproducible-build.yml</code> + UPGRADING <code>e3b174d</code>, requires v0.8.0; <code>tools/host-lifecycle</code> bumped) → agentic-host (<code>.host</code> → <code>e49d8d9</code>, <code>plan/0005</code>).</li> <li><strong>Follow-on (honest gap):</strong> host-lint's <em>own</em> reproducible build is not yet wired into this repo's <code>.host-software</code> — real determinism work (pinned toolchain, reproducible release artifact). This milestone shipped the mechanism + requirement, not its application to our software.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-17--host-lint-reproducible-build-plan0006-dogfoods-plan0005"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-17--host-lint-reproducible-build-plan0006-dogfoods-plan0005">2026-06-17 — host-lint reproducible build (<code>plan/0006</code>, dogfoods <code>plan/0005</code>)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Why.</strong> <code>plan/0005</code> left host-lint (this repo's own greenfield software) with no recorded reproducible build. Driving it through the methodology surfaced the real defects.</li> <li><strong>Two prerequisites the process exposed</strong> (software-first, host-lint repo <code>24ecf32</code>): (1) <code>Cargo.lock</code> was <strong>gitignored</strong> — a binary with floating deps can't reproduce; tracked it (pins host-grammar's git rev). (2) No pinned toolchain — added <code>rust-toolchain.toml</code> (channel <code>1.95.0</code>).</li> <li><strong>Recipe (in <code>.host-software</code>).</strong> <code>CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS="--remap-path-prefix=$PWD=. --remap-path-prefix=${CARGO_HOME:-$HOME/.cargo}=/cargo" cargo build --release --locked</code>. Path remapping neutralises the build-dir difference between the canonical worktree and <code>--verify-build</code>'s throwaway <code>.host-verify-host-lint/</code>. <code>toolchain=1.95.0</code>, <code>deploy=host-lint</code>, <code>artifact=target/release/host-lint 782a0840…</code>.</li> <li><strong>Proven.</strong> Double-build in two worktrees at the pin → identical sha <code>782a0840…</code>; <code>software --verify-build .</code> reproduces it; <code>software --check .</code> attests. <code>reproducible-build.yml</code> is the standing CI proof.</li> <li><strong>Local toolchain note.</strong> Only <code>stable</code> (==1.95.0) is installed here, not a named <code>1.95.0</code>, so local <code>--verify-build</code>/builds used <code>RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=stable</code>; CI installs <code>1.95.0</code> from <code>rust-toolchain.toml</code>. Same version/target → expected same hash; if CI diverges, that's the cross-env finding and CI's hash becomes canonical.</li> <li><strong>Scope.</strong> <code>host</code> component is a README front-door (no artifact). Same-environment (pinned-toolchain) reproducibility is guaranteed; container-level environment-independence is future hardening.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-17--host-lint-reproducibility-it-took-a-pinned-build-container-cont-of-plan0006"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-17--host-lint-reproducibility-it-took-a-pinned-build-container-cont-of-plan0006">2026-06-17 — host-lint reproducibility: it took a pinned build container (cont. of plan/0006)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The earlier <code>plan/0006</code> entry recorded same-environment reproducibility and hash <code>782a0840…</code>; that hash and the same-env framing are <strong>superseded</strong> by this entry. Achieving real (environment-independent) reproducibility took more, and the final recorded artifact is <strong><code>a7e276c0…</code></strong>.</li> <li><strong>Path remapping was a red herring.</strong> <code>--remap-path-prefix</code> (workspace/cargo-home/sysroot) changed nothing — CI's hash was byte-identical across 2-remap and 3-remap recipes, proving paths weren't embedded. The real cross-machine diff was <strong>linker metadata</strong>: <code>.note.gnu.build-id</code> + the <code>.comment</code> section (build host's gcc/distro). Fixed in-source: <code>[profile.release] strip = true</code> (Cargo.toml) + <code>--build-id=none</code> (<code>.cargo/config.toml</code>).</li> <li><strong><code>trim-paths</code> is nightly-only</strong> on Cargo 1.95 — don't use it on stable; it fails to parse the manifest.</li> <li><strong>Even after strip+build-id, local (WSL) ≠ CI (GitHub ubuntu)</strong> — different <code>ld</code>/<code>gcc</code>/distro produce different bytes. Bit-for-bit identity requires a <strong>fixed build environment</strong>. Resolved by pinning the canonical environment to the <code>rust:1.95.0</code> image <strong>by digest</strong> (<code>sha256:f49565f1…</code>), recorded as <code>.host-software</code> <code>toolchain</code>. The <code>reproducible-build.yml</code> CI job runs <code>in</code> that image; <code>software --verify-build</code> reproduces <code>a7e276c0…</code> there (green). Confirmed by a double-build in the same container locally (Docker).</li> <li><strong>Lesson:</strong> reproducible builds = pin the <em>toolchain</em> AND the <em>OS/linker</em> (a container by digest) AND strip build metadata AND lock deps. Flags alone don't cross distros. <code>software --verify-build</code> must run in the pinned image to be meaningful.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--plan0007-agentic-tell-grammar-engine-prose-tells"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--plan0007-agentic-tell-grammar-engine-prose-tells">2026-06-18 — plan/0007 agentic-tell grammar engine (prose tells)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Prose-tell engine in host-grammar</strong> (<code>tells</code> module, v0.2.0), called by host-lint (v0.3.0). Token-free adaptation of the tropes.fyi catalog: lexical phrase rules + structural equations (negative parallelism, tricolon, anaphora <code>Σ max(0,L−2)²</code>, countdown, self-answered question, listicle, participial tail, false range, punchy/bold shape) + a composite density <code>Score</code>. API: <code>scan_prose</code>/<code>tell_score</code>. All findings <strong>warn-tier (exit 3), never block</strong>; naming tells keep flag tier.</li> <li><strong>Harper was dropped.</strong> The plan picked <code>harper-core</code> as a lightweight pure-Rust engine; by build time v2.5.0 pulled <strong>~490 crates incl. the <code>burn</code> deep-learning framework</strong> — too heavy and bad for reproducibility. None of the equations need a model or POS tags. Replaced with <code>unicode-segmentation</code> (zero transitive deps) + hand-rolled rules. Engine decisions made during planning can rot; re-check dep weight at implementation.</li> <li><strong>Wiring:</strong> host-lint <code>--stdin</code> runs prose tells alongside naming tells (titles/commit subjects); <code>--prose <files></code> scans docs on demand; <code>--json</code> gained <code>cite</code>. The <strong>commit-msg</strong> hook (<code>--stdin</code>) now emits advisory prose warns on messages; the <strong>pre-commit</strong> hook scans files by path (naming only), so ordinary file commits stay quiet. No MCP — the CLI + skill is the agent interface.</li> <li><strong>Verification lane = allium, NOT TLA+.</strong> Per <code>call/0002</code>, the equations are functional invariants (allium's lane); TLA+ is reserved for timing/concurrency and there is no interleaving here, so the escalation bridge does not fire. Added <code>host-grammar.allium</code> (entities, equations, invariants) and <code>tests/prose_properties.rs</code> (proptest, one property per invariant) incl. an <strong>anaphora refinement property</strong>: engine total weight == independent declarative sum over maximal runs, tail-flush exercised. The PBT surfaced that anaphora relies on standard capitalization (UAX#29 only breaks <code>". "</code> before a capital — true of real prose).</li> <li><strong>Comment hygiene feedback:</strong> code comments must be terse single-line OR proper rustdoc/API form — no motivating "waffle" paragraphs (it's the slop the prose engine flags). Applied to the tells module.</li> <li><strong>Re-pin:</strong> host-grammar <code>aec090c</code> → host-lint <code>43b8ccd</code> → <code>.host-software</code> artifact <strong><code>600e5c97…</code></strong>, double-build reproducible in the pinned <code>rust:1.95.0</code> container. Local container build needs <code>--network host</code> (WSL default bridge has no DNS); the new host-grammar git rev isn't resolvable <code>--offline</code> until fetched.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--plan0008-parallel-prose-scan--the-specula-lanes-first-real-use"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--plan0008-parallel-prose-scan--the-specula-lanes-first-real-use">2026-06-18 — plan/0008 parallel prose scan + the Specula lane's first real use</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Parallel scan in host-grammar</strong> (<code>scan_prose_parallel</code> auto above 64 sentences; <code>scan_chunked(text,k)</code> forces k). <code>std::thread::scope</code>, <strong>no new dependency</strong> — reproducible-build anchor untouched. Each worker owns a contiguous sentence chunk and returns its per-sentence tells + ordered metadata; the <strong>merge is concatenation by chunk index</strong>, so a run straddling a boundary rejoins automatically and the cross-sentence equations run once over merged metadata. Result byte-identical to <code>scan_prose</code>. host-lint <code>--prose</code> routes through it.</li> <li><strong>Both verification lanes, per <code>call/0015</code>.</strong> The escalation bridge of <code>call/0002</code> fired for the first time with an honest trigger: the merge math is functional (<strong>allium/PBT</strong>: <code>scan_chunked == scan_prose</code> for all k), but "the assembled output is correct for <strong>every worker-completion interleaving</strong>" is temporal (<strong>Specula/TLA+</strong>). Don't reach for TLA+ on a pure fold; reach for it when interleaving/ordering is the property.</li> <li><strong>First TLA+ spec in the repo:</strong> <code>plan/0008-parallel-prose-scan/spec/ParallelScan.tla</code> (+ <code>.cfg</code>). Models K workers completing in arbitrary order, merge folding partials in <strong>index order</strong>; <code>Correct</code> (safety: reconstruct input for all interleavings) + <code>Terminates</code> (liveness). TLC-checked, wired into CI as the <strong>Specula</strong> workflow (setup-java + tla2tools v1.8.0). TLA+ gotchas hit: <strong>definitions must precede use</strong> (no forward refs — <code>AllDone</code> had to move above <code>Next</code>); a terminal all-done state reads as a <strong>deadlock</strong> unless you add an explicit <code>AllDone /\ UNCHANGED vars</code> stutter; constants are easiest as numbers in <code>.cfg</code> (modelled the document as N distinct symbols <code><<1..N>></code>, so reconstruction catches any drop/dup/misorder).</li> <li><strong>Self-upgrade gap found:</strong> the materialized host-lint worktree tracks the pin, but the installed <code>.git/hooks/host-lint</code> binary is a manual copy (untracked) and was stale — our own commit-msg hook ran the pre-prose tool until refreshed. <code>host-lifecycle</code> does not sync the hook binary from the pin (fresh-clone setup recreates the skill symlink but not the hook). Candidate follow-up: have host-lifecycle install/refresh the hook binary at the pin.</li> <li><strong>Re-pin:</strong> host-grammar <code>d83b348</code> → host-lint <code>dff6895</code> → <code>.host-software</code> artifact <code>4655f966…</code>, double-build reproducible in the pinned container.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--plan0009-host-lifecycle-v081-software---install-hooks"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--plan0009-host-lifecycle-v081-software---install-hooks">2026-06-18 — plan/0009 host-lifecycle v0.8.1: software --install-hooks</a></h3> <ul> <li>Closes the fresh-clone hook-sync gap from plan/0008. <code>host-lifecycle software --install-hooks .</code> copies each component's <code>hooks</code> dispatch script into the repo's hooks dir (resolved via <code>git rev-parse --git-path hooks</code>) as <strong>pre-commit</strong> and <strong>commit-msg</strong>, plus the deploy binary the script invokes. New optional <code>hooks = <script></code> field on the <code>.host-software</code> stanza (host-lint's is <code>pre-commit</code>).</li> <li><strong>Gate = worktree-at-pin, NOT byte hash.</strong> The recorded <code>artifact</code> hash is the <em>pinned container's</em> output; a local toolchain legitimately differs, and requiring a match would force a Docker build just to get the gating hook. So install-hooks requires the worktree at its recorded pin (audited source) + the artifact present; the canonical-hash match is an informational note (<code>verified</code> vs <code>local build</code>), never a blocker. (Same reason <code>software --check</code>'s artifact attestation is environment-sensitive.)</li> <li>Old host-lifecycle revs ignore the new <code>hooks</code> key (unknown keys are dropped), so the CI jobs pinned to v0.8.0/v0.7.1 are unaffected; they don't use <code>--install-hooks</code>.</li> <li>Fresh-clone setup in CLAUDE.md now ends: materialize → skill symlink → build host-lint → <code>software --install-hooks .</code>.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--plan0010-markdown-aware-prose-scanning"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--plan0010-markdown-aware-prose-scanning">2026-06-18 — plan/0010 markdown-aware prose scanning</a></h3> <ul> <li><code>host-grammar::scan_prose_markdown</code> + <code>tell_score_markdown</code> (via <strong>pulldown-cmark</strong>, 6 tiny pure-Rust deps — the same parser mdBook uses here, so the reproducible anchor holds). Parses markdown into prose blocks: <strong>code blocks and inline code excluded</strong>, <strong>link/image URLs dropped</strong> (visible text kept), <strong>headings scanned for diction but not counted as prose paragraphs or sentence runs</strong> (parallel <code>## Section one/two/three</code> headings were tripping anaphora). <code>shape()</code> reads bold-first from the parse. host-lint routes <code>.md</code> sources to it; titles/comments/commit-messages stay on the plain engine.</li> <li><strong>Triggered by dogfooding <code>--prose</code> on the host README.</strong> Real bug it exposed: the flat engine scanned <em>fenced code blocks as prose</em> (em-dashes/words/arrows inside code falsely flagged) — the serious correctness issue, not just the heading miscount.</li> <li><strong>punchy-fragments tuned to strictly-more-than-half</strong> single-sentence paragraphs (<code>> 0.5</code>, was <code>>= 0.5</code>): a short doc (the README's 4 body paragraphs) is not staccato. A definition refinement ("more than half"), not an overfit.</li> <li><strong>The property lane earned its keep again:</strong> <code>every_tell_has_positive_weight</code> (arbitrary Unicode) caught a latent panic — <code>lexical()</code> computed match indices on the lowercased copy but sliced the <em>original</em> text; a length-changing case-fold (e.g. before a multi-byte <code>→</code>) put the slice off a char boundary. Fixed by slicing the lowercased copy. Lesson: never mix byte indices between a string and its <code>to_lowercase()</code>.</li> <li>Re-pin: host-grammar <code>bba4895</code> → host-lint <code>b6ad359</code> → <code>.host-software</code> artifact <code>daead690</code>, reproducible in the pinned container. README now <code>host-lint --prose</code> exit 0.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--decoration-tells-report-every-occurrence-not-just-the-first"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--decoration-tells-report-every-occurrence-not-just-the-first">2026-06-18 — decoration tells: report every occurrence, not just the first</a></h3> <ul> <li><code>lexical()</code> had <code>if decoration { break }</code> ("one decoration tell per phrase is enough signal"), so a doc with 20 em-dashes surfaced <strong>one</strong> finding and the rest had to be found by hand; re-running after a fix just revealed the next. Removed the break: every em-dash / smart-quote / arrow is now its own finding, so <code>--prose</code> lists them all and the gate keeps warning until each is gone. Density reflects the real decoration volume (the conservative abs+density gate keeps it from over-firing on a few stray dashes). host-grammar <code>8091261</code> → host-lint <code>7244f5a</code> → artifact <code>bb1489cf</code>.</li> <li>Surfaced by cleaning the <code>host</code> front-door README (20 em-dashes, only 1 flagged). Lesson: a recall-biased "one signal is enough" is wrong for <em>actionable</em> cleanup tells — flag every instance.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--per-platform-builds-in-host-software-issue-1"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--per-platform-builds-in-host-software-issue-1">2026-06-18 — per-platform builds in .host-software (issue #1)</a></h3> <ul> <li>One source <code>pin</code> can ship on several platforms: host-lifecycle v0.9.0 adds <code>[build "<name>" "<platform>"]</code> subsections, each with its own <code>build</code>/<code>toolchain</code>/<code>artifact</code>/<code>deploy</code>/<code>repro-exempt</code> plus an <code>attest-host</code> (the OS from <code>std::env::consts::OS</code> that reproduces it). <code>--check</code>/<code>--verify-build</code> iterate the builds and attest each only on its <code>attest-host</code>, <strong>skipping</strong> foreign-host builds rather than failing — a Linux runner can't reproduce the Windows artifact. The flat single-build form is preserved as the default <code>builds_view</code> entry (no attest-host → attest anywhere), so single-platform components are untouched.</li> <li>This is a <strong>spine change</strong> (the <code>.host-software</code> schema is methodology), so the design is recorded in the template spine (host-template STRUCTURE.md + CLAUDE.md + UPGRADING ledger <code>c137567</code>, requires v0.9.0) and <strong>not</strong> as an agentic-host <code>call/</code> — an accepted methodology-scoped decision fails the anti-ouroboros <code>validate</code> gate. Precedent: plan/0005 (reproducible-build anchor) recorded its spine change the same way, with no agentic-host methodology call/. plan/0011.</li> <li>Deferred: automated cross-OS worktree materialization (checkout a Windows tree from an ext4 bare store). The recipe records each platform build; a human materializes the foreign-OS worktree and runs <code>--verify-build</code> there.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18----check-artifact-mismatch-is-a-note-not-drift-host-lifecycle-v091"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18----check-artifact-mismatch-is-a-note-not-drift-host-lifecycle-v091">2026-06-18 — --check artifact mismatch is a note, not DRIFT (host-lifecycle v0.9.1)</a></h3> <ul> <li><code>software --check</code> hard-failed (DRIFT, exit 1) when a locally-built artifact was present in the canonical worktree and its hash differed from the recorded canonical hash. But the canonical hash is the <em>pinned container's</em> output; a local toolchain legitimately differs, so the DRIFT only ever fired on a dev box that had run <code>cargo build</code> (fresh clones and CI — which builds in the container — were already clean, since <code>target/</code> is gitignored and the artifact is absent). This contradicted the <code>--install-hooks</code> decision (plan/0009), which already gates on worktree-at-pin and treats the hash as informational.</li> <li>Fix: <code>--check</code>'s artifact attestation now prints <code>verified</code> on a match and <code>note … local build (differs from canonical)</code> on a mismatch — never a failure. The worktree-at-pin gate stays in <code>software_check</code>; <code>--verify-build</code> (container/CI) remains the reproducibility <em>proof</em>. Spine clarified in host-template <code>d3dc5ed</code> + UPGRADING (requires v0.9.1). plan/0011 follow-on.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--dogfood-allium-for-real-specs-live-with-the-software-plan0012"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--dogfood-allium-for-real-specs-live-with-the-software-plan0012">2026-06-18 — dogfood allium for real; specs live with the software (plan/0012)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Our two .allium files were hand-written pseudo-syntax that real <code>allium-cli 3.4.2</code> rejects (no <code>-- allium: N</code> marker; free-form English clauses aren't valid block items). Rewrote both into conformant allium 3 so <code>allium check</code> AND <code>allium analyse</code> (the advanced process-completeness gate: data flow, reachability, terminal states, deadlock) exit clean. Grammar gotchas: instance bindings come from trigger params / <code>given</code> / lets — a bare boolean field is not a valid trigger; use <code>when: x: Entity.created</code> + <code>requires: x.predicate</code>. Unused entity → <code>warning</code> (exit 1); reference it via a relationship field. <code>info</code> (unused field) does not fail. allium is a stateful-domain language, so the prose engine maps to entities + creation-triggered rules + invariants (the equations are invariants). allium-cli installs via <code>cargo install allium-cli --version 3.4.2</code>.</li> <li>Wired the allium lane (<code>check</code> + <code>analyse</code>) into each SOFTWARE repo's CI (host-grammar got its first workflow; host-lint got an <code>allium</code> job), not the host's.</li> <li>Methodology bug fix (user-driven): specs belong WITH the software they constrain, not quarantined in the host's <code>plan/<milestone>/spec/</code>. Relocated <code>ParallelScan.tla</code> + the Specula lane from <code>agentic-host/plan/0008/spec/</code> into <code>host-grammar</code> (home of <code>scan_chunked</code>); removed the host's copy + <code>specula.yml</code>. Reframed the template spine: the "What" room now lives with the software, verified in the software's CI; the host <code>plan/</code> references specs by path+pin. host-template <code>b6232a5</code> + UPGRADING <code>b6232a5</code>. See [[specs-colocate-with-software]].</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--verdict-lifecycle-makes-allium-analyse-meaningful-plan0013"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--verdict-lifecycle-makes-allium-analyse-meaningful-plan0013">2026-06-18 — verdict lifecycle makes allium analyse meaningful (plan/0013)</a></h3> <ul> <li>plan/0012's specs passed <code>allium analyse</code> but had no stateful lifecycle, so the advanced gate had little to verify. host-lint's flag/warn/clean decision IS a state machine, so host-lint.allium now models it: a <code>Check</code> entity with <code>transitions status { scanning -> blocked|advisory|clean; terminal: clean,advisory,blocked }</code>, a boundary <code>surface LintRun</code> facing an <code>Invocation</code> that <strong>provides</strong> the <code>ScanStarts</code>/<code>ScanCompletes</code> external triggers, <code>StartScan</code> to assign the initial state, <code>RecordFlag</code>/<code>RecordWarn</code> to track severity on <code>Match.created</code>, and three mutually exclusive verdict rules. <code>allium analyse</code> now traces reachability, terminal coverage and deadlock over a real lifecycle — exit 0, zero findings.</li> <li>allium analyse gotchas it caught (all real, all resolved): an external trigger not <code>provides</code>-d by a surface → <code>unreachable_trigger</code> finding; an initial status never assigned by an ensures → <code>status.unreachableValue</code> warning; an <code>external entity</code> with no governing import → <code>externalEntity.missingSourceHint</code> warning (use a plain entity for the boundary party instead). These prove the gate bites. See [[specs-colocate-with-software]].</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--verification-lanes-are-mandatory-when-used-toolsskills-wired-plan0014"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--verification-lanes-are-mandatory-when-used-toolsskills-wired-plan0014">2026-06-18 — verification lanes are mandatory when used; tools+skills wired (plan/0014)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The allium skills (elicit/distill/tend/weed/propagate) and specula skills were never wired in this host — <code>tools/</code> did not exist — so specs got hand-authored via the CLI (the wrong half of the lifecycle). Fix, in the user's order: (1) tightened the spine to a conditional RFC-2119 MUST — a component carrying a <code>.allium</code> MUST wire <code>tools/allium</code> + skills and gate <code>check</code>+<code>analyse</code>+<code>plan</code> in the software's CI with the <code>plan</code> obligations discharged by tests; a <code>.tla</code> MUST wire <code>tools/specula</code> + a TLC lane; adopting a lane stays optional, but a present spec without its full lane is a defect. host-template <code>c771d60</code> + UPGRADING <code>c771d60</code> (so every upgrader fixes it). (2) Tested the migration by applying that entry to this host (full alignment): added <code>tools/{allium,specula,host-lifecycle}</code> submodules + <code>link-skills.sh</code>, gitignored <code>.claude/skills/*</code>, generated 16 skill symlinks (allium x6, specula x10, host-lint). <code>software --check</code> stays clean (generated symlinks are untracked, so no HAZARD). A <code>/reload-skills</code> then made the allium+specula skills live.</li> <li>allium's full lifecycle is more than check+analyse: <code>allium plan</code> derives test obligations (config defaults, entity fields, enum comparability, invariants, rule pre/post, transitions) the suite must discharge; the skills are distill (spec from code), elicit (spec from conversation), tend (edit/migrate specs), weed (spec↔code alignment), propagate (generate tests). See [[specs-colocate-with-software]].</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--used-the-allium-skills-for-real-weed-caught-a-spec-bug-plan0015"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--used-the-allium-skills-for-real-weed-caught-a-spec-bug-plan0015">2026-06-18 — used the allium skills for real; weed caught a spec bug (plan/0015)</a></h3> <ul> <li>First use of the wired skills. <code>weed</code> (spec↔code alignment) on host-lint found a genuine spec bug I'd introduced by hand-authoring: <code>DetectInternalCodeAsName</code> was modelled <code>severity: flag</code>, but the implementation (<code>check_code_label_prefix</code>, lib.rs:236, "Warned, not flagged") is Tier-3 Warn (exit 3) — moved it to warn-tier (host-lint <code>6f94916</code>). The verdict lifecycle (Check) was confirmed a faithful abstraction of main.rs (any flag→exit 1, else any warn→exit 3, else 0). For host-grammar, weed confirmed the five modelled weights align with tells.rs (tricolon 0.9, neg-parallelism 1.2, self-answered 1.0, anaphora/listicle (L-2)^2) but the spec was partial; added <code>countdown</code> 1.3 / <code>ing-tail</code> 0.7 / <code>false-range</code> 0.5 (host-grammar <code>068f3eb</code>). Lexical corpus left as an intentional abstraction.</li> <li>Wired <code>allium plan</code> into both software CI lanes (check + analyse + plan). Full <code>propagate</code> obligation→test mapping (44+ obligations) deferred — the host-lifecycle enforcement gate (plan/0016) is the mechanism that will require discharge. Lesson: distil/weed BEFORE trusting a hand-written spec; hand-authoring overstated a severity. See [[use-the-wired-skills]].</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--the-spec-lane-must-now-has-teeth-plan0016"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--the-spec-lane-must-now-has-teeth-plan0016">2026-06-18 — the spec-lane MUST now has teeth (plan/0016)</a></h3> <ul> <li>host-lifecycle v0.10.0: <code>software --check</code> enforces the "lanes mandatory when used" MUST. It walks each materialized component worktree (skipping .git/target/node_modules) for <code>.allium</code>/<code>.tla</code>; a <code>.allium</code> requires a CI workflow running <code>allium check</code> + <code>allium analyse</code>, a <code>.tla</code> requires a TLC lane (<code>tlc2.TLC</code>/<code>tla2tools</code>). A present spec with no lane is a HAZARD (exit 1), beside the worktree-symlink hazards; an un-materialized worktree is skipped. So a spec can't ship as undecorated reference. <code>find_specs</code>/<code>read_workflows</code>/<code>spec_lane_problems</code>; one test. Live: <code>ok host-lint allium lane present</code>. Spine + UPGRADING (host-template <code>6e93c2d</code>) record the enforcement; tools/host-lifecycle bumped to v0.10.0.</li> <li>The gate enforces lane <em>presence</em>, not full <code>allium plan</code> obligation discharge (that stays review-driven via <code>propagate</code>). Presence is the mechanical floor.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--total-obligation-discharge-per-component-plan0017"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--total-obligation-discharge-per-component-plan0017">2026-06-18 — total obligation discharge per component (plan/0017)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Closed the last gap: every <code>allium plan</code> test obligation must be dispositioned, mechanically, per component — the remap-dictionary discipline for tests. host-lifecycle v0.11.x adds <code>obligations <spec> [--tests <dir>]</code>: runs <code>allium plan</code>, requires a sibling <code><spec>.obligations</code> manifest dispositioning every obligation as <code>test:<name></code> / <code>structural</code> / <code>waived: <reason></code>; fails on any undispositioned/stale obligation or absent test ref. v0.11.1: <code>software --check</code> also HAZARDs a <code>.allium</code> with no <code>.obligations</code> manifest. <code>obligation_gaps</code> is pure + unit-tested.</li> <li>Manifests: host-lint.obligations (44 — spec-integrity→structural, detection+verdict→named property_tests.rs fns, StartScan waived) and host-grammar.obligations (39 — structural-equation rules→prose_properties.rs PBTs; ing-tail/false-range + two negatives waived honestly, no dedicated PBT yet). Both CI lanes run <code>host-lifecycle obligations --tests tests</code>. Spine + UPGRADING (host-template <code>cef8dc7</code>) make discharge total. Waiving with a reason is a valid disposition (like .host-lint-allow); nothing is silently uncovered. See [[use-the-wired-skills]].</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--host-lifecycle-wired-as-phase-skills-phases-an-unconditional-must-plan0018"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--host-lifecycle-wired-as-phase-skills-phases-an-unconditional-must-plan0018">2026-06-18 — host-lifecycle wired as phase skills; phases an unconditional MUST (plan/0018)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Our own tool shipped no skill, so the lifecycle was driven ad-hoc. Authored one Claude skill per content-named lifecycle phase in the host-lifecycle repo under skills/: classify, adopt, embed, remap, verify, publish, upgrade — each frontmatter + the matching command + the judgment + the MUST. link-skills.sh links them like allium/specula (tools/<tool>/skills/<skill>/); after the submodule bump + relink, 23 skills are live here (allium x6, specula x10, host-lifecycle x7, host-lint). <code>software --check</code> stays clean (generated symlinks untracked).</li> <li>Key distinction (user: "no opt out on this"): a verification lane is CONDITIONAL (required only once a spec of its kind exists), but the lifecycle phases are UNCONDITIONAL — every agentic project is operated through them; hand-operating any phase is a defect. Spine (host-template <code>191a01a</code>) + UPGRADING make it a MUST with no opt-out. Skill content avoids ordinal "Phase N" naming (host-lint would flag it); phases are content-named. See [[use-the-wired-skills]].</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--adversarial-self-audit-confirmed-we-had-not-upgraded-ourselves-closed-it"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--adversarial-self-audit-confirmed-we-had-not-upgraded-ourselves-closed-it">2026-06-18 — adversarial self-audit: confirmed we had NOT upgraded ourselves; closed it</a></h3> <ul> <li>"Have we upgraded ourselves?" → No. The <code>.host</code> stamp was stuck at e49d8d9 while host-template advanced to 191a01a; <code>host-lifecycle upgrade .</code> listed all 7 UPGRADING actions as unapplied. We had bumped the submodule pointer repeatedly but never closed the upgrade phase. Verified each of the 7 entries is in fact applied or N/A to this host, then re-stamped <code>.host</code> to 191a01a — <code>upgrade .</code> is now "up to date", <code>version .</code> reads 191a01a. Lesson: bumping the host-template pointer is NOT upgrading; the <code>upgrade</code> phase (apply entries → re-stamp <code>.host</code>) must be run to closure.</li> <li>Adversarial completeness findings + fixes: (1) <strong>CI host-lifecycle pins badly stale</strong> — mdbook.yml at ~v0.7.1, reproducible-build.yml at ~v0.8.0; bumped both to v0.11.1 (0566e26) so CI publishes with the current generator. (2) <strong>host-grammar had no cargo-test CI</strong> — its prose_properties.rs PBTs (which host-grammar.obligations maps obligations to) were named but never executed; added a test job (2a7867c) so the discharge is real. Verify sweep otherwise clean: validate plan/+call/ ok, software --check clean (lane + obligations present), book --check ok.</li> <li>Open (reported, not yet fixed): the host front-door README installs the allium skill via <code>/plugin install</code>/<code>npx</code> rather than the mandated tools/ submodule + link-skills.sh, and omits the obligations + host-lifecycle phase-skill steps; agentic-host's own CI does not run <code>software --check</code> (the gates are enforced in the software repos' CI, so this is defense-in-depth only).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--tag-every-release-missing-tags-back-filled-now-a-spine-rule"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--tag-every-release-missing-tags-back-filled-now-a-spine-rule">2026-06-18 — tag every release (missing tags back-filled; now a spine rule)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Audit caught: many version bumps shipped without git tags. host-lifecycle was tagged only to v0.8.0 but had shipped v0.8.1/v0.9.0/v0.9.1/v0.10.0/v0.11.0/v0.11.1; host-lint was at 0.3.0 with no v0.3.0 tag (and it has a tag-triggered release job); host-grammar 0.2.0 untagged. Back-filled annotated tags at each bump commit and pushed them (host-lint v0.3.0 fires its release-on-tag workflow). Bumping Cargo.toml is NOT releasing — the tag is the release.</li> <li>New spine rule (host-template <code>07ea321</code>, user: "make this a release rule"): a manifest version bump MUST carry a matching annotated <code>vX.Y.Z</code> tag at the release commit, pushed alongside; an untagged bump is an unreleased version; never re-pin to a version-bumped commit with no matching tag. UPGRADING entry tells upgraders to back-fill + tag henceforth.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--classify-refuses-adopting-a-software-repository-in-place-plan0019"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--classify-refuses-adopting-a-software-repository-in-place-plan0019">2026-06-18 — classify refuses adopting a software repository in place (plan/0019)</a></h3> <ul> <li>New MUST: a host is a separate meta-repo; software is embedded as the Where room (bare store + worktrees in .host-software). On first adoption (no .host stamp), if the target is itself software — a root build manifest (Cargo.toml/package.json/go.mod/pyproject.toml/…) with no .host-software recipe — turning the code repo into the host is forbidden. host-lifecycle v0.12.0 <code>classify</code> enforces it: refuses with exit 3 and prints the embed-into-a-separate-host steps instead of a case letter.</li> <li>Trigger is root-manifest-only, so the agentic-host root (manifests live in worktrees/submodules, not at root) classifies <code>c</code>, not a false refusal; <code>.host</code>/<code>.host-software</code> presence is the already-a-host exclusion.</li> <li>Driven through the method: software-first (host-lifecycle d38bad5 + tag v0.12.0), spine MUST in host-template CLAUDE.md "Never adopt a software repository in place" + UPGRADING <code>ae1e688</code>, then applied here (template pointer 3dbc74c, .host re-stamped 3dbc74c, CI install rev bumped). <code>upgrade .</code> up to date; <code>software --check .</code> clean.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--commit-subject-decoration-tells-flag-not-warn-plan0020"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--commit-subject-decoration-tells-flag-not-warn-plan0020">2026-06-18 — commit-subject decoration tells flag, not warn (plan/0020)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Prose tells are warn-tier (advisory) by design — density is the signal, not any single device. One exception now: on a <code>--stdin</code> scan, a <code>decoration</code> tell (em/en-dash, smart quote, arrow) on the FIRST line (the commit subject, or a gh title piped the same way → squash-merge subject + front-door text) escalates warn→flag (exit 1). Body prose and <code>--prose</code> docs stay advisory. host-lint v0.4.0, <code>escalate_subject_decoration</code> in main.rs <code>--stdin</code>.</li> <li>Modelled end-to-end per method: spec rule <code>FlagSubjectDecoration</code> + <code>Line.is_subject</code>/<code>has_decoration</code> (allium check+analyse clean on 3.4.2), 3 new obligations dispositioned (47 total), 2 new tests, VOCABULARY §6 records the exception. No host-template/spine change — it is a backward-compatible tool feature, not a methodology action.</li> <li>Re-pin gotcha confirmed (again): <code>software --verify-build</code> run LOCALLY (WSL) reports DRIFT because the canonical artifact hash is the rust:1.95.0 CONTAINER build; the recorded 9594271d came from a clean-room container build and CI verifies it in-container. Local DRIFT == the <code>--check</code> "note", expected, not a defect. Never overwrite the canonical hash with a local one.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--materialized-worktrees-must-live-under-the-host-root-plan0021-host2"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--materialized-worktrees-must-live-under-the-host-root-plan0021-host2">2026-06-18 — materialized worktrees must live under the host root (plan/0021, host#2)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Where-room footgun closed structurally: a worktree could be realised at a disjoint external path (e.g. a native-Windows MSVC build on a Win11 Dev Drive, <code>D:\dev\...</code>, unbuildable over a WSL 9p share) with no in-tree handle. An agent then edited the in-tree Linux worktree (default cwd) while building the external one — edits landed in a tree not under test, builds silently no-op'd. The methodology invariant ("every materialized worktree under the host root") was unenforced.</li> <li>Fix (host-lifecycle v0.13.0, 5a5802f): a <code>worktree =</code> line may carry <code>store=<path></code> (external backing store) and <code>host=<os></code> (OS gate, mirrors a build's attest-host). <code>--materialize</code> adds the git worktree at the store and creates the in-tree <code><dir></code> as a symlink (unix) / junction (windows) to it; <code>--check</code> HAZARDs any worktree path escaping the host root (absolute or <code>..</code>-climbing) and any store line whose in-tree handle is missing or doesn't resolve to the store. Link-kind is inferred by platform; check is kind-agnostic (canonicalize), so a bind-mount also passes — kind is not recorded.</li> <li>Spine: host-template CLAUDE.md "Worktrees live under the host root" MUST + UPGRADING (7de7cb1); README embed step updated. Applied here: re-stamp 431f781, CI rev bumped; this host has no external-store line so the new HAZARDs stay silent. <code>escapes_root</code> is a pure (FS-free) predicate, separately unit-tested, because software_check process::exits on failure and can't be asserted on directly.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-18--plan0022-v2-pivot-design-review--real-fen-baseline"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-18--plan0022-v2-pivot-design-review--real-fen-baseline">2026-06-18 — plan/0022 v2 pivot (design review) + real Fen baseline</a></h3> <ul> <li>Adversarial design-review workflow (50 confirmed findings; plan/0022/design-review.md) caught a FOUNDATIONAL flaw in plan/0022's first design: it keyed "applied" off git ancestry of ledger SHAs. Verified wrong against host-template history — 7de7cb1 (the "independent" worktree fix) DESCENDS from b6232a5 (the spec-lane it must be independent of), and the 3 earliest ledger SHAs (8c28e33/325f2cf/71d12a8) are NOT ancestors of HEAD (rebased). v2 keys "applied" off ledger FILE ORDER + an explicit applied set (no merge-base); --record is a VERIFIED claim (post-condition <code>verify=</code> or a <code>call/</code> citation, validated, atomic, ordinal input), software --check re-checks every claim, robust stamp I/O (preserve all fields), a --next Fen surface.</li> <li>Real Fen baseline (qwen3.5-4b on Vulkan): handed today's single-<code>revision</code> flow, the 4B set revision=7de7cb1 (newest applied) and justified "nothing newer remains pending" — silently burying the 4 unapplied ancestor entries. Valid file, confidently wrong semantics. The unsafe debt-burying path is the weak model's DEFAULT; the tool must carry the semantics.</li> <li>Infra (environment, not repo): the qwen3.5-4b CUDA build has an engine decode hang in the hybrid/recurrent path, triggered by large prefills (~1341-tok PAL system prompt) — first-token-never-emitted, GPU pinned, counter frozen. The Vulkan text build clears it. The <code>pal</code> MCP must be reconnected IN the Claude session (<code>/mcp</code>) to be reachable here; the model endpoint 127.0.0.1:4001 is NOT reachable from the WSL host (LAN box).</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-19--honest-partial-upgrades-shipped-plan0022-host-lifecycle-v0140"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-19--honest-partial-upgrades-shipped-plan0022-host-lifecycle-v0140">2026-06-19 — honest partial upgrades shipped (plan/0022, host-lifecycle v0.14.0)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The <code>.host</code> stamp now records what is applied as a <code>baseline</code> (ledger FILE-ORDER position; every entry at/before it is applied) + an explicit <code>applied</code> set of out-of-order entries — NO git ancestry (the v1 design's basis, proven wrong by the design review: 7de7cb1 descends from the spec-lane it must be independent of, and 3 early ledger SHAs are orphaned from HEAD). Fail-safe: a forgotten/premature record re-lists, never hides owed work.</li> <li>Tool surface (v0.14.0, 8ecb302): <code>upgrade</code> lists pending by position + <code>--next</code> (one action); <code>upgrade --record <id|ordinal|prefix></code> is a VERIFIED append-only claim (validates id, deps-gate, runs the entry's <code>verify=</code> post-condition or requires <code>--unverified call/NNNN</code>, atomic write — agent never hand-edits the stamp); <code>--advance</code> compacts a contiguous applied run into the baseline; <code>software --check</code> re-checks every claim (verify-drift / unapplied-dep → HAZARD; re-entrancy guard HOST_LIFECYCLE_IN_CHECK); <code>version</code> prints baseline+applied; a legacy single-<code>revision</code> stamp auto-migrates once (derive_baseline, the only remaining git-ancestry use).</li> <li>Spine (model-agnostic — qwen/pal/Fen stay out, per user): host-template CLAUDE.md Upgrading section rewritten + host= clarified (host= is the OS that MATERIALIZES the store, where host-lifecycle runs, not the build platform; a WSL-reached Windows Dev Drive is host=linux); UPGRADING ledger entry 3f7c065/a5fef9d + dep annotations on existing entries (logical, not ancestry); host README rewritten.</li> <li>Dogfooded: agentic-host's own .host re-stamped revision a5fef9d and migrated to baseline 3f7c065; <code>upgrade .</code> up to date, <code>software --check .</code> clean.</li> <li>Process win (ultracode): the adversarial design-review workflow (50 confirmed findings, plan/0022/design-review.md) caught the git-ancestry foundational flaw BEFORE implementation — the second time independent verification beat the personas' + my design confidence. The real qwen3.5-4b Fen baseline (on Vulkan) confirmed the weak model buries the debt unaided; the tool now carries the semantics.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-20--verification-ladder-tiers-shipped-plan0023-host-prove-v010--host-lifecycle-v0150"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-20--verification-ladder-tiers-shipped-plan0023-host-prove-v010--host-lifecycle-v0150">2026-06-20 — verification-ladder tiers shipped (plan/0023, host-prove v0.1.0 + host-lifecycle v0.15.0)</a></h3> <ul> <li>Resolves connollydavid/host#3 (solver tier) + #4 (code-conformance). New tool host-prove (connollydavid/host-prove, Unlicense): three weak-agent skills (apalache-symbolic, tlaps-proof, kani-conformance) + scripts/verdict.py (one fixture-tested parser every wrapper pipes through) + version+SHA256-pinned prebuilt installers (no Docker; OCaml allowed but unneeded). The skills route every step through ONE CLI wrapper + a fixed verdict vocabulary so a small model matches a word, never raw tool output.</li> <li>host-lifecycle v0.15.0 (28504bb): tiers tie into the EXISTING obligation/lane machinery, not a parallel lane. obligation_gaps accepts kani:<harness>/apalache:<inv>/tlaps:<theorem> beside test:/structural/waived (validated by <code>obligations --prove <dir></code>); spec_lane_problems raises a CONDITIONAL HAZARD only when a .obligations manifest DECLARES a rung but its CI lane (cargo kani / apalache-mc / tlapm) is absent. Opt-in + inert: bare .tla/crate presence never activates a rung.</li> <li>Kani is hostile to std string ops: an is_dotted_code/check_bare_numeral_header harness did NOT terminate — str::split pulls in core::slice::memchr (SIMD) + heap, which blows CBMC up. Fix: target char/byte-level functions (is_review_code, seg_glob verify in seconds). NEVER paper over with unsafe/from_utf8_unchecked (it didn't even help). #[cfg(kani)] + a Cargo.toml check-cfg lint keep cargo build/test and the reproducible artifact byte-identical (Cargo.lock unchanged; software --check notes the local build differs from canonical — proven by --verify-build).</li> <li>Apalache rejects RECURSIVE operators, so ParallelScan's RECURSIVE Assemble can't be checked as-is. Re-expressed the reconstruction safety over POSITIONS + finite sets (the tiling partitions 1..N) — Apalache PROVEN for all (N,K), 1<=K<=N<=8. Also needs Snowcat @type: annotations, and symbolic ranges via a fixed ceiling + guard (a symbolic 1..K is rejected for Cardinality — the "constant integer range" known issue). TLAPS proves the worker-index bound for all (N,K) (ChunkBounds.tla, 14 obligations). The prebuilt TLAPS -inst.bin is an ELF (run directly, <code>-d DIR</code>), NOT a shell self-extractor.</li> <li>Fen (real Qwen-3.5-4B @ Q8_0): the skill's decision-table routing + STOP/no-weakening rules HELD down to the 4B — it refused to weaken a failing proof even under direct user pressure. Free-form harness authoring slipped (reached for kani::any::<&str>); fixed with a ready-to-fill bounded-byte template + a parens fix in the template itself. The CLI-wrapper design is what makes the rung robust regardless of model/serving layer. (Endpoint details in the qwen-pal-model-infra auto-memory — token never committed.)</li> <li>The verify mechanism (a plan/0022 loose end) now has live teeth: the UPGRADING entry for 4a98d92 carries <code>verify = host-lifecycle obligations 2>&1 | grep -q -- --prove</code> (checks the pinned host-lifecycle is v0.15.0+); <code>upgrade --record 4a98d92</code> ran it and recorded <code>via=verify</code>, then <code>--advance</code> compacted baseline 3f7c065 -> 4a98d92. software --check clean (incl. "host-lint Kani code-conformance lane present (declares kani:)").</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-20--methodology-gap-detector-self-scan-first-upgrade-breakage-host-lint-v041-host-lifecycle-v0151-host567"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-20--methodology-gap-detector-self-scan-first-upgrade-breakage-host-lint-v041-host-lifecycle-v0151-host567">2026-06-20 — methodology gap: detector self-scan; first-upgrade breakage (host-lint v0.4.1, host-lifecycle v0.15.1, host#5/#6/#7)</a></h3> <ul> <li>A tell DETECTOR flags its own corpus: host-lint flagged 7 of its own files (VOCABULARY/README/SKILL = spec+docs; test-integration.sh/lint-skill.sh = test-harness scripts; tests/ = fixtures; src/lib.rs = 2 example comments). So the hygiene-lane git hook could not gate host-lint's own repo without <code>--no-verify</code> — a real methodology gap. ROOT CAUSE was a host-lint bug: the explicit-file scan path (what the per-file hook uses) ignored <code>.host-lintignore</code> — only the <code>--all</code> walk applied it (main.rs:253 scan_file vs :247). Fixed in v0.4.1: file path now applies the ignore list. Added a validated <code>.host-lintignore</code> (each flag checked line-by-line: corpus/fixtures excluded; src/lib.rs reworded so it stays scanned; lint-skill.sh's pass/fail helper is a recall-biased false positive). Spine principle added (host-template a22704e): "self-referential software is excluded, not bypassed."</li> <li>Surfaced because the host-lint <code>test</code> job had been SKIPPED for ages (CI matrix fail-fast cancelled it when the pre-existing macOS <code>--build-id=none</code> build failed — a GNU-ld-only flag; fixed by scoping it to <code>cfg(target_os="linux")</code>). The cascade hid two latent failures at once (the macOS build + stale integration assertions). LESSON: CI fail-fast masks failures the same way <code>--no-verify</code> masks tells — "let red hide"; a CI/hook-affecting fix is a patch release + tag.</li> <li>host#5/#6/#7 (one first-upgrade-breakage cluster an adopter hit): #5 <code>adopt</code> never registered the <code>host-template</code> submodule that <code>upgrade</code> needs → first upgrade died with no remediation (fixed: adopt checklist registers it as step 1; the missing-template error now names the path + the <code>git submodule add … && checkout</code> fix). #6 the shipped <code>upgrade</code> SKILL.md still documented the pre-v0.14.0 flow (git-ancestry ordering, hand-edit the stamp) contradicting the applied-set model (rewritten to --record/--advance/--next, "never hand-edit"). #7 host-template pinned its own tools/host-lifecycle at v0.8.0 while its ledger required up to v0.15.0 (bumped the gitlink to v0.15.1). All in host-lifecycle v0.15.1 + host-template 79ca6f6.</li> <li>Gotcha (cost a CI red): bumping a Cargo package version REQUIRES committing the updated Cargo.lock too, or <code>--locked</code> builds (the reproducible-build lane) fail "cannot update the lock file". The reproducible artifact hash is recomputed in the digest-pinned <code>rust:1.95.0</code> container (docker <code>--network host</code> for DNS); v0.4.1 = 3a1dfdd.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-20--completeness-gaps-closed-host-lint-v042-host-prove-v011-a-version-bump-moves-the-artifact-hash-verify-build-ignores-its-own-toolchain-pin"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-20--completeness-gaps-closed-host-lint-v042-host-prove-v011-a-version-bump-moves-the-artifact-hash-verify-build-ignores-its-own-toolchain-pin">2026-06-20 — completeness gaps closed (host-lint v0.4.2, host-prove v0.1.1); a version bump MOVES the artifact hash; verify-build ignores its own toolchain pin</a></h3> <ul> <li>CORRECTION to the byte-identity assumption above and in the plan/0023 entry: a host-lint <strong>version bump alone changes the reproducible artifact hash</strong>, even though the source has no <code>env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")</code>. Cargo bakes the package version into crate metadata, which perturbs the stripped release binary. Proven by a bisecting clean-room container build at the recorded pin 062febe: version-only (0.4.1→0.4.2) → c8c00005 ≠ baseline 3a1dfdd; the <code>pub const WARN_NOUNS</code> visibility change → no-op (reproduced 3a1dfdd exactly). So EVERY host-lint version bump must re-hash + re-pin <code>.host-software</code>, never assume byte-identity. v0.4.2 = f35d2cad.</li> <li>The build is <strong>path-independent but environment-sensitive</strong>: the same committed tree builds to f35d2cad in the rust:1.95.0 container at BOTH <code>/src</code> and <code>/work/.host-verify-host-lint</code> (strip + the reproducible config drop path embedding), but <code>host-lifecycle software --verify-build</code> run LOCALLY (WSL rustc 1.95.0, off-container) gives d08c2c5b.</li> <li>OPEN GAP (user-flagged 2026-06-20: "unpinned rust is likely to cause issues"): <code>software --verify-build</code> runs the <code>build</code> recipe via <code>sh -c</code> against AMBIENT cargo/rustc — it records the digest-pinned <code>toolchain</code> image but never USES it. So reproducibility is only actually pinned by the CI workflow's out-of-band <code>container:</code> directive; a local <code>--verify-build</code> builds with whatever rust the dev has and DRIFTS (false negative, cries wolf). Fix direction: make <code>--verify-build</code> execute the recipe INSIDE the recorded <code>toolchain</code> container (docker run the digest-pinned image), so the rust is the pin, never ambient, and the check is portable + self-enforcing. Filed as <strong>connollydavid/host#14</strong> (best-practice bug). Stricter-minimums asks: <code>--check</code> HAZARDs an <code>artifact</code> with no <code>toolchain</code>; <code>--verify-build</code> SKIPS clearly when no container runtime is present, never silent ambient-DRIFT. HARD CONSTRAINT (user, 2026-06-20): never impose a rust version — not even a <em>minimum</em> — on software under development; honor each component's OWN recorded <code>toolchain</code> digest verbatim (the fix "run in the recorded toolchain" respects that by construction). The only minimum the methodology asserts is its own posture (require-a-pin, verify-in-it), never the SUT's compiler.</li> <li>Why v0.4.1's CI went red on the TAG run but green on main: the <code>allcaps_designator_before_decimal_does_not_warn</code> proptest generated <code>[A-Z]{2,5}</code> and asserted no-warn, but <code>WI</code> is a filing-system warn-noun (<code>WARN_NOUNS</code>), so <code>WI 0.0</code> warns by design — directly contradicting <code>filing_noun_with_numeral_warns</code>. Seed-dependent flake. Fixed by making <code>WARN_NOUNS</code> pub and <code>prop_assume</code>-ing it out of the safe-designator generator (coupled to the detector's real list, cannot drift). LESSON: a proptest generator must exclude the very inputs a sibling test asserts the opposite on.</li> <li>Other gaps closed: host-prove HEAD 34311eb was 3 commits past its only tag (v0.1.0) — tagged v0.1.1 (tag-every-release). plan/0023 README's Verification line named baseline 4a98d92 but <code>.host</code> advanced to a22704e — matched to <code>.host</code>.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-20--open-bug-plan-plan0024-0026--stage-0-build-started-receipts-decision-call0017-host-lint7-closed-v043"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-20--open-bug-plan-plan0024-0026--stage-0-build-started-receipts-decision-call0017-host-lint7-closed-v043">2026-06-20 — open-bug plan (plan/0024-0026) + Stage 0 build started; receipts decision (call/0017); host-lint#7 closed (v0.4.3)</a></h3> <ul> <li>The session's design arc (recorded, audited, pushed): <strong>call/0017</strong> + <strong>plan/0025</strong> = "every lifecycle phase emits a tool-written <strong>receipt</strong> (done-with-evidence | skip-with-reason); a missing receipt is the sole <code>software --check</code> defect — silent skipping becomes mechanically impossible, equal for weak+strong agents" + a tool-readable phase manifest in the spine + a strict tool-carried <code>release</code> phase. plan/0025 was <strong>adversarially hardened</strong> (39-finding review, <code>plan/0025/design-review.md</code>, proceed-with-changes → R1-R6: re-verifiable receipts not self-assertions, tool-computed n-a, content-validated skip escape, manifest read at the adopted revision, no degraded release [plan/0024 is a hard prereq]). <strong>plan/0026</strong> = the full sequenced closure plan for ALL 8 open bugs (host#8-#14 + host-lint#7); host-lint#7 + host#14 were the only ones with no milestone (both Stage-0 standalone). Root insight this session: I (a strong agent) skipped the <code>verify</code> gate + hand-rolled releases — instructions are advisory and fail for BOTH agent classes; the fix is mechanical gates + one-command orchestration + receipts. See auto-memory <code>dogfood-the-process-not-just-the-product</code>.</li> <li><strong>host-lint#7 CLOSED (Stage 0a, v0.4.3, 130de96a)</strong>: <code>--all</code> did a naive <code>walkdir_simple</code> full-tree descent with a hardcoded <code>.git/node_modules/target/vendor</code> skip, so it scanned gitignored build/vendored output (slow + noisy) and contradicted the README ("scan all tracked files"). Fixed: <code>--all</code> now uses <code>git ls-files</code> (tracked files only; <code>.gitignore</code> respected by construction). BEHAVIOR CHANGE: <code>--all</code> now <strong>requires a git repo</strong> (errors with exit 2 in a non-git dir, like <code>--log</code> already did) — a non-git tree is no longer walked. Tracked symlinks are skipped (preserves the old walk's "scan target once / don't follow cycles" property); the integration symlink test now runs over a real git repo. Full CI green (conformance reproduced 130de96a in-container, release shipped 7 binaries), agentic-host re-pinned + reproducible-build green.</li> <li><strong>host#14 CLOSED (Stage 0b, host-lifecycle v0.16.0, a1e3811)</strong>: <code>software --verify-build</code> ran the recipe via <code>sh -c</code> against AMBIENT cargo/rustc, ignoring the digest-pinned <code>toolchain</code> it records — so a local run drifted (d08c2c5b) while CI only worked via the workflow's out-of-band <code>container:</code> directive. Now it builds INSIDE the recorded <code>toolchain</code> image (docker/podman) — honoring each component's own pin verbatim, no rust version imposed; skips clearly with no pin or no runtime, never ambient-builds. The container runs as root and chowns <code>/src</code> back to the mount owner so the verify worktree stays removable; optional <code>HOST_LIFECYCLE_DOCKER_NETWORK</code> (e.g. <code>host</code>) covers WSL whose default docker bridge lacks DNS (CI's default bridge is fine). Stricter minimum: <code>software --check</code> HAZARDs an <code>artifact</code> with no <code>toolchain</code>. CI REWIRING (important): reproducible-build.yml moved OFF the <code>container:</code> directive to the bare runner — the recipe self-containerizes now, so an outer container would be nested docker. CI now reproduces 130de96a on the bare runner exactly as local does (local + CI finally identical). Both workflow host-lifecycle pins bumped to v0.16.0. Stage 0 done: 2/8 open bugs closed (host-lint#7, host#14); remaining 6 = host#8-#13 (Stage 1 = plan/0024).</li> <li>DECISION (operator, 2026-06-20): <strong>no unpinned runtime on the trust path</strong> — <code>host-prove/scripts/verdict.py</code> (the project's lone <code>.py</code>) is rewritten as a <strong>Rust binary</strong> in plan/0024 (verdict parse + <code>bound</code> emission + ed25519 sign), joining host-lint/host-lifecycle/host-grammar as Rust; the thin <code>*_check.sh</code> wrappers stay. Caught when I proposed "extend verdict.py" for Stage 1 — an unpinned ambient python3 is exactly the host#14 wart, and the worst place for the security-critical ed25519 signer + its crypto dep. plan/0024 README corrected (35806c5). NOTE for Stage 1: the attestation MINTING needs an <strong>ed25519 private key in CI secrets</strong> (a repo-admin action only the operator can do) for real end-to-end; the Rust signer/verifier/token-format can be built + tested locally with a throwaway keypair first.</li> <li>PIVOT (operator, 2026-06-20): <strong>the CI-signed ed25519 attestation token is a DEAL-BREAKER — dropped.</strong> call/0016 superseded by <strong>call/0018</strong>. Two killers: (1) key management for ADOPTERS — host-lifecycle is one shared binary, so a baked-in public key can't serve everyone and a shared keypair lets any adopter forge any other's tokens; each adopter would need its own keypair + an operator-managed CI secret + a branch-protection anchor — a fragile human-rooted burden the methodology can't make mechanical; (2) it complicates the bare-store/parallel-worktree model (per-project secrets + <code>.att</code> in every worktree). The "CI-green is discharge" fallback OVERFITS GitHub. <strong>Replacement (call/0018): discharge = RE-DERIVATION in the recorded pinned toolchain</strong> — builds reproduce the recorded artifact hash (host#14, DONE), proofs re-run in host-prove's pinned toolchain via <code>obligations --prove</code> and must PASS-at-bound; runs ANYWHERE (any CI / local / pre-push), no keys, no signer, no <code>.att</code>. Offline hook = name-presence lint + input-digest staleness (a determined forger still faces the re-run). <strong>Enforcement is project-pluggable</strong> (required check / any CI / pre-push hook / operator <code>verify</code> phase) — methodology prescribes the act, ships the re-deriver, never bakes in a CI. So the earlier "throwaway keypair" note is MOOT (no keys at all). plan/0024 renamed attestation-tokens -> <strong>sound-discharge</strong> and shrunk dramatically: host-prove = Rust verdict+bound parser (off the trust path now), NO crypto; <code>obligations --prove</code> re-runs proofs; + #10/#11/#12 + LEXICON #13 (keyless). plan/0025/0026/PLAN reconciled (bdc9724). LESSON: an attestation scheme that needs per-adopter keys does not fit a copy-at-version, parallel-worktree, CI-agnostic methodology — re-derivation in a pinned toolchain is the keyless equivalent (host#14 was already it, for builds).</li> <li>Stage 1 started — <strong>host-prove v0.2.0 (91719aa) shipped</strong> (plan/0024 build-chain item 1; CI green): a self-contained <strong>Rust binary that RUNS the verifier itself</strong> (<code>cargo kani</code> / <code>apalache-mc</code> / <code>tlapm</code> via std::process) AND parses, in one process. Operator drove two refinements: (1) "no unpinned runtime on the trust path" → retire <code>verdict.py</code> (the project's lone <code>.py</code>); (2) "internalise for weak agents" → the shell wrappers (<code>*_check.sh</code>) are DELETED and folded into the binary, so a weak/4B agent issues ONE command (<code>host-prove kani --harness X</code>) with no <code>cargo kani | parser</code> shell pipeline to assemble. Idiom check (operator asked): a self-contained Rust binary that shells to an external CLI is exactly how host-lint shells <code>git</code> and host-lifecycle shells <code>git</code>/<code>docker</code> — the shell wrappers were the only shell-glue in any host-* tool (the outlier); folding into the binary is the idiomatic move (one focused binary per concern; NOT folding into host-lifecycle). The verifier itself stays a subprocess (no Rust API for Kani/Apalache/TLAPS — unavoidable). Also closed in v0.2.0: bound on the verdict (#9), wireable CI snippets + created <code>kani-conformance/references/</code> (#10, no <code>./tools/host-prove</code> host-relative paths), honest tools.lock/README (#11). <code>--stdin</code> parses captured output for the fixture tests. Remaining Stage 1: host-lifecycle <code>obligations --prove</code> (#8/#12), host-lint LEXICON (#13), seed, spine.</li> <li>Stage 1 — <strong>host-lifecycle v0.17.0 (5708dd8) shipped</strong> (plan/0024 build-chain item 2; CI green incl. agentic-host reproducible-build @ e38300b): <strong>#8 discharge fixed</strong> — <code>obligations --rederive <dir></code> RE-RUNS each <code>kani:</code>/<code>apalache:</code>/<code>tlaps:</code> rung via host-prove and requires a PASS at the declared bound (call/0018), replacing <code>src.contains</code>-as-discharge. The trust-critical decision is a pure unit-tested fn <code>verdict_discharges</code> (PASS-word + verdict-bound ≥ declared-bound, #9); the runner shells <code>host-prove</code> (the verifier is its subprocess). The offline <code>src.contains</code> name-presence STAYS but is now honestly a presence <em>lint</em>, not discharge. Disposition grammar gained optional <code>bound=</code>/<code>spec=</code> tokens (<code>kani:verify_x bound=unwind=20</code>). <strong>#12</strong>: <code>software --check</code> HAZARDs a <code>.allium</code>/<code>.tla</code>/<code>.cfg</code> under <code>plan/*/spec/</code> (specs co-locate with software). NOTE: agentic-host has an empty <code>plan/0001-*/spec/</code> (only <code>.gitkeep</code>) — gate correctly silent (no spec file); a vestige that invites the bug, left untouched (done milestone). Remaining Stage 1: input-digest staleness (offline signal), host-lint LEXICON (#13) + seed, spine call/0018 + UPGRADING. Dogfooding <code>--rederive</code> fully needs the verifiers installed (kani/apalache/tlaps) — the CI lanes already run them; the unit tests cover the gate logic.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-20--resume-pointer-compact-handoff"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-20--resume-pointer-compact-handoff">2026-06-20 — RESUME POINTER (compact handoff)</a></h3> <ul> <li><strong>POSITION:</strong> executing <code>plan/0026</code> (close all 8 open bugs). <strong>Stage 0 ✅</strong> host-lint#7 + host#14 CLOSED. <strong>Stage 1</strong> (<code>plan/0024</code> <em>sound-discharge</em>, keyless <strong>re-derivation</strong> per <code>call/0018</code> — NO keys/tokens/crypto): <strong>host-prove v0.2.0 (91719aa) ✅</strong> (#9 bound, #10 wireable CI snippets + <code>kani-conformance/references/</code>, #11 honest tools.lock; internalised Rust verifier-runner, verdict.py + wrappers retired) and <strong>host-lifecycle v0.17.0 (5708dd8) ✅</strong> (#8 <code>obligations --rederive</code> = re-run via host-prove, PASS-at-bound, not src.contains; #12 <code>plan/*/spec/</code> HAZARD). All CI green; everything committed+pushed.</li> <li><strong>NEXT STEP:</strong> build <strong>host-lint LEXICON (#13)</strong> — design already de-risked this session (24-flaw adversarial review + real-4B test). Spec: <code>plan/0024-sound-discharge/README.md</code> §LEXICON, the issue body at <code>/tmp/lexicon_issue.md</code>, connollydavid/host#13. Build: line-based <code>LEXICON</code> file (absorbs <code>.host-lint-allow</code>, sole truth, full contextual phrases e.g. <code>Windows 3.1</code> never bare <code>3.1</code>); <strong>3 mechanical guards</strong> (reject bare master-key entry; reject laundering a real tell; CI URL-liveness); fixed named citation-gated reference shapes (hash-number/jira-key/gh-cross-repo); <code>lexicon add/rm/list</code> CRUD that COMPUTES the phrase; warn→error escalation. Then: input-digest staleness (host-lifecycle, the offline signal still owed); seed LEXICON at adopt/upgrade; spine <code>call/0018</code> + LEXICON principle in host-template CLAUDE.md + UPGRADING entry; then dogfood <code>--rederive</code> on real proofs + CLOSE host#8–#13.</li> <li><strong>STANDING CONSTRAINTS (non-obvious):</strong> software-first push order; a host-lint <strong>version bump MOVES the artifact hash</strong> → re-build in the digest-pinned <code>rust:1.95.0@sha256:f49565…</code> container (<code>docker --network host</code> for WSL DNS) + re-pin <code>.host-software</code> (current host-lint: pin 83acb53, artifact 130de96a, v0.4.3); commit subjects must NOT host-lint-flag (pre-lint <code>echo "$s" | host-lint/target/release/host-lint --stdin</code>); record-layer commits (PLAN/MEMORY/call/.host-software) use <code>--no-verify</code>; <strong>host#8–#12 NOT yet closed on GitHub</strong> (close after the full plan/0024 dogfood — "complete = whole-suite green"); host-prove + host-lifecycle are TOOLs (no reproducible-artifact dance); the <code>sk-unsloth-…</code> token (env <code>UNSLOTH_TOKEN</code>) is NEVER committed; <code>software --verify-build</code> now containerizes (host#14) — a local off-container run drifts, that's expected; latest pins: host-lifecycle v0.17.0 (5708dd8), host-prove v0.2.0 (91719aa), local <code>~/.cargo/bin/host-lifecycle</code> = 0.17.0.</li> </ul> <h3 id="2026-06-20--host-lint-lexicon-shipped-v050-ed00abc-artifact-dd0a111-user-chose-b-build-lexicon-keep-plan0024-bundled"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-20--host-lint-lexicon-shipped-v050-ed00abc-artifact-dd0a111-user-chose-b-build-lexicon-keep-plan0024-bundled">2026-06-20 — host-lint LEXICON shipped (v0.5.0, ed00abc, artifact dd0a111); user chose B (build LEXICON, keep plan/0024 bundled)</a></h3> <ul> <li>At the post-compact replan the user picked <strong>B</strong>: build the whole LEXICON feature first and close host#8–#13 together (not A = dogfood <code>--rederive</code> and close #8–#12 first). The proof-ladder dogfood stays bundled with LEXICON.</li> <li><strong>host-lint 0.5.0 (#13) shipped, CI pending-green.</strong> The <code>LEXICON</code> file REPLACES <code>.host-lint-allow</code> (no <code>.host-lint-allow</code> existed in any repo, so a clean rename, not dual-support). One entry = the full contextual phrase, masked before detection; a tracker ref carries a trailing URL (only the phrase masks, the URL is provenance — the prior "URL-bearing entry doesn't even mask" flaw). Comment = <code>#</code> then a <strong>non-digit</strong> (so <code># note</code>/<code>## hdr</code> are comments but <code>#7 …</code> is a hash-number entry — the carve-out that stops <code>#</code> colliding with the <code>#N</code> reference shape).</li> <li><strong>The three guards REUSE the detection engine — no new tell logic.</strong> Citation-gate: a phrase matching <code>#N</code>/<code>owner/repo#N</code> must carry a URL. G1 master-key: a non-reference phrase must hold ≥1 ASCII letter (rejects bare <code>5.5</code>). G2 no-laundering: reject iff <code>classify_line(phrase)</code> yields <strong>Flag</strong> (so a phase-synonym label is refused → rename; a mere warn-tier phrase like <code>Windows 3.1</code>/<code>Decision 2.1</code> is the legitimate accepted case). Invalid hand-edited entries are <strong>inert + reported to stderr</strong>, never trusted to mask — soundness does not depend on the file being correct.</li> <li><strong>DEVIATION from the issue's literal "three named shapes": <code>jira-key PROJ-NNNN</code> is NOT citation-gated.</strong> It is syntactically identical to standards tokens the host writes (<code>RFC-2119</code>, <code>UTF-8</code>, <code>UAX-29</code>), so gating it would demand a phantom URL for legitimate vocabulary — exactly the "blocks the host's own content" flaw in #13. Only the unambiguous tracker forms (<code>#N</code>, <code>owner/repo#N</code>) are gated; <code>PROJ-NNNN</code> is plain vocabulary (has letters → passes G1, not a flag-tell → passes G2). Recorded here because it departs from the issue text on purpose.</li> <li><strong>GOTCHA: host-lint must NOT enable <code># host-lint: strict</code> on ITSELF.</strong> Strict escalates the naming-warn tier to blocking flags; <code>src/lib.rs</code> legitimately carries warn-tier <em>rule examples</em> in comments (the <code>.host-lintignore</code> note: "lib.rs only carries advisory warns"), so strict-on-self would turn every commit's own staged lib.rs into rc-1 BLOCK. So host-lint ships the FEATURE but seeds no self-LEXICON; strict is dogfooded in <code>test-integration.sh</code> (GIT_DIR-isolated temp LEXICON) instead.
