Run each printed commit/push/tag in the worktree (gh auth switch first), collect each new pin+musl-hash.</li> <li><strong>Re-pin <code>.host-software</code></strong> (pins + the recomputed musl artifact hashes + toolchain + build + deps-bundle) and <strong>back-fill release receipts</strong>, ATOMIC in one host commit (the recurring gate goes RED on a push that adds a recipe without its receipt).</li> <li><strong>Spine migration in the SAME atomic commit</strong>: bump the host-template submodule pointer to <code>455fba8</code>, advance <code>.host</code> (record <code>ecce498</code> applied), so <code>upgrade .</code> is clean.</li> <li><strong>Reinstall</strong>: the gate driver from the released 0.20.0 commit (<code>cargo install --path ... --force</code>); host-lint hooks (<code>software --install-hooks .</code> — after <code>release host-lint</code> the canonical musl binary is at <code>target/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl/release/host-lint</code> and RUNS on WSL, so it installs the canonical binary, retiring the plan/0028 local-build workaround).</li> <li><strong>Gate</strong> with the released pinned 0.20.0 binary: <code>software --check .</code> (incl. the deps-bundle drift check vs each deps-bundle.lock — must match), <code>software --verify-build .</code> (hermetic, offline, reproduces every musl artifact), <code>book --check</code>, the commit-hook tell test. Whole-suite green.</li> <li><strong>CI rewire LAST</strong>: agentic-host <code>reproducible-build.yml</code> + the cold-clone job host-lifecycle pin <code>6fa94cf</code> → the 0.20.0 commit.</li> <li>Records: plan/0032 README status, PLAN.md row, MEMORY.</li> </ol> <p>SUBTLETIES (reasoned, apply them):</p> <ul> <li><strong>Do NOT bump host-lifecycle's host-lint git dep</strong> during the Cutover (keep <code>1386e9a</code>). The vendor-v1 bundle vendored host-lint@1386e9a; host-lint's 0.8.2 re-release is a version-only bump (no library-code change), so host-lifecycle's binary is unaffected and the bundle stays valid. Bumping it would force a vendor-v2.</li> <li><strong>host-prove is hermetic without --network none</strong>: it has zero deps and builds <code>--offline</code>. The feature only passes <code>--network none</code> for deps-bundle components, so host-prove's <code>--verify-build</code> runs with the ambient network setting but fetches nothing (deterministic). Its CI release job (Gate 6) uses <code>--network none</code> explicitly. No contradiction.</li> <li><strong>The recipe edit must precede <code>release</code></strong> (release reads the on-disk recipe to build), and the edited <code>.host-software</code> must NEVER be pushed to main with a stale hash/pin — one atomic commit carries everything (step 4/5).</li> <li>Standing gotchas: <code>gh auth switch --user connollydavid</code> before every push; no-decoration commit subjects (lint with the local host-lint binary; the musl one at <code>software/host-lint/main/target/x86_64-unknown-linux-musl/release/host-lint</code> runs on WSL); cd to repo root before <code>host-lifecycle</code> commands (working-dir trap).</li> </ul> <h2 id="2026-06-22--plan0032-cutover-complete-the-milestone-is-done"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-22--plan0032-cutover-complete-the-milestone-is-done">2026-06-22 — plan/0032 CUTOVER COMPLETE (the milestone is done)</a></h2> <p>The hermetic static-musl cutover landed; the playbook above is fully executed. Shipped: host-lint <code>v0.8.2</code> (pin <code>ba479258</code>, artifact <code>a099c27d</code>), host-prove <code>v0.2.2</code> (pin <code>3ca95fc0</code>, artifact <code>520cdd10</code>), host-lifecycle <code>v0.20.0</code> (pin <code>a38b0c07</code>, artifact <code>7d090334</code>, the <code>deps-bundle</code> feature) — each a static <code>x86_64-unknown-linux-musl</code> build reproduced offline in <code>clux/muslrust:1.95.0-stable@sha256:15a72a4a…</code>, the two bundle-bearing tools against <code>vendor-v1</code> (sha <code>f1141763…</code>) under <code>--network none</code>, host-prove against an empty source set. One atomic agentic-host commit <code>86e19db</code> re-pinned <code>.host-software</code>, back-filled the three release receipts, recorded <code>ecce498</code> applied (the spine MUST), and bumped the <code>host-template</code> pointer to <code>455fba8</code>. The released <code>0.20.0</code> binary gated green: <code>software --check</code>, <code>software --verify-build</code> (all three reproduce offline), <code>book --check</code>, and the commit-hook tell test; <code>software --install-hooks .</code> installed the canonical musl host-lint itself (verified against its hash), so the plan/0028 local-glibc-build workaround is RETIRED. agentic-host CI re-pinned to the certified 0.20.0 in <code>eff82b8</code> (reproducible-build.yml ×2 + mdbook.yml, all <code>6fa94cf</code>/<code>629e9c32</code> → <code>a38b0c0</code>; the old binary lacks the deps-bundle feature and could not verify the new hermetic recipe). Records: <code>call/0021</code> (the instance-scoped software decision; the MUST stays in the template), PLAN.md row, plan/0032 README "Landed" table; commit <code>e1e947c</code>. Confirmed reasoned subtleties held: host-lifecycle's host-lint git dep stayed at <code>1386e9a</code> (vendor-v1 still valid, no vendor-v2); host-prove needed no <code>--network none</code>. The installed gate driver is now 0.20.0 (<code>cargo install --path software/host-lifecycle/main</code>). Watch the producer release-job CI (host-lint/host-lifecycle/host-prove already carry a <code>release:</code> job on <code>v*</code>) plus the agentic-host reproducible-build + Site runs for whole-suite green.</p> <h2 id="2026-06-22--plan0033-complete-host15-closed-self-consistent-book"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-22--plan0033-complete-host15-closed-self-consistent-book">2026-06-22 — plan/0033 COMPLETE: host#15 closed (self-consistent book)</a></h2> <p>Closed <code>connollydavid/host#15</code> ("clean fully" ruling) via a full lifecycle run. Two defects in the methodology's own published book. (A) <code>host-lifecycle book</code> generated a home-overview link to a <code>README.md</code> room landing; mdBook serves that page at <code>index.html</code> but rewrites the in-content link to <code>README.html</code> (a 404). Fix: a <code>served_link</code> helper maps a <code><dir>/README.md</code> landing to <code><dir>/index.md</code> (the served page), and the five room nav part-titles use a colon separator in place of the em-dash. Two new unit tests (<code>served_link_maps_readme_landings_to_index</code>, <code>generated_nav_titles_carry_no_em_dash</code>); 74 tests green. Shipped as host-lifecycle <code>v0.20.1</code> (pin <code>40374d1</code>, musl artifact <code>662cb632</code>), built offline against <code>vendor-v1</code> (bundle still valid; host-lint dep stayed <code>1386e9a</code>). (B) Spine prose-clean: <code>host-template</code> CLAUDE.md (~96 em-dashes + negative-parallelism/false-range/arrow) and STRUCTURE.md (~17 em-dashes + arrows/anaphora) reworded to <code>host-lint --prose</code> clean, meaning preserved; <code>host-template</code> <code>5a82e00</code> (126 lines, in-place). Atomic agentic-host commit <code>77f02e3</code> re-pinned host-lifecycle + back-filled the release receipt + bumped the host-template pointer; CI install pins moved to v0.20.1 in <code>697c61b</code>.</p> <p><strong>NON-OBVIOUS CONSTRAINT for any future spine prose edit:</strong> <code>host-template/UPGRADING.md</code> carries SEVEN <code>verify = grep -rqs "<phrase>" host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> post-conditions (for applied upgrades), and <code>host-lifecycle software --check</code> re-verifies every applied claim. So editing CLAUDE.md prose can break an applied-upgrade re-verification (a loud HAZARD) if it touches a verify phrase. The seven phrases to preserve verbatim: "Self-referential software is excluded", "discharged by re-derivation", "boxed in the file, not path-excluded", "every phase emits a receipt", "the producer of a tool embeds it", "Prose hygiene is the same lane", "reproduce them offline from pinned inputs". All were kept byte-for-byte and <code>software --check</code> stayed green. Also: <code>host-lint --prose</code> fires <code>ai-diction</code> on the word "harness" even inside the inline code identifier <code>`kani:<harness>`</code>; this is a code-span false positive on a load-bearing disposition token (sibling to <code>apalache:<inv></code>/<code>tlaps:<theorem></code>), left as the one accepted residual flag (cannot rename without changing documented syntax). The book generator's <code>home_page</code> uses a repo's root <code>README.md</code> verbatim when present (agentic-host has one), so the served_link fix is exercised by the generated-overview path (unit-tested) rather than agentic-host's own <code>docs/index.md</code>.</p> <p>plan/0034 (resolves <code>connollydavid/host#16</code>, the emergent positional <code>box N</code> tell): detection + the reflective grammar-growth doctrine. Several non-obvious facts. (1) <strong>A naming tell lives in host-lint, not host-grammar.</strong> The <code>FLAG_TERMS</code> noun list and <code>VOCABULARY.md</code> are host-lint's own; host-lint pulls only <code>is_numeral</code> and the prose-trope engine (<code>scan_prose_*</code>, <code>tell_score</code>) from host-grammar. So the box shape graduated into host-lint (<code>FLAG_TERMS += box, boxes, steps</code>, plus an <code>is_num_range</code> so a <code>4-8</code> range after the noun flags); host-grammar was untouched. Shipped host-lint <strong>v0.9.0</strong> (<code>666b0da</code>, musl artifact <code>cd3277e0</code>), <code>--change-class adds-flag</code> (the tool computed 0.8.2 -> 0.9.0), re-pin <code>61227e5</code>. (2) <strong>The glued hyphen-digit form (<code>box-1</code>, <code>phase-2</code>) cannot be LEXICON-escaped, so it is out of scope.</strong> I implemented it first; it made a pre-existing lib.rs comment (<code>section-5</code>) and adopter terms like <code>level-3 cache</code> newly flag, and a glued token has no numeral-free prefix to declare (the <code>LEXICON</code> guard refuses a phrase that is itself flag-tier). That violates the presumptive-flag-plus-escapable invariant the doctrine enshrines, so I dropped it (same class as the scoped-out <code>PHASE30</code> glued evasion). The escapable case — a genuine quantity like <code>decode step 2</code> — is handled by declaring the numeral-free contextual <strong>prefix</strong> <code>decode step</code> (verified empirically: declaring <code>decode step 2</code> is refused; <code>decode step</code> masks the quantity while a real <code>step 3</code> still flags). (3) <strong>host-lint's own source is dogfooded against its new binary</strong>: adding a rule can make existing example comments flag, and the spine rule is to reword the example, not mute the file (<code>.host-lintignore</code> already notes lib.rs's comments were reworded). (4) The doctrine is authored in the <strong>spine</strong> (<code>host-template/CLAUDE.md</code> <code>27d815b</code>, UPGRADING <code>6d3075b</code>, agentic-host adopted via pointer bump + <code>upgrade --record 27d815b</code> -> <code>e31a31e</code>): the shared tell corpus is living and grows by reflective practice, discovery mechanical-first (sweep history) and operator-validated, reflection prompted at the verify gate, with two distinct authorities (local operator owns the <code>LEXICON</code>; the shared-grammar maintainer validates universality and releases the graduation) and the asymmetry that legitimacy is project-local while a tell is shared. A project-local ban surface was deliberately NOT built (YAGNI; every tell seen so far is universal) and is NOT promised in the spine. (5) <code>host-lifecycle upgrade --record <id> <dir></code> records an applied UPGRADING entry (writes <code>via=verify</code>); <code>host-lifecycle</code> has no <code>verify</code> subcommand (the gate is the <code>verify</code> skill).</p> <p>plan/0034 CI follow-up — <strong>a release that changes <code>src/lib.rs</code> must re-derive the kani obligation digests, a step the release flow does not automate.</strong> Each <code>kani:</code> obligation with <code>inputs=src/lib.rs</code> records a <code>git hash-object</code> of that input in <code>host-lint.obligations.digests</code>; the box-rule change to <code>lib.rs</code> made those digests STALE. Local <code>host-lifecycle software --check</code> (v0.20.1) did NOT catch it, but the CI allium job's <code>host-lifecycle obligations host-lint.allium --tests tests --prove src</code> (pinned v0.18.1) DID (<code>STALE ... re-derive + --record-digests</code>), so the v0.9.0 push went red on the <strong>allium</strong> job only. The sanctioned <code>obligations --rederive --record-digests</code> drives the proof through <strong>host-prove</strong>, whose local spawn of <code>cargo kani</code> fails with ENOENT on the <code>/mnt/c</code> WSL mount even though direct <code>cargo kani</code> runs <code>VERIFICATION:- SUCCESSFUL</code> and kani 0.67.0 is set up (<code>~/.kani/kani-0.67.0</code>) — a local host-prove-invocation gap, not a proof failure. The digest is deterministic (<code>git hash-object src/lib.rs</code>), so with the proof verified by both the CI kani job and a direct local run, it was recorded directly: <code>73322a7</code> (only <code>host-lint.obligations.digests</code>, artifact <code>cd3277e0</code> unchanged), and the <code>.host-software</code> pin advanced one CI-commit past the v0.9.0 tag (<code>b4b75b1</code>, the artifact-preserving precedent). <strong>The v0.9.0 RELEASE itself was fine</strong> — the tag run's <code>release</code> job was green and all six platform binaries published (<code>cd3277e0</code>-equivalent); only the parallel allium lint job failed, so the tag badge reads failure while the release is complete. Lesson: after any host-lint source change, re-derive + re-record the kani digests before tagging, or the producer CI's obligations-<code>--prove</code> lane fails. The digest fix was then promoted into a clean <strong>v0.9.1</strong> point release (<code>host-lifecycle release host-lint --change-class neither</code>, <code>592aeeb</code>, artifact <code>eb521a2c</code>): tagging the digest-fixed source makes the whole v0.9.1 tag CI green (allium + release jobs), the clean way to supersede a tag whose run was red, without rewriting the v0.9.0 tag. A version bump changes the artifact hash (the version string is embedded), but leaves <code>src/lib.rs</code> untouched, so the kani digest stays valid. Final host-lint pin: <code>592aeeb</code> / <code>eb521a2c</code>.</p> <p>plan/0034 <strong>COMPLETE</strong> (2026-06-24): <code>connollydavid/host#16</code> closed. Detection (host-lint <strong>v0.9.1</strong>, pin <code>592aeeb</code> / artifact <code>eb521a2c</code>) plus the reflective grammar-growth doctrine in the spine (<code>host-template</code> <code>27d815b</code> + UPGRADING <code>6d3075b</code>, adopted in agentic-host at <code>e31a31e</code>) are shipped; the weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B, thinking off) classified the clear cases 5/5; the skipped kani-digest receipt was recorded and promoted into the v0.9.1 point release. <strong>Whole-suite CI green across all repos, zero in-flight</strong>: agentic-host Site + Reproducible build on HEAD <code>7a82efc</code>, host-lint v0.9.1 (main + tag, every job), host-template, host-grammar, host-lifecycle, host-prove (host is the tracker, no CI). The <code>/tmp/yarn-agentic</code> review clone was removed. Named follow-ups, none started: mechanize the candidate-tell harvest + the verify-gate reflection prompt in the skills; a constrained project-local ban surface only if a genuinely non-universal tell ever appears; fix the local host-prove -> <code>cargo kani</code> ENOENT on the <code>/mnt/c</code> WSL mount (CI is unaffected).</p> <p>plan/0035 <strong>COMPLETE</strong> (2026-06-24): mechanized the plan/0034 doctrine's discovery (the named follow-up). (1) <strong>host-lint <code>gather</code> subcommand</strong> (v0.9.2, <code>dd7304e</code>, artifact <code>03292e56</code>): the inverse of the flag scan. It runs <code>git log</code> and scans tracked markdown headers itself (one command, parse-free), surfaces a recurring word-then-numeral shape the grammar does NOT catch (dropping <code>FLAG_TERMS</code>, the <code>PREV_SKIP</code>/<code>UNITS</code> allowlists, a <code>#</code> reference, a four-or-more-digit year or hash, and a unit-bearing quantity), and reports the residue ranked; advisory (exit 0, never gates), recurrence at least twice. <code>gather_candidates</code> + <code>GATHER_STOP</code> in lib.rs, a <code>gather</code> subcommand dispatch in main.rs (mirrors <code>lexicon</code>). The dogfood on agentic-host surfaced a real candidate, <code>lens</code> (the <code>## Lens 1/2/3</code> design-review headers, a positional-naming tell the lane misses). (2) <strong>Reflection wiring</strong>: the host-lifecycle <code>verify</code> and <code>adopt</code> skills gained a <code>## Reflect</code> step (run gather, operator triages: propose upstream, declare in <code>LEXICON</code>, or leave). (3) <code>call/0022</code> records the software decision (gather surfaces mechanically, the operator validates, the tool never auto-graduates a tell nor auto-bans one; Scope = host-lint + host-lifecycle). No project-local ban surface (YAGNI). (4) Weak-agent (Qwen-3.5-4B) triage 3/3: a universal positional label -> PROPOSE, a hardware designator -> DECLARE, a verb-count -> LEAVE. <strong>Two process lessons applied this milestone</strong>: (a) re-derive the kani digest BEFORE tagging (so v0.9.2 CI went green first try, unlike v0.9.0); (b) a skill FEATURE change ships as a version bump, not a silent artifact-preserving pin-advance past the tag, so the host-lifecycle skill wiring was promoted to a v0.20.2 point release (<code>717276f</code>, artifact <code>ea9f01db</code>) with the CI install pins in <code>mdbook.yml</code> + <code>reproducible-build.yml</code> bumped. The artifact-preserving pin-advance is reserved for a pure CI-fix with no new capability.</p> <p>Deferred-item closure campaign (2026-06-24, operator-directed): before working <code>plan/0036</code> (the reconcile-internal-contradictions sweep, which is opened but deliberately left for the END), close every deferred item across plans 0001-0035; track any NEW contradiction found into <code>plan/0036</code>, one release at a time, following the full lifecycle (verify gate, <code>host-lifecycle release</code>, receipts). A complete cross-checked inventory found 3 genuinely-open (the rest were 7 stale-but-done notes and ~17 decided-against non-goals). <strong>Done so far:</strong> (Stage 1) corrected the 7 stale notes in place to record where each actually landed; (Stage 2) stamped the 4 open-ended escape-hatches dormant (Win32s case-(c), bare-store-optional, arch-attest, project-local ban); (Stage 3) <strong>closed the plan/0029 residuals via host-lifecycle v0.21.0</strong> (<code>bf23391</code>, artifact <code>a3364020</code>): <code>software --teardown [--item] [--force]</code> removes a component's worktrees + bare store and refuses to destroy a worktree with uncommitted/unpushed work without <code>--force</code>; a branch-collision HAZARD in <code>software --check</code> (case-insensitive and worktree-admin-leaf); 77 tests + clippy clean; Fen-4B reached the targeted <code>--teardown --item</code> command unaided; whole-suite green (host-lifecycle CI main+tag, agentic-host Site + reproducible-build cold-clone at the new pin). The change-class for a new subcommand is <code>feature</code> (minor: 0.20.2 -> 0.21.0). <strong>Latent bug found, not yet fixed:</strong> <code>host-lifecycle release ... --preview</code> is silently ignored by the release CLI arg parser, so a "preview" runs the real build/bump; harmless here (the real release path is correct) but worth folding a fix into the next host-lifecycle release. <strong>Remaining campaign work:</strong> plan/0030 D4 (wire the prose recheck portably; also clears <code>plan/0036</code> finding #1) and the receipts-family re-homing as its own milestone. The re-homing's dual-format support must be ONGOING, not a one-time shim (copy-at-version: adopters cross the boundary at their own pace), following the existing legacy-<code>.host</code>-stamp auto-migrate-on-read precedent: an <code>UPGRADING</code> ledger entry per adopter plus permanent old-layout read tolerance.</p> <p>Campaign Stage 4 DONE (2026-06-24): plan/0030 D4 wired + plan/0036 prose-gate contradiction resolved, via <strong>host-lifecycle v0.22.0</strong> (<code>167d2e6</code>, artifact <code>bb6fc4eb</code>). The portability problem (agentic-host's host-lint is embedded Where software, not on PATH) dissolved on a key realization: <strong>host-lifecycle already links the <code>host_lint</code> crate</strong>, so the prose audit runs IN-PROCESS. New <code>host-lifecycle prose <dir></code> mirrors <code>host-lint --docs</code> (git ls-files .md walk honoring <code>.host-lintignore</code>, <code>scan_prose_text</code>, the same Flag->1/Warn->3 verdict) with zero PATH dependency. The spine <code>verify</code> recheck now chains <code>host-lifecycle prose .</code> after <code>validate</code> (<code>lifecycle.manifest</code>, spine <code>e280a8d</code> + UPGRADING <code>641efef</code>, adopted applied=<code>e280a8d</code>); CLAUDE.md reconciled; the <code>--preview</code>/<code>--next</code> release flag (silently ignored before) is fixed. <strong>The gate caught two real regressions while landing</strong>, proving it non-vacuous: (a) a decoration em dash in <code>call/0021:10</code>, a genuine post-D5 regression that slipped in because D4 was not yet wired (reworded); (b) my own commit message "finding #1", a positional-reference tell (Flag) the commit-msg hook blocked (reworded). <strong>Lesson on engine identity:</strong> the stale PATH <code>host-lint</code> binary's <code>--docs</code> UNDER-reported (missed the call/0021 em dash); the gate uses host-lifecycle's linked engine (<code>host-grammar</code> 9d51468, the same pin host-lint v0.9.2 carries), which is authoritative. After a host-lifecycle release, install the new binary to PATH before running <code>software --check</code>, since the recheck shells <code>host-lifecycle prose</code>. Whole-suite green (host-lifecycle CI main+tag, agentic-host Site + reproducible-build running the in-process prose recheck in CI). Remaining: the receipts-family re-homing (Stage 5).</p> <p>Campaign Stage 5 DONE (2026-06-24): the receipts-family re-homing, <strong>100% tool-driven</strong>, via <strong>host-lifecycle v0.23.0</strong> (<code>6e15e01</code>, artifact <code>76be6d9e</code>). plan/0037. The <code>migrate-receipts <dir></code> subcommand moves the applied-set out of <code>.host</code> into <code>.host-receipts</code> and splits the operational receipts into a new <code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>, idempotently; the dual-format reader (<code>read_applied_ids</code>, <code>read_all_receipts</code>, <code>applied_file</code>) unions BOTH layouts so the gate stays green across the boundary and an un-migrated adopter is read correctly (permanent back-compat). The 6-of-8 ontology ambiguity the prior review flagged is resolved by one rule: a receipt is methodology-version (<code>.host-receipts</code>) only for <code>adopt</code> and <code>upgrade</code>; everything else host-lifecycle runs is operational (<code>.host-lifecycle-receipts</code>). Writes route by that rule (<code>append_receipt</code>, the <code>upgrade --record</code> applied-write, <code>--advance</code> compaction). Spine <code>ac32d1c</code> (the stamp/receipts description) + UPGRADING <code>4d2ebe9</code>, adopted <code>applied=ac32d1c</code>. agentic-host dogfooded the migration through the tool (8 applied lines moved, 26 operational receipts split); <code>software --check</code> 0 HAZARDs on the migrated layout, <code>--verify-build</code> reproduces, whole-suite green, Fen-4B reached <code>migrate-receipts .</code> unaided. <strong>Lessons:</strong> (a) the verify gate (plan/0030 D4) caught an <code>ing-tail</code> trope in plan/0037's own README during the release and BLOCKED it, a live proof the gate works; (b) <code>host-lifecycle prose .</code> / <code>host-lint --docs</code> only scan TRACKED files (git ls-files), so a NEW untracked doc is NOT prose-checked until committed or <code>git add</code>-ed (my pre-commit check on plan/0037 was vacuous); (c) <code>migrate-receipts --help</code> has no positional, so it defaulted dir to <code>.</code> and migrated the repo root (the <code>--</code>-flag is skipped by the arg finder) — harmless here since it WAS the intended dogfood, but a reminder that these subcommands default the dir to the cwd. The whole deferred-item closure campaign (Stages 1-5) is now complete; plan/0036 (the contradiction sweep) remains the next milestone, with its prose-gate finding already resolved.</p> <p>RESUME ANCHOR (2026-06-24, written before a compact): plan/0036 is OPEN with a <strong>settled design</strong>, ready to build in order. A second deep audit (after the campaign; drift was expected) plus a de-risk review (five lenses + Qwen-3.5-4B, recorded in <code>plan/0036-reconcile-internal-contradictions/design-review.md</code>, verdict proceed-with-major-revisions) reshaped it. <strong>The contradictions are symptoms of a missing migration reflective practice</strong>: a spine change propagates but nothing re-reads the project's own restatements of methodology, so STRUCTURE.md / README.md / CLAUDE.md staled across plan/0012, plan/0023, plan/0029. The <strong>doctrine</strong> (the root-cause, integral fix, per operator: "show, don't tell" via the audit): widen the existing "grows by reflective practice" spine doctrine to ONE self-blindness principle with two arms, <strong>gather</strong> (forward, tells, graduates upstream, cadence-driven) and <strong>reconcile</strong> (backward, the project's own restatements, fixed locally, fired by a specific spine move). Settled rules from the review: prefer pointing over paraphrasing; scope is machine-checkable and <strong>annotation-backed</strong> (not the inoperable "describes vs uses" the 4B could not apply); the trigger is conditional via a new <code>UPGRADING</code> <code>restates =</code> field, and <strong>for a development host the verify gate is the binding trigger</strong> (agentic-host authors its own spine changes, so <code>upgrade --record</code> never fired for the drifts); three-way disposition (reword live, box frozen, forward-correct the immutable); a <strong>sibling <code>validate</code> check</strong> HAZARDs an <code>accepted</code> call/ whose Scope names host-template (closes the call/0017 class). Drop the count-vs-stanzas tool shape as noise. <strong>Ordered run = tasks #63 -> #64 -> #65 -> #66</strong> (the plan/0036 README "Build order"): (1) seed spine truth data (tool-family list + verification-model datum) in host-template; (2) host-lifecycle reconcile check + validate-scope check + <code>restates=</code> read, release + re-pin; (3) widen the spine doctrine + UPGRADING entry + wire reconcile into upgrade/adopt/verify skills; (4) agentic-host adopt, then dogfood the seven symptom findings (root CLAUDE.md three-lanes/host-prove; STRUCTURE.md room map; CLAUDE.md migration pointer; README host-prove; call/0017 supersede; host-prove pin to tag; orphan <code>plan/0001-foundation/spec/</code>; PLAN.md Skill-Hardening box) THROUGH the new check, then verify + whole-suite green. On resume, read <code>plan/0036/README.md</code> and <code>design-review.md</code>, then execute #63 first. agentic-host main is clean at the pushed HEAD.</p> <hr /> <h2 id="plan0036-63-64-done-spine-truth-data--host-lifecycle-reconcilevalidate-scope-v0240"><a class="header" href="#plan0036-63-64-done-spine-truth-data--host-lifecycle-reconcilevalidate-scope-v0240">plan/0036 #63-#64 done: spine truth data + host-lifecycle reconcile/validate-scope (v0.24.0)</a></h2> <p>#63 seeded the spine truth data in <code>host-template/lifecycle.manifest</code> as two new stanzas AFTER the phase stanzas (the phase parser skips a non-<code>[phase]</code> header and ignores their keys; a dedicated <code>parse_spine_facts</code> reads them): <code>[family] tools = host-lint host-lifecycle host-prove host-grammar</code> and <code>[verification] drivers = host-lint allium specula host-prove</code> (the rung-drivers; host-grammar is NOT a driver, host-lint is). host-template cdd0eff, host pointer-bump 94201e8.</p> <p>#64 shipped host-lifecycle <strong>v0.24.0</strong> (commit 4742525, tag v0.24.0, artifact b3251b18, re-pinned, host ebe512b). New: (a) <code>reconcile <dir></code> — annotation-backed: walks tracked .md (the prose-audit walk), finds inline <code><!-- host-reconcile: KIND --></code> markers, checks each against spine truth/layout. KINDS = family, verification, where-root, spec-path (the <code>RECONCILE_KINDS</code> const). Scope is the annotated set, NOT a judgment — the one-time drift discovery is a human audit, the annotation makes recurrence mechanical. (b) <code>validate</code> now HAZARDs an accepted call/ whose <code>Scope:</code> names host-template (closes the call/0017 class). (c) <code>UPGRADING</code> <code>restates =</code> field parsed onto <code>Upgrade</code>; <code>validate_ledger</code> gates its kinds to RECONCILE_KINDS. 86 tests, clippy clean.</p> <p><strong>Sequencing decision (greenness):</strong> the new <code>validate</code> check flags call/0017 (still accepted), and <code>validate call/</code> is in the verify-gate recheck. So the CI host-lifecycle <code>--rev</code> bump was DELIBERATELY DEFERRED from #64 to #66 — CI keeps installing v0.23.0 (green) until #66 supersedes call/0017 and bumps the revs together. The release gate itself stayed green because <code>run_verify</code> shells <code>host-lifecycle</code> from PATH = the installed v0.23.0 (no host-template check) at release time. After #64 I installed v0.24.0 locally for the #65/#66 dogfood, so LOCAL <code>validate call/</code> now exits 1 on call/0017 — a deliberate local-red window closed in #66. Annotation form is inline-trailing (same source line / table row as the restatement).</p> <hr /> <h2 id="plan0036-65-done-two-arm-doctrine-in-the-spine--reconcile-skill-wiring-host-lifecycle-v0242"><a class="header" href="#plan0036-65-done-two-arm-doctrine-in-the-spine--reconcile-skill-wiring-host-lifecycle-v0242">plan/0036 #65 done: two-arm doctrine in the spine + reconcile skill wiring (host-lifecycle v0.24.2)</a></h2> <p>Spine (host-template d5a0034 + UPGRADING d85658f, host pointer 3c36f3e): CLAUDE.md's living-grammar doctrine widened to ONE self-blindness principle with two arms — <strong>gather</strong> (forward, tells, graduates upstream) and <strong>reconcile</strong> (backward, the project's own restatements, fixed local, never propagates). States prefer-pointing, annotation-backed machine-checkable scope, the conditional+host-aware trigger (<code>restates=</code> field; <strong>the verify gate is the binding trigger for a development host</strong>), three-way disposition, and the sibling validate-scope check. The verify-phase <code>recheck</code> in lifecycle.manifest now chains <code>&& host-lifecycle reconcile .</code>, so the gate enforces reconcile. UPGRADING entry keyed d5a0034 requires host-lifecycle v0.24.2. Skills wired: <code>verify</code> (binding reconcile in Reflect), <code>adopt</code> (full reconcile once), <code>upgrade</code> (new Reflect step, fires on a recorded <code>restates=</code>).</p> <p><strong>call/0017 superseded early (3ed423c/60b3c2b).</strong> The new <code>validate</code> check (v0.24.0) flags an accepted call/ whose Scope names host-template, and <code>validate call/</code> is in the gate recheck — so with v0.24.x installed the gate was RED until call/0017 was superseded. Rather than dodge the check by downgrading the PATH binary, I acted on what it found (the honest dogfood): forward-corrected call/0017 to <code>Status: superseded by the methodology spine (host-template @ 617e420 ...)</code>. So one of the seven symptom findings landed in #65; #66 accounts for all seven. The em-dash in my first Status reword tripped the prose gate (decoration trope, Warn → prose exits 3 → gate RED); reworded to a comma clause. Lesson: the prose recheck blocks on Warn (not just Flag), because <code>host-lifecycle prose</code> exits 3 on a warn and the <code>&&</code> recheck treats nonzero as red.</p> <p><strong>Backtick fix (v0.24.2, the non-obvious one).</strong> reconcile scans for the literal <code><!-- host-reconcile: KIND --></code> marker. The spine CLAUDE.md and UPGRADING.md <em>document</em> that syntax, and <strong>every case-(a) adopter copies the spine CLAUDE.md verbatim</strong>, so an adopter's own <code>reconcile .</code> would false-positive on the quoted example (unknown kind <code>KIND</code>). Fix: <code>reconcile_scan</code> skips a marker that opens inside an inline-code span (odd backtick count before it) — documentation of the syntax, not a live directive. A real annotation is a bare HTML comment on a restatement line. Three host-lifecycle releases this session: v0.24.0 (reconcile+validate-scope+restates), v0.24.1 (skills, tagged but never pinned — superseded before re-pin), v0.24.2 (backtick fix; current pin 9a1a586 / b214f090). CI host-lifecycle <code>--rev</code> still on v0.23.0, bumped in #66 with the final verify.</p> <hr /> <h2 id="plan0036-complete-66-adopted--dogfooded-the-reconcile-arm-all-seven-symptoms-reconciled-whole-suite-green"><a class="header" href="#plan0036-complete-66-adopted--dogfooded-the-reconcile-arm-all-seven-symptoms-reconciled-whole-suite-green">plan/0036 COMPLETE (#66): adopted + dogfooded the reconcile arm; all seven symptoms reconciled; whole-suite green</a></h2> <p>agentic-host adopted the two-arm doctrine (<code>upgrade --record d5a0034</code>, applied-set now in .host-receipts). The reconcile dogfood ran THROUGH the new check: I annotated the drifted restatements with inline <code><!-- host-reconcile: KIND --></code> markers (still drifted), ran <code>host-lifecycle reconcile .</code>, it flagged exactly four — README.md:4 (family omits host-prove), CLAUDE.md:24 (verification omits host-prove), STRUCTURE.md:9 (spec under plan/), STRUCTURE.md:11 (Where not software/) — then I reworded each to match the spine until reconcile was clean. CLAUDE.md:9 (already lists all four family tools) was annotated to guard against future drift. The seven symptom findings are all resolved: 1-4 via the reconcile dogfood, 5 (call/0017) superseded in #65, 6 (host-prove pin) by releasing v0.2.3 (3d1bba7, artifact a322e0f) which absorbs the CI-only commit into a release tag so the pin no longer sits past its tag, 7 (orphan plan/0001-foundation/ removed — was empty+untracked; PLAN.md Skill-Hardening box checked with the crates.io deferral noted). Final local sweep all green (validate plan/+call/, software --check, reconcile, prose, book --check). HEAD = 49f3ef4, whole-suite CI green (agentic-host reproducible-build + site; host-template; host-lifecycle v0.24.2; host-prove v0.2.3).</p> <p><strong>LESSON (CI/recheck coupling):</strong> a host-template pointer bump that adds a NEW tool subcommand to the verify-phase <code>recheck</code> (here <code>&& host-lifecycle reconcile .</code>) MUST be accompanied by the CI host-lifecycle <code>--rev</code> bump in the same push. I bumped the pointer (3c36f3e) but deferred the CI rev to the end (49f3ef4), so CI ran v0.23.0 (no <code>reconcile</code> subcommand) against the new recheck and the reproducible-build job's <code>software --check</code> was RED for that window (3c36f3e..0324b16). HEAD is green, but the intermediate reds were avoidable. The recheck command and the CI-installed binary that runs it are a unit; bump them together.</p> <hr /> <h2 id="plan0038-complete-the-spine-and-front-door-now-prose-gate-themselves"><a class="header" href="#plan0038-complete-the-spine-and-front-door-now-prose-gate-themselves">plan/0038 COMPLETE: the spine and front-door now prose-gate themselves</a></h2> <p>The prose rule was stated in the spine but enforced only on adopted projects (via the verify-phase recheck), never on the meta repos that author it. plan/0038 closes that: host-template (<code>9268d76</code>, 90 warns reworded to zero across 11 docs) and host (<code>1dc9cb9</code>, 29 warns in its single README) are prose-clean and each carries a <code>Prose</code> GitHub Actions gate pinned to host-lifecycle v0.24.2 (<code>host-lifecycle prose .</code>, fail on any trope). host's is its FIRST CI workflow ever. Both green in CI; agentic-host pointer + host re-pin in <code>0813795</code>; whole-suite green. The seven <code>UPGRADING</code> verify phrases stayed byte-for-byte (no applied-upgrade re-verification broke). No <code>UPGRADING</code> ledger entry: adopters already run the prose gate via the verify phase, so this only brought the meta repos under the existing rule. The bulk reword was done by four parallel subagents (one per file-group), then verified centrally (prose zero on both repos + the seven phrases intact + <code>software --check</code> green) and the diffs reviewed.</p> <p><strong>LESSON (ai-diction is density-weighted, not a per-token flag).</strong> The <code>harness</code> ai-diction on <code>`kani:<harness>`</code> (plan/0033 called it an "accepted residual that cannot be reworded") is NOT a hard false positive. ai-diction words carry weight 0.5 ("one word is never a verdict"); the gate is an absolute/ density threshold over the DOCUMENT. host-template/CLAUDE.md had <code>harness</code> TWICE (the <code>kani:<harness></code> token at :355 and prose "named harness" at :365); the two summed past the gate, flagging :355. In isolation a single <code>harness</code> line is clean. Rewording the :365 prose occurrence ("the named harness, invariant, or theorem" -> "the rung's named target") dropped the count to one and cleared the flag, no token rename, no engine change. Corollary: <code>scan_prose_text</code> (the prose audit) takes NO allowlist, so a LEXICON entry cannot mask a prose trope (LEXICON masks only the naming audit, <code>scan_text_with_allow</code>); a legitimate-but-flagged prose word is cleared by rewording/reducing density or boxing in a <code>host-lint:ignore</code> fence, never by LEXICON.</p> <hr /> <h2 id="plan0039-cut-the-reconcile-arm-evolves-to-concept-as-uri-designed-not-built"><a class="header" href="#plan0039-cut-the-reconcile-arm-evolves-to-concept-as-uri-designed-not-built">plan/0039 cut: the reconcile arm evolves to concept-as-URI (designed, not built)</a></h2> <p>The operator rejected EVERY inline form of the reconcile annotation on aesthetic grounds (a trailing comment, a comment-pair wrapping a span, a markdown link carrying the directive, an out-of-line metadata block). The objection is structural, not cosmetic: any inline annotation is checker machinery sitting in the prose, and clean prose is the whole point. The 5 shipped <code><!-- host-reconcile: KIND --></code> annotations (plan/0036) were deleted from agentic-host's CLAUDE.md/README/STRUCTURE.</p> <p><strong>The settled replacement (refined-B):</strong> each methodology concept is a URI. One canonical definition at a stable <code>{#id}</code> anchor on a concepts page (generated from <code>.host-software</code> for the project-scoped ones, so it cannot drift); everywhere else POINTS to it with a relative-path link (<code>concepts.md#components</code>) instead of restating. Validated empirically: stock mdBook v0.5.2 resolves the relative <code>#id</code> links with no custom generator pass; the real Qwen-3.5-4B authored pointer links 3/3 (incl. the <code>verifiers</code> route the inline classification step had failed) because pointing carries no "is this a restatement?" judgment, the exact step that exhausted the 4B on every inline form. Lineage cited outward: DRY/SSOT (Hunt & Thomas), reflection-on-action (Schön), Linked Data (Berners-Lee). Citation principle (operator): kind to predecessors and peers OUTWARD; rigorous with ourselves INWARD.</p> <p><strong><code>family</code> -> <code>components</code>:</strong> "family" was an agentic-host overfit (host-* tools happen to share a prefix); the methodology's own word for the <code>.host-software</code> set is "components". Renamed across the host-lifecycle binary + its tests + the spine <code>lifecycle.manifest</code> <code>[components]</code> stanza, NO ALIAS (an alias split validation across two surfaces — the <code>restates=</code> ledger-gate uses RECONCILE_KINDS, the assertion-match used the alias — so a <code>restates = family</code> flagged unknown; operator vetoed the alias, "fix properly"). Suite green (86/86).</p> <p><strong>The bite lesson (two cast reviews, both this session):</strong> link-integrity ("the link resolves") is NOT the bite the inline check had. A dropped component is simply never linked, so nothing catches the absence; "definitions match SSOT" is tautological (generated from it). So refined-B needs a <strong>coverage check</strong> (every <code>.host-software</code> component/verifier MUST be referenced somewhere, drop-fails-by-absence) + a <strong>declared-anchor check</strong> (target must be a known concept id, not any resolving fragment). Cutover is <strong>deprecate-then-retire</strong>, fail-safe: a retired checker HARD-FAILS on a surviving annotation (never silently inert, the cold-adopter fail-unsafe Bly flagged); a bookless adopter SKIPS, not no-ops (Orin). Retiring a days-old spine mechanism is itself churn, so deprecate first, retire one spine revision later via UPGRADING.</p> <p><strong>First-mover gap (operator decision):</strong> agentic-host's annotations are already deleted, so its restatements (the tool list, the layout) are briefly UNGUARDED until the coverage check ships. Operator accepted this as a calculated risk; an adopter is never asked to take it.</p> <p><strong>State at write-up:</strong> plan/0039 cut + PLAN.md row committed (558da13). The <code>family</code>-><code>components</code> rename, the manifest <code>[components]</code>, and the 5 doc deletions are UNCOMMITTED in the working tree (the binary change deploys via <code>host-lifecycle release</code>, not an agentic-host commit, since <code>software/</code> is gitignored). The build (concepts page, the 3 checks, deprecate inline, spine doctrine rewrite, UPGRADING, release) is pending.</p> <p><strong>Aside:</strong> filed connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1 — <code>host-lifecycle next <dir></code> returns <code>0000</code> for a directory with no numbered entries (e.g. the host root) instead of erroring, a fail-unsafe footgun at the weak-agent bar. <code>next plan</code> -> <code>0039</code> and <code>next call</code> -> <code>0023</code> are correct.</p> <h2 id="plan0039-design-refined-and-build-started-2026-06-24-corrects-the-entry-above"><a class="header" href="#plan0039-design-refined-and-build-started-2026-06-24-corrects-the-entry-above">plan/0039 design refined and build started (2026-06-24, corrects the entry above)</a></h2> <p>The plan/0039 entry above is superseded on three points; corrected here (append-only). (1) <strong>No generated <code>concepts.md</code>.</strong> All four concept definitions live at <code>{#id}</code> anchors in <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> and are pointed at directly (<code>[text](STRUCTURE.md#id)</code>); a page of links back to one doc is redundant. The tool carries the concept vocabulary (<code>CONCEPT_IDS</code>, spine-level); the coverage check guards drift. Vocabulary is spine, values are project-local. (2) <strong>The lifecycle manifest is hardened to phases-only</strong> (<code>manifest --check</code> rejects any non-<code>[phase]</code> stanza); <code>components</code>/<code>verifiers</code> are sourced from the project's own <code>.host-software</code>, never the shared spine — separation of concerns, no overfit creeps back. (3) <strong><code>host</code> is the front door, not "repo-self".</strong> Of the <code>.host-software</code> members the four tools are <code>components</code>; the single-file <code>host</code> is set apart by <code>front-door = true</code>. agentic-host (the dev environment) is NOT a <code>.host-software</code> member and must stay invisible to adopters; the old comment calling the <code>host</code> member "the project's own repo" was wrong. The front-door principle: one spine, everything else a copy-at-version or a pointer, never a restatement. Landed in the host-lifecycle worktree: project-local sourcing, manifest hardening, the three checks (link-integrity, declared-anchor, coverage). Pending: convert agentic-host's docs to pointers, spine doctrine, release.</p> <h2 id="plan0039-complete-concept-as-uri-shipped-as-host-lifecycle-v0250-2026-06-25"><a class="header" href="#plan0039-complete-concept-as-uri-shipped-as-host-lifecycle-v0250-2026-06-25">plan/0039 complete: concept-as-URI shipped as host-lifecycle v0.25.0 (2026-06-25)</a></h2> <p>The reconcile arm now sources project facts from <code>.host-software</code> (components are the <code>[software]</code> members minus a <code>front-door = true</code> member; verifiers are a <code>[verification]</code> drivers stanza) and runs three checks over the tracked docs: link-integrity, declared-anchor, and coverage (each project-local home names its full <code>.host-software</code> set). The lifecycle manifest is hardened to phases-only (<code>manifest --check</code> rejects any other stanza). agentic-host's own docs are converted to concept-as-URI: four homes in <code>STRUCTURE.md</code> (<code>{#components}</code> etc.), CLAUDE.md/README pointing at them. The spine doctrine + the <code>UPGRADING</code> entry (<code>[upgrade 7be692f]</code>) are in host-template (<code>e4f6207</code>); <code>call/0023</code> records the software decision.</p> <p><strong>Adversarial review + a real Qwen-3.5-4B run caught two holes before release:</strong> a <code>{#id}</code> must sit at the END of a heading (mdBook slugifies a start-placed one to a different id, so a start-placed home passed reconcile but 404'd) and <code>home_section</code> must run to the next <em>same-or-higher</em> heading (the 4B authored a sub-headed home whose members fell outside a stop-at-any-heading section, falsely failing coverage). Both fixed in code with tests. The 4B authored a home, a <code>[verification]</code> stanza, and an annotation-to-pointer migration once the doctrine <em>showed</em> the exact forms (the worked example) — it near-misses unaided. Released v0.25.0 (commit <code>0f2a4da</code>, artifact <code>e3e1ef02</code>), re-pinned, dogfood-verified (reconcile + <code>software --check</code> green on the released binary).</p> <p><strong>Process slip recorded:</strong> <code>host-lifecycle prose</code> scans <strong>tracked</strong> files (via <code>git ls-files</code>), so prose-check a NEW doc only AFTER <code>git add</code>/commit — checking an untracked file reports a false clean. Two diction tropes (false-range from a <code>from … to</code> pair, negative-parallelism from "honors no other") slipped into committed docs this way and were fixed in a follow-up. See [[ai-diction-traps-in-my-writing]].</p> <h2 id="plan0042-complete--the-receipted-task-graph-host-lifecycle-v0260-2026-06-25"><a class="header" href="#plan0042-complete--the-receipted-task-graph-host-lifecycle-v0260-2026-06-25">plan/0042 complete — the receipted task graph (host-lifecycle v0.26.0, 2026-06-25)</a></h2> <p>In-plan tasks became first-class. A task is an anchored <code>### </code> heading under <code>## Build sequence</code>, keyed <code>plan/NNNN#anchor</code> (a stable global id, not a position), with <code>- depends:</code> (a local <code>#anchor</code> or a cross-milestone <code>plan/NNNN#anchor</code>), <code>- verify:</code> (a shell command, or <code>attested <call/NNNN | operator></code>), and <code>- inputs:</code> (files a mechanical verify covers). The project's tasks form ONE graph across milestones; the tool derives the parallel frontier (tasks whose deps all carry a done receipt), default linear. Shipped in host-lifecycle v0.26.0 (<code>430876c</code>, artifact <code>52e8414a</code>): the <code>tasks</code> subcommand (status / check / record / rederive / new), the <code>.host-task-receipts</code> ledger (a deliberate third receipt kind, <code>call/0024</code>), the per-task gate in <code>software --check</code>, the task-anchor link checker, and <code>tasks --new</code> (scaffolds the heading). Spine doctrine in host-template <code>2229dbb</code> + UPGRADING <code>71a1613</code> (requires v0.26.0), adopted. Whole-suite CI green across all three repos.</p> <p><strong>Operator rulings (overrode the five-lens review's re-scope):</strong> build the graph NOW as forward infrastructure for <em>arbitrary-complexity adopters</em> — "no consumer in agentic-host's own linear history" is the wrong lens, the consumer is the adopter. A NEW top-level <code>.host-task-receipts</code> file (over the ontology objection that a task receipt is operational). <strong>Adopter-facing and mandatory.</strong> The bare <code>#N</code> GitHub-reference unsafety is a <em>future</em> plan (enforce full URLs); plan/0042's task references use the full relative-path anchor, so it was reframed: anchoring is for <em>stable task identity</em>, not the <code>#N</code> collision (the review showed no plan ever references a step by number).</p> <p><strong>Two design corrections surfaced during the build (both implemented + tested):</strong></p> <ol> <li><strong>The gate is completion-aware.</strong> A task with no receipt is a HAZARD only when its milestone is marked complete (read from the README <code>## Status</code>); an OPEN milestone's tasks are pending, not gated. This matches the operator's "every <em>completed</em> milestone owes a receipt" scope; the first per-task-always-HAZARD gate over-fired on open milestones (0040/0041 are open).</li> <li><strong>Fenced code blocks are masked</strong> in both the parser and the reference check, so example syntax in a doc (a worked example, a ledger stanza) is not parsed as a real task or flagged.</li> </ol> <p><strong>Staleness took both halves of <code>call/0018</code>:</strong> a mechanical <code>done</code> records its <code>inputs</code> digest and goes STALE on input drift (cheap offline); <code>tasks --rederive</code> re-runs the command and refreshes it. An attested <code>done</code> resolves its citation (weaker, labeled as such). A verify that changed since the receipt is stale; an orphan receipt (anchor removed) is a reverse-drift HAZARD.</p> <p><strong>4B (Fen) validation:</strong> the form is fail-safe. The 4B authors <code>depends</code>/<code>verify</code>/<code>inputs</code> well (within-milestone six of six, cross-milestone four of four) but slips on the exact <em>anchor</em> form when hand-typing; <code>tasks --new</code> carries the anchor (the fill-in-the-blank fold-back), and any residual slip (e.g. over-qualifying a local ref) fails loud as a dangling-dependency HAZARD. Frame authoring as the prerequisite question ("what must finish first?"), never "what runs in parallel?" See [[qwen-4b-weak-agent-eval]].</p> <h2 id="plan0041-complete--next-fails-closed-host-lifecycle-v0261-2026-06-25"><a class="header" href="#plan0041-complete--next-fails-closed-host-lifecycle-v0261-2026-06-25">plan/0041 complete — next fails closed (host-lifecycle v0.26.1, 2026-06-25)</a></h2> <p><code>host-lifecycle next <dir></code> silently returned <code>0000</code> when pointed at a directory with no <code>NNNN-slug</code> entries (the host root, a typo, an empty room), so a garbled argument produced a plausible-but-wrong number. Now it fails closed: a non-directory or a numberless directory exits non-zero (code 2, the tool's existing path-error code) with a diagnostic and a did-you-mean drawn from the <em>known rooms</em> (so a generated/build dir like <code>book/</code> is never suggested). The fresh-empty-room case folds into fail-closed with no special flag.</p> <p><strong>The form was set by a real Qwen-3.5-4B (Fen) ergonomics check, via pal.</strong> The rope HTTPS token had rotated (401), so the run went through the <strong>pal MCP front-end</strong> (<code>mcp__pal__chat</code>, model <code>qwen3.5-4b</code>, same backend at <code>http://127.0.0.1:4001/v1</code>, framed-as-user-content) — a working fallback when rope's bearer is stale; ask the user for the new rope token only when a true system prompt is needed. Fen recovered the intended room from every candidate form and preferred the did-you-mean line, and recognized the empty room as a first-entry case unaided (no affordance needed). <code>call/0025</code>; closed connollydavid/host-lifecycle#1. Dogfooded as a receipted task graph (plan/0042).</p> <h2 id="plan0040-complete--the-front-door-check-re-scoped-by-adversarial-review-host-lifecycle-v0270-2026-06-25"><a class="header" href="#plan0040-complete--the-front-door-check-re-scoped-by-adversarial-review-host-lifecycle-v0270-2026-06-25">plan/0040 complete — the front-door check, re-scoped by adversarial review (host-lifecycle v0.27.0, 2026-06-25)</a></h2> <p>The single-file front door (the <code>host</code> repo, the <code>.host-software</code> member marked <code>front-door = true</code>) restates the spine and is outside any host's verify gate, so it stales silently (it had already dropped the <code>release</code> phase). The original plan was to <em>generate</em> its spine-derived sections from <strong>hand-authored "front-door fragments" carried in the template</strong>.</p> <p><strong>The adversarial review killed that mechanism (two independent lenses, unanimous): a template fragment is a SECOND SOURCE OF TRUTH.</strong> <code>front-door --check</code> would prove <code>README == regenerate(fragment)</code> and never <code>fragment == CLAUDE.md</code> — it relocates the drift rather than removing it, the exact self-blindness reconcile (plan/0036) closes, one layer up. Proven by two live drifts: the omitted <code>release</code> phase, and the version pins (Rust/allium/TLA+/Temurin), which have <strong>no structured canonical home at all</strong> (scattered across prose + per-component CI YAML inside materialized worktrees).</p> <p><strong>Re-scope (the lesson): generate ONLY from structured data the tool already reads.</strong> The shipped <code>front-door</code>/<code>front-door --check</code>: (a) <strong>coverage</strong> of the lifecycle phases against <code>lifecycle.manifest</code> (checked as a backtick token, since "release" recurs in prose like "GitHub releases") and of the wired tools against <code>.host-software</code> <code>[verification]</code> drivers + the lifecycle engine — the plan/0039 coverage bite, applied to a link-free doc; (b) byte-exact <strong>generation</strong> of the <code>.host</code> stamp block from the tool's format. The pins/lanes-rule/tool-prose have no home and stay <strong>authored, not gated</strong> (a structured pin home is a named follow-up); don't pretend a prose fragment makes them drift-proof.</p> <p><strong>Wiring is agentic-host-local: a separate step in the reproducible-build CI</strong> (where the spine sources are materialized), NOT the shared spine recheck — so no adopter without a front door runs it and no UPGRADING entry is owed. The front-door repo can't run the check itself (it carries neither the manifest nor <code>.host-software</code>); that asymmetry is recorded. <code>call/0026</code>. Dogfooded as a receipted task graph. <strong>General lesson: a generator is drift-proof only for facts with a real structured source; for sourceless facts, coverage or an honest "authored, not gated" beats a fake generator that gives false assurance.</strong> See [[reconcile-moves-to-concept-uri]].</p> <h2 id="decision-review-of-plan0040--plan0041--three-outcomes--the-entrance-generalization-2026-06-25"><a class="header" href="#decision-review-of-plan0040--plan0041--three-outcomes--the-entrance-generalization-2026-06-25">Decision review of plan/0040 + plan/0041 — three outcomes + the entrance generalization (2026-06-25)</a></h2> <p>After shipping plan/0040 (front-door check, v0.27.0) and plan/0041 (fail-closed next, v0.26.1), a decision review settled three things and spun out plan/0043.</p> <ol> <li> <p><strong>Front-door phases: keep COVERAGE (not generation), decided on methodology-aesthetic grounds.</strong> Both designs passed the Fen bar (Fen honored a generated-region "do not edit" marker AND fixed a coverage-flagged omission), so safety did not decide it. The decisive precedent is plan/0039's "no checker machinery in the prose" (why reconcile dropped inline annotations): a <code><!-- generated --></code> marker is machinery in teaching prose. And the spine ITSELF restates phase NAMES in clean prose (the manifest holds the structured fields). <strong>The routing principle: generate what is naturally a format/data block (the <code>.host</code> stamp), coverage-check what is naturally prose (phase/tool names), never a generation marker in teaching prose.</strong> So coverage was right, not the under-delivery I feared in the review. Decision rule: <strong>when multiple designs pass the weak-agent bar, decide on methodology-aesthetic fit.</strong></p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Fail-closed next exit code = 2 confirmed; the earlier 4B preference for 1 was an EDUCATION GAP.</strong> Re-ran the Fen probe with the convention documented (1 = unexpected/internal error, 2 = expected logic/usage error): Fen then routed next-on-a-numberless-dir to 2 and a genuine disk fault to 1. So exit 2 stands (tool-consistent with adopt/next arg errors); document the convention so it is not a gap for the next maintainer (fold a comment in at the next host-lifecycle release).</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>The capability is OPT-IN / REUSABLE, not agentic-host-only → plan/0043 (entrance-check).</strong> The front-door's shape (a self-contained doc that restates the spine and CANNOT link) recurs for an adopter-authored standalone <code>SKILL.md</code> and any project's operator-and-agent landing page. Operator ruling (the standing "build for arbitrary-complexity adopters" value): make it opt-in/reusable, superseding plan/0040 decision-4 + the scope half of call/0026. <strong>Named <code>entrance</code> by Qwen-3.5-4B data</strong> — <code>landing</code> mis-cued (deployment), <code>threshold</code> (numeric limit), <code>welcome</code> (greeting); <code>foyer</code> was the model's metaphor-first pick but "too domestic for software"; operator chose <code>entrance</code> (clear, fits host/rooms/guest, software-resonant). <code>front-door</code> renamed with no alias (the plan/0039 way). The declaration stays OUT of the prose (a <code>.host-software</code> flag generalizing <code>front-door = true</code>, never a marker — outcome 1). The standalone sibling of <code>reconcile</code> (reconcile = pointers for linkable docs; entrance = coverage + generation for can't-link docs).</p> </li> </ol> <p><strong>pal/Fen ops note (reinforced):</strong> the rope HTTPS token is still rotated; use pal. pal's injected "comprehensive response / use continuation_id" footer repeatedly drove the 4B into a format-loop when the probe ALSO demanded a strict format ("output ONLY") — judge by the converged reasoning BEFORE the loop, and keep probe prompts short with no conflicting format demand. See [[qwen-4b-weak-agent-eval]], [[qwen-pal-model-infra]].</p> <p><strong>Entrance rename landed standalone, with a deprecate-then-retire migration (2026-06-25).</strong> The operator pulled the <code>front-door</code> → <code>entrance</code> rename forward out of plan/0043 (which keeps the opt-in generalization), as a standalone hardwired/agentic-host-local change. Shipped as TWO host-lifecycle releases: <strong>v0.28.0</strong> = the rename with no alias (subcommand + the <code>.host-software</code> flag <code>front-door = true</code> → <code>entrance = true</code> + code + CI), change-class <code>removes-flag</code> (a renamed public flag is Breaking → pre-1.0 minor bump); <strong>v0.28.1</strong> = the migration, change-class <code>neither</code> (a backward-compat shim → patch). <strong>The migration is plan/0039's deprecate-then-retire:</strong> <code>parse_project_facts</code> accepts a legacy <code>front-door = true</code> as the entrance (so a pre-rename <code>.host-software</code> is NOT silently demoted to a component), and the <code>entrance</code> command warns and names the rename so the old spelling never passes silently; the shim is slated for removal a release later. The full plan/0039 retire step (a hard-fail + an adopter UPGRADING entry) waits until the generalization makes entrances adopter-facing. <code>call/0027</code> records it. <strong>Operator directives that shaped it:</strong> comments stay FORWARD-LOOKING (no "renamed from front-door" provenance asides — I had added several and was told to strip them); the MIGRATION is the one place the old spelling lives ("let migration deal with transition"). <strong>Lesson: a rename with no alias OWES a migration</strong> — without the shim a stray <code>front-door = true</code> silently becomes a component (a confusing HAZARD), the silent failure plan/0039's fail-safe forbids.</p> <p><strong>plan/0043 COMPLETE: the entrance check generalized to a singleton <code>[entrance]</code> stanza (host-lifecycle v0.29.0, 2026-06-25).</strong> Any project declares ONE entrance in a <code>[entrance]</code> stanza in <code>.host-software</code>: <code>member</code> (the <code>[software]</code> member it belongs to, set apart from <code>components</code>), <code>document</code> (the file within it, default <code>README.md</code>, so a <code>SKILL.md</code> or a landing page is reached by path), <code>restates</code> (<code>true</code> for all, or a subset of the closed vocabulary <code>phases</code>/<code>tools</code>/<code>stamp</code>). <code>host-lifecycle entrance --check</code> holds it complete against the declared concepts; the standalone sibling of <code>reconcile</code> (reconcile = pointers for linkable docs; entrance = coverage + generation for self-contained docs). Spine doctrine + adopter UPGRADING entry in host-template <code>ba86125</code>; agentic-host migrated its <code>.host-software</code> from the legacy per-member <code>entrance = true</code> to the stanza. <code>call/0028</code> records it; supersedes call/0026 scope, completes call/0027.</p> <p><strong>The journey (operator-shaped):</strong> the three-reviewer design review returned RE-SCOPE (defer the generalization); the <strong>operator OVERRULED → EXPAND</strong> (see [[review-findings-are-requirements-not-descope]]). A three-reviewer <strong>code review</strong> of the diff then caught TWO blocking defects before release: (1) the parse <code>problems</code> field reached only the <code>entrance</code> command, so a typo'd <code>member</code> left the real front door in <code>components</code> and reconcile demanded STRUCTURE.md name it — the call/0027 silent demotion RELOCATED to another consumer; fix = surface <code>problems</code> in <code>reconcile</code> too. (2) an empty <code>restates =</code> parsed to "check nothing" and reported clean (fail-open); fix = a problem. <strong>Lesson: a parse-<code>problems</code> guard must fire in EVERY consumer of the parsed facts, not just the command that owns the feature.</strong></p> <p><strong>Two gotchas worth keeping:</strong> (a) <strong>UPGRADING entry key must be a PUSHED commit.</strong> I keyed it to the pre-amend SHA (dangling, never pushed); <code>upgrade</code>/CI resolve the key by git ancestry, so a fresh clone can't find it. Fix = re-key to the pushed doctrine commit. Don't amend-then-key; commit the doctrine, push, THEN key the entry to that pushed SHA. (b) <strong>exit-code convention: the codebase is the standard linter one</strong> — <code>0</code> clean, <code>1</code> the check found drift/HAZARDs, <code>2</code> cannot-proceed-on-input (missing/malformed file, <code>next</code> numberless dir). The earlier #6/gather-data framing ("1=unexpected, 2=expected-logic", so a <code>--check</code> drift → 2) mis-described the 29 <code>exit(1)</code> sites; the code comment + gather-data were corrected to the real convention rather than changing the code. (c) <strong>The entrance is the single set-apart entry point</strong> (a non-component member); a doc INSIDE a real tool component is not the singleton entrance (a recorded boundary, not a code change).</p> <p><strong>UPDATE — the shim retirement landed in plan/0043 (host-lifecycle v0.30.0, 2026-06-25).</strong> The operator chose to retire NOW and to FOLD it into plan/0043 ("it was connected"), not a separate plan: a surviving per-member <code>front-door = true</code> / <code>entrance = true</code> is a loud problem in EVERY consumer (the parser pushes it; entrance and reconcile exit <code>2</code>), never the entrance. Spine retirement doctrine + UPGRADING <code>de8a517</code> (requires v0.30.0); the live spine teaches only the stanza. The UPGRADING-key gotcha was handled cleanly this time (commit the doctrine + entry with a placeholder, push, THEN re-key to the pushed commit, no amend). <strong>New gotcha: a task receipt and its task must land together (task commit first).</strong> The audited-docs rule split the infra commit (the <code>.host-task-receipts</code> receipt for <code>#retire-shim</code>) from the plan/0043 commit (the <code>#retire-shim</code> TASK); committing the receipt first left an orphan-receipt HAZARD at that intermediate commit, so its CI went red (the HEAD, carrying both, is green). Next time: commit the milestone-doc task BEFORE the receipt, or land them in one commit.</p> <p><strong>plan/0044 (prose-lane LEXICON, host-lint v0.10.0, <code>f1474e8</code>, artifact <code>5106ee7a</code>, closes host-lint #16).</strong> The <code>--prose</code>/<code>--docs</code> lanes now consult the per-repo LEXICON: <code>scan_prose_text</code> masks a declared phrase with the naming lane's <code>mask_allowed</code> before BOTH the per-tell scan AND the document density score (<code>tell_score</code>), so a declared <code>rehost harness</code> clears the ai-diction trope on <code>harness</code> within that phrase and a standalone occurrence still flags. <code>mask_allowed</code> is byte-length-preserving, so offsets into the masked text still index the original for the reported excerpt. No new abuse surface: the same provenance-gated entries the naming lane masks with (a letter required, no flag-tier laundering, tracker-ref URL), and the density denominator is the SENTENCE count, so a mask only subtracts its own weight and can never inflate the denominator to dilute other tells. No spine change: the living-grammar doctrine ("a legitimate tell-shaped token stays in the LEXICON") is MET, not changed, so no host-template UPGRADING entry (operator agreed). <strong>Data-gathering gotcha: a multiple-choice option that asserts a false premise contaminates the weak-agent result.</strong> The first abuse-guard probe offered an option claiming LEXICON requires multi-word phrases; <code>validate_lexicon_entry</code> enforces no such rule (a single legitimate word is a valid entry). Fen accepted the false premise, so the run was discarded and re-run with honest options; Fen then chose lane-consistency <code>(A)</code>, the surgical word boundary <code>(ii)</code>, and "no new muting power" <code>(b)</code>, with the "separate stricter prose allowlist" dissent available and not chosen. Ground probe options in the actual code before asking the model. The run went direct against rope with a real system prompt (the user rejected pal, whose injected system prompt caused a format-loop); the <code>10696b8f…</code> rope token worked this session (the earlier "confirmed rotated" note was wrong, or it was un-rotated).</p> <p><strong>Obligation digests stale on ANY src change, and <code>host-lifecycle release</code> does NOT re-derive them (plan/0044, host-lint v0.10.0 → v0.10.1).</strong> Editing <code>src/lib.rs</code> for the prose-mask change staled two kani-backed obligations (<code>rule-success.DetectInternalCodeAsName</code>, <code>rule-failure.DetectInternalCodeAsName.1</code>, both <code>inputs=src/lib.rs</code>) tracked in <code>host-lint.obligations</code> / <code>host-lint.obligations.digests</code>. The release gate runs only <code>validate + prose + reconcile</code>, NOT the obligations lane, so v0.10.0 (<code>f1474e8</code>) shipped with stale digests and host-lint's OWN CI (the <code>allium</code> job: <code>host-lifecycle obligations host-lint.allium --tests tests --prove src</code>) went red AFTER the tag. Fix: re-derive with <code>PATH=<host-prove-release-dir>:$PATH host-lifecycle obligations host-lint.allium --tests tests --prove src --rederive . --record-digests</code> — it needs <strong>host-prove on PATH</strong> (built at <code>software/host-prove/main/target/release/host-prove</code>), and <code>--rederive .</code> NOT <code>--rederive src</code> (else it joins to <code>src/src/lib.rs</code> and cannot hash). It re-runs the Kani proofs (both SUCCESSFUL here) and writes <code>host-lint.obligations.digests</code>. <em><em>Lesson: after editing a host-</em> component's source, re-derive obligation digests and commit <code>*.obligations.digests</code> as PART of the release, before tagging.</em>* The operator chose to <strong>bump v0.10.1</strong> (a clean patch carrying the fresh digests; new artifact <code>941126c9</code>, since the embedded version changes the binary) rather than force-push the <code>v0.10.0</code> tag forward (the auto-classifier blocks moving a published tag): <code>v0.10.0</code> stays the feature tag at <code>f1474e8</code>, <code>v0.10.1</code> (<code>0c2bfc3</code>) is the pinned release.</p> <h2 id="2026-06-26--plan0045-complete-the-embedded-prose-engine-catches-up-host-lifecycle2"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-26--plan0045-complete-the-embedded-prose-engine-catches-up-host-lifecycle2">2026-06-26 — plan/0045 COMPLETE: the embedded prose engine catches up (host-lifecycle#2)</a></h2> <p><code>connollydavid/host-lifecycle#2</code> closed. plan/0044 gave host-lint's prose lane the LEXICON mask, but host-lifecycle runs that lane in-process for its verify-phase prose recheck while pinning the <code>host_lint</code> crate at <strong>v0.8.1</strong>, whose <code>scan_prose_text</code> predates the mask (signature <code>(input, source, matches)</code>, no allowlist param). So the embedded engine could not consult LEXICON even in principle, and an adopter with a legitimate declared domain noun on a governed surface would hit a permanently-failing verify recheck (0 flags, advisory warns, <code>prose</code> still <code>exit(3)</code>). The bug is <strong>latent</strong>: NO repo in the tree carries a LEXICON yet, so a constructed fixture (a tracked doc with <code>harness</code> twice + a sibling LEXICON declaring the phrase) demonstrates it — <code>host-lint --docs</code> masks (exit 0) while <code>host-lifecycle prose</code> warns (exit 3) on the same repo.</p> <p><strong>Fix = share the engine into the lib (operator chose shared-lib over replicate-in-host-lifecycle, a 2-release path over 1, to close the drift CLASS not the instance).</strong> <code>Lexicon</code>, <code>load_lexicon</code>, and <code>run_docs</code> moved from host-lint's <strong>binary</strong> (<code>main.rs</code>) into the <code>host_lint</code> <strong>library</strong> as <code>pub</code> items; <code>run_docs</code> now returns <code>Result<Vec<Match>,String></code> instead of <code>process::exit</code> (the binary prints+exits, an embedder surfaces it). host-lint's CLI is byte-identical (LEXICON honored exit 0, undeclared warn exit 3, git-failure message+exit 2 unchanged). host-lifecycle's <code>prose_audit</code> shrank to <code>host_lint::load_lexicon(root).phrases_lc</code> + <code>host_lint::run_docs(root,&allow,&ignore)</code> — the hand-reimplemented <code>--docs</code> walk is gone, so walk+loader+mask are all shared and cannot drift. Released <strong>host-lint v0.10.2</strong> (<code>5a9d2c5</code>, artifact <code>85c1fc58</code>) and <strong>host-lifecycle v0.30.1</strong> (<code>46d481c</code>, artifact <code>23c27bff</code>).</p> <p><strong>The predicted vendor-v2 finally happened.</strong> The 2026-06-22 plan/0032 Cutover note warned "Do NOT bump host-lifecycle's host-lint git dep ... Bumping it would force a vendor-v2." This milestone IS that bump (host-lint dep <code>1386e9a</code> v0.8.1 → <code>5a9d2c5</code> v0.10.2), so the shared offline bundle was regenerated: <code>cargo vendor --locked --sync ../../host-lifecycle/main/Cargo.toml vendor</code> from host-lint's manifest, deterministic tar (<code>--sort=name --mtime=@0 --owner=0 --group=0 --numeric-owner | gzip -n</code>), published as <strong><code>vendor-v2</code> on connollydavid/host-lint</strong> (sha <code>4e49536bf1ae45c88c2118f14cf823cf7854e781ec5536ed214afcebe8f8881c</code>). host-lifecycle's <code>deps-bundle</code> (<code>.host-software</code> + <code>deps-bundle.lock</code>) re-pinned to vendor-v2; <strong>host-lint stays on vendor-v1</strong> (its closure is unchanged and its build ignores the host-lint-crate entry, so v0.10.2 built fine on v1). Two release gotchas hit and resolved: (a) the first <code>release host-lifecycle</code> FAILED at the offline build because <code>.host-software</code> still pointed at vendor-v1 (no host-lint v0.10.2 source) — fix the deps-bundle pin BEFORE the release; (b) a release that fails after the version bump leaves <code>Cargo.toml</code>/<code>Cargo.lock</code> bumped (0.30.0→0.30.1), so re-running double-bumps to 0.30.2 — <strong>revert the version to 0.30.0 (keeping the dep change) before re-running</strong> so it lands on 0.30.1.</p> <p><strong>Vendor-leftover false-HAZARD (new gotcha).</strong> Producing the bundle left <code>vendor/</code> in <code>software/host-lint/main</code> (gitignored). <code>software --check</code>'s spec-lane scan (<code>find_specs</code>) walks the <strong>filesystem, not gitignore</strong>, so it found <code>vendor/host-grammar/spec/ParallelScan.tla</code> + the vendored <code>apalache:</code>/<code>tlaps:</code> obligations and raised 3 phantom HAZARDs against host-lint (whose OWN obligations are <code>kani:</code>-only, identical at both pins). <strong><code>rm -rf vendor vendor-config.toml</code> after producing a bundle</strong>, then re-check. Cleared immediately.</p> <p>Whole-suite: <code>.host-software</code> re-pinned (host-lint, host-lifecycle, host); host-template submodule pointer + host worktree bumped (their <code>prose.yml</code> CI moved <code>9a1a586</code> v0.24.2 → v0.30.1); agentic-host CI (reproducible-build ×2, mdbook) moved <code>94984a1</code> → <code>46d481c</code>; release receipts recorded; plan/0045 build sequence dogfooded as 5 anchored receipted tasks. <code>software --check</code> clean (all at pin, no hazards); local host-lifecycle reinstalled to v0.30.1. <strong>No spine change</strong> — the doctrine (a legitimate token stays in the per-project LEXICON, both lanes) is met, not changed; no UPGRADING entry. The deferred follow-up still stands (PLAN.md): enforce full GitHub URLs over bare <code>#N</code> refs.</p> <p><strong>plan/0045 follow-up — the kani-digest gotcha bit again (I should have pre-empted it).</strong> host-lint <strong>v0.10.2's CI was born red</strong> on the allium job's "Lint obligations (inputs not stale)" step: my <code>src/lib.rs</code> edit changed <code>git hash-object src/lib.rs</code>, so the two <code>DetectInternalCodeAsName</code> kani-obligation digests in <code>host-lint.obligations.digests</code> (<code>131dca5e…</code>, recorded at v0.10.1) went STALE. The kani PROOFS themselves passed (✓ kani job) and all six platform binaries built — only the staleness lint failed. This is the EXACT plan/0034 + plan/0044 warning ("after any host-lint source change, re-derive + re-record the kani digests BEFORE tagging"), and I tagged v0.10.2 without it. <strong>Fixed artifact-preserving</strong> (plan/0034 precedent): re-recorded the digests to the fresh <code>4698c20e0b254f3b7a361f7cac3d81c194bb1386</code> (the digests file is not compiled, so the binary stays <code>85c1fc58</code>), committed on host-lint main (<code>9d2d81d</code>, only <code>host-lint.obligations.digests</code> changed), and advanced the <code>.host-software</code> host-lint pin <code>5a9d2c5</code> → <code>9d2d81d</code> with the artifact hash UNCHANGED. host-lint main CI on <code>9d2d81d</code> is green. <strong>No cascade</strong>: the digest fix does not touch the host-lint LIBRARY code, so host-lifecycle stays embedding host-lint @ <code>5a9d2c5</code> (vendor-v2 still valid, no host-lifecycle re-release). The <strong>v0.10.2 TAG badge stays red</strong> on the obligations-lint job (the binaries published; moving a published tag is blocked by the auto-classifier), the same accepted state as plan/0034's red v0.9.0 tag — main is green at the pinned commit and the recorded artifact is the released one. The digest was recorded directly (not via <code>obligations --rederive</code>, whose local host-prove→<code>cargo kani</code> spawn still ENOENTs on the <code>/mnt/c</code> WSL mount); sound because <code>git hash-object</code> is deterministic and the proof is independently CI-verified. Whole-suite green: host-lint main <code>9d2d81d</code>, host-lifecycle v0.30.1, host-template + host Prose, agentic-host Site + Reproducible build (<code>f293999</code>, the <code>--verify-build</code> reproduces <code>85c1fc58</code> and <code>23c27bff</code> offline).</p> <p><strong>Promoted the digest fix to a clean tag (operator asked "should we re-release?" — yes, per precedent).</strong> The artifact-preserving pin-advance left the pinned commit (<code>9d2d81d</code>) UNtagged and the v0.10.2 tag badge red, but plan/0034 and plan/0044 both PROMOTED the interim digest fix into a clean tagged point release rather than leaving the red tag (v0.9.0→v0.9.1, v0.10.0→v0.10.1). Followed that: released <strong>host-lint v0.10.3</strong> (<code>63348a6</code>, new artifact <code>753ac4f6</code> — the version string changes the binary; digests stay fresh since <code>src/lib.rs</code> is untouched by a version bump), tagged at the digest-fixed source so its CI is green, and re-pinned <code>.host-software</code> to it. <strong>No cascade</strong>: host-lifecycle stays embedding host-lint @ <code>5a9d2c5</code> (v0.10.2 library, engine-identical to v0.10.3), so no vendor-v3 and no host-lifecycle re-release; the host-lint TOOL pin (v0.10.3) and host-lifecycle's embedded LIBRARY (v0.10.2) legitimately differ, as host-lifecycle sat on host-lint v0.8.1 while the tool was at v0.10.1. <strong>Lesson reinforced: re-derive kani digests BEFORE the first tag</strong> and the whole v0.10.x detour disappears. Pinned release is now host-lint <strong>v0.10.3</strong> / <code>63348a6</code> / <code>753ac4f6</code>.</p> <h2 id="2026-06-26--plan0046-complete-the-generated-book-moves-to-mdbook-freeing-docs-host-lifecycle3"><a class="header" href="#2026-06-26--plan0046-complete-the-generated-book-moves-to-mdbook-freeing-docs-host-lifecycle3">2026-06-26 — plan/0046 COMPLETE: the generated book moves to mdBook/, freeing docs/ (host-lifecycle#3)</a></h2> <p><code>connollydavid/host-lifecycle#3</code> closed. The reporter filed it as "<code>book</code> clobbers a tracked <code>docs/</code>" (a destructive-<code>book</code> data-loss bug).
